Friday, June 24, 2022 

8:26 AM 

Human 

Adaptive. Cooperative. Enduring. 

  

Fynn's eyes snap open as he draws in a deep breath. He feels his rifle slip away and just barely manages to grab it before it falls over the side of his hunting stand. He quickly looks around. The forest is different, but something about himself feels off as well. He feels stronger and tougher than before. His limbs move with much greater force than when he climbed this stand mere hours ago. He's still wearing the same clothes, but they no longer fit properly. The young man grabs his rifle, climbs down from his stand, and looks around. He checks his phone and when the screen remains dark he knows that something is wrong.  

— Fynn's awakening in the New World 

  

Adaptable and unpredictable, humans have a body that can form to fit any need and a mind that is pushed towards no particular alignment. 

  

Widely Varied 

Humans have a variety of different physiques, ranging from tall and gaunt, to small and plump. Their musculature is usually covered by a protective layer of fat. Their bodies are built to adapt and outlast. For their size, they can take a substantial amount of punishment before their bodies fail them, but aside from this natural resilience, their body is made to be molded. It can be trained to prioritize raw strength over endurance, hardiness over intelligence, or whatever is needed of it. Adaptability is its greatest strength, something that allows humans to inhabit every niche with at least some success. This variance also makes it impossible accurately to predict any individual human’s capabilities. Their physical adaptability is paired with an equally adaptable mind. New skills can be easily picked up and either performed decently or perfected over a long period of time. 

Humans are between 1.5 meters (5 feet) and just over 2 meters (6 feet) tall. Adult human females are, on average, substantially physically weaker and smaller than their male counterparts, but can endure hardship for longer. Humans can have a wide variety of skin tones ranging from pale white to ebony black. 

Keen Senses and a Keen Mind 

Humans have good senses across the board. Their eyes see in high detail across a wide spectrum of colors, with good depth perception, but sub-par low-light vision. This lack of darkvision is the only real, glaring weakness in the human species that can be easily exploited by most other creatures, sapient or not. Their hearing and sense of smell are decent. 

Just like all their other features, the human mind is adaptable. It can't process information particularly fast, or retain it particularly well, however, it can do both and everything else at least passably well. 

Humans have a gender ratio of one to one. After puberty, humans grow coarse hair almost all over their bodies and females have a monthly menstruation cycle. This adds to human adaptability, as they can decide to reproduce whenever it is convenient, though limits their fertility up to a certain age. Human children are born after a 9-month pregnancy. They rely on their mother for sustenance for the first year and fully mature after about 20 years. After reaching around thirty, their physical capabilities and health start to deteriorate as they age. Humans can live to be up to 100 years old. 

Built for Adaptability  

Humans benefit and suffer from a body built for everything. As omnivores, humans can eat a wide variety of food and technically even survive on a completely vegetarian diet. As humans are unable to produce certain vitamins, they must consume fresh fruits regularly or suffer serious illness. 

Humans are particularly resistant to starvation and can survive it for abnormally long periods of time, without being too crippled by its effects. Considering their size, durability, endurance, and intelligence, humans can also survive on surprisingly small amounts of energy, only increasing their survivability. In times of plenty, they can gather reserves that are then expended during times of need. This makes it easier for humans to survive dire times without their numbers substantially decreasing and once conditions become better, they are always in the perfect position to take full advantage of whatever is offered them. However, there is no niche they fill, and those who are made to live in a specific niche always outperform them. 

 

Friday, June 24, 2022 

8:26 AM 

Human 

Adaptive. Cooperative. Enduring. 

  

Fynn's eyes snap open as he draws in a deep breath. He feels his rifle slip away and just barely manages to grab it before it falls over the side of his hunting stand. He quickly looks around. The forest is different, but something about himself feels off as well. He feels stronger and tougher than before. His limbs move with much greater force than when he climbed this stand mere hours ago. He's still wearing the same clothes, but they no longer fit properly. The young man grabs his rifle, climbs down from his stand, and looks around. He checks his phone and when the screen remains dark he knows that something is wrong.  

— Fynn's awakening in the New World 

  

Adaptable and unpredictable, humans have a body that can form to fit any need and a mind that is pushed towards no particular alignment. 

  

Widely Varied 

Humans have a variety of different physiques, ranging from tall and gaunt, to small and plump. Their musculature is usually covered by a protective layer of fat. Their bodies are built to adapt and outlast. For their size, they can take a substantial amount of punishment before their bodies fail them, but aside from this natural resilience, their body is made to be molded. It can be trained to prioritize raw strength over endurance, hardiness over intelligence, or whatever is needed of it. Adaptability is its greatest strength, something that allows humans to inhabit every niche with at least some success. This variance also makes it impossible accurately to predict any individual human’s capabilities. Their physical adaptability is paired with an equally adaptable mind. New skills can be easily picked up and either performed decently or perfected over a long period of time. 

Humans are between 1.5 meters (5 feet) and just over 2 meters (6 feet) tall. Adult human females are, on average, substantially physically weaker and smaller than their male counterparts, but can endure hardship for longer. Humans can have a wide variety of skin tones ranging from pale white to ebony black. 

Keen Senses and a Keen Mind 

Humans have good senses across the board. Their eyes see in high detail across a wide spectrum of colors, with good depth perception, but sub-par low-light vision. This lack of darkvision is the only real, glaring weakness in the human species that can be easily exploited by most other creatures, sapient or not. Their hearing and sense of smell are decent. 

Just like all their other features, the human mind is adaptable. It can't process information particularly fast, or retain it particularly well, however, it can do both and everything else at least passably well. 

Humans have a gender ratio of one to one. After puberty, humans grow coarse hair almost all over their bodies and females have a monthly menstruation cycle. This adds to human adaptability, as they can decide to reproduce whenever it is convenient, though limits their fertility up to a certain age. Human children are born after a 9-month pregnancy. They rely on their mother for sustenance for the first year and fully mature after about 20 years. After reaching around thirty, their physical capabilities and health start to deteriorate as they age. Humans can live to be up to 100 years old. 

Built for Adaptability  

Humans benefit and suffer from a body built for everything. As omnivores, humans can eat a wide variety of food and technically even survive on a completely vegetarian diet. As humans are unable to produce certain vitamins, they must consume fresh fruits regularly or suffer serious illness. 

Humans are particularly resistant to starvation and can survive it for abnormally long periods of time, without being too crippled by its effects. Considering their size, durability, endurance, and intelligence, humans can also survive on surprisingly small amounts of energy, only increasing their survivability. In times of plenty, they can gather reserves that are then expended during times of need. This makes it easier for humans to survive dire times without their numbers substantially decreasing and once conditions become better, they are always in the perfect position to take full advantage of whatever is offered them. However, there is no niche they fill, and those who are made to live in a specific niche always outperform them. 

Cooperate and Conquer  

Humanity is a highly cooperative species and individuals are usually willing to endure substantial hardship for the good of the community, or some other ideal. This makes it easy for humans to form massive organizations that can act quickly and decisively. The ability to organize further strengthens and compliments human adaptability and is ultimately their greatest strength. While freedom and independence are usually sought after, most humans abandon these ideals when it is pragmatic or convenient. This ability to work together is a great strength, but can also be an exploitable weakness. Most humans as individuals are not very ambitious and are satisfied with a convenient life that satisfies their needs, as long as they are not worse off than their peers. A trait that compliments their ability to work together to create stable, large-scale societies. However, at the same time they produce extremely ambitious individuals that dedicate themselves to certain goals with supernatural zeal. This extreme variance means that there is always a human available that is ready to face whatever challenge there is. 

Blank Slates 

While they are often individualistic, humans always come together in times of strife. They yearn for structure and routine and to control their surroundings, yet at the same time want to be free of constraints and go against social norms. As humans naturally tend to be both individualistic, yet at the same time strive for order, they alternate between chaos and lawfulness, making them neutral. Humans tend to avoid conflict but will gladly kill other creatures and each other if it's convenient. 

Humans are selfish, but empathetic creatures that usually tend to act in their own self-interest. However, they naturally empathize with the pain of others and usually dislike inflicting it. They also tend to be competitive, but usually feel discomfort when seeing others suffer and naturally want to help those weaker than themselves. As humans are constantly split towards their own selfishness and empathy they tend toward the true neutral alignment. Humans are unique in that their tendencies cancel each other out. Their instincts push them in every and thus no direction, giving them the ability to adapt to changing conditions with little effort. 

  

Human Features 

As a human your survivability and superb capability to adapt grant you several abilities.  

  

Physique 

Ability Score Increase. An ability score of your choice increases by 1. 

Size. Humans are between 1.5 meters (5 feet) and just over 2 meters (6 feet) tall and have a wide variety of physiques. Your size is Medium. 

Encumberment. Your maximum encumberment equals your Strength score multiplied by 4. 

Base Armor Class. 10 

Speed. Your speed equals 10 meters (30 feet).  

Age. You reach physical maturity around 20 and can live to be around 100 years old. 

  

Health 

Hit Dice. Your Hit Die is a d8. You start at 0th level with 2d8 and gain an additional d8 every level. 

Hit Points at 0th level. 9 (or 2d8) + your Constitution modifier. 

Hit Points at Higher LeveIs. 5 (or 1d8) + your Constitution modifier every level after 0th. 

  

Vitality 

Vitality at 0th level. 11 (or 2d8+2). 

Vitality at Higher LeveIs. 6 (or 1d8+1) every level after 0th. 

  

Survival 

Night's Rest. Sleep for 8 hours and expend 3 rations to gain the benefits of a night's rest. 

Long Rest. Spend 7 days performing light activity and expend 21 rations to gain the benefits of a long rest. 

Nutrition. You are an omnivore and require a diet that includes both meat, fruits, and vegetables. You cannot survive on any one food source (without serious illness). 

Hunger. You may begin a rest without expending rations from your inventory. If you do, increase your hunger by an amount equal to the rations not expended. Refer to the Human Starvation table to see at what hunger level you gain what level of exhaustion (see the condition). 

  

Human Starvation 

Hunger 

	

Exhaustion 

	

Hunger 

	

Exhaustion 




8 

	

Level 1 

	

54 

	

Level 4 




30 

	

Level 2 

	

66 

	

Level 5 




42 

	

Level 3 

	

90 

	

Level 6 

  

Predisposition  

As a human you love self-expression, yet also need structure in your life causing you to tend both toward the chaotic and the lawful alignment. Humans feel empathy and usually don't want to cause other creatures harm, yet are also selfish and as much prone to random acts of kindness as sadism. As a human, you tend towards the true neutral alignment. 

  

Special Traits 

Human Endurance. Increase your starting vitality by 2 and the vitality you gain every level by 1 (already included in Vitality). 

Ethnicity. Your patron's ethnicity determines their looks and culture. Choose one from the options provided. 

  

A New Old Body 

When you remain human, your body is reassembled mostly as it was before the fog. Regardless of your patron's gender, you will retain your old gender and most of your looks. However, as the capabilities of your body are altered to fit those of your patron, you may still experience massive changes to your physique. For example, a terminally ill obese man who has a strong female barbarian as his patron will remain a man but will find his form altered to be as athletic and healthy as that of his patron. 

  

  

Human Ethnicities 

Different environments cause different cultures and adaptations in humans to emerge.  

  

Border Lords 

In ships or caravans, these people travel across the world. From sanctuary to sanctuary they go, spreading the will of their gods in exchange for divine blessings and boons. They originally hail from the deep south, however, due to their openness and tendency to adopt the children of slain foes, they have heavily mixed with other groups and cultures in their travels. Still, most border lords have brown skin. 

The only places these folk settle are where a divine sanctuary is found and then only to guard the sacred site against desecration. They are known for their use of divine magic and the chaos they spread wherever they go. Family and personal honor are central to border lord society. Most either serve a god directly as a prophet of that god’s church or the community through a paladin order. The border lords actively hunt all things undead and have always despised the elves, who many of them see as forsaken by the gods or demonic in nature, due to their resistance to divine influence. This animosity has cooled somewhat since the fall of their empire, as the Confederation and its lich-emperor provided a new foe. 

Ability Score Increase. Your Charisma score increases by 1. 

Predisposition. Most border lords are paladins or prophets. 

Divine Mission. You gain proficiency in the Performance and Persuasion skills. 

  

Campestrians 

These men are the most attentive and peaceful of their kind. 

The campestrians serve their draconic masters with incredible zeal. Some campestrians are governed by lords, some by councils or parliaments. All of them are ruled by dragons. Their societies are built around their flying overlords and are made to bend around their wills. They craft them commodities and construct their masters beautiful lairs and monuments. In exchange, the dragons keep their lands safe and defend them in times of war. Their strongest warriors are those who protect those they serve. It is tradition that humans wield no weapons in the presence of a dragon and these warriors are no exception. They fight with nothing but their bodies and guard their masters with supernatural awareness. However, in recent times, dragons have begun using s1pellcasting riders to increase their own effectiveness in combat, spawning a whole new cast of dragon rider mages. 

Ability Score Increase. Your Dexterity score increases by 1. 

Predisposition. Most campestrian warriors are mages or monks. 

Artisans. You gain proficiency in the Acrobatics and Insight skills. 

  

Confederates 

These men are the strongest and most militant of their kind. 

Under the leadership of a man turned lich, these folk freed themselves from their masters and ground the elven empire into dust. From its ashes rose the Confederation and tore across the land. Despite his defeat at the Avalanche Pass, the emperor's presence ensured the loyalty of the city-states that formed the Confederation, until recently. Even without their undying lord, the legions march forth to conquer and defend, while at home the common folk toil to support their state. Over all this watches the unblinking eye of the Association of Mages with their scribes, magisters, and enforcers. The unity provided by a single lord has caused trade and enterprise to flourish, and allowed many commoners to accumulate vast fortunes. The people of the Confederation took their place in the world by force and as they move into the future, those that stand before them will join their ranks or fear their marching feet. 

Ability Score Increase. Your Strength score increases by 1. 

Predisposition. Most confederate humans are fighters or mages. 

Legion Training. You gain proficiency in the Athletics and Persuasion skills. 

  

Desert Folk 

These men are the most intellectual and artistic of their kind. 

After their arrival in the Other World, humans settled on the coasts of the great desert. The sun scorches these lands free of monsters and those few horrors that can survive the endless dunes of sand are easily kept at bay with walls of stone. Some of these humans lived as nomads, but most joined the elves in their great cities of stone. The elves here had developed a vibrant culture of artistry and commerce which they readily incorporated the new arrivals into. Once the elven empire fell the elven aristocracy fled and were replaced by human kings. They did not expect an attack from their brothers, now that the elves were gone. They were wrong and without an effective military, they were forced to join the Confederation. The kings of the sands became governors of the lich now called a god emperor and their prosperity increased within the union. Yet they still plot and scheme, ready to purge themselves of their new ruler when the opportunity arises. 

Ability Score Increase. Your Intelligence score increases by 1. 

Predisposition. Most desert folk humans are fighters or rogues. 

Universal Education. You gain proficiency in the History and Investigation skills. 

  

Nords 

These men are the toughest and most enduring of their kind. 

The nords live in the forests and tundras of the deep north. Their lands are inhospitable and winters are even longer than in other regions. While the Other World's long years mean that the few really warm weeks of the year make it possible for many hardy plants to populate those regions, the humans of these regions have to endure long periods of extreme scarcity, broken by periods of plenty. To help them through those times of need, many set out in ships to sail the oceans, either as traders or as raiders. They are built for enduring the harsh conditions of their homelands and the equally bad conditions on the high seas and bolster their forces with witchcraft. 

Ability Score Increase. Your Constitution score increases by 1. 

Predisposition. Most nord humans are barbarians or witches. 

Northern Star. You gain proficiency in the Intimidation and Survival skills. 

  

Wildeborn 

These men are the wildest, most in tune with nature of their kind. 

Most of the Other World is covered in endless, primeval forests and the wildeborn are those humans that inhabit these wild lands. The long years mean that these regions have long and warm summers and suffer from equally long and freezing winters. This kind of environment breeds many dangerous predators and the abundance of plant life provides the perfect environment for small enclaves of dryads. However, the marshes are also breeding grounds for insects and the diseases they carry, especially during the warm summers. The humans that inhabit these lands must navigate these dangers and the land itself. They usually live in small, fortified villages, surrounded by fields, that have no overarching government. All of these people train as warriors, relying on spears, bows, and arrows to defeat the horrors of the dark forests. Despite their lack of a central government, they have a group of elite fighters that is famous even beyond their wild lands. These rangers train in secluded places. Bolstered with magical weapons and potions, they wander along the path. It leads them from place to place where they assist the secluded communities with everything from particularly dangerous monsters, ailments, adjudication, and keeping the gene pool from becoming stale. 

Ability Score Increase. Your Wisdom score increases by 1. 

Predisposition. Most wildeborn are rangers or witches of the alchemy coven. 

Wilderness Survival. You gain proficiency in the Survival and Medicine skills. 

  

Variant Human 

Humans travel far and wide and there is much overlap between different human societies. Humans of mixed heritage or that are part of smaller groups not large enough to be considered an ethnicity make up a substantial part of the Other World’s human population. 

Ability Score Increase. An ability score of your choice increases by 1. 

Skill. You gain proficiency in one skill of your choice. 

  

  

 

 

 

 

 

 

Archive 

 

Grit. You can survive starvation and thirst for longer than other species (already included in your starvation table). 

 

 

Language. You can speak, read, and write Draconic. Draconic is melodious language that sounds deep and terrifying when spoken out loud. It has a very low number of different sounds, which are all growls, hisses and huffs of different length and volume. Draconic uses the dwarven alphabet. 

 

Conflicting change.

Sunday, April 25, 2021 

8:54 AM 

Half-Humans 

Diverse. Unique. Rare. 

 

Among the many humanoid species that inhabit the Other World and the New, hybridization between different species is possible, but it is limited to humans and specific races like elves, dwarves, and giants. The physiology of these species shares enough similarities that interbreeding is possible, resulting in hybrid offspring with unique combinations of traits. However, most hybrids are sterile due to the genetic differences between their parents' species. The rare exceptions are the result of genetic mutations that allow for fertile offspring. Due to the rare nature of these mutations, these hybrids will usually have children with a pure-blood, mixing trace amounts of other species into the gene pools of some species, which makes future hybridization more viable. Half humans that wish to have their own children also have the option of copulating with a wildling, which can almost always be successful. Female hybrids also have the option of having a changeling child. 

Humanity's endurance and adaptability are its most dominant traits and all hybrids share this efficient physiology. This rarely leaves any opportunity for other specie's traits to modify the diet or sleeping requirements imposed by the human nature. 

 

Half-Human Features 

As a hybrid human, your human heritage forms the basis of your biology and provides you with the following traits regardless of your non-human parent.   

Physique 

Ability Score Increase. An ability score of your choice increases by 1. 

Size. Half-Humans are between 1 meters (3 feet) and just below 3 meters (10 feet) tall, depending on the species of their non-human parent. Your size is Medium. 

Encumberment. Your maximum encumberment equals your Strength score multiplied by 4. 

Base Armor Class. 10 

Speed. Your speed equals 10 meters (30 feet).  

  

Health 

Hit Dice. Your Hit Die is a d8. You start at 0th level with 2d8 and gain an additional d8 every level. 

Hit Points at 0th level. 9 (or 2d8) + your Constitution modifier. 

Hit Points at Higher LeveIs. 5 (or 1d8) + your Constitution modifier every level after 0th. 

  

Vitality 

Vitality at 0th level. 11 (or 2d8+2). 

Vitality at Higher LeveIs. 6 (or 1d8+1) every level after 0th. 

  

Survival 

Night's Rest. Sleep for 8 hours and expend 3 rations to gain the benefits of a night's rest. 

Long Rest. Spend 7 days performing light activity and expend 21 rations to gain the benefits of a long rest. 

Nutrition. You are an omnivore and require a diet that includes both meat, fruits, and vegetables. You cannot survive on any one food source (without serious illness). 

Hunger. You may begin a rest without expending rations from your inventory. If you do, increase your hunger by an amount equal to the rations not expended. Refer to the Starvation table of your non-human parent to see at what hunger level you gain what level of exhaustion (see the condition). 

  

Special Traits 

Hybrid Endurance. Increase your starting vitality by 2 and the vitality you gain every level by 1 (already included in Vitality).

Half-Humans 

Diverse. Unique. Rare. 

 

Among the many humanoid species that inhabit the Other World and the New, hybridization between different species is possible, but it is limited to humans and specific races like elves, dwarves, and giants. The physiology of these species shares enough similarities that interbreeding is possible, resulting in hybrid offspring with unique combinations of traits. However, most hybrids are sterile due to the genetic differences between their parents' species. The rare exceptions are the result of genetic mutations that allow for fertile offspring. Due to the rare nature of these mutations, these hybrids will usually have children with a pure-blood, mixing trace amounts of other species into the gene pools of some species, which makes future hybridization more viable. Half humans that wish to have their own children also have the option of copulating with a wildling, which can almost always be successful. Female hybrids also have the option of having a changeling child. 

Humanity's endurance and adaptability are its most dominant traits and all hybrids share this efficient physiology. This rarely leaves any opportunity for other specie's traits to modify the diet or sleeping requirements imposed by the human nature. 

 

Half-Human Features 

As a hybrid human, your human heritage forms the basis of your biology and provides you with the following traits regardless of your non-human parent.   

Physique 

Ability Score Increase. An ability score of your choice increases by 1. 

Size. Half-Humans are between 1 meters (3 feet) and just below 3 meters (10 feet) tall, depending on the species of their non-human parent. Your size is Medium. 

Encumberment. Your maximum encumberment equals your Strength score multiplied by 4. 

Base Armor Class. 10 

Speed. Your speed equals 10 meters (30 feet).  

  

Health 

Hit Dice. Your Hit Die is a d8. You start at 0th level with 2d8 and gain an additional d8 every level. 

Hit Points at 0th level. 9 (or 2d8) + your Constitution modifier. 

Hit Points at Higher LeveIs. 5 (or 1d8) + your Constitution modifier every level after 0th. 

  

Vitality 

Vitality at 0th level. 11 (or 2d8+2). 

Vitality at Higher LeveIs. 6 (or 1d8+1) every level after 0th. 

  

Survival 

Night's Rest. Sleep for 8 hours and expend 3 rations to gain the benefits of a night's rest. 

Long Rest. Spend 7 days performing light activity and expend 21 rations to gain the benefits of a long rest. 

Nutrition. You are an omnivore and require a diet that includes both meat, fruits, and vegetables. You cannot survive on any one food source (without serious illness). 

Hunger. You may begin a rest without expending rations from your inventory. If you do, increase your hunger by an amount equal to the rations not expended. Refer to the Starvation table of your non-human parent to see at what hunger level you gain what level of exhaustion (see the condition). 

  

Special Traits 

Hybrid Endurance. Increase your starting vitality by 2 and the vitality you gain every level by 1 (already included in Vitality). 

Non-Human Heritage. Your patron's non-human parent determines their looks and broader physiology. If a trait, ability, or prerequisite requires you to be a specific species you are eligible if either of your parent species would fulfill requirement. Choose one from the options provided. 

 

Nonhuman Parent 

The species of the nonhuman parent grants the hybrid several traits on top of what their human side provides. Humans can hybridize with dwarves, elves, and giants. The traits each hybrid provides are listed below. 

 

Dwarf-Human Hybrid 

Human-Dwarf hybrids, often referred to as "Dwarman" or "Halfdwarves," result from the union between a human and a dwarf. These hybrids have the facial hair of their dwarven parent, with both genders having a beard, making them easily distinguishable from purebred humans. However, aside from this feature, they are difficult to tell apart from short and stout humans. Most halfdwarves are typically between just over 1 meter (3 feet) to a little over 1.5 meters (5 feet) tall, and inherit their dwarven parent's resistance to poison and their ability to navigate by vibrations. On average, they also tend to live slightly longer than purebred humans. While halfdwarves are usually sterile due to the genetic differences between humans and dwarves, there are rare cases where they can reproduce with humans, dwarves, or even with each other. 

In the Other World, halfdwarves are quite rare, especially in dwarven communities due to how difficult it is for a dwarven woman to give birth to a halfdwarf baby, due of the size of its head. In mountain dwarf society, the treatment of those few halfdwarves that are born depends heavily on their clan. Some clans outright kill any halfdwarf children, while others accept them readily into their society. Halfling and human communities, on the other hand, are usually quite accepting of these hybrids, seeing them as unique individuals with the potential to bring together different cultures and perspectives. 

Ability Score Increase. Your Intelligence score increases by 1. 

Age. You reach physical maturity around 20 and can live to be up to 150 years old. 

Predisposition. Your dwarven heritage intensifies the lawful tendencies of your human side and causes you to tend towards a lawful alignment. Aside from that the neutral alignment predispositions of your parents complement each other. 

Dwarven Resilience. You have advantage on saving throws against poison, and you have resistance against poison damage (explained in the Combat section). 

Tremorsense. You have the ability to detect creatures by the vibrations they create in the ground upon which they tread. As long as you are in contact with a solid surface, any creature in contact with the same surface no more than 10 meters (30 feet) away is revealed to you. 

 

Halfdwarf Starvation

Elf-Human Hybrid 

Halfelves are the result of unions between elves and humans, possessing a unique blend of elven grace and human versatility. They inherit pointed ears from their elven parent and share their agility, while also having the endurance and adaptability of humans. Halfelves are often difficult to distinguish from humans, with only yellow streaks in their irises and slightly pointed ears making them different from a particularly athletic human. While they are exclusively female, they are capable of bearing male children with both humans and elves. Though the chance remains just as slim as with all others who bear the iceborn curse within their blood. 

In the elven communities of the Other World, halfelves are viewed as lesser versions of elves, worthy of scorn and derision. In fact, the term "halfelf" is considered a slur, signifying their perceived impurity and lack of worth. Female elves who carry a halfelf to term face exile for muddling the bloodline and sullying the purity of the elven race. 

Human communities, on the other hand, tend to be much more accepting of halfelves. In fact, some humans view them as a great boon to their bloodline, as the infusion of elven blood counteracts humanity's greatest weaknesses: its inability to see in the dark by granting halfelves elven infravision and a humanity's relatively short lifespan by almost doubling it. There are even those who believe that halfelves are a superior version of humans. In secret, they work on creating the perfect blend of man and elf that has the benefits of its elven heritage, but is still capable of creating a stable bloodline. 

Ability Score Increase. Your Dexterity score increases by 1. 

Age. You reach physical maturity around 25 and can live to be up to 200 years old. 

Predisposition. Your elvish side amplifies the side of your human nature that yearns for self-expression and freedom, causing you to tend towards the chaotic alignment. Your human side provides you with a natural sense of empathy, making it easier to overcome the selfish tendencies of your elven side, causing you tend towards the neutral alignment. 

Infravision. You can see in dim light, darkness and absolute darkness within 30 meters (90 feet) of you as if it were bright light and camouflage, smoke and fog do not impede your vision. You can't discern color in darkness, smoke, or fog, only shades of red and are unable to see the color red outside of your infravision. You cannot learn to read any language you didn't know before your transformation, except Elvan, and can't use non-elvan skill books. 

Resolute. You have advantage on saving throws against being charmed. 

 

Halfelf Starvation

Giant-Human Hybrid 

When born to a jotunn, a human-giant hybrid is referred to as a "Goliath," while their orcish counterpart is known as a "Halforc." However, the general term "Halfgiants" is often used to refer to all kinds of giant hybrids. 

Thanks to their giant lineage, halfgiants possess impressive size and strength. Their robustness and temperament also come from their giant side, but they also inherit the adaptability and versatility of humans. Although they often resemble massive humans, they possess heavily pigmented and thick skin, which is a distinct characteristic inherited from their giant lineage. While male halfgiants typically do not develop horns, some may develop a thick brow from which the horns would usually grow in a full-blooded giant. Standing between well over 2 meters (6 feet) to just under 3 meters (9 feet) tall, their thick bones and natural musculature make them appear smaller from a distance but all the more imposing up close. Despite their giant blood, halfgiants are unable to digest grass. Although most are sterile due to the genetic differences between humans and giants, there are rare cases of halfgiants reproducing with humans, giants, or even with each other. When born to a human mother, the birth of a halfgiant is similar to that of a pure-bred human child. 

In the Other World, halfgiants are rare but are perhaps the most common hybrid. Due to the alliance between the confederate humans and the orcs, there was a substantial amount of crossbreeding. Halfgiants are highly valued as mercenaries in both communities. For giants, a halfgiant can access areas they are too large for, while remaining cable of keeping up with other giants. For humans they provide abnormal strength and bulk. Thanks to their on average impressive physical attributes, and their lack of fertility makes them popular as short-term partners with both humans and giants. While most halfgiants live in human communities, giants are generally also indifferent to relationships between humans and giants. 

Ability Score Increase. Your Strength score increases by 1. 

Age. You reach physical maturity around 20 and can live to be around 100 years old. 

Predisposition. The general freedom oriented outlook of giants and their heard instincts combine with your human ones, causing you to tend towards a chaotic alignment. The tendency to meet violence with violence and kindness with kindness of your giant side compliments the same human tendency and causes you to strongly favor the neutral alignment. 

Brave. You have advantage on saving throws against being frightened. 

Darkvision. You can see in dim light within 20 meters (60 feet) of you as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You can't discern color in darkness, only shades of gray. 

Powerful Build. You count as one size larger when determining your carrying capacity, the weight you can push, drag, or lift, and the creatures you can grapple.

Harpy 

Sunday, May 9, 2021 

1:17 PM 

Harpy 

Curious. Keen. Talented. 

  

Kathrin snaps open her eyes. She sees the sky as a blurred blue mess through her glasses. The metal frame is pressing against the side of her skull, but it feels dull, as though there is some sort of fabric covering her head. Behind her, the young woman can feel the cold, hard touch of the parking lot pavement against her back. She tries to move her arms and immediately realizes that her fingers feel stretched and heavy. As she lifts them to take off her glasses it's as though she's moving large fans. Her hands completely block out the sky. She fingers for her glasses and pulls them away with her thumb and index finger, both of which feel way too long.  

She stifles a scream. Her body is covered in brown feathers. Her hands are large wings and her body more resembles a bird than that of a human. 

— Kathrin's awakening in the New World 

  

Harpies are humanoids with feathers, dexterous claws on their legs and wings instead of arms. Their eyes are excellent and their voices unmatched. 

  

Avian Folk 

Harpies have humanoid bodies, with large chests and big wings instead of arms. Their body is covered in feathers that can have a variety of different colors but usually range from white to brown. Their shins and feet are naked and from them extend sharp, dexterous claws that can be used to grab prey and manipulate objects. These claws are more like hands, with several joints each and a wide range of movement. Since their shin is so long, they can reach up to their face. In addition to these claws, harpies have a long and strong thumb and a shorter index finger in the middle of their wings that is usually used to hold on to things or manipulate objects while on the ground. 

The wings of harpies are relatively small when compared to their body, but are flexible. This makes long-distance sustained flight taxing since the harpy can't glide without constantly generating more lift by beating their wings. It does however allow for fast and precise maneuvering. By closing their wings halfway and beating them with high frequency, a harpy can even temporarily hover almost in place for a few seconds. 

Mimicry  

Harpies are equipped with very good eyes and ears. As long as there is enough light, they can see in high fidelity over vast distances. They are smart, though their minds focus more on processing information quickly than it does on understanding it. They are great at thinking on their feet and very perceptive, however when it comes to logical thinking and making complicated deductions they usually fall short. 

Harpies can hear and produce very high sounds. Their ears are covered by feathers, but extend down and to the back of their heads. Their voices are universally considered beautiful, as even without any training at all they can modulate it well. While they often have trouble recalling events and pictures harpies can memorize sounds and reproduce them next to perfectly with ease.  

Feathered Mammals 

Despite their appearance, harpies are mammals with an equal amount of males and females. Young harpies are born featherless and first grow fluffy grey or brown feathers with bright spots. They learn to fly between the age of three and four. When they reach puberty after the age of 14 the last of their fluffy feathers are replaced with their smooth and colored adult coats. Females have a monthly menstruation cycle and carry children for 9 months before birth. Harpies fully mature after about 20 years. After reaching around thirty, their physical capabilities and health start to deteriorate as they age. 

A harpy can live for up 100 years. 

What color and kind of feather coat is considered attractive varies between harpy communities, but all of them love singing and find strong and beautiful voices attractive.

Feathered Mammals

A harpy can live for up 100 years. 

What color and kind of feather coat is considered attractive varies between harpy communities, but all of them love singing and find strong and beautiful voices attractive. 

Built for Flight 

Harpies are lightly built and can survive on little. However, they are dependent on a constant supply of food. Harpies can eat almost everything, from roots and berries to animals they kill. They are however by nature predators and prefer the taste of meat to that of other food. 

Harpies are social creatures. In the Other World groups of them would often swarm potential prey. Using their voices they would lure victims into the open and then either attack them directly or preferably pull them off a ledge to fall to their deaths. 

Harpies require 8 hours of sleep per day. Even while asleep their talons can retain a grip, allowing them to sleep upright on a perch. 

Unleashed Talent 

Harpies are attentive and pay attention to everything that's happening around them, including what other humanoids are doing. They can almost be called nosy. From above they watch what the creatures below do and few harpies have a problem circling an interesting area, or sitting in a good vantage point for hours waiting for something to happen. Their patience and ability to spot details are natural. Despite their patience, most harpies dislike thinking and deliberating for very long. When a problem appears, they act quickly and decisively by nature and whether that decision was right or wrong will probably be realized later, or ignored completely. 

Harpies have an innate passion for song and the ability to easily memorize and mimic almost any sound makes them excellent musicians. Few harpies will miss an opportunity to show off their vocal skills. 

  

Harpy Features 

As a harpy, your wings, keen eyesight, and unique physique grant you several natural abilities. 

  

Physique 

Ability Score Increase. Your Charisma score increases by 1.  

Ability Score Decrease. Your Constitution score decreases by 1. 

Size. Harpies are from around 1.5 meters to 2 meters tall. Your size is Medium. 

Encumberment. Your maximum encumberment equals your Strength score multiplied by 4. 

Base Armor Class. 10 

Speed. Your speed equals 6 meters (20 ft.).  

Flight. You have a flying speed of 15 meters (50 ft.). To use this speed, you can’t be wearing medium or heavy armor. 

Age. You reach physical maturity around 20 and can live to be to be up to 100 years old. 

  

Health 

Hit Dice. Your Hit Die is a d8. You start at 0th level with 2d8 and gain an additional d8 every level. 

Hit Points at 0th level. 9 (or 2d8) + your Constitution modifier. 

Hit Points at Higher LeveIs. 5 (or 1d8) + your Constitution modifier every level after 0th. 

  

Vitality 

Vitality at 0th level. 9 (or 2d8). 

Vitality at Higher LeveIs. 5 (or 1d8) every level after 0th. 

  

Survival 

Night's Rest. Sleep for 8 hours and expend 3 rations to gain the benefits of a night's rest. 

Long Rest. Spend 7 days performing light activity and expend 21 rations to gain the benefits of a long rest. 

Nutrition. You are an omnivore and require a diet that includes both meat, fruits, and vegetables. You cannot survive indefinitely on any one food source (without serious illness). 

Hunger. You may begin a rest without expending rations from your inventory. If you do, increase your hunger by an amount equal to the rations not expended. Refer to the Harpy Starvation table to see at what hunger level you gain what level of exhaustion (see the condition).  

  

Harpy Starvation 

Hunger 

	

Exhaustion 

	

Hunger 

	

Exhaustion 




5 

	

Level 1 

	

36 

	

Level 4 




20 

	

Level 2 

	

44 

	

Level 5 




28 

	

Level 3 

	

60 

	

Level 6 

  

Predisposition  

As a harpy you are emotional and tend to act quickly and decisively, causing you to tend toward the chaotic alignment. Many harpies come across as arrogant and aloof, but you probably won't actually feel that way. You have predatory instincts, but an innate need to cooperate with others. As such you have an innate tendency toward the neutral alignment. 

  

Special Traits 

Keen Eyesight. You are proficient in the Perception skill. If you are already proficient you double your proficiency bonus instead (expertise).  

Mimicry. You can attempt to mimic any sound you've heard, or the voice of a creature you heard speak for at least a minute. If you succeed a Performance check against a creature's passive perception that creature can't tell the sound is an imitation. 

Natural Talent. You are proficient in the Performance skill. If you are already proficient you double your proficiency bonus instead (expertise). 

Talons. You can use the talons on your feet as hands while in flight and the fingers on your wings while on the ground. Your unarmed attacks deal 1d4 slashing damage and have the finesse property. 

Ethnicity. Your patron's ethnicity determines their looks and culture. Choose one from the options provided. 

  

Harpy Ethnicities 

Different environments and survival tactics have caused physiological differences to emerge between different harpy cultures. There are two such subraces in Verold: the common and northern harpies. 

Common Harpy 

Common harpies are tall but have slender and light builds. 

The common harpy inhabits the warm south and central parts of the Confederation, as well as all warm coastal regions. Their songs shake many people out of bed in the early mornings, though most confederate cities have laws against their public performances. While many citizens would love to get rid of these 'arrogant animals' and consider them no more than beasts, harpies are the go-to messengers in the Other World. They can traverse large distances quickly and do so gladly for a bit of pay.  

Harpies have no real kingdoms of their own. They rarely settle and usually migrate between the settlements of other races, with their own parallel societies. Common harpies dominate the art communities of every race that accepts their presence. The precision with which a harpy can modulate their voice can only be approached by merfolk and no human or elf can hope to beat them. Thanks to their surprisingly dexterous talons harpy artists travel around the world, creating wonderful artworks that include everything from paintings to statues. The wide range of movement also makes it possible for them to wield bows and similar weapons. 

Both male and female common harpies sport a wide variety of different feather coats, depending on the region they live in, ranging from almond to bright white. 

Ability Score Increase. Your Dexterity score increases by 2. 

Predisposition. Most common harpies are bards or rogues. 

Light Build. You are medium but count as a small creature when determining your carrying capacity and how you wield weapons and items. Your maximum encumberment equals your Strength score multiplied by 2 (instead of 4). 

  

Northern Harpy 

Northern harpies are large and strong, with thick wings that provide a lot of lift, but are not too maneuverable. 

The northern harpies inhabit the cold mountains, tundras, and coastlines of the deep north. Their larger wings can provide massive amounts of lift, granting them the ability to fly at high altitudes and lift the large fauna of these regions. However, as good as their feathers are at protecting from the cold, they interfere with the movement of their legs. Without a large range of movement in every direction, it is all but impossible for these harpies to wield bows or similar weapons while in flight. The only way to use them is to land and use the fingers on their wings to aim the weapon. This is rarely an issue though as magic is quite prevalent in these harpy communities and most harpies who would consider learning to use bows learn magic instead. 

Northern harpies in the other world usually live in secluded communities in the mountains. In the glaciers of the deep north exists a harpy kingdom, but in most other regions they live in small tribes that use a hidden cave or mountaintop abode as a base of living. Anything more would attract either the elves or dragons that they share their habitat with, depending on the region they live in. 

Male northern harpies sport coats of black feathers, with white around the stomach, and are substantially larger than females. Female northern harpies usually have coats that are light brown or grey.  

Ability Score Increase. Your Strength score increases by 2. 

Predisposition. Most northern harpies are barbarians or paladins. 

Burly Build. You cannot wield bows and have disadvantage on attacks made with firearms and crossbows. You can use the fingers on your wings to wield these weapons normally, reducing your flying speed to 0 while you have a weapon equipped that way.

Sunday, April 25, 2021 

8:54 AM 

Description of waking up as that race. 

-XXX's awakening in the new world 

 

 

Short Description 

 

Physical Description 

 

Senses and Mental Capabilities 

 

Reproduction and Growing up 

 

Survival 

 

Personality 

 

Race Features 

Short Description  

 

Physique 

Ability Score Increase. Your Dexterity score increases by 2. 

Size. Elves are from just under, to well over 2 meters tall and have slender builds. Your size is Medium. 

Base Armor Class. 10 

Speed. Your speed equals 10 meters (30 ft.)  

Age. You reach physical maturity around 30 and can live to be to be up to 300 years old. 

 

Health 

Hit Dice. Your Hit Die is a d8. You start at 0th level with 1d8 and gain an additional d8 every level. 

Hit Points at 0th level. 1d8 (or 5) + your Constitution modifier. 

Hit Points at Higher LeveIs. 1d8 (or 5) + your Constitution modifier + the Bonus Hit Points granted by your class every leveI. 

 

Vitality 

Vitality Dice. Your Vitality Die is a d8. You start at 0th level with 1d8 and gain an additional d8 every level. 

Vitality at 0th level. 1d8 (or 5) 

Vitality at Higher LeveIs. 1d8 (or 5) + the Bonus Vitality granted by your class every leveI. 

 

Survival 

Night's Rest. Sleep for 4 hours and expend 4 rations to gain the benefits of a night's rest. 

Long Rest. Spend 7 days performing light activity and expend 28 rations to gain the benefits of a long rest. 

Nutrition. You are a carnivore and as such can survive exclusively on meat. You gain some nourishment from fruits and vegetables, but many, especially onions and garlic, make you sick and cannot be survived on indefinitely (without serious illness). 

Hunger. You may begin a rest without expending rations from your inventory. If you do, increase your hunger by an amount equal to the rations not expended. Refer to the Elf Starvation table to see at what hunger level you gain what level of exhaustion (see the condition).

Subrace. Your patron's subrace determines their looks and culture. Choose one from the options provided. 

 

Racial Subraces 

Different circumstances have resulted in natural and social adaptations in elves separated by time, space and culture. There are three such subraces on verold, the High Elves, Common Elves and Dark Elves. If your patron cannot be identified as one of the listed, for example because their parents were not of the same sub-race, choose the Variant Elf option. 

 

High Elf 

The high elves are the tallest and strongest of the elves. In addition to naturally well-muscled bodies, they have pale white skin. 

In the other world, most of them live in the mountains and deep forests of the north as clans of marauders that descend upon the Avalanche Confederation during winter, plundering, killing and burning in the vain hope that someday they may rebuild the empire they once had. They come, either in ships from across the sea, or as warbands from the mountains. As the remaining elite soldiers and nobles of that fallen empire, they count many mages and rangers within their ranks and their culture revolves around combat and the practice of elemental magic. From the moment they are born, they are trained in the ways of combat and how to independently survive in the wilderness they call home. Even those few elves that surrendered during the war and now live in cities secretly pass on their violent ways to their offspring. 

Ability Score Increase. Your Strength score increases by 1. 

Predisposition. Most high elves are mages or rangers. 

Elven Weapon Training. You have proficiency with the longsword, shortsword, spear and glaive. 

Mage Ancestry. At 1st level, you learn the Channel I spell of an elemental lore of your choice. If your class gives you access to an elemental lore, you instead learn an additional spell.

Changeling (All forms) 

Sunday, April 25, 2021 

8:54 AM 

Changeling 

Adaptable. Duplicit. Shapeshifter. 

  

Arik's eyes snap open. His entire body convulses as his consciousness explodes through it and fills the lifeless shell with new life. Slowly his eyes open. His vision is blurred. As it comes into focus he tries to move and as his head falls to the side he sees a woman lying next to him. Her hair is white and her ears pointed, but she's wearing the clothes of his girlfriend. Focusing on her awakens a new instinct. It is like there is a new muscle running beneath every inch of his skin. Curious, he leans into the instinct and does what his body knows must be done. His eyes jump over her body, translating the features of her body into commands that Arik's new skin obeys. It feels like ripples are moving across his body and a strong itch accompanies it. After a few seconds, he breaks his focused gaze and realizes that his body has become identical to the unconscious elf beside him. Only his old clothes are still the same. 

  

— Arik's awakening in the New World 

  

Adaptable and ever-changing, changelings can mimic the appearance of anyone they see. Deception and subterfuge come naturally to them, meaning that even the wariest will have trouble seeing through their disguise. Only the touch of silver may reveal their true nature. 

  

Shapeshifters 

Changelings have the ability to rapidly alter their physical form using magic, however, they do have a true form which they may either assume willingly or slowly revert to if their body runs out of the strength required to maintain its form. Prolonged stress may also cause imperfections in a changelings appearance that allow its true form to show through. 

A changeling can mimic the appearance of any humanoid they see, as long as that humanoid is roughly the same size as the changeling. When they wish to do so, their form shifts into their true form, before rapidly assuming the shape of their new form and finally taking on its coloration. This process usually takes no more than a few seconds. 

Changelings can only assume another creature's form and voice. They cannot alter their own biology and have the same distinct smell regardless of what form they take. 

Limited Imitation 

The features of a changeling's true form mimic that of their mother's physique. For example, a changeling born to an elven mother will have a slender body and pointed ears. This has some impact on their physical characteristics, but a changeling does not inherit any special biological capabilities their mother may have. 

In their true form, changelings have chalk-white skin that, just like their hair, is perfectly uniform in color. Even their irises are colored perfectly white, with their milky-black pupils being the only colored part of their bodies. 

Changelings have decent senses but lack the ability to see in darkness and since they cannot alter their own biology, they do not receive any of the special senses those they mimic may possess. 

Odd Ones 

Changelings cannot reproduce on their own and require a mother of another race to carry their children. Despite having the ability to imitate both male and female forms, changelings must assume a male form and mate with a female dwarf, elf, fairy, human, or giant to produce offspring. Regardless of their mate's race, the resulting child is always a changeling. 

Changelings gain the ability to shapeshift around the age of 14 and reach adulthood around 20. They can live to be around 150 years old. Their mother's race has no impact on how quickly a changeling matures, or how long they live. 

A changeling is born in its true form, but mimicking its mother's skin color. This true form is a mixture of the mother's looks and the changeling father's true form. Changelings cannot willingly alter their looks until they reach puberty, at which point their ability begins revealing itself. 

Changeling (All forms) 

Sunday, April 25, 2021 

8:54 AM 

Changeling 

Adaptable. Duplicit. Shapeshifter. 

  

Arik's eyes snap open. His entire body convulses as his consciousness explodes through it and fills the lifeless shell with new life. Slowly his eyes open. His vision is blurred. As it comes into focus he tries to move and as his head falls to the side he sees a woman lying next to him. Her hair is white and her ears pointed, but she's wearing the clothes of his girlfriend. Focusing on her awakens a new instinct. It is like there is a new muscle running beneath every inch of his skin. Curious, he leans into the instinct and does what his body knows must be done. His eyes jump over her body, translating the features of her body into commands that Arik's new skin obeys. It feels like ripples are moving across his body and a strong itch accompanies it. After a few seconds, he breaks his focused gaze and realizes that his body has become identical to the unconscious elf beside him. Only his old clothes are still the same. 

  

— Arik's awakening in the New World 

  

Adaptable and ever-changing, changelings can mimic the appearance of anyone they see. Deception and subterfuge come naturally to them, meaning that even the wariest will have trouble seeing through their disguise. Only the touch of silver may reveal their true nature. 

  

Shapeshifters 

Changelings have the ability to rapidly alter their physical form using magic, however, they do have a true form which they may either assume willingly or slowly revert to if their body runs out of the strength required to maintain its form. Prolonged stress may also cause imperfections in a changelings appearance that allow its true form to show through. 

A changeling can mimic the appearance of any humanoid they see, as long as that humanoid is roughly the same size as the changeling. When they wish to do so, their form shifts into their true form, before rapidly assuming the shape of their new form and finally taking on its coloration. This process usually takes no more than a few seconds. 

Changelings can only assume another creature's form and voice. They cannot alter their own biology and have the same distinct smell regardless of what form they take. 

Limited Imitation 

The features of a changeling's true form mimic that of their mother's physique. For example, a changeling born to an elven mother will have a slender body and pointed ears. This has some impact on their physical characteristics, but a changeling does not inherit any special biological capabilities their mother may have. 

In their true form, changelings have chalk-white skin that, just like their hair, is perfectly uniform in color. Even their irises are colored perfectly white, with their milky-black pupils being the only colored part of their bodies. 

Changelings have decent senses but lack the ability to see in darkness and since they cannot alter their own biology, they do not receive any of the special senses those they mimic may possess. 

Odd Ones 

Changelings cannot reproduce on their own and require a mother of another race to carry their children. Despite having the ability to imitate both male and female forms, changelings must assume a male form and mate with a female dwarf, elf, fairy, human, or giant to produce offspring. Regardless of their mate's race, the resulting child is always a changeling. 

Changelings gain the ability to shapeshift around the age of 14 and reach adulthood around 20. They can live to be around 150 years old. Their mother's race has no impact on how quickly a changeling matures, or how long they live. 

A changeling is born in its true form, but mimicking its mother's skin color. This true form is a mixture of the mother's looks and the changeling father's true form. Changelings cannot willingly alter their looks until they reach puberty, at which point their ability begins revealing itself. 

Built to Transform 

The body of a changeling is built around the ability to transform. Their bodies require a good deal of energy to facilitate the changes it has to make. Changelings are omnivores and can survive on a diet consisting of plants, meat, or both. They have no preference either way and suffer no drawbacks if they choose to subsist on a single food source. 

The exact way a changeling’s body facilitates its transformations is unclear. The transformation itself is magical, but once complete does not continue to radiate magic. A changeling can modify its bone structure with extreme precision, allowing it to rapidly shift its internal organs to modify the shape of its body. The changeling cannot modify its strength or other physical abilities and is limited by their default physique. A flaw in their disguise is also that their transformation cannot affect their scent and creatures with acute noses can detect the changeling's unique musk with ease. 

Just like all other shapeshifters and creatures incorporating wild magic into their biology, the changeling is vulnerable to silver. It does not seem to be directly toxic to them (most likely because their internal organs do not contain the organic structures that facilitate transformations and cause this vulnerability). However, prolonged or intense contact with silver causes the changeling's disguise to literally melt away. Their skin loses its color and structural integrity, causing it to wrinkle and deform before it resets to its default state. This state being the changeling's true form. Being forced into their true form this way is excruciatingly painful, but not lethal to the changeling. 

Attention Seekers 

Changelings are extroverted by nature. They have an innate need to be with others and to 'fit in' with whatever culture they are currently in. Most changelings instantly accept any norms or customs used and attempt to mimic the behaviors of those around them. They are naturally extroverted and rarely uncomfortable when working with others. These traits are nearly universal, as they aid the changeling in remaining undetected. After all, who would suspect the open-minded and tolerant person who just wants to be close to others of being more than they seem? 

Changelings feel an innate disgust towards their true form and uncomfortable while in it (most changelings describe walking around in their true form as similar to being naked). The act of transforming is also something that most changelings feel shame about (similar to how most people have no problems undressing but prefer not to be watched during the act). It is not uncommon for a changeling to hide their transformations even from those who already know of their true nature. 

Infiltrators 

In the Other World, changelings have no culture of their own. They live among the other races in secret, integrating themselves into communities as strangers or by replacing those others. Most changelings prefer to assume the forms of strangers or the recently dead. It is not unheard of for changelings to be asked to replace someone. Some also specifically murder or kidnap people to take their place. This form of infiltration is particularly feared, though exceptionally rare and if it ever occurs the changeling will usually only assume the victim's form for a short period of time, for a specific purpose. The fear of being replaced has led most nobles to wear silver jewelry, eat and drink from silver table wear and keep dogs specifically trained to detect the unique scent of a changeling, which makes most long-term replacements unfeasible. When a changeling does replace someone, it is usually a peasant, or as part of a gig. 

  

Acquired Species 

Changelings are created when a changeling has a child with another humanoid. The traits of this species are applied on top of a creature's natural species. Choose your base species from either Dwarf, Elf, or Human from this book or another species that specifically states its members may become changelings.  

  

Changeling Features 

The ability to change your appearance at will and the physical features provided by your ancestry grant you several abilities. 

  

Special Traits 

Ability Score Decrease. Your ability scores each decrease by 1. 

Predisposition. Changelings are extroverted and have an innate need to fit in. They always tend towards the alignments most prominent in the groups they are in and usually have the same predispositions as the race of their mother. 

Duplicity. You are proficient in the Deception and Persuasion skills. If you are already proficient you double your proficiency bonus instead (expertise). 

Shapechanger. As an action, you change your appearance and your voice. You determine the specifics of the changes, including your coloration, hair length, and sex. You can also adjust your height by up to 30 centimeters (1 foot). You can make yourself appear as a member of another race, though none of your game statistics change. You can't duplicate the appearance of an individual you've never seen, and you must adopt a form that has the same basic arrangement of limbs that you have. You cannot use this trait to imitate or modify clothes or similar accessories. 

You stay in the new form until you use an action to revert to your true form, or assume another form.  

When you are dealt damage by a silvered or magical weapon or come into contact with a silver object, you must succeed a Constitution saving throw or be revealed as a changeling. The DC of this save equals 10 + the damage taken.

Classes 

Friday, July 16, 2021 

7:30 PM 

 

A class is an archetype that describes a set of capabilities your patron has accumulated throughout their life. The features you are provided are abstractions. Just like hit points and vitality are abstractions of your physical condition that don't exist in the 'real' game world, class features assign names and numbers to certain capabilities. For example, the fighter's feature Action Surge represents a warrior's ability to push themselves beyond their limits to perform several actions quickly in a way that none that haven’t trained specifically for it can. To the fighter, using this ability is not them deciding to trigger Action Surge, but them shoring up their own resolve into a burst of focus and speed, similar to a final push at the end of a marathon. 

While playing, it will usually be easier to simply refer to a capability by its abstract name when you decide to use it so that your GM and other players immediately know what you want to do. However, it is important to remember that your in-game self has no concept of the features of its class and will never refer to them by their name. The only major exceptions to this rule are spells, where the arcane techniques spells represent do have specific names given to them by the people of the Other World and the new. 

  

When you wake up after the fog you will only have access to a few proficiencies; the most basic capabilities your class can provide. Just like class features, levels are also an abstraction. They represent the fact that you are gaining experience and are unlocking the ingrained knowledge of your patron through the dreams you have about them every night. Your in-game self has no concept of levels, though they will still most likely be able to gauge their power level. 

Each class entry in this chapter includes a table summarizing the benefits you gain at every level, followed by a detailed explanation of each one. 

The twelve classes, listed in the Classes table, can on their own and by combing them through multi-classing, be used to describe almost any set of skills someone in the Other World may have acquired. 

  

  

Class 

	

Description 

	
 

Complexity 




Barbarian 

	

A sturdy front-liner focused on melee combat that can shrug off most physical attacks. 

	

Low 




Bard 

	

A versatile spellcaster that grows more powerful the longer their performance lasts and specializes in dialog and diplomacy. 

	

Low 




Druid 

	

A shapeshifting, support-focused spellcaster in their humanoid form that  can talk to and transform into animals. 

	

Medium 




Fighter 

	

A versatile warrior that specializes in outlasting their opponents and gaining superiority through their equipment and a small set of versatile martial maneuvers. 

	

Low 




Mage 

	

A spellcaster that specializes in one (or more) of six elemental lores that each provide unique and powerful abilities. They provide a good balance between versatility and firepower. 

	

High 




Monk 

	

A martial artist that avoids damage using supernatural awareness and strikes enemies down with a large number of highly accurate melee attacks. 

	

Low 




Paladin 

	

A warrior that blends the use of armor and mundane weapons with divine magic to support their allies and deliver devastating attacks against single foes. 

	

Medium 




Prophet 

	

A spellcaster that focuses on manipulating the minds of others through their connection to the divine. 

	

Medium 




Ranger 

	

A warrior that enhances their martial prowess with wild magic and special maneuvers. A ranger focuses on versatility and can be just as effective on their own as in a group. 

	

Medium 




Rogue 

	

A warrior that specializes in outmaneuvering and taking down their foes one at a time. The rogue relies on a broad range of skills to outmatch their foes. 

	

Medium 




Sorcerer 

	

A spellcaster that has a natural aptitude for a specific elemental lore that provides an unmatched amount of firepower. 

	

High 




Witch 

	

A crafter that creates powerful magic items, potions, and wands that allow them to cast a huge variety of spells and easily swap out the spells they can cast. 

	

High 



Conflicting change.

Way of the Berserker 

Friday, January 8, 2021 

10:37 PM 

Way of the Berserker 

Passion unleashed. 

  

Berserkers completely submerge themselves in their passion, allowing it to consume them entirely and granting them an unmatched presence on the battlefield. Their approach is simple: charge your enemies and smash them with a relentless onslaught. 

Especially the orcs in the west rely on this way, channeling years of atrocities and betrayal committed against them into furious passion, but this way is by far the most common among all barbarians. 

  

Frenzy 

Starting when you choose this source at 3rd level, you go into a frenzy when you rage. In addition to the usual effects, for the duration of your rage you can use your bonus action to make a single melee weapon attack. 

  

Berserker's Charge 

Also beginning at 3rd level, when you enter into a rage, your speed doubles for the rest of your turn. You can only use this additional speed to move toward an enemy. Additionally, you can take the Dash action as a bonus action on your subsequent turns while you are raging. 

  

Mindless Rage 

Beginning at 6th level, you are immune to being charmed or frightened while raging. If you are charmed or frightened when you enter your rage, the effect is suspended for the duration of the rage. 

  

Intimidating Presence 

You are a monster. A warrior so terrifying, that your opponent's wither at the mere sight of you.  

At 9th level, you can use your bonus action to frighten someone. When you do so, choose one creature that you can see within 10 meters (30 feet) of you. If the creature can see or hear you, it must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw (DC equal to 8 + your Proficiency Bonus + your Strength modifier) or be frightened of you until the end of your next turn. On subsequent turns, you can use your action to extend the duration of this effect on the frightened creature until the end of your next turn. This effect ends if the creature ends its turn out of line of sight or more than 30 meters (90 feet) away from you. 

If the creature succeeds on its saving throw, you can't use this feature on that creature again for 24 hours.

Soldier 

Friday, January 8, 2021 

10:09 PM 

Soldier  

Fight as one 

  

As a soldier, your patron relies on precise and well-coordinated teamwork. They are the troops that hold the line. With shield, sword, pike, or in this world usually rifle in hand, they stand side by side with their fellow soldiers and face the charging enemy. They advance together, they train together and they die together. This kind of training is common in the armies of the Other World and from your patron you have inherited their ability for tactical teamwork. 

  

Improved Critical 

Beginning when you choose this subclass at 3rd level, your weapon attacks score a critical hit on a roll of 19 or 20. 

  

Troop Tactics 

Starting at 7th level, you can use the Help action as a bonus action. 

  

Legion Tactics 

Starting at 10th level, you are a master of the battlefield. Your ability to work with others is unsurpassed. You can use the Help action to aid an ally within 5 meters of you, rather than 1 meter of you, as long as the target can see or hear you. 

  

Superior Critical 

Starting at 10th level, your weapon attacks score a critical hit on a roll of 18-20.

Thursday, April 28, 2022 

10:54 PM 

 

School of the Path 

Dance of Spells 

  

The school of the path focuses on blending spell and defensive weapon work. 

Groups of mages that mostly act on their own, as is often the case with special forces or guerrillas, require a broad range of skills. Integral part of their training is the so-called path. A journey that hones these mages' ability to work in small groups or on their own, inspired by their wilder brethren, the rangers. Mages of the path supplement the use of magic with defensive weapon techniques.  

Not unlike in a dance, the users of this so-called bladesong rapidly alternate between the martial movements of magic and strokes of their weapon, integrating one into the other. This state makes dodging, maintaining spells, and attacking easier. However, it is draining to use and only suited for short engagements. The name bladesong is a relic from the high elven warriors that originally developed this technique and often refer to the distinct noises of spellcasting as 'singing'. 

  

Training in Spell and Blade 

When you adopt this school at 2nd level, you gain proficiency with light armor and simple weapons. 

  

Bladesong  

As a bonus action, you expend 1 vitality to enter the bladesong, freeing the flow of magic through your body. While not wearing medium or heavy armor your Bladesong grants the following benefits: 

•You gain a bonus to your AC equal to your (highest) Spellcasting Ability modifier (minimum of +1). 

•Your speed increases by 2 meters (10 feet). 

•You have advantage on Strength (Athletics) and Dexterity (Acrobatics) checks. 

•You gain a bonus to any Constitution saving throws you make to maintain concentration on a spell. The bonus equals your Spellcasting Ability modifier (minimum of +1). 

At the start of each of your turns, you must expend an additional 1 vitality to maintain the Bladesong. 

  

Extra Attack 

Starting at 6th level, you can attack twice, instead of once, whenever you take the attack action on your turn. During your blade song, you may replace one of these attacks with a spell that has a casting time of 1 action or less. 

  

Dance of Victory 

Finally, at 10th level, you add your Spellcasting Ability modifier (minimum of +1) to the damage of your melee weapon attacks while your Bladesong is active.

Thursday, April 28, 2022 

8:22 PM 

School of the Warrior (Remove) 

Balanced Combat 

  

The school of the warrior encompasses mages that rely entirely on magic to act as their offense and defense. 

Mages form the backbone of any army and are thus the ones most likely to be focus-fired by enemy missile units and spellcasters alike. While most lores encompass at least one technique (spell) that can be used to directly counter an enemy attack, mages that work in a military unit must be able to defend against unexpected attacks. They train to weave their magic so that it forms a defensive shield that can thwart mundane attacks or disrupt the integrity of enemy spells. They strike fast and thwart any would-be assassins that make it past their fellows. 

  

Arcane Deflection 

At 2nd level, you weave your magic to fortify yourself against harm. When you are hit by an attack or you fail a saving throw, you can use your reaction and expend 1 vitality to gain a +2 bonus to your AC against that attack or a +4 bonus to that saving throw. 

  

Decisive Action 

Also at 2nd level, you are trained to move quickly. You can give yourself a bonus to your initiative rolls equal to your (highest) Spellcasting Ability modifier. 

  

Antipathic Channel 

Starting at 6th level, the magic you channel passively wards off harm. While you maintain concentration on a spell, you have a +2 bonus to AC and all saving throws. 

  

Efficient Defense 

Finally, at 10th level, your defense is perfectly incorporated into your casting. Using Arcane Deflection consumes no vitality and its effectiveness increases. You gain a +4 bonus to your AC or a +6 bonus to saving throws instead of the bonuses provided until now.
