Sunday, April 18, 2021 

8:22 AM 

Tradition of the Fist 

Focus on the flow 

  

Monks of the Way of the Fist understand how important it is to focus your efforts into a single endeavor. Each finger on the hand represents a fundamental belief: concentration, reaction, equilibrium, speed, and control. To truly master yourself, you need to take all five of these aspects and bring them together. Just like fingers close into a fist. Once every aspect of your being is focused on a single task, you will be able to make the most of every action you take. Be it in combat, or in your day-to-day life. It is no wonder then, that this tradition is the most popular, as supernatural awareness and single-minded focus have endless applications outside of smashing someone's face in. 

  

Closing the Fist 

Starting when you choose this tradition at 3rd level, you learn to make the most of your maneuvers. Whenever you successfully use a maneuver, you can impose one of the following effects on that target: 

•It must succeed a Strength or Dexterity saving throw or be knocked prone. 

•It must succeed a Strength saving throw or be pushed up to 5 meters (15 feet) away from you. 

•It can't take reactions until the end of your next turn. 

A maneuver is successful if an attack roll made as part of the maneuver hits the target or the target fails a saving throw against it. Any single maneuver can only trigger this ability once. 

  

Vigilance 

At 6th level, you become adept at moving with the flow of battle. You have advantage on attack rolls you make while it is not your turn in combat. 

  

Perfect Flow 

At 10th level, you have mastered going with the flow of battle and reacting to it. After taking a reaction, you may move up to half your speed without provoking attacks of opportunity and make a single unarmed attack.

Saturday, July 9, 2022 

11:25 PM 

Oath of Devotion 

Those who swear an oath of devotion see their destiny tied to a specific way of life. They are the ones who travel between settlements, spreading the good message of their ideology. They are the ones who stand for uncompromising principles and they are the ones who seek to convince the world of their way. 

Their presence alone inspires those who share their goals and hardens their resolve against all who would oppose their mission. 

  

Oath Spells  

You gain the following oath spells at the paladin levels listed. 

Oath of Ancients Spells 

Paladin Level 

	
 

Spells 




3rd 

	

Benediction, Protection of Faith 




5th 

	

Communion, Suggestion 




9th 

	

Hypnotize, Speak 




  

  

Channel Resolve 

When you take this oath at 3rd level, you gain the following two Channel Resolve options. 

You may expend 2 vitality to summon one of the two effects described below.  

After you use this ability, double the vitality cost of subsequent uses until you finish a long rest. 

  

•Holy Emissary. You can use your Channel Divinity to augment your presence with divine power. As a bonus action, you grant yourself a +5 bonus to Charisma (Persuasion) checks for the next 10 minutes. 

•Weapon of Devotion. As an action, you can imbue one weapon that you are holding with your resolve, using your Channel Resolve. For 1 minute, you add your Charisma modifier to attack rolls made with that weapon (with a minimum bonus of +1). If resistance or another effect reduces the damage of a hit (including resistance or immunity, you deal the difference between the damage you rolled and the damage you dealt as psychic damage. 

If you lose contact with the weapon, or if you fall unconscious, this effect ends. 

  

Aura of Alacrity 

At 7th level, you emanate an aura that fills you and your companions with supernatural speed, allowing you to race across a battlefield in formation. Your walking speed increases by 3 meters (10 feet). In addition, if you aren't incapacitated, the walking speed of any ally that starts their turn within 3 meters (10 feet) of you increases by 3 meters (10 feet) until the end of that turn. 

  

Glorious Defense 

Finally at 10th level, when you or another creature you can see within 1 meter (5 feet) of you is hit by an attack, you can use your reaction to grant a bonus to the target's AC against that attack, potentially causing it to miss. The bonus equals your Charisma modifier (minimum of +1). If the attack misses, you can make one weapon attack against the attacker as part of this reaction, provided the attacker is within your weapon's range.

Prophet- 

Saturday, December 19, 2020 

10:52 AM 

Prophet 

Power of the Gods 

  

A human man stands atop a stage. His eyes glow with purple light, as a light green mist hangs in the air. His followers, their minds opened by the bliss, are pulled into a magnificent realm of the man's creation. All of them stare in wonder as their prophet begins his sermon. 

The female giant's eyes glow purple. She stares at the kobold jumping around her and growls. A burst of psychic static tears through the little man’s mind like a bullet. He squeaks and falls over, frothing at the mouth, his mind a shattered mess of random thoughts and madness. 

A male elf gives the female leader of the bandits a single seductive wink. In an instant, her aggression melts away and is replaced by lust. The rest of her gang look at her with surprise as she gives the elf a wink and invites him and his friends to their hideout, instead of demanding their supplies. 

  

Prophets are priests of a god that have learned to control and focus the power of their deity and use it to influence the minds of others. Through techniques not unlike those of elemental magic they can use their bodies as conduits for divine energy, however, this connection works both ways.  

  

Masters of the Mind 

Divine magic, as the name suggests, is the power of the gods manifesting in this world. The gods hold sway over the souls and thus the minds of mortals. However, they cannot interact with the physical world. Through study, prophets can learn arcane techniques that allow them to channel energy through a specific god and influence the emotions, and eventually even the thoughts, of other living beings.  

These powers make prophets terrifying foes. With a few words they can make a mighty warrior flee in fear. Their gaze attacks the soul itself, causing it to lose control over its body, or rending its connection to it entirely. With a wink they can dominate their foes’ minds, turning them into little more than their puppets. 

  

Conduits of the Gods 

Most prophets in the Other World live their lives as leaders of cults or temples devoted to their gods. The gods are a force of chaos. They allow people to manipulate others and those who use their powers often act in erratic ways to satisfy the urges their god's influence causes. In some lands, where their powers are frowned upon or outright banned, prophets operate in the shadows. Much can be gained by subtly, or openly, influencing the minds of others. However, few people take kindly to such manipulation. Most see it as a violation of the highest degree and it's not hard to see why prophets are either held in the highest regard by their community, or mercilessly persecuted. 

  

Building a Prophet 

Some lucky few appear in the void and are conscious enough to choose their patron. Perhaps you are one of those. When choosing the prophet whose body you will inherit, there is one thing to consider: What god does that prophet channel their power from?  

Most people are not given a choice of who their patron is and like most, you probably just woke up and soon realized that you have access to divine magic. The deity through which you channel your power doesn't just grant your powers. It influences you and compels you to do certain things. You are not forced to act upon these urges and may resist, or even drown out your god's influence. You may also indulge in it and completely give in to what the force of chaos residing on the other side of the veil compels you to do. However, all good things come in moderation and if you selfishly overindulge, chaos will follow and your allies may be driven away. Will you use your powers to manipulate those around you into giving you what you want? Will you start a cult of brainwashed drones that fulfill your every whim, or will you use your powers sparingly and only to peacefully resolve conflicts that would otherwise end in bloodshed? Whether you use your abilities for good or evil and what good or evil even mean, is up to you. Either way, you will be a force of uncertainty, for both your allies and foes can never be sure whether what they are feeling is real, or caused by your influence.

Prophet Features 

Primary Ability Scores: Determined by the god you draw your power from. (Refer to your subclass for details)

CLASS FEATURES 

As a prophet, you gain the following class features. 

  

Prophet Proficiencies 

Starting at 0th level, the prophet class provides you with the following proficiencies: 

Armor: Light Armor, Medium Armor, Shields 

Weapons: Simple Weapons 

Saving Throws: Your spellcasting ability and Wisdom or Charisma 

Skills: Choose two from Arcana, History, Insight, Medicine, Nature, Perception, Religion, and Survival 

  

Bonus Vitality 

As a prophet, you add your Spellcasting Ability modifier to your starting vitality at 0th level and to the vitality you gain whenever you take a level in this class. Your spellcasting ability is determined by your Domain. Refer to the description of your Domain to see what it is. 

 

Multiclassing 

Ability Score Minimum: Spellcasting Ability 13 

When you multiclass into the prophet, you gain the following proficiencies: 

Armor: Light Armor, Medium Armor, Shields 

  

Domain 

As a 0th level prophet, the type of deity your patron channeled their powers through grants you additional proficiencies. Available domains are described at the end of the class description. Your choice also determines the abilities you receive at 1st, 2nd, 6th, and 10th level. Additionally, every domain resonates with a different ability, meaning that your domain also determines your spellcasting ability. 

  

Spellcasting 

As a prophet, your patron focused on drawing power from a god using techniques they trained and committed to ingrained memory. See the chapter on spellcasting for the general rules on using magic. 

Divine Magic 

The mental defenses all living creatures have make influencing their minds almost impossible, but by using the gods and the forces of chaos as a gateway, prophets can interfere with the minds of others. To that end, you choose a domain through which you channel your power (this is your subclass). Your subclass determines your spellcasting ability. The chapter on spellcasting provides a list of the spells you have access to in the subchapter on divine magic. 

Learning Spells 

When you gain your 1st level, you learn the Channel Divinity I spell and 4 novice spells of your choice from the Divine Magic spell list. You learn 2 additional spells of your choice whenever you gain a level. These spells must be of a level you are able to cast. 

Whenever you gain access to a new spell level, you automatically learn the corresponding Channel Divinity spell, which does not count against the number of spells you may choose. The Prophet Spell Level table tells you when you gain access to spells of greater complexity (higher level). 

Spellcasting Ability 

Your subclass determines your spellcasting ability for your prophet spells. You use the ability score determined by your subclass whenever a spell refers to your spellcasting ability. In addition, you use your ability modifier when setting the saving throw DC for a prophet spell you cast and when making an attack roll with one. 

Spell Save DC = 8 + your Proficiency Bonus + your Spellcasting Ability modifier 

Spell Attack Modifier = your Proficiency Bonus + your Spellcasting Ability modifier 

Vitality 

When you begin to cast a spell, decrease your current vitality by the amount listed as the spell's Vitality Cost. If you reach 0 Vitality, you fall unconscious and the spell you were casting fails unless you make a final push. (See the general rules on Spellcasting for details). 

  

Prophet Spell Level 

Spell Level 

	

Prophet Level 




Novice 

	

1st 




Apprentice 

	

3rd 




Adept 

	

5th 




Expert 

	

7th 




Master 

	

9th 

  

Divine Fervor 

Also starting at 1st level, service to your domain gives you power. Your subclass determines how you can gain Divine Fervor. When you gain Divine Fervor, the next divine spell you cast has its vitality cost halved. Divine Fervor does not stack and lasts until it is used or one minute (10 Turns) has passed. 

 

Sense Sanctuary 

Also starting at 1st level, if you spend a night's rest in an area, you know if there are any divine sanctuaries in a 10-kilometer (6-mile) radius and their general locations. 

  

Channel Domain 

At 2nd level, you gain the ability to channel divine power directly from your deity to manifest a powerful effect. You start with two such effects: Destroy Undead and an effect determined by your domain. Your domain grants you an additional effect when you reach 10th level. 

When you use your Channel Domain, you choose which effect to manifest. You must then finish a night's rest to use your Channel Domain again. 

Some Channel Domain effects require saving throws. When you use such an effect from this class, the DC equals your spell save DC, as determined by your subclass 

Beginning at 6th level, you can use your Channel Domain twice between rests, and finally, at 10th level, you can use it three times between rests. When you finish a night's rest, you regain your expended uses. 

Whenever you finish a night's rest in a sanctuary of your god, you gain a temporary use of your Channel Domain that lasts until you use it. You cannot have more than one temporary Channel Domain at a time. When you use Channel Domain, you decide whether you expend a normal or a temporary use.   

Using a Channel Domain ability in a sanctuary of your god does not cause you to expend a use. 

  

Channel Domain: Turn Undead 

As an action, use your Channel Domain to target each undead of your choice no more than 10 meters (30 feet) away. Each target must succeed a Charisma saving throw or be frightened of you for 1 minute. If an undead is destroyed while frighten this way, any spell or ability that would allow it to return to life has no effect. 

  

Ability Score Improvement or Feat 

When you reach 4th level, and again at 8th level, you can increase one attribute score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two attribute scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an attribute score above 20 using this feature. Alternatively, you may choose a feat from the Feat list. 

 

Domains 

Gods are formed from the emotions of living beings and the set of emotions that forms a god is its domain. The domain a prophet connects to influences them constantly and also determines the boons they receive from their god. 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Channel Domain: Destroy Undead 

As an action, use your Channel Domain to target an undead no more than 20 meters away and shred the echo of a soul that allows it to manifest. If the undead you target has 20 hit points or fewer, it is destroyed and any ability that would allow it to return to life has no effect. If this effect does not destroy the undead, the action is wasted but the use of your Channel Domain is not expended. 

Conflicting change.

Glory Domain 

Tuesday, December 22, 2020 

7:04 PM 

Glory Domain 

I am tyranny. I am tooth and claw. The fist, undenied. As long as you vie for the sharpest blades and loudest voice, I will enforce your rule. 

  

Every soul desires glory. To see itself above others. To be significant. It requires power and leaves no room for those too weak to take what they want. The sanctuaries of this domain instill a feeling of fear and insignificance.  

In the Other World, Eor is often the name of this domain's god. He strives for the rule of the strong tyrant, conquest, and endless war. He despises equality and peace. He is worshiped by warriors, tyrants, and the ambitious. He particularly favors lycans. His followers strengthen themselves to become significant and create hierarchies based on power. In the New World many call him Thor or know him as the archangel Camael. 

  

---- 

Driven by the need for significance, power, and the accumulation of strength at all costs. 

•Pagan Interpretation: Thor In this setting, Thor is not just a protector, but the embodiment of the warrior who could not be bested. He represents the raw strength and drive to become the "heaviest" force in the room. His followers seek to emulate his booming presence and indomitable power. 

•Abrahamic Interpretation: Camael Viewed as the Archangel of Strength and Severity. Camael is the "Shard" associated with the raw power of God and the drive to enforce one's will upon the world. Followers pray to Camael when they seek the strength to overcome impossible odds or the ruthlessness to make difficult sacrifices for a higher position. 

"I am tyranny. I am tooth and claw. The fist. The first and the last. As long as the living vie for sharper blades and louder voices, I am strengthened. By my design the strongest rise to their righteous place. 

It seeks those driven by greed and offers easy power. 

"I am power. I am the fist. As long as the living vie for sharper blades and louder voices, I am strengthened. 

It seeks those driven by greed and offers easy power. 

------ 

 

  

Ambition Domain Proficiencies 

At 0th level, you gain the following proficiencies: 

Armor: Heavy Armor 

Weapons: Martial Weapons 

  

Spellcasting Ability 

Your spellcasting ability for divine spells is Strength. 

  

Fervor of Significance 

Whenever you apply a condition to a creature against its will, another creature gives you the Help action, or another creature applies a condition to you with your consent (you must be willing), you gain Divine Fervor. 

  

Channel Domain: Smite of Domination 

Starting at 2nd level, you can use your Channel Domain to crush a failing foe. When you hit a creature with a weapon or unarmed attack, or it fails a saving throw against one of your divine spells, you can use your Channel Domain to cause to deal an additional 3d6 psychic damage to the target.

It seeks those driven by greed and offers easy power. 

"I am power. I am the fist. As long as the living vie for sharper blades and louder voices, I am strengthened. 

It seeks those driven by greed and offers easy power. 

------ 

 

  

Ambition Domain Proficiencies 

At 0th level, you gain the following proficiencies: 

Armor: Heavy Armor 

Weapons: Martial Weapons 

  

Spellcasting Ability 

Your spellcasting ability for divine spells is Strength. 

  

Fervor of Significance 

Whenever you apply a condition to a creature against its will, another creature gives you the Help action, or another creature applies a condition to you with your consent (you must be willing), you gain Divine Fervor. 

  

Channel Domain: Smite of Domination 

Starting at 2nd level, you can use your Channel Domain to crush a failing foe. When you hit a creature with a weapon or unarmed attack, or it fails a saving throw against one of your divine spells, you can use your Channel Domain to cause to deal an additional 3d6 psychic damage to the target. 

  

Extra Attack 

Starting at 6th level, you can attack twice, instead of once, whenever you take the attack action on your turn. 

  

Channel Divinity: Exploit 

Finally at 10th level, you can use your Channel Divinity to find a creature's weak point.  

As a bonus action, you use your Channel Divinity to gain advantage on your next attack roll. If the attack hits, it is a critical hit. 

 

 

 

the humans and elves, while Davoon did so for the merfolk. , but in this world Thor is the most common name, a tribute to the ancient Norse warrior god who could not be bested. 

Sense Soul 

At 6th level none can hide from you.  

You can sense the approximate location of any creature in a 100 meter radius around you. This ability can penetrate most barriers, but is blocked by 1 meter of stone, wood or dirt, 10 centimeters of metal or a thin sheet of lead. Constructs are not detected.      

 

 

Revelation of Significance 

As a 1st level prophet of ambition, you are inspired to be important. To gather as much fame (or infamy), wealth and power as possible. At all costs. 

When you fail an ability check, attack roll, or saving throw, you may deal 1d6 psychic damage to yourself and reroll the d20. Then increase the damage this ability deals by 1d6 until you finish a night's rest. 

This psychic damage ignores any resistance or immunity you may have. 

 

 

 

 

 

Within every mortal soul sits the knowledge that it is significant. It is an innate need to be needed and headed. To be more than just another grain of sand. Those who follow the domain of ambition seek to be recognized. To grow in power. To unleash the potential within themselves regardless of the sacrifices they must make. They wish to become artists of incredible renown, leaders wielding power without peer, and warriors that will be remembered for the ages. At worst it causes some to take drastic measures to make themselves feel good, like turning to acts of extreme cruelty. Others enhance their own position by belittling and undermining others. At its best, the will to become the best leads to a sense of purpose and a competitive spirit that brings out the best in all. 

The sanctuaries of this domain instill a feeling of fear and insignificance. A feeling that one is surrounded by foes and must act quickly and decisively. The voices from beyond speak of competition and all that matters is getting ahead. Those who experience it describe that it instills a competitiveness within them that drives them to become better than others. 

In the Other World Eor represented this concept for most cultures.  

 

 

Every soul desires glory. To see itself above others. To feel the world yield. To leave a mark. It comes naturally to those who can overcome resistance, rise to the top, and crush anyone in their way. It requires power and leaves no room for those too weak to take what they want. Yet those who follow it risk becoming arrogant tyrants and an obsession with being seen that can hollow out every quieter virtue. 

The sanctuaries of this domain instill a feeling of fear and insignificance. A feeling that one is surrounded by foes and must act quickly and decisively. The voices from beyond speak of competition and all that matters is getting ahead. Those who experience it describe that it instills a competitiveness within them that drives them to become better than others. 

In the Other World this domain was favored by leaders of cults of personality that reveled in the ability to dominate others. Its temples were places of rigorous hierarchy where the strongest dominate the weakest by strength of body and mind. 

 

            

Conflicting change.

Valor Domain 

Tuesday, December 22, 2020 

11:02 PM 

Valor Domain 

I am freedom. I am breath and motion. The spirit, unbound. I will be by your side as you rise until you stand as your truest self. 

  

Every soul desires valor. To never fail. To be free. It requires precision and leaves no time for self-doubt. The sanctuaries of this domain instill a feeling of unmatched confidence in those that step foot within them.  

In the Other World, Kin is often the name of this domain's god. He was almost universally admired and used to inspire children everywhere. He strives to make every person the best they can be, free, and independent. He despises hierarchies and complacency. His followers devote themselves to personal perfection and self-sufficiency. In the New World many call him Tyr or know him as the archangel Michael. 

 

------ 

Driven by freedom, personal perfection, and the untamed spirit that refuses to break. 

•Pagan Interpretation: Tyr Associated with the ultimate discipline and the bravery to sacrifice a hand for the greater victory. Tyr represents the skilled warrior who relies on dexterity, technique, and absolute confidence in their own potential rather than brute force. 

•Abrahamic Interpretation: Michael The Archangel Michael is the General of the Host and the Prince of Light. He represents the "Shard" of God that champions bravery and the breaking of chains (liberation). He is the ideal for those who seek to perfect their martial skills to strike down evil with supernatural precision. 

------------  

 

 

 

Valor Domain Proficiencies 

At 0th level, you are proficient in the Acrobatics skill and your proficiency bonus is doubled on checks relating to it (expertise). If you are already proficient in Acrobatics, you gain proficiency in a different skill of your choice from the prophet skill list. 

You are also proficient with martial weapons. 

  

Spellcasting Ability 

Your spellcasting ability for divine spells is Dexterity. 

 

Fervor of Freedom 

Whenever you hit a creature with an attack, succeed a saving throw, or are missed by an attack, you gain Divine Fervor. 

 

Channel Domain: Guided Strike 

Starting at 2nd level, you can use your Channel Domain to strike with supernatural confidence. When you make an attack roll, you can use your Channel Domain to gain a +10 bonus to the roll. You make this choice after you see the roll, but before the GM says whether the attack hits or misses. 

  

Extra Attack 

Starting at 6th level, you can attack twice, instead of once, whenever you take the attack action on your turn. 

  

Channel Domain: Break the Chains 

Finally at 10th level, you can use your Channel Domain and your bonus action to target a creature no more than 10 meters (30 feet) away from you. All effects affecting it that can be ended by succeeding a saving throw instantly end. 

Valor Domain 

Tuesday, December 22, 2020 

11:02 PM 

Valor Domain 

I am freedom. I am breath and motion. The spirit, unbound. I will be by your side as you rise until you stand as your truest self. 

  

Every soul desires valor. To never fail. To be free. It requires precision and leaves no time for self-doubt. The sanctuaries of this domain instill a feeling of unmatched confidence in those that step foot within them.  

In the Other World, Kin is often the name of this domain's god. He was almost universally admired and used to inspire children everywhere. He strives to make every person the best they can be, free, and independent. He despises hierarchies and complacency. His followers devote themselves to personal perfection and self-sufficiency. In the New World many call him Tyr or know him as the archangel Michael. 

 

------ 

Driven by freedom, personal perfection, and the untamed spirit that refuses to break. 

Pagan Interpretation: Tyr Associated with the ultimate discipline and the bravery to sacrifice a hand for the greater victory. Tyr represents the skilled warrior who relies on dexterity, technique, and absolute confidence in their own potential rather than brute force. 

Abrahamic Interpretation: Michael The Archangel Michael is the General of the Host and the Prince of Light. He represents the "Shard" of God that champions bravery and the breaking of chains (liberation). He is the ideal for those who seek to perfect their martial skills to strike down evil with supernatural precision. 

------------  

 

 

 

Valor Domain Proficiencies 

At 0th level, you are proficient in the Acrobatics skill and your proficiency bonus is doubled on checks relating to it (expertise). If you are already proficient in Acrobatics, you gain proficiency in a different skill of your choice from the prophet skill list. 

You are also proficient with martial weapons. 

  

Spellcasting Ability 

Your spellcasting ability for divine spells is Dexterity. 

 

Fervor of Freedom 

Whenever you hit a creature with an attack, succeed a saving throw, or are missed by an attack, you gain Divine Fervor. 

 

Channel Domain: Guided Strike 

Starting at 2nd level, you can use your Channel Domain to strike with supernatural confidence. When you make an attack roll, you can use your Channel Domain to gain a +10 bonus to the roll. You make this choice after you see the roll, but before the GM says whether the attack hits or misses. 

  

Extra Attack 

Starting at 6th level, you can attack twice, instead of once, whenever you take the attack action on your turn. 

  

Channel Domain: Break the Chains 

Finally at 10th level, you can use your Channel Domain and your bonus action to target a creature no more than 10 meters (30 feet) away from you. All effects affecting it that can be ended by succeeding a saving throw instantly end. 

If you are being affected by an effect that can be ended by succeeding a saving throw at the start of your turn, you may use your Channel Domain to end it instantly (no action required). 

If the target of this ability is downed and/or unconscious from being reduced to 0 hit points, it regains 1 hit point. 

 

 

 

 

 

: Kin, for the elves and humans, Monshoom for the Merfolk of Verold's coasts and Durin for the dragons of Campestria to name a few. Tyr is a common name 

Absolute Determination 

At 6th level none can move you from your path to glory. You are immune to the charmed condition. 

 

Revelation of Growth 

As a 1st level prophet of valor, you are compelled to become your best self and be free. You are also proficient in all saving throws. 

 

 

Every soul desires valor. To never falter. To be free. It comes naturally to those who act quickly, who will not let themselves be tied down, who bend instead of breaking. It requires precision and leaves no time for self-doubt. Yet those who follow it risk overconfidence, contempt for caution and an addiction to the feeling of success. 

The sanctuaries of valor instill a feeling of unmatched confidence in those that step foot within them. It whispers into their ears, telling them that they can achieve anything they want. Those who experience this describe the god as a presence that believes in your potential with such zeal, that it inspires you to believe it too. 

In the Other World, this domain was almost universally admired and used to inspire children everywhere. Though prayed to by many and counting the most followers, it formed no organized cults. Its temples usually monasteries dedicated to personal perfection. Its prophets were often sparks of revolution: voices that preached courage, swift action, and the will to be free. Especially among the Border Lords they drove swift decisions and unbinding action. 

 

Conflicting change.

Knowledge Domain 

Sunday, December 20, 2020 

11:55 AM 

Knowledge Domain 

I am curiosity. I am the winding path. The new, known. There is so much to see and know, and I will open your eyes.  

 

Every soul desires knowledge. To be curious. To be awed. It requires the ability to understand the things around you and learn how things work. The sanctuaries of this domain instill a feeling of deep awe.  

In the Other World Cartouche is the name often given to the deity of this domain. He strives to further understanding of all things, to have science and technology advance, and to discover new things. He despises ignorance and the end of pursuit. His followers are scientists, explorers, and detectives that attempt to uncover the hidden truths of the world. In the New World many call him Heimdall or know him as the archangel Raziel. 

 

 

------------ 

Driven by curiosity, the scientific method, and the truth hidden within the Void. 

•Pagan Interpretation: Odin The All-Father who sacrificed his eye for wisdom. Followers of this domain revere Odin as the ultimate seeker who wandered the planes and hung from Yggdrasil to understand the fundamental runes of reality. He represents the "star-filled void" of true sight. 

•Abrahamic Interpretation: Raziel Known as the "Keeper of Secrets" or the "Angel of Mysteries." Raziel is believed to be the "Shard" that holds the knowledge of the universe, standing close to the throne of God to record all that is said. Followers believe he allows them to peer through the veil and see the world's code (truesight). 

It seeks those driven by curiosity and offers enlightenment. 

 

-----------------  

  

Knowledge Domain Proficiencies 

At 0th level, you are proficient in the Investigation and Perception skills and your proficiency bonus is doubled on checks relating to these two (expertise). If you are already proficient in one of the named skills, you gain proficiency in a different skill of your choice from the prophet skill list. 

  

Spellcasting Ability 

Your spellcasting ability for divine spells is Intelligence. 

  

Fervor of Curiosity 

Whenever an opponent succeeds a saving throw against your spells, fail an ability check, or fail a saving throw, you gain Divine Fervor.  

 

Channel Domain: Reveal Mistake  

Starting at 2nd level, you can use your Channel Domain to realize flaws in your actions or plans. When you make an ability check, saving throw, or attack roll you can use your Channel Domain to reroll it after seeing the result, but before you know if it succeeds or fails. You get a bonus to this reroll equal to your prophet level. 

 

Potent Casting 

Starting at 6th level, you add your Intelligence modifier to the psychic damage you deal with any prophet spell.

Fervor of Curiosity

Potent Casting 

Starting at 6th level, you add your Intelligence modifier to the psychic damage you deal with any prophet spell. 

  

Channel Domain: True Seeing 

Finally at 10th level, you can use your Channel Domain to reveal the true nature of the world. Purple veins crisscross your eyes as they turn into a star-filled void. Your vision pierces through the veil and you receive a glimpse of the Void beyond.  

You may use your action to gain truesight out to a range of 40 meters (120 feet) for 1 minute. This ability requires concentration, as though you were concentrating on a spell. Truesight allows you to see in normal and magical darkness, see invisible creatures and objects, automatically see through illusions and detect if an object or creature is being affected by magic (but not by what kind). Furthermore, you can see into the Void. 

 

------------------------------ 

 

Every mortal soul contains a free spirit that desires to see the world. Those who follow their curiosity devote themselves to experiencing everything it has to offer. At its worst, this desire results in a search for truth in conspiracies and the abandonment of morals in pursuit of discovery. At its best, it leads to wonderful enlightenment and the discovery of real truth through the scientific method. 

The sanctuaries of this domain instill a feeling of awe. Those that enter receive a glimpse of how much the world still has to offer them and become inspired to unearth every secret, be it hidden by an ocean, forest, or quantum physics. 

 

 

 

 

Starting at 6th level, you can call on your patron to alter fate in your favor. When you make an ability check or a saving throw, you can use this feature to add a d10 to your roll. You can do so after seeing the initial roll but before any of the roll’s effects occur. 

 

Once you use this feature, you can’t use it again until you finish a short or long rest.  

 

 

 by the elves and humans, but the giants liked to refer to it as Mon. In this world Odin is the name most used here, in tribute to the norse god that sacrificed his eye in the pursuit of knowledge. 

 

Absolute Truth  

At 6th level none can deceive you. You know if you are being told a lie. You cannot be deceived and know if a creature attempts to conceal information from you. This ability is not fooled by fancy wording or similar tricks. 

If a creature is resistant to psychic damage, this ability has no effect on them. If a creature is immune to psychic damage, it always seems like it is telling the truth. 

 

 

 

you are blessed by exploring new paths and learing from every failure. Whenever you miss an attack or fail an ability check or saving throw, you gain an amount of Temporary Vitality equal to your Proficiency Bonus. This temporary  

 

 

 gain the ability to call on you become proficient in the following skills: Arcana, History, Nature, or Religion. 

Your proficiency bonus is doubled for any ability check you make that uses either of those skills. 

 

 

When you roll Initiative, you can add your Intelligence modifier to the roll. 

 

Your curiosity makes you an expert in all things new and interesting. You add half your Intelligence modifier rounded up to any skill check you make.

Duty Domain 

Saturday, January 9, 2021 

11:06 AM 

Duty Domain 

I am community. The sworn word. The service given, freely. You must bear one another as your burdens are your bonds. 

 

Every soul desires duty. To be part of a greater whole. To contribute. It requires perception, restraint, moral clarity, and the ability to act rightly without needing certainty. The sanctuaries of this domain instill a feeling of happiness and safety.  

In the Other World, Helvetica is often the name of this domain's god. She strives for community, equality and service to others. She despises abandonment and tyranny. She is worshiped by parents, soldiers, and those who wish to selflessly contribute to something greater. Her followers attempt to build societies based on equality and compassion. In the New World many call her Frigg or know her as the archangel Uriel. 

 

--------------- 

Driven by resolve, community, protection, and the safety of the group. 

•Pagan Interpretation: Frigg The Watcher. Heimdall represents the unblinking eye that guards the community. His followers are the sentinels who stand on the walls, ensuring that the "utopia" of their society remains safe from threats. He embodies the "Battle Commands" that rally the troops. 

•Abrahamic Interpretation: Uriel Often depicted as the Sentinel of Eden standing guard with a fiery sword, Uriel represents the "Shard" of vigilant protection and service. He is the guardian of the faithful, the one who watches over the flock and issues the commands that keep the community bound together against darkness. 

It seeks those with unflinching resolve and offers companionship. 

 

----------------- 

  

Duty Domain Proficiencies 

At 0th level, you gain the following proficiencies: 

Armor: Heavy Armor 

Weapons: Martial Weapons 

 

Spellcasting Ability 

Your spellcasting ability for divine spells is Wisdom. 

  

Fervor of Contribution 

Whenever you move within reach of a hostile creature or take the Dash or Disengage action, you gain Divine Fervor. 

 

Channel Domain: Battle Commands 

Starting at 2nd level, you can use your Channel Domain to rally others in battle.  

Attack Order. As a bonus action, choose one friendly creature that can hear or see you and use your Channel Domain. That creature may immediately use its reaction to make one attack. 

Maneuvering Order. As a bonus action, choose a creature that can hear or see you and use your Channel Domain. That creature may move up to its speed without provoking attacks of opportunity. 

Rally Order. As a bonus action, choose a creature being affected by a temporary condition that can be ended with a saving throw and use your Channel Domain. The creature may immediately attempt a saving throw against that effect. 

Duty Domain

I am community. The sworn word. The service given, freely. You must bear one another as your burdens are your bonds.

In the New World many call her Frigg or know her as the archangel Uriel. 

 

--------------- 

Driven by resolve, community, protection, and the safety of the group. 

Pagan Interpretation: Frigg The Watcher. Heimdall represents the unblinking eye that guards the community. His followers are the sentinels who stand on the walls, ensuring that the "utopia" of their society remains safe from threats. He embodies the "Battle Commands" that rally the troops.

He is the guardian of the faithful, the one who watches over the flock and issues the commands that keep the community bound together against darkness. 

It seeks those with unflinching resolve and offers companionship. 

 

----------------- 

  

Duty Domain Proficiencies 

At 0th level, you gain the following proficiencies: 

Armor: Heavy Armor 

Weapons: Martial Weapons 

 

Spellcasting Ability 

Your spellcasting ability for divine spells is Wisdom. 

  

Fervor of Contribution 

Whenever you move within reach of a hostile creature or take the Dash or Disengage action, you gain Divine Fervor. 

 

Channel Domain: Battle Commands 

Starting at 2nd level, you can use your Channel Domain to rally others in battle.  

Attack Order. As a bonus action, choose one friendly creature that can hear or see you and use your Channel Domain. That creature may immediately use its reaction to make one attack. 

Maneuvering Order. As a bonus action, choose a creature that can hear or see you and use your Channel Domain. That creature may move up to its speed without provoking attacks of opportunity. 

Rally Order. As a bonus action, choose a creature being affected by a temporary condition that can be ended with a saving throw and use your Channel Domain. The creature may immediately attempt a saving throw against that effect. 

  

Potent Casting 

Starting at 6th level, you add your Wisdom modifier to the psychic damage you deal with any prophet spell. 

  

Channel Domain: Call of Duty 

Finally at 10th level, your god's power removes all hesitation. At the start of combat, you may use your Channel Domain to give yourself and one creature of your choice that can hear or see you advantage on its initiative roll. 

 

 

, but in the new world, Heimdall is the name most often used for it. 

 

Revelation of Contribution 

Also at 1st level, you can invoke the power of duty and cooperation to drive an ally to attack. If you cast a divine spell and target an ally with the spell, that ally can use their reaction immediately after you finish casting to make one weapon attack against a creature of your choice that you can see. 

If the spell targets more than one ally, you choose the ally that may make the attack. 

 

 

Within every soul lies a sense of duty. It is a need to further some greater good, to champion a cause greater than oneself. At its worst, it creates mindless drones that commit horrific atrocities in the name of their ideal. At its best, this need creates a tightly-knit community that can weather any storm. 

Those who enter a sanctuary of this domain are overwhelmed by a feeling of safety and community. The god embraces all in what many describe as a soothing psychic hug. Few things are as relaxing as this god's embrace and those who experience it are inspired to build a utopia, together. 

In the Other World, Helvetica was the name many used to refer to this god. 

 

 

Conflicting change.

Love Domain 

Tuesday, December 22, 2020 

10:42 AM 

Love Domain 

I am connection. I am the bridge between breaths. The warm thread that ties strangers. As long as life flourishes, I will be there to guide it together. 

 

Every soul desires love. To be with someone. To lust. It requires the strength of character to open oneself to others and draw them in. The sanctuaries of this domain instill an overwhelming feeling of lust. 

In the Other World Ostara is a common name for the god of this domain. She strives for people to connect, and spread life. She favors the immortal in particular. She despises loneliness and endings. She wants people to live and be together everywhere and forever. Her followers are lovers. They seek the lonely, spread the gift of life and try to connect with as many as they can. In the New World many call her Freyja or know her as the archangel Haniel. 

 

----------------------- 

Driven by connection, passion, beauty, and magnetic companionship. 

•Pagan Interpretation: Freyja & Freyr The twin deities are worshipped together as the dual aspects of this domain—representing both the lust/passion (infatuation) and the fertility/growth of love. They are the faces of the "Twin Faced God" mentioned in the lore, embodying the magnetic pull between souls. 

•Abrahamic Interpretation: Haniel The Archangel of Joy and Grace. Haniel is the "Shard" associated with the beauty of God and the connectivity between living beings. Followers invoke Haniel to foster harmony, charm their enemies, and realize the "Revelation of Connection" that stops a blade with a simple look. 

It seeks those who act with determination and offers companionship. 

------------------------- 

  

Love Domain Proficiencies 

At 0th level, you are proficient in the Deception and Persuasion skills and your proficiency bonus is doubled on checks relating to these two (expertise). If you are already proficient in one of the named skills, you gain proficiency in a different skill of your choice from the prophet skill list. 

  

Spellcasting Ability 

Your spellcasting ability divine spells is Charisma. 

  

Fervor of Connection 

Whenever you target a willing creature with a spell or ability or use the help action, you gain Divine Fervor. 

  

Channel Domain: Charming Presence 

Starting at 2nd level, you can use your Channel Domain to infatuate those around you. 

As an action, you make a seductive gesture. Each creature of your choice that can see or hear you within 10 meters (30 feet) of you must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or be charmed by you until the end of your next turn or until the charmed creature takes any damage. You can also cause any of the charmed creatures to drop what they are holding when they fail the saving throw. 

  

Potent Casting 

Starting at 6th level, you add your Charisma modifier to the psychic damage you deal with any prophet spell.

Spellcasting Ability

Fervor of Connection

Channel Domain: Charming Presence 

Starting at 2nd level, you can use your Channel Domain to infatuate those around you. 

As an action, you make a seductive gesture. Each creature of your choice that can see or hear you within 10 meters (30 feet) of you must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or be charmed by you until the end of your next turn or until the charmed creature takes any damage. You can also cause any of the charmed creatures to drop what they are holding when they fail the saving throw. 

  

Potent Casting 

Starting at 6th level, you add your Charisma modifier to the psychic damage you deal with any prophet spell. 

  

Channel Domain: Infatuation 

Finally at 10th level, you may use your Channel Domain to cause your enemies to become stricken by your beauty. 

As a bonus action, you can expend a use of your Channel Domain to impose disadvantage on a creature's attacks against you and on its saving throws against your spells and abilities until the start of your next turn. 

  

 

 

 

 in the other world, in the new world Freyja and Freyr are often used to refer to the twin gods of love and fertility. 

 

 

Revelation of Connection 

Also at 1st level, those around you feel an immediate connection to you. Even those who wish to harm you will find their hands hesitate before every strike.  

While you are not wearing armor you may add your Charisma modifier to your Armor Class. 

 

 

 

 

The domain of love and community embodies the desire to be with someone. Those who follow it have magnetic personalities and devote themselves to life itself. For there is nothing that provides happiness like love. Love between friends, family, and, well, lovers. It is thus no surprise that the love domain not only compels its followers to spread love, but to act on it as well. At their worst its followers abuse those around them as they act upon their lust without reason or restraint. At its best, this domain spreads comradery, love, and awe throughout the world. 

Those who enter a sanctuary of this domain are met with an overwhelming feeling of happiness and lust. 

This domain is usually represented by the twin faced god Ostara. 

 In the New World many call her Freyja or know her as the archangel Haniel. 

Conflicting change.

Ranger 

Sunday, December 27, 2020 

8:32 PM 

Ranger 

Versatile and independent 

  

A female elf sits in a tree, overlooking the road below. As she sees the bandits approach, she silently pulls out her revolver and as she clenches her fist, her skin hardens and takes on a bark-like appearance. They pass her by and just as they do, she jumps off her perch. Before they can react, she makes a pirouette, delivering lethal shot upon lethal shot from her trusty firearm. 

A human man lays his machete on the ground and places five rifle rounds next to it. He unrolls his tools, lights up a gas cooker, and begins brewing. Mushrooms and flowers are crushed and the liquid filtered from them. As magic is infused over a low flame a grey liquid with a pink sheen is the final result. Careful not to touch the potion, he smears it on his blade and ammunition. Those vampires won't even know what hit them. 

Hissing a quick thanks to his hawk companion, the male kobold takes aim at the young red dragon relaxing in the shadow of a half-collapsed department store. He pulls out his compound bow and focuses his gaze on the dragon. Mystical powers mark the creature as his prey and draw his gaze onto its weak points. He lifts his weapon and silently takes aim at the creature that thinks it can take whatever it wants without consequence. 

  

Rangers combine martial prowess with wild magical powers to annihilate their foes. These warriors are meant to operate alone, combining the survivability of conventional martial training and enhance it with the power of wild magic. Most Rangers go on The Path, a journey on which they prove that they can fight and survive independently, or at least in a small group. 

  

Self-Sufficient  

Rangers are highly skilled professionals who are trained to work independently, making them the ultimate self-sufficient explorers and survivors. They embody the saying "Jack of all trades, master of none," as they are not equipped with a stockpile of spells like other magic users, but instead focus on mastering a select few wild magic techniques that enhance their other abilities. Rangers attune their bodies to the world and internalize the magic they use, making the spells they know easier to cast. However, this also means that they cannot change the spells they have internalized, and each technique takes significant effort to learn. 

Despite lacking the many specialized maneuvers of other martial warriors, rangers possess unparalleled knowledge of the lands they roam. Additionally, the ability to speak sylvan and thus communicate with any living thing means that a ranger is never truly lost. In combat, rangers can execute only a few techniques, splitting their focus in a way that neither makes them mighty martial artists nor powerful spellcasters. Nevertheless, they can take the best of both worlds and use it as needed, making them highly adaptable to any situation. 

Thanks to their exceptional skills, rangers can accomplish many tasks alone that would typically require an entire team. From tracking and hunting to scouting and surveying, rangers are unsurpassed in their ability to thrive in even the most challenging environments. Overall, rangers are a force to be reckoned with, combining their expertise in survival, combat, and wild magic to achieve feats that few others can.  

  

Lone Wolf  

In the Other World, rangers are predominantly found as members of elite combat units or remote tribes. However, they are particularly prevalent among the wildeborn, who were the pioneers of this unique combat style. These skilled professionals are typically employed as sentinels to safeguard the borders of the wilderness, using their expertise to not only warn of incoming enemies but also engage them in advance. Rangers are adept at slowing down enemy advances or entirely repelling attacks using hit-and-run tactics. Alternatively, they can scout ahead of warbands to ensure that there are no nasty surprises ahead.

8:32 PM 

Ranger 

Versatile and independent 

  

A female elf sits in a tree, overlooking the road below. Mushrooms and flowers are crushed and the liquid filtered from them. As magic is infused over a low flame a grey liquid with a pink sheen is the final result. These warriors are meant to operate alone, combining the survivability of conventional martial training and enhance it with the power of wild magic. Most Rangers go on The Path, a journey on which they prove that they can fight and survive independently, or at least in a small group. 

  

Self-Sufficient  

Rangers are highly skilled professionals who are trained to work independently, making them the ultimate self-sufficient explorers and survivors. They embody the saying "Jack of all trades, master of none," as they are not equipped with a stockpile of spells like other magic users, but instead focus on mastering a select few wild magic techniques that enhance their other abilities. However, this also means that they cannot change the spells they have internalized, and each technique takes significant effort to learn.

Additionally, the ability to speak sylvan and thus communicate with any living thing means that a ranger is never truly lost. In combat, rangers can execute only a few techniques, splitting their focus in a way that neither makes them mighty martial artists nor powerful spellcasters. Nevertheless, they can take the best of both worlds and use it as needed, making them highly adaptable to any situation. 

Thanks to their exceptional skills, rangers can accomplish many tasks alone that would typically require an entire team. From tracking and hunting to scouting and surveying, rangers are unsurpassed in their ability to thrive in even the most challenging environments. Overall, rangers are a force to be reckoned with, combining their expertise in survival, combat, and wild magic to achieve feats that few others can.  

  

Lone Wolf  

In the Other World, rangers are predominantly found as members of elite combat units or remote tribes. However, they are particularly prevalent among the wildeborn, who were the pioneers of this unique combat style. These skilled professionals are typically employed as sentinels to safeguard the borders of the wilderness, using their expertise to not only warn of incoming enemies but also engage them in advance. Rangers are adept at slowing down enemy advances or entirely repelling attacks using hit-and-run tactics. Alternatively, they can scout ahead of warbands to ensure that there are no nasty surprises ahead. 

A master ranger is a relentless force, impossible to pin down, and capable of unleashing devastating attacks. Facing an expert ranger in combat is a disheartening experience, as they strike from nowhere with relentless precision, leaving opponents with no chance to fight back. Their unparalleled skills in survival, combat, and wild magic make them a formidable force to be reckoned with, both on and off the battlefield. 

Overall, rangers in the Other World are a rare and invaluable asset, feared by their enemies and revered by their allies. Their unique combination of skills and expertise allows them to operate effectively in the harshest environments, making them an essential component of any elite combat unit or remote tribe. 

 

Building a Ranger 

Whether by choice or chance, you have been blessed with the body of an elite warrior, and with it, the potential for greatness. As a ranger, you are a master of survival, possessing the skills necessary to thrive in even the harshest of environments. But with wild magic flowing through your veins, your abilities are enhanced beyond measure. 

Your connection to the untamed forces of nature grants you an instinctual understanding of the world around you. You can feel the ebb and flow of the elements, and draw upon their power to augment your own. Whether you need to move with blinding speed or strike with devastating force, your wild magic gives you the edge you need to succeed. 

As a ranger, you are a valuable asset to any team, but you also have the option to operate alone. With your skills and magic, you can tackle even the most daunting of challenges by yourself. And while you may not excel in any one area, you are versatile enough to adapt to any situation. 

Will you choose to use your skills to help others, protecting those who cannot protect themselves? Or will you give in to your wilder impulses, taking what you want through hit-and-run attacks? Perhaps you will find a balance between the two, doing what you must to survive while also aiding those in need. 

Whatever path you choose, know that as a wild magic-wielding ranger, you are a force to be reckoned with. Your skills and magic make you a valuable ally and a formidable opponent, and your choices will shape the world around you. 

  

Ranger Features 

Primary Ability Scores: Strength/Dexterity, Wisdom

CLASS FEATURES 

As a ranger, you gain the following class features. 

  

Ranger Proficiencies 

Starting at 0th level, the ranger class provides you with the following proficiencies: 

Armor: Light Armor, Medium Armor, Shields 

Weapons: Simple Weapons, Martial Weapons 

Saving Throws: Strength or Dexterity, Wisdom 

Skills: Choose three from Acrobatics, Animal Handling, Arcana, Athletics, Deception, History, Insight, Intimidation, Investigation, Mechanics, Medicine, Nature, Perception, Performance, Persuasion, Religion, Science, Sleight of Hand, Stealth, and Survival 

 

Multiclassing 

Ability Score Minimum: Strength or Dexterity and Wisdom 13 

When you multiclass into the ranger, you gain the following proficiencies: 

Armor: Light Armor, Medium Armor, Shields 

Weapons: Simple Weapons, Martial Weapons 

Skills: One skill from the ranger skill list 

  

Spellcasting 

As a ranger, your patron trained to weave wild magic into martial combat. 

Wild Magic 

When you gain your 1st level, you learn how to control the natural world around you and gain access to wild magic. The chapter on spellcasting provides a list of the spells you have access to in the subchapter on wild magic. 

Learning Spells 

At 1st level, you learn the Channel Wild I spell. You may then choose a total of 2 spells from the wild magic spell list. Whenever you gain a level, you may choose 1 new spell from the wild magic spell list. Whenever you gain access to a new spell level, you automatically learn the corresponding Channel Wild spell, which does not count against the number of spells you may choose. The Ranger Spell Level table tells you when you gain access to spells of greater complexity (higher level). Once you know a spell, you may cast it at any time, as long as you have enough vitality. 

For every ranger spell that you know, excluding Channel Wild spells, decrease your maximum vitality by an amount equal to the spell's level (1 for novice, 2 for apprentice, and 3 for adept level spells). 

Spellcasting Ability 

Wisdom is your spellcasting ability for your ranger spells, since their power derives from your understanding of nature. You use your Wisdom whenever a spell refers to your spellcasting ability. In addition, you use your Wisdom modifier when setting the saving throw DC for a ranger spell you cast and when making an attack roll with one. 

Spell Save DC = 8 + your Proficiency Bonus + your Wisdom modifier 

Spell Attack Modifier = your Proficiency Bonus + your Wisdom modifier 

Vitality 

When you begin to cast a spell, decrease your current vitality by the amount listed as the spell's Vitality Cost. If you reach 0 Vitality, you fall unconscious and the spell you were casting fails unless you make a final push. (See the general rules on Spellcasting for details). 

  

Ranger Spell Level 

Spell Level 

	

Ranger Level 




Novice 

	

1st 




Apprentice 

	

5th 




Adept 

	

9th 

  

Maneuvers 

As a ranger you learn maneuvers, special actions that can be taken in combat, to blend with your wild magic. 

Maneuvers 

Maneuvers are special, almost magical, spell-like abilities that warriors may employ in combat. They represent bursts of extreme focus and physical power that push their user to the limit of their ability. 

The chapter on spellcasting provides a list of maneuvers you have access to in the subchapter on maneuvers. 

Learning Maneuvers 

At 2nd level, you learn 2 maneuvers and an additional 1 every even level thereafter (4th level, 6th level, 8th level, and 10th level). Refer to the Ranger Maneuver Level table to see at what level you gain access to higher-level maneuvers.  

Maneuver Ability 

Strength or Dexterity (your choice) is the ability for your ranger maneuvers, as they rely on your martial prowess. You use the chosen ability score whenever a maneuver refers to your maneuver ability. In addition, you use the modifier of the chosen ability when setting the saving throw DC for maneuvers you use. When a maneuver calls for an attack, you use the attack modifier you normally use for the spell, unarmed or weapon attack you decide to use. 

Maneuver Save DC = 8 + your Proficiency Bonus + your Strength or Dexterity modifier (your choice) 

Vitality 

When you initiate a maneuver, decrease your current vitality by the amount listed as the maneuver's Vitality Cost. If you reach 0 Vitality, you fall unconscious, and the maneuver you were initiating fails unless you make a final push. (See the general rules on Spellcasting for details). 

  

Ranger Maneuver Level 

Maneuver Level 

	

Ranger Level 




Beginner 

	

2nd 




Veteran 

	

6th 




Elite 

	

10th 

  

Fighting Style 

At 2nd level, you adopt a particular style of fighting as your specialty. Choose one of the following options. You can't take a Fighting Style option more than once, even if you later get to choose again. 

  

ARCHERY 

You gain a +2 bonus to attack rolls you make with non-firearm ranged weapons. 

DUELING 

When you are wielding a melee weapon in one hand and no other weapons, you gain +2 bonus to damage rolls with that weapon. 

QUICK 

While not wearing armor, your speed is increased by 1m. and you gain a +1 bonus to your AC. 

SHARPSHOOTER 

Your ranged attacks (including spells) ignore half and three-quarters cover and you do not suffer disadvantage at long range. 

SPELLSWORD 

After using your action to cast a spell, you may use your bonus action to make a single attack with a 1 handed weapon. 

  

Ranger's Path  

When you reach your 3rd level, the path your patron took reveals itself. The path is a journey initiate rangers are sent on to prove to that they can survive on their own or in a small group. The path a ranger takes determines the rest of their life and the path your patron traveled determines which abilities you gain here and then again at 6th, and 10th level. 

  

Ability Score Improvement or Feat 

When you reach 4th level, and again at 8th level, you can increase one attribute score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two attribute scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an attribute score above 20 using this feature. Alternatively you may choose a feat from the Feat list. 

  

Extra Attack 

Beginning at 5th level, you can attack twice, instead of once, whenever you take the Attack action on your turn. 

  

Ranger’s Path 

The Ranger's Path is a sacred journey that every initiate must undertake to become a full-fledged ranger. It is a test of survival, strength, and resourcefulness. Each ranger group trains differently, and the path they send their initiates on reflects their unique outlook on the world and their training. But the Ranger's Path is not just a test of survival skills. It is also a spiritual journey, designed to connect the initiates with the natural world and the wild magic that flows through it. As the initiates navigate the path, they must learn to attune themselves to the rhythms of nature, to understand the language of the animals, and to tap into the power of wild magic.

Path of the Hunter 

Sunday, December 27, 2020 

10:40 PM 

The Path of the Hunter 

Survive by the Hunt 

  

Rangers who follow the path of the hunter are among the most skilled predators in the Other World. They have unparalleled knowledge of their prey and the lands where they live.  

These rangers specialize in tracking down their marks, whether they are dangerous beasts, elusive predators, or even intelligent humanoids. They often work as bounty hunters, that are hired to track and capture or kill specific targets. Through wild magic they can gain a deep understanding of the habits and behaviors of their prey. This allows them to predict their movements, set effective traps and take lethal shots. Their expertise in hunting means that they can take on even the most difficult prey. 

Despite their formidable skills, rangers of this path are often solitary figures, preferring to work alone or in small groups. They are often seen as somewhat aloof, and are not known for their social skills. However, they are respected and admired for their abilities as hunters, and are often sought out for their expertise when it comes to dealing with dangerous creatures. 

  

Hunter Magic 

Starting at 3rd level, you learn additional spells that do not count against the spells you may learn when you reach that levels. Refer to the Slayer Spells table to see what these spells are. If you already know one of the listed spells, you may learn a different spell of your choice. 

  

Slayer Spells 

Ranger Level 

	
 

Spells 




3rd 

	

Hunter's Mark, Zephyr Strike 




5th 

	

Barkskin 




9th 

	

Resistance 




  

Favored Foe 

Starting when you take this path at 3rd level, when you hit a creature with an attack, or affect it with a spell, you may spend 2 vitality to probe its defenses and mark it as your favored foe. When you do so, you learn whether the creature has any damage or condition immunities, resistances, or vulnerabilities and what they are. 

After probing a creature, your damaging spells and attacks deal an additional amount of damage equal to your Proficiency Bonus to the target. If you enhance an attack with one or more spells, this ability still only triggers once for that attack.  

You can only have one favored foe at a time and when you mark a new creature, the effect ends on any creature previously marked 

  

Adaptive Defense 

At 7th level, you gain extra resilience against your prey’s assaults on your mind and body. Whenever a favored foe forces you to make a saving throw and whenever you make an ability check to escape that target’s grapple, you have advantage on the roll of the d20. 

  

Predicted Shot 

Finally at 10th level, you are a master hunter and have learned to predict and precisely track the movements of a creature your favored foes. Attacks against a favored foe have advantage.

Monday, December 21, 2020 

10:27 AM 

 

Spellblade 

Expert at arcane trickery 

 

Spellblades train to combine magic with precise attacks. Underground mages, and cultists of the elementals are particularly fond of combining the roguish skillsets of their members with what little magic their organizations can gather. In addition to just enhancing their capabilities with magic, they focus on weaving their spells into their tactics. This makes learning these spells even more difficult than usual but makes for a deadly combination. Most spellblades favor the Lore of Light. 

  

Spellcasting 

Starting when you choose this subclass at 3rd level, you gain the ability to cast spells that your patron trained and committed to ingrained memory. See the chapter on spellcasting for the general rules on using magic. 

Elemental Magic 

As a spellblade, you choose the lore your patron practiced. Choose from the Lore of Force, Light, Frost, Lightning, Fire, or Life. Your lore not only determines your spellcasting ability but also what spells you have access to. The chapter on spellcasting provides detailed descriptions of each lore and the spells they have access to in the subchapter on elemental magic. Refer to the spell list of your lore to see what these spells are and what effects they have. 

Learning Spells 

At 3rd level, you learn the Channel I spell of your lore and 2 Novice spells. Whenever you gain a level, you may choose 1 new spell from your lore of a level you have access to. 

Whenever you gain access to a new spell level, you automatically learn the corresponding channel spell, which does not count against the number of spells you may choose at that level. Any spell you learn is committed to your memory and can be cast at any time, as long as you have the needed vitality. Refer to the Spellblade Spell Level table to see when you gain access to a new level of spells. 

Spellcasting Ability 

Your lore determines your spellcasting ability for your spellblade spells. You use the ability score determined by your lore whenever a spell refers to your spellcasting ability. In addition, you use your ability modifier when setting the saving throw DC for a spellblade spell you cast and when making an attack roll with one. 

Spell Save DC = 8 + your Proficiency Bonus + your Spellcasting Ability modifier 

Spell Attack Modifier = your Proficiency Bonus + your Spellcasting Ability modifier 

Vitality 

When you begin to cast a spell, decrease your current vitality by the amount listed as the spell's Vitality Cost. If you reach 0 Vitality, you fall unconscious and the spell you were casting fails unless you make a final push. (See the general rules on Spellcasting for details). 

  

Spellblade Spell Level 

Spell Level 

	
 

Character Level 




Novice 

	

3rd 




Apprentice 

	

7th 




 

Spell Tactics 

When you reach 6th level, the technique with which you interweave sword and sorcery becomes near perfect allowing you to jump into combat even faster. You may add your spellcasting ability modifier to your initiative. 

  

Arcane Ambush 

Finally at 10th level, you are a master of combining magical and martial techniques. If you are hidden from a creature when you cast a spell on it, the target has disadvantage on the first saving throw it makes against that spell. 

  

  

Archive 

Merging magic with precise attacks requires a special type of training and discipline, but those who master it become formidable adversaries in any battle. Underground mages and elemental cultists are known to value these hybrid skillsets, incorporating them into their own rogue-like methodologies. While adding magic to their arsenal enhances their abilities, these practitioners also focus on weaving their spells into their tactics, making it even more challenging to master the art of spellblades. Those who achieve this, however, become a deadly combination of stealth, deception, and magic, and are highly sought after in the most perilous of situations. Interestingly, most spellblades tend to favor the Lore of Light, utilizing its illuminating properties to enhance their precision and combat prowess.

Monday, May 16, 2022 

7:32 PM 

Thief 

Experts at the job 

 

Thieves specialize in the larcenous arts. Burglars, bandits, cutpurses, and other criminals typically follow this archetype, but so do rogues who prefer to think of themselves as professional treasure seekers, explorers, delvers, and scavengers. In addition to improving your agility and stealth, you learn skills useful for moving through the ruins of the old world. 

 

Fast Hands 

Starting at 3rd level, you can use the bonus action granted by your Cunning Action to make a Dexterity (Sleight of Hand) check, use your thieves' tools to disarm a trap or open a lock, or take the Use an Object action. 

 

Second-Story Work 

When you choose this archetype at 3rd level, you also gain a climbing speed equal to your walking speed. If you already have a climbing speed equal to your walking speed, increase your speed by 2 meters instead. 

In addition, when you make a running jump, the distance you cover increases by a number of meters equal to your Dexterity modifier. 

 

Supreme Sneak 

Starting at 6th level, you have advantage on a Dexterity (Stealth) check if you move no more than half your speed on the same turn. 

 

Thief's Reflexes 

Finally at 10th level, you have become adept at laying ambushes and quickly escaping danger. You can take two turns during the first round of any combat. You take your first turn at your normal initiative and your second turn at your initiative minus 10.  

This feature does not function if you are surprised.

Friday, March 26, 2021 

2:07 PM 

Spark of the Plains 

Wind of the plains 

  

The strongest of winds blow across the endless green plains, where the mightiest of beasts live. Sorcerers imbued with an elemental spark of the plains command force.  

Force magic suffuses your body. Green energy pulses through your veins and causes your hair to billow when you're angry. Your will can seep from your body into an object. Steel, wood, or even stone is placed under your control. At a whim, your weapon flies to do your bidding. 

  

Force Spellcasting 

Starting at 1st level, force magic suffuses your being. You gain access to the Lore of Force and learn the Channel Force I spell. When you gain access to a new spell level at 3rd, 5th, 7th and 9th level, you also learn the corresponding Channel Force spells. 

Your spellcasting ability for sorcerer spells is Strength. 

 

Heart of Force  

Starting at 1st level, your every move exudes strength. You are resistant to force damage and ignore difficult terrain. 

 

Body of the Plains 

Starting at 6th level, you automatically succeed saving throws against effects that would move you against your will. 

 

Force Immunity 

Finally, at 10th level, you are immune to force damage. 

 

 

 

 

Steps of the Unyielding 

 

Deflecting Winds 

Also at 1st level, you may expulse your magic to fortify yourself against harm. When you are hit by an attack or you fail a saving throw, you may use your reaction to gain a +2 bonus to your AC against that attack or a +4 bonus to that saving throw. 

  

Moving Wind 

Starting at 6th level, you know how to ride the flow of your magic. Immediately after you use your action to cast a Force spell of Novice level or higher, you can fly up to 5 meters (15 feet) in a straight line to an unoccupied space. You must land at the end of this movement or you fall. This movement does not provoke attacks of opportunity. 

  

Blessing of the Gales 

Finally at 10th level,  the magic you channel helps ward you against harm. While concentrating on a force spell, you have a +2 bonus to AC and all saving throws.

Body of the Plains

Force Immunity

You must land at the end of this movement or you fall. This movement does not provoke attacks of opportunity. 

  

Blessing of the Gales 

Finally at 10th level,  the magic you channel helps ward you against harm. While concentrating on a force spell, you have a +2 bonus to AC and all saving throws. 

 

 

 

 

Moving Wind 

For every 5 meters (15 feet) you move or fall on your turn, you gain a Force Charge. You may expend a charge The next time you deal force damage with a spell or attack, you consume all charges. For every charge consumed, you deal an additional 2 force damage and push the target 1 meter away from you. You lose any unspent charge at the start of each of your turns.  

 

The maximum number of kinetic charges you can have at a time equals your Proficiency Bonus + your Sorcerer Level. 

Conflicting change.

Friday, March 26, 2021 

2:08 PM 

Spark of the Desert 

Contrast of light 

  

In the desert, the darkest night meets the brightest day. Sorcerers imbued with an elemental spark of the desert command light. 

There is a glow within you that shines through even the most oppressive darkness. Your eyes change color with your mood and when the sun shines upon your form, a warm light seems to emanate from your body. At your behest, this inner light pours from your body in all colors of the rainbow and forms into weapons of pure radiance. They have no mass, and as they glide through the flesh of your foes, the living matter sears and melts at the shimmering moonlight's corrupting touch. 

  

Light Spellcasting 

Starting at 1st level, light magic suffuses your being. You gain access to the Lore of Light and learn the Channel Light I spell. When you gain access to a new spell level at 3rd, 5th, 7th and 9th level, you also learn the corresponding Channel Light spells. 

Your spellcasting ability for sorcerer spells is Dexterity. 

  

Heart of Light 

Starting at 1st level, your skin seems to darken in shadows and lighten when touched by the sun. You are resistant to radiant damage, are immune to being blinded by intense light, and treat scorching hot weather as though it were pleasant. 

  

Body of the Desert 

Starting at 6th level, you can see normally in absolute darkness, both magical and nonmagical, to a distance of 40 meters (120 feet) and may see through illusions. 

  

Radiant Immunity 

Finally, at 10th level, you are immune to radiant damage.

Friday, March 26, 2021 

2:45 PM 

Spark of the Mountain 

Ice piercing the sky 

  

The mountains rise high to where the cold winds howl. Sorcerers infused with a spark of the mountain command frost. 

Your skin is cold to the touch. The blood within your veins crystalizes as it leaves your body and when you die your body freezes into a solid block of ice. Still, you are alive. The magic contained within you can be projected outwards with little more than a thought to turn the world around you as cold as the spark within your soul. 

  

Frost Spellcasting 

Starting at 1st level, frost magic suffuses your being. You gain access to the Lore of Frost and learn the Channel Frost I spell. When you gain access to a new spell level at 3rd, 5th, 7th and 9th level, you also learn the corresponding Channel Frost spells. 

Your spellcasting ability for sorcerer spells is Constitution. 

  

Heart of Frost  

As a spark of the mountain, the chill within your heart seeps into your skin and alters your appearance. 

Starting at 1st level, your skin is cool to the touch. You are resistant to cold damage, immune to the chilled condition, and treat cold and freezing cold weather as though it were pleasant.  

 

Body of the Mountain  

Starting at 6th level, you ignore difficult terrain caused by snow and ice. 

 

Cold Immunity 

Finally, at 10th level, you are immune to cold damage. 

 

  

 

 

Friday, March 26, 2021 

2:45 PM 

Spark of the Mountain
