The magic contained within you can be projected outwards with little more than a thought to turn the world around you as cold as the spark within your soul. 

  

Frost Spellcasting 

Starting at 1st level, frost magic suffuses your being. You gain access to the Lore of Frost and learn the Channel Frost I spell. When you gain access to a new spell level at 3rd, 5th, 7th and 9th level, you also learn the corresponding Channel Frost spells. 

Your spellcasting ability for sorcerer spells is Constitution. 

  

Heart of Frost  

As a spark of the mountain, the chill within your heart seeps into your skin and alters your appearance. 

Starting at 1st level, your skin is cool to the touch. You are resistant to cold damage, immune to the chilled condition, and treat cold and freezing cold weather as though it were pleasant.  

 

Body of the Mountain  

Starting at 6th level, you ignore difficult terrain caused by snow and ice. 

 

Cold Immunity 

Finally, at 10th level, you are immune to cold damage. 

 

  

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Steps of Snow and Ice 

 

 

Magic Resistance 

Finally at 10th level, you have advantage on saving throws against spells.  

 

 

 

Cold Ward 

The cold within you protects you from harm by forming a ward. Whenever you cast a Force spell, the ward gains a number of Hit Points equal to the amount of vitality you expend to cast the spell (including upcast costs but excluding the cost of metamagic applied to the spell). When you finish a short rest the ward's hit points becomes 0. Whenever you take damage, the ward takes the damage instead, and if you have any Resistances or Vulnerabilities, apply them before reducing the ward's Hit Points. If the damage reduces the ward to 0 Hit Points, you take any remaining damage. While the ward has 0 Hit Points, it can't absorb damage, but its magic remains. 

Whenever you cast an Abjuration spell with a spell slot, the ward regains a number of Hit Points equal to twice the level of the spell slot. Alternatively, as a Bonus Action, you can expend a spell slot, and the ward regains a number of Hit Points equal to twice the level of the spell slot expended. 

 

Once you create the ward, you can't create it again until you finish a Long Rest. 

 

Conflicting change.

Friday, March 26, 2021 

2:08 PM 

Spark of the Coast 

Current of the waves 

  

The sea ebbs and flows like the currents of electric energy. Sorcerers imbued with an elemental spark of the coast command lightning.  

There is a charge to your body. All in your presence feel it and those who touch you may have a jolt shoot through their body. Your very being is suffused with electrical energy and using this power you can project fields of energy over incredible distances. The sky responds to your call and the weather shifts on your whim. 

  

Lightning Spellcasting  

Starting at 1st level, lightning magic suffuses your being. You gain access to the Lore of Lightning and learn the Channel Lightning I spell. When you gain access to a new spell level at 3rd, 5th, 7th and 9th level, you also learn the corresponding Channel Lightning spells. 

Your spellcasting ability for sorcerer spells is Intelligence. 

 

Heart of Lightning 

Starting at 1st level, a charge seems to emanate from your body. You are resistant to lightning damage and immune to the shocked condition. 

 

Body of the Coast 

Starting at 6th level, you gain blindsight out to a range of 20 meters (60 feet), allowing you to perceive your surroundings without relying on sight. 

 

Lightning Immunity 

Finally, at 10th level, you are immune to lightning damage. 

 

 

 

 

 

Eyes of the Currents 

 

Weather Sense 

At 1st level, you may perform a 1 minute ritual. At the end of the ritual, you can accurately predict what the weather will be like up to a number of days equal to your Intelligence modifier + your Sorcerer level.  

  

Storm's Rebuke 

Also at 1st level, your inner charge can punish attackers. When a creature within 1 meter (5 feet) of you that you can see hits you with an attack, you can use your reaction and spend 3 vitality to cause lightning to blast the creature. It makes a Dexterity saving throw, taking 2d8 lightning damage and being shocked on a failed saving throw, and half as much damage and not being shocked on a successful one. 

  

Cyclone Strike 

At 6th level, when you deal lightning damage to a Large or smaller creature, you can cause the blast of electric energy to push it up to 3 meters (10 feet) away from you.

Also at 1st level, your inner charge can punish attackers. When a creature within 1 meter (5 feet) of you that you can see hits you with an attack, you can use your reaction and spend 3 vitality to cause lightning to blast the creature. It makes a Dexterity saving throw, taking 2d8 lightning damage and being shocked on a failed saving throw, and half as much damage and not being shocked on a successful one. 

  

Cyclone Strike 

At 6th level, when you deal lightning damage to a Large or smaller creature, you can cause the blast of electric energy to push it up to 3 meters (10 feet) away from you. 

  

Weather Control 

Also at 6th level you gain limited control over the weather in your region. 

After using your Weather Sense ability, you can perform a 1 hour ritual and pay 20 Vitality to either delay a weather phenomenon that will occur by up to a number of days equal to your Intelligence modifier (minimum of 1) or to have it appear up to the same amount of days early. The weather event is replaced by clear weather on the day it would have occurred. A weather phenomenon can only be influenced once. If another creature attempts to influence the same weather phenomenon, they make an ability check with their spellcasting modifier against your spell save DC. On a success their influence replaces yours. On a fail, their spell has no effect. Neither is aware whether their spell has succeeded or not. 

  

One With the Sky and Sea 

Finally at 10th level, you gain a flying and swimming speed of 10 meters (30 feet) and the ability to hover. If you already have a flying or swimming speed, it is increased by 5 meters (15 feet) and you become able to hover if you couldn't already. 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Conflicting change.

Monday, March 7, 2022 

10:54 AM 

Spark of the Forest 

Growth of the world 

  

The forests of the world hum with the endless cycle of birth and death. Sorcerers infused with a spark of the forest are wellsprings of life, vitality, and life magic. 

Your body has a healthy glow to it and your wounds seem to heal at an unnatural rate. Those in your presence feel a bit more awake and energetic as life force streams from your body. By your will, it forms into spells that invigorate the living and banish malignant influences.  

  

Life Spellcasting 

Starting at 1st level, life magic suffuses your being. You gain access to the Lore of Life and learn the Channel Life I spell. When you gain access to a new spell level at 3rd, 5th, 7th, and 9th level, you also learn the corresponding Channel Life spells. 

Your spellcasting ability for sorcerer spells is Charisma. 

 

Heart of Life 

Starting at 1st level, your skin is perfectly smooth and unmarred. You are resistant to acid and necrotic damage. 

 

Body of the Forest 

Starting at 6th level, you gain the ability to regenerate injuries. If you have less than your maximum hit points at the start of your turn, you expend a hit die and regain hit points equal to the rolled amount plus your Charisma modifier. This trait does not stop you from being instantly killed. 

  

Acid Immunity 

Finally, at 10th level, you are immune to acid damage. 

 

 

 

------------- 

 

 

Monday, March 7, 2022 

10:54 AM 

Spark of the Forest 

Growth of the world 

  

The forests of the world hum with the endless cycle of birth and death. Sorcerers infused with a spark of the forest are wellsprings of life, vitality, and life magic. 

Your body has a healthy glow to it and your wounds seem to heal at an unnatural rate. Those in your presence feel a bit more awake and energetic as life force streams from your body. By your will, it forms into spells that invigorate the living and banish malignant influences.  

  

Life Spellcasting 

Starting at 1st level, life magic suffuses your being. You gain access to the Lore of Life and learn the Channel Life I spell. When you gain access to a new spell level at 3rd, 5th, 7th, and 9th level, you also learn the corresponding Channel Life spells. 

Your spellcasting ability for sorcerer spells is Charisma. 

 

Heart of Life 

Starting at 1st level, your skin is perfectly smooth and unmarred. You are resistant to acid and necrotic damage. 

 

Body of the Forest 

Starting at 6th level, you gain the ability to regenerate injuries. If you have less than your maximum hit points at the start of your turn, you expend a hit die and regain hit points equal to the rolled amount plus your Charisma modifier. This trait does not stop you from being instantly killed. 

  

Acid Immunity 

Finally, at 10th level, you are immune to acid damage. 

 

 

 

------------- 

 

 

 

 

 

 

The wellspring of life and vitality within your heart sends living magic through your body and alters your appearance. 

 

 

At 5th level, life flows from your body. You stop ageing and the magic that courses through your veins heals any wounds you suffer within moments. If you have less than your maximum hit points at the start of your turn, you expend a hit die and regain hit points equal to the rolled amount + your Charisma modifier. If you take acid, fire, or radiant damage this trait doesn't function at the start of your next turn. This trait does not stop you from being instantly killed. 

Finally at 10th level, a golden light shines from your heart and spreads through your body when you cast your sorcerer spells. You are immune to acid damage. 

You may suppress these effects by beginning to concentrate (no action required) as though you were concentrating on a spell. They remain suppressed until you lose concentration. 

  

Balm of Life 

At 3rd level, you become able to draw healing energy from your body and imbue it into a living being. You have a pool of life-giving magic represented by a number of d8s equal to your sorcerer level. 

As a bonus action, you can choose one creature you can see within 30 meters (90 feet) of you and spend a number of those dice equal to half your sorcerer level rounded up or less. Roll the spent dice and add them together. The target regains a number of hit points equal to the total + your Charisma modifier. 

You regain all expended dice when you finish a night's rest. 

  

Flow of Magic 

Starting at 6th level, magic flows more readily in your presence. Whenever you or an ally within 1 meter (5 feet) of you casts a spell, you may spend 1 vitality to upcast that spell once, provided you aren't incapacitated. You can use this feature only once per turn. 

  

Wellspring 

As an action, you may expend an amount of vitality of your choice (minimum 1). When you do, a pulse of golden light emanates from your body. Until the start of your next turn, every creature of your choice, other than yourself, no more than 10 meters (30 feet) away gains temporary vitality equal to 1d4 + the vitality you expended. 

  

Conflicting change.

Friday, April 2, 2021 

8:41 AM 

Path of the Witcher 

Survive by Witchcraft 

  

Rangers that follow the path of the witcher learn the secrets of witchcraft. They brew simple potions and employ a technique not unlike that used to create staffs, to infuse magical pathways into the surface of their weapons. These magical pathways allow the witcher to set their weapons ablaze with elemental magic. Not only do these rune-like, glowing markings look very cool, they have the added advantage of not requiring a weapon to be reforged from scratch to be implemented. Of course unlike a witch’s enchantments, their use requires specialized training, meaning only witcher rangers can use the magical enhancements they apply to their weapons. 

Often also referred to as the witch's grooms or witch guard, most witchers in the Other World can be found traveling between, and learning from, the witches of the wildeborn clans. 

  

Brewing 

When you take this path at 3rd level, you gain the ability to brew potions as a witch does. A potion is a combination of herbs and other ingredients infused with magic to create a certain effect when ingested or injected. They are usually stored in small bottles that can be downed in a quick swig. 

Brewing a Potion 

At the end of a long rest, you decide which potions you spent that time brewing and receive those that you brewed. 

Brewing requires a kitchen or a witch's workshop (see the project). Crafting potions in the wild requires an alchemy kit (see the item), which you may craft by expending 4 scrap (half its value). 

Infusion Cost 

Every potion has a infusion cost, representing the amount of time and effort preparing the ingredients, brewing the potion, and finally performing the magical infusion requires. To brew a potion you must expend a number of infusions equal to its cost and you must know its formula.  

Formulas 

A formula is the steps, both mundane and arcane, needed to properly brew a potion. Most potions include a short description of how they are made, including their effects and most notable ingredients. These ingredients represent what witches living in temperate climates usually use. Depending on where they live, a witcher will have altered their formulas to use the local fauna and in most cases, witchers have the ability and knowledge to substitute any ingredient they are unable to find with a different one. 

The chapter on spellcasting provides a list of the formulas you have access to in the subchapter on formulas. 

Formulas Known 

At 1st level, you learn Infuse Formula I and 2 novice formulas of your choice. Whenever you gain a level in the ranger class you may choose one additional formula to learn of a level that you have access to. The Witcher Formula Level table below tells you when you gain access to a new level of formulas. Whenever you gain access to a new level of formulas, you learn the corresponding Infuse Potion formula. 

Brewing Ability 

Intelligence is your infusion ability for your ranger formulas, since their quality depends on your knowledge of the techniques used to craft them. You use your Intelligence whenever a formula refers to your infusion ability. In addition, you use your Intelligence modifier when setting the saving throw DC for a ranger formula you brew. 

Formula Save DC = 8 + your Proficiency Bonus + your Intelligence modifier 

 

Witcher Formula Level  

Formula Level 

	
 

Ranger Level 




Novice 

	

3rd 




Apprentice 

	

7th 




  

Infusions 

During a long rest (1 week of downtime), you gain a number of Infusions:  

Infusions = 1 + your Ranger level.  

The infusions you gain from this feature do not stack with those gained from other sources (such as a witch's Infusions feature). 

 

Elemental Imbuement 

Also at 3rd level, you learn to infuse your weapon with a simple enchantment only you can use. 

Over the course of a long rest, you may enchant your weapons, spending 1 infusion for every melee weapon, or 5 pieces of ammunition you enchant. These enchanted armaments require a special technique to activate and can only be used by a creature with this ability. They require no attunement. 

As you make an attack with an enchanted weapon or piece of ammunition, you may activate the enchantment (no action required). On a hit, the enchanted weapon or piece of ammunition deals an additional 1d4 acid, cold, force, fire, lightning, or radiant damage (your choice when you create the weapon or ammunition), which increases to 1d6 at 6th level and 1d8 at 9th level.  

  

Adaptable Imbuement 

Starting at 7th level, you no longer decide whether the weapons or ammunition you infuse with your elemental imbuement ability deal additional acid, cold, force, fire, lightning, or radiant damage in advance. Instead, you decide which damage type the enchantment deals when you activate it.  

  

Explosive Imbuement 

Finally when you reach 10th level, you gain the ability to make the enchantments you infuse explosive. When you activate one of your enchantments, you can choose to make it explosive. Upon impact, the weapon deals normal damage and then erupts in a burst of elemental energy. Every creature in a 1-meter radius sphere centered on the target takes damage equal to your elemental imbuement die, while the target takes the maximum damage of that die. You may target a point instead of a creature with an explosive attack. 

Conflicting change.

Wand Idea 

Tuesday, 08 July 2025 

19:24 

Wands can only be used properly be witches, requiring a special attunement slot that only witches have (or a feat can provide). 

Crafting a wand is very personal and complicated, requiring the careful imbuement of spells.  

System:  

Each spell has an infusion cost and the wand is crafted like any other magic item. Witch level determines possible spell level. Channel spells require 1 infusion to infuse and always have access to the channel level equal to the maximum spell level the infusing witch can infuse. Infusing a channel spell provides access to all of its casting options. 

Option: Spell must be programed in as upcast, a spell infused as non upcast cannot be cast as upcast. A spell infused as upcast cannot be cast at a lower power. 

Conflicting change.

Equipment 

Wednesday, July 28, 2021 

10:21 PM 

Chapter 4: Equipment (toc) 

The settlements market is a bustling hub of activity, located in what was once a lavish restaurant. Though the market may appear haphazardly arranged, with cobbled-together stalls and random boxes strewn about, it is the largest trading post in the region and offers an array of locally machined guns and artifice munitions. While the selection of weapons may be limited, it is a vital hub for those starved of supplies. 

The right gear can mean the difference between life and death. In the New World survivors can acquire everything from modern machine guns to magical potions. The only problem is actually finding all these things. Buying them is usually not an option. Without modern supply lines, there is only one way to build new modern electronics or other advanced equipment, and that is by cobbling it together from salvage. Old World tech is more valuable than gold and few will part with their treasures willingly. In the following chapter, you will find descriptions of the most common weapons and other equipment that you may find during your journey. 

  

Starting Equipment (toc) 

You start your new life in the New World with whatever you have on hand when the fog comes. Work with your GM to determine how your adventure starts and what you will have on hand when the apocalypse begins. As the fog comes in, any electronics near you that rely on sensitive semiconductors (phones, modern cars, computers: anything that has a microchip inside it) are irreparably destroyed. Devices that are turned off during the coming of the fog may survive. However, with the power grid completely offline you will have a hard time operating and charging even those devices that still work. 

  

Weight 

All armor, weapons, and items have a weight listed next to them. This number is based on the item's weight in kilograms, but aside from its weight it also represents its general bulk and how hard they are to store and lug around.  

Encumberment is determined by adding together the weight of all items you are wearing or carrying. If your encumbrance ever exceeds your carrying capacity, your speed is halved until you decrease your encumbrance below your maximum encumbrance. If your encumbrance increases over your maximum encumbrance, your speed is reduced to 0 and you do not benefit from any increase to your speed. 

Maximum Encumberment is the maximum amount of weigh you can carry. It equals your Strength score multiplied by a number determined by your race (see Encumberment in your race description). This is the weight that you can carry constantly and without it inhibiting your maneuverability. To make full use of your carrying capacity you need to wear a backpack. Otherwise, you can only carry what you're holding in your hands or have equipped. 

 

Inventory 

As previously alluded to, there are two ways you may carry items. An item may either be in your inventory or equipped. 

Inventory items are stored in a backpack, or deep in your pockets. They require an action to retrieve. 

Equipped items are being worn or carried in a way that allows you easy access to them at any time. You may wear headgear, a cloak, one suit of armor, one weapon or piece of equipment for your left hand, one weapon or piece of equipment for your right hand, a backpack to store your items, one pair of boots, and three quick access items. Any item in one of your quick access slot can be whipped out quickly (no action required) as part of whatever you wish to do with the item. For example, if you have a sword equipped to your left hand, you may unsheathe it as part of the action used to make an attack.

Wealth 

Saturday, February 20, 2021 

3:02 PM 

Wealth 

Even after the apocalypse, folk will trade. Goods will be exchanged for services and vice versa. Perhaps that enclave of dryads is producing magical arrows or living clothes the players might be interested in. Players may simply trade the resources they have found or items they don't need for something else. Since normal money is worthless in the new world, people have to rely on good old-fashioned trade. The two most important resources and thus currencies are food, represented by Rations, and material, represented by Scrap. 

Rations are bits of food and drink. They can be anything from grain to steak to soda that provides roughly 1000 kilocalories of sustenance and includes the water you need to drink. However, some food items might be particularly valuable and worth more rations than their calorie value may imply. Rations are usually either found or gained from butchering animals and (if you're so inclined) people. For simplicities sake, each ration has a weight of 0.5. Rations are an important resource as your character and any companions consume a daily amount of rations whenever they take a night’s rest. 

Scrap is any kind of valuable material that can't simply be gathered from the environment. It can be anything from a few screws or a high-tech motion sensor to chemicals and is needed for building things. A single item might be worth a dozen scrap or not even one. For simplicities sake, each unit of scrap has a weight of 0.5. The trade goods that are summarized under the term scrap have a similar worth no matter where you go and can thus be used as a currency. 

  

These two resource types are tracked in units on your character sheet. A single unit of ration has the same value as a single unit of scrap. 

  

Resources 

Scrap and Rations are needed by everyone and will usually be accepted by anyone willing to trade. Settlements do not track these resources individually, since they can simply trade or convert one into the other through labor or trade. Any scrap or rations placed into a settlement are converted into resources and can be withdrawn as either scrap or rations. 

There may be situations where this is not possible. For example, in times of extreme scarcity, as during a famine, rations may just not be available. Your GM decides if withdrawing rations or scrap is currently possible. 

  

Credits 

Factions may also provide Credits to players. Credits don't take up inventory space but are specific to a faction. They are tracked on your character sheet next to your relations with that faction. A single credit has the same value as a resource and can be traded in for anything in that faction's trading post or other place where services are provided by that faction. 

Credits are usually given as quest rewards by bigger factions that run a trade operation. They can also be gained by trading things in without receiving any immediate compensation. 

  

Value 

Every item has a value, representing how rare, difficult to build, useful, and thus coveted it is in the New World. Traders will usually be willing to sell an item to you for a number of resources (scrap or rations) equal to the item's value. In most cases, they will be willing to buy that same item for half its listed value from you. See the next section on wealth to know what scrap and rations are. 

If an item has a value of 1, merchants will be willing to sell that item, but will (in most circumstances) be unwilling to pay you anything for it, unless you trade it in with other objects. 

Merchants will usually exchange 2 of one resource (scrap or rations) for 1 unit of another. For example, giving a trader 2 scrap will pay for 1 ration.

Equipment Sizes 

Thursday, December 30, 2021 

5:40 PM 

Equipment Sizes 

In the New World you can use or wear any equipment that you find on your adventures, within the bounds of common sense. For example, a tiny fairy would be swallowed up by a human's combat armor, and a large, burly giant might be able to use an imp's leather armor as a hat, but not much more. However, this rule is subject to common sense. For example, an imp could easily adapt a human's bulletproof vest for their own use. The equipment described in the following sections is designed to be used by medium creatures, which most humanoids are. Creatures that are not medium have special rules that determine what equipment they can and can't wear. These rules are described below. 

Most equipment is made to be used by a specific creature. For example, a soldier's body armor is made to fit them, but small modifications can be made fairly easily to adapt a piece of equipment to its new owner. It might no longer look as nice, but those living in the apocalypse can't be choosy and it is assumed that survivors make any necessary modifications during their downtime. 

  

Medium 

All humans are medium and the majority of humanoids are too. It is the default size for all equipment and the only size Old World equipment comes in. Whenever a piece of equipment is mentioned without a size tag, assume it is made for a medium creature. 

  

Tiny 

Tiny creatures have difficulty using weapons that small and medium creatures can wield comfortably. Tiny creatures may not use weapons made for larger creatures unless they have the Light property. Tiny creatures may not use weapons for larger creatures to engage in two-weapon fighting, even if they have the Light property, just as a medium creature cannot use two two-handed weapons for two-weapon fighting. 

Due to their inability to use weapons made for larger creatures, tiny creatures usually wield weapons better suited to their size. Such weapons add the Tiny and Finesse weapon properties, in addition to any other properties, and remove the Reach property if the weapon possesses it normally. 

Weapons with the tiny property deal reduced damage, as per the Tiny Weapons Damage table below, and weigh one-quarter the normal weight for that type of weapon. Ranged weapons, thrown weapons, and thrown improvised weapons with the tiny property have ranges equal to half of their normal range, rounded down to the nearest meter. Firearms have their ammunition type changed to the new damage die. Weapons with the Tiny property ignore the restrictions described in the preceding paragraph since they are size appropriately for tiny creatures. 

Armor made/adapted for use by tiny creatures has its weight and value quartered.

Small 

Small creatures can wield most of the weapons designed for medium creatures comfortably, except for weapons with the Heavy property.

Weapons with the Tiny property ignore the restrictions described in the preceding paragraph since they are size appropriately for tiny creatures. 

Armor made/adapted for use by tiny creatures has its weight and value quartered.

Small 

Small creatures can wield most of the weapons designed for medium creatures comfortably, except for weapons with the Heavy property. 

Armor made/adapted for use by small creatures has its weight and value halved. A small creature can adapt any medium armor for its own use with a Crafting Kit during a night’s rest (no check or scrap required). 

  

Large 

Large creatures ignore the Two-Handed property on medium weapons and any medium weapon that doesn't have the heavy property is considered to be light for them. 

Since large creatures often waste much of their strength when wielding normal weapons, they may opt to use weapons specifically made for them. A weapon made for a large creature receives the Large and reach property in addition to any other properties it has. If the weapon already has the Reach property, increase its reach by 1 meter (5 feet). Ranged weapons that are not firearms have their normal and long range increased by half the weapon's normal range. You also increase the number of damage dice the weapon uses by one. For example instead of 1d8, a large longsword deals 2d8 slashing damage. The value and weight of large weapons are doubled. 

Armor made for large creatures has its weight and value doubled.

Weapons 

Weapons 

Wednesday, June 16, 2021 

10:45 AM 

Weapons 

While anyone can pick up any weapon not too heavy to lift, training with a weapon increases your effectiveness with it, represented by proficiency. Your class grants proficiency in certain weapons, representing the training and experience your patron had with using certain weapons.  

This section concerns itself with more medieval weapons, such as clubs, bows, longswords, and daggers. The weapons table shows the most common weapons used in the New World, their price and weight, the damage they deal when they hit, and any special properties they possess. Every weapon is classified as either melee or ranged. A melee weapon is used to attack a target within 1 meter (5 feet) of you, whereas a ranged weapon is used to attack a target at a distance. The weapons here are archetypes and do not represent any weapon in particular. For example, a club can be anything from a baseball bat to a golf club or police baton. When you find a specific weapon, your DM will tell you what archetype it falls under. There are three categories of weapons: simple, martial, and modern. Most people in the Other World could use simple Weapons with proficiency. These weapons include clubs, maces, and other weapons often found in the hands of commoners. Martial Weapons, including swords, axes, and polearms, require more specialized training to use effectively. Most warriors in the Other World that didn't rely entirely on magic used martial weapons as these weapons are generally just superior to their more simple counterparts. Firearms are modern weapons and have a section dedicated just to themselves, outlining their use and traits. 

Weapon Proficiency 

Proficiency with a weapon allows you to add your Proficiency Bonus to the Attack roll for any Attack you make with that weapon. If you make an Attack roll using a weapon with which you lack proficiency, you do not add your Proficiency Bonus to the Attack roll. 

  

Weapon Properties 

Many Weapons have special Properties related to their use, as shown in the Weapons table. 

  

Ammunition. You can use a weapon that has the ammunition property to make a ranged attack only if you have ammunition to fire from the weapon. Each time you Attack with the weapon, you expend one piece of ammunition. Drawing the ammunition from a Quiver, case, or other container is part of the attack (you need a free hand to load a one-handed weapon). At the end of the battle, you can recover half your expended Ammunition by taking a minute to search the battlefield. If you use a weapon that has the ammunition property to make a melee attack, you treat the weapon as an improvised weapon (see “Improvised Weapons” later in the section). A sling must be loaded to deal any damage when used in this way. 

  

Finesse. When making an Attack with a finesse weapon, you use your choice of your Strength or Dexterity modifier for the Attack and Damage Rolls. You must use the same modifier for both rolls. 

  

Heavy. Small creatures have disadvantage on attack rolls with heavy weapons. A heavy weapon is too heavy to be wielded by a tiny creature. 

  

Light. A light weapon is small and easy to handle, making it ideal for use when fighting with two weapons. 

  

Non-Lethal. A weapon that deals non-lethal damage causes a creature reduced to 0 hit points with it to be downed stable (it does not need to make death saving throws). If a non-lethal weapon is used against a downed creature, it fails a death save, but remains stable. A non-lethal weapon can still kill a creature instantly. 

  

Range. A weapon that can be used to make a ranged attack has a range in parentheses after the ammunition or thrown property. The range lists two numbers. The first is the weapon’s normal range in meters, and the second indicates the weapon’s long range. When attacking a target beyond normal range, you have disadvantage on the attack roll. You can’t Attack a target beyond the weapon’s long range. To convert its range into feet, simply divide the number by 3. 

  

Reach. This weapon adds 1 meter (5 feet) to your reach when you Attack with it, as well as when determining your reach for opportunity attacks with it. 

 

Reload. You can fire a set amount of shots with this weapon before you need to reload. Reloading a weapon is an action. The number of shots you may take before needing to reload is shown in brackets. For example "(1)" means this weapon can only be used to make one attack before it must be reloaded.  

  

Special. A weapon with the Special property has unusual rules governing its use, explained in the weapon’s description (see Special Weapons later in this section). 

 

  

Thrown. If a weapon has the thrown property, you can throw the weapon to make a ranged attack. If the weapon is a melee weapon, you use the same ability modifier for that attack roll and damage roll that you would use for a melee attack with the weapon. For example, if you throw a Handaxe, you use your Strength, but if you throw a Dagger, you can use either your Strength or your Dexterity, since the Dagger has the finesse property. 

  

Two-Handed. This weapon requires two hands to attack with it. 

  

Versatile. This weapon can be used with one or two hands. A damage value in parentheses appears with the property. This is the damage the weapon deals when it is used with two hands to make a melee attack. 

  

Improvised Weapons 

Sometimes you don’t have your weapons and have to attack with whatever is at hand. An Improvised Weapon includes any object you can wield in one or two hands, such as broken glass, a table leg, a frying pan, a tire, or a severed arm. 

Often, an Improvised Weapon is similar to an actual weapon and can be treated as such. For example, a table leg is akin to a club. At the DM’s option, you may use a random object as though it were a weapon you are proficient with. 

An object that bears no resemblance to a weapon deals 1d4 damage (the GM assigns a damage type appropriate to the object) and can usually be wielded using either Strength or Dexterity for both melee and ranged attacks (light property). If you use a ranged weapon to make a melee attack (such as bashing an enemy with the butt of your rifle) or throw a melee weapon that does not have the thrown property, it also deals 1d4 damage.  

An improvised thrown weapon has a normal range of 10 meters (30 feet) and a long range of 20 meters (60 feet). An improvised weapon used by a large creature deals 2d4 damage and has a normal range of 20 meters (60 feet) and a long range of 40 meters (120 feet). An improvised weapon used by a tiny creature deals 1 damage and has a normal range of 5 meters (15 feet) and a long range of 10 meters (30 feet). 

  

Cold Iron Weapons 

Some monsters, especially the fey, are vulnerable to arsenic, often called cold iron. Cautious survivors invest to have a witch of the forge coven (see the class description) plate their weapons with arsenic to turn them into cold iron weapons. 

  

Silvered Weapons 

Some monsters that have immunity or resistance to nonmagical weapons are susceptible to silver weapons, and most monstrosities and shapeshifters that are exceptionally resistant to most attacks are vulnerable to it. Silvering is usually done by a witch of the forge coven, but anyone can silver a single weapon or five pieces of ammunition by expending 50 scrap and succeeding a DC 15 Mechanics check (as though you were crafting an item). 

  

Special Weapons 

Weapons with special rules are described here. 

Lance. You have disadvantage when you use a lance to attack a target within 1 meter (5 feet) of you. Also, a lance requires two hands to wield when you aren’t mounted. 

Net. A large or smaller creature hit by a net is restrained until it is freed. A net has no effect on creatures that are formless, or creatures that are huge or larger. A creature can use its action to make a DC 10 Strength check, freeing itself or another creature within its reach on a success. Dealing 5 slashing damage to the net (AC 10) also frees the creature without harming it, Ending the effect and destroying the net.

Archive 

Dart. A dart may be used to deliver a substance, usually poison or a potion concentrate (see Infuse Potion II), to the target. It is used as ammunition by the blowgun (see the simple ranged weapon) and dartgun (see the simple firearm). 

 

 

Large Weapons 

Large humanoids can wield weapons specifically designed for their increased strength and size. A large weapon deals an additional die of damage and has its range and weight doubled. Medium creatures have disadvantage on attack rolls when wielding a large weapon. Small and tiny creatures cannot wield a large weapon. 

 

Tiny Weapons 

Tiny humanoids are too small to wield normal weapons and require special versions of weapons made just for them. Tiny weapons deal reduced damage, as per the table below, and weigh one quarter the normal weight for that type of weapon, and have half the range of the normal weapon (if any). Ranged weapons, thrown weapons, and thrown improvised weapons with the tiny property have ranges equal to half of their normal range, rounded down to the nearest meter and to a minimum range 1 meter.

Dienstag, 14. März 2023 

01:29 

Firearm Accessories 

As if firearms weren’t dangerous enough, a number of accessories can increase their utility or efficiency. A firearm accessory can be equipped or unequipped from a weapon as an action. 

  

Weapon Accessories 

Object 

	

Weight 

	

Value 

	
 

Crafting Check 




Bayonet 

	

1 

	

4 

	

DC 5 Mechanics 




Illuminator 

	

0.5 

	

8 

	

DC 10 Technology 




Laser sight 

	

0.5 

	

10 

	

DC 10 Technology 




Reflex Sight 

	

0.5 

	

12 

	

DC 10 Technology 




Scope 

	

0.5 

	

20

Scope, Nightvision 

	

1

Suppressor 

	

 1

DC 10 Technology 




  

Bayonet. You attach a short blade to the tip of the barrel of the weapon. The bayonet allows you to use your firearm as a melee weapon with the finesse property that deals 1d4 piercing damage on a hit. 

Illuminator. An illuminator is a small flashlight that mounts to a firearm, freeing up one of the user’s hands. It functions as a standard flashlight (see the item). A charge lasts for 5 hours of continuous use. 

Laser Sight. This small laser mounts on a firearm, and projects a tiny red dot on the weapon’s target. Switching the laser on or off requires a bonus action. While active, a laser sight increases your attack modifier by 1 and you have disadvantage on stealth checks. A charge lasts for 5 hours of continuous use. 

Reflex Sight. Your attack modifier increases by 1. A weapon can only have either a scope or a reflex sight, not both. 

Scope. A scope is a sighting device that makes it easier to hit targets at long range. A scope doubles a ranged weapon’s normal and maximum range. Your attack modifier increases by 1, but you have disadvantage on attacks against creatures not more than 5 meters (15 feet) away. If you know the exact distance to your target (as from a rangefinder), you may use your bonus action to fire at your weapon's long range without disadvantage. A weapon can only have either a scope or a reflex sight, not both. 

Nightvision Scope. In addition to the normal effects of a scope, while wielding a weapon with this attachment you may choose to look through this scope to gain darkvision out to a range of 60 meters (180 feet). While using this scope you cannot succeed Wisdom (Perception) checks. A charge lasts for 5 hours of continuous use. 

Suppressor. A suppressor fits on the end of a firearm and reduces the sound it makes when fired. Making an attack still reveals you to the attacked creatures.

Conventional explosives are rare, but effective in the new world. Crafting them requires valuable ingredients and chemical explosives are slowly replaced by alchemy and artifice.

Special Grenades and Explosives Descriptions 

Grenades and explosives with special rules are described here. 

Blasting Charge. A blasting charge requires a detonator to set off. It can also be detonated by dealing 3 fire damage to it. 

   

Dynamite. A small stick of explosive with a fuse. Additional sticks can be set off at the same time if they are within the burst radius of the first stick, increasing the damage and burst radius of the explosion. Each additional stick increases the damage by +1d6 (maximum 7d6) and the burst radius by 1 meter (maximum 8 meters). Using dynamite requires a source of flame to ignite the fuse or a detonator. 

 

Flashbang. Every creature in this grenade's burst radius must succeed a DC 13 Constitution saving throw or be blinded for 1 minute. An affected creature may repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on a success. 

  

Landmine. A landmine is an explosive device designed to be detonated either by stepping on it, via a tripwire, or another trigger. As an action you may place the mine in a space no more than 1 meter (5 feet) away. When a creature enters the mine's space, the mine detonates. The triggering creature automatically fails its saving throw against the explosion and has its movement speed halved until it finishes a long rest, or its current hit points are increased to their maximum. A creature may spot the mine by succeeding a DC 10 Wisdom (Perception) check. A creature that knows the mine's location may move through its space without setting it off. 

  

Poison Gas Grenade. A poison gas grenade fills all squares within its burst radius with a cloud of poison gas. It disperses after 1 minute. A breeze disperses the cloud in 2 rounds and a gale disperses it at the end of your next turn. The gas has no effect during a storm.

Special Grenades and Explosives Descriptions 

Grenades and explosives with special rules are described here. 

Blasting Charge. A blasting charge requires a detonator to set off. It can also be detonated by dealing 3 fire damage to it. 

   

Dynamite. A small stick of explosive with a fuse. Additional sticks can be set off at the same time if they are within the burst radius of the first stick, increasing the damage and burst radius of the explosion. Each additional stick increases the damage by +1d6 (maximum 7d6) and the burst radius by 1 meter (maximum 8 meters). Using dynamite requires a source of flame to ignite the fuse or a detonator. 

 

Flashbang. Every creature in this grenade's burst radius must succeed a DC 13 Constitution saving throw or be blinded for 1 minute. An affected creature may repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on a success. 

  

Landmine. A landmine is an explosive device designed to be detonated either by stepping on it, via a tripwire, or another trigger. As an action you may place the mine in a space no more than 1 meter (5 feet) away. When a creature enters the mine's space, the mine detonates. The triggering creature automatically fails its saving throw against the explosion and has its movement speed halved until it finishes a long rest, or its current hit points are increased to their maximum. A creature may spot the mine by succeeding a DC 10 Wisdom (Perception) check. A creature that knows the mine's location may move through its space without setting it off. 

  

Poison Gas Grenade. A poison gas grenade fills all squares within its burst radius with a cloud of poison gas. It disperses after 1 minute. A breeze disperses the cloud in 2 rounds and a gale disperses it at the end of your next turn. The gas has no effect during a storm. 

When a creature enters the cloud's space for the first time on a turn or starts its turn there, that creature must make a DC 13 Constitution saving throw, or take 2d6 poison damage. 

  

Smoke Grenade. A smoke grenade fills all squares within its burst radius with smoke. The area covered by the smoke is heavily obscured. It disperses after 1 minute. A breeze disperses the smoke in 2 rounds and a gale disperses it at the end of your next turn. The smoke has no effect during a storm. 

  

Tear Gas Grenade. A tear gas grenade fills all squares within its burst radius with a cloud of irritant that causes eyes to burn and fill with tears. It disperses after 1 minute. A breeze disperses the cloud in 2 rounds and a gale disperses it at the end of your next turn. The gas has no effect during a storm. 

When a creature enters the cloud's space for the first time on a turn or starts its turn there, that creature must make a DC13 Constitution saving throw, or be incapacitated and blinded until the start of its next turn, as it coughs and wretches. A creature that is immune to poison damage is immune to this effect. 

  

Detonators 

Detonators are designed to be used in conjunction with one of the aforementioned explosives. 

Detonators

Proximity. When combined with a Blasting Charge and armed as an action, the creature arming the explosive may designate up to a 5-meter (15 feet) radius. When a creature enters that radius, the charge detonates. 

Remote. When combined with a Blasting Charge and armed as an action, another creature with a compatible radio may detonate the charge as a bonus action. 

Timed. When combined with a Blasting Charge and armed as an action, the creature arming the explosive sets a timer that may be of any length. The explosive detonates at the end of that timer.

Consumables 

Tuesday, December 28, 2021 

12:36 PM 

Consumables 

Listed here are consumable items that are not magical in nature and may be found in old world stockpiles. For a list of potions, refer to the section on potion formulas in the chapter on witchcraft.

Alcohol. As an action you may drink the contents of this bottle. You must succeed a DC 5 Constitution saving throw or be poisoned until you finish a night's rest. 

Antibiotics. As an action you may take these antibiotics. You have advantage on saving throws against diseases that can be cured with the Channel Life II ability Lesser Cure until you finish a long rest. 

Cannabis. Spend 1 minute smoking a joint to become poisoned for 5 hours. 

Coffee. Spend 10 minutes brewing and consuming a cup of coffee. Drinking it provides 2 temporary vitality until you finish your next night’s rest. 

Contraceptive. As an action you may ingest this drug. For the next 7 days you cannot be impregnated by non-magical means. 

Hallucinogenic. As an action you may ingest this drug to become poisoned for 10 hours. 

Painkiller. As an action you may inject yourself with a dose of painkillers. For the next hour you do not fall prone and are not incapacitated while downed. You still make death saves and may die from failing three. 

Stimulant. As an action you can smoke or ingest this drug. For the next 8 hours you ignore the effects of exhaustion, except death at level six. 

Tranquilizer. As an action you can ingest this poison. At the end of your next turn, you fall unconscious and enter a dreamless sleep. The effect ends if you take damage, or someone uses an action to shake or slap you awake. 

You can use this poison to coat one slashing or piercing weapon or a piece of non-firearm ammunition. Applying the poison takes an action. Once applied, the poison affects the next creature hit by the coated weapon. 

Tobacco. Spend 1 minute smoking a cigarette to gain 4 temporary vitality until you finish your next night’s rest. 

Vaccine. As an action you may inject yourself with a dose of vaccine. You are immune to the disease specified by the vaccine.

Mittwoch, 11. September 2024 

16:12 

Skill Books 

A skill book provides a passive bonus to one or more of your skills, as long as it remains in your inventory and is written in a language you can read. You can benefit from more than one skill book at a time, but having multiple copies of the same skill book provides no additional benefit. The bonus provided by a skill book is lost when the item is removed from your inventory. Copying a skill book requires scrap equal to half its value and a successful DC 15 Science check.

Customization Options 

Thursday, December 30, 2021 

5:54 PM 

Chapter 5: Customization Options (toc) 

 

The combination of ability scores, race, class, and who you were before the fog define who you are in the New World. However, your development does not need to be entirely defined by your patron and your life before the apocalypse. There are two other ways you can further customize your character: multiclassing and feats. 

 

Multiclassing (toc) 

Multiclassing allows you to gain levels in multiple classes. Within the context of the new world there are two ways you may multiclass. The first is the simplest: during your travels you are given a level in a class. An elemental spirit may mark you as a sorcerer, after performing a great service to a master swordsman he may agree to train you. Gaining a level through gameplay, usually as a reward, is a great boon that is not granted lightly and often requires a significant time investment. Gaining a level this way requires no prerequisites and has no impact on your character level. These "special" levels exist in a vacuum outside your normal experience based character progression and can only be advanced through time and dedication. 

The second way to multiclass is through standard level progression after you have gained your 1st level. This way is based on the premise that your patron shifted their focus from one area of expertise to another, or was part of a group that spilt their focus on studying techniques that blur the lines between two classes. This option is represented by you choosing to take a level in a new class when you gain enough experience to advance to a new level, instead of gaining one in your current class. Your levels in all your classes are added together to determine your character level. For example, if you have three levels in mage and two in fighter, you're a 5th-level character. 

As you advance in levels, you might primarily remain a member of your original class with just a few levels in another class, or you might change course entirely. Your progression always represents your patron's journey through life, as you unlock the abilities they learned first and are thus most familiar with first, before gaining access to what they ingrained later in life. You might even start progressing in a third or fourth class should you realize that your patron split their focus further. Compared to a single-class character of the same level, you'll sacrifice some powerful abilities in exchange for versatility. 

 

Prerequisites 

To qualify for gaining a new class through leveling, you must meet the ability score prerequisites for both your current class and your new one, as shown in the Multiclassing Prerequisites table. This represents the fact, that if your patron did shift their focus between different paths, the physical capabilities they trained while training need to reflect that. For example, a barbarian who decides to multiclass into druid class must have a Wisdom score of 13 or higher, because there is no way a person who spent years studying life magic would have less than 13 Charisma. 

You also cannot multiclass before you have reached your 1st level. You choose your patron's primary class when you create your character, which provides you with certain proficiencies at 0th level. When you gain your 1st level, it must be in that primary class. 

 

Multiclassing Prerequisites 

Class 

	
 

Ability Score Minimum 




Barbarian 

	

Constitution 13 




Bard 

	

Charisma 13 




Druid 

	

Wisdom 13 




Fighter 

	

Strength or Dexterity 13 




Mage 

	

Spellcasting Ability 13 




Monk 

	

Dexterity and Wisdom 13 




Paladin 

	

Strength and Charisma 13 




Prophet 

	

Spellcasting Ability 13 




Ranger 

	

Strength or Dexterity and Wisdom 13 




Rogue 

	

Dexterity 13 




Sorcerer 

	

Spellcasting Ability 13 




Witch 

	

Intelligence 13 




 

Levels and Class Features 

When you multiclass into a class, you do not start at 0th level as you do when creating a character and you do not receive the proficiencies that class would provide at 0th level. Instead you gain the class's 1st level features and the proficiencies detailed in the class's Multiclassing section. If the class you are multi-classing into requires you to select your subclass at level 0, you must select your subclass at level 1. You do not gain any features provided by the subclass at level 0, but abilities with no level association (such as those that tell you your spellcasting ability) still apply.  

Experience Points 

The experience point cost to gain a level is always based on your total character level, as shown in the Character Advancement table, not your level in a particular class. So, if you are a prophet 6/fighter 1, you must gain enough XP to reach 8th level before you can take your second level as a fighter or your seventh level as a prophet. 

Proficiency Bonus 

Your proficiency bonus is always based on your total character level, not your level in a particular class. For example, if you are a fighter 3/rogue 2, you have the proficiency bonus of a 5th-level character, which is +3. 

Extra Attack 

If you gain the Extra Attack class feature from more than one class, the features don't add together. You can't make more than two attacks with this feature unless it says you do (as the fighter's version of Extra Attack does). 

Unarmored Defense 

If different classes grant you features that allow you to add an ability modifier to your armor class, you choose which is added. You can only add one ability modifier to your armor class unless the ability explicitly states otherwise.  

 

Proficiencies 

When you gain your first level in a class other than the class you chose to be your first at 0th level, you do not gain all of the new class's starting proficiencies. You instead gain the usually smaller selection of proficiencies listed in the class description under Multiclassing, or shown in the Multiclassing Proficiencies table. 

 

Multiclassing Proficiencies 

Class 

	
 

Proficiencies Gained 




Barbarian 

	

Shields, simple weapons, martial weapons 




Bard 

	

Light armor, one skill from the bard skill list 




Druid 

	

One skill from the druid skill list 




Fighter 

	

Light armor, medium armor, shields, simple weapons, martial weapons 




Mage 

	

Simple weapons 




Monk 

	

Monk Weapons (see Martial Arts) 




Paladin 

	

Light armor, medium armor, shields, simple weapons, martial weapons 




Prophet 

	

Light armor, medium armor, shields 




Ranger 

	

Light armor, medium armor, shields, simple weapons, martial weapons, one skill from the ranger skill list 




Rogue 

	

Light armor, simple weapons, one skill from the rogue skill list 




Sorcerer 

	

- 




Witch 

	

Light armor, medium armor, shields 




 

 

Hit and Vitality Dice 

You gain the hit points and vitality from your new class as described for levels after 1st. 

You add together the Hit Dice granted by all your classes to form your pool of Hit Dice. If the Hit Dice are the same die type, you can simply pool them together. For example, both the fighter and the ranger have a d10, so if you are a ranger 5/fighter 5, you have ten d10 Hit Dice (plus your initial racial hit die). If your classes give you Hit Dice of different types, keep track of them separately. If you are a ranger 5/prophet 5, for example, you have five d10 Hit Dice and five d6 Hit Dice.

Feats 

Thursday, December 30, 2021 

6:03 PM 

Feats 

A feat represents a talent or an area of expertise that gives you special capabilities. It embodies training, experience, and abilities that you acquired on your own while adventuring in the new world and are completely independent of your patron. 

At certain levels, your class gives you the Ability Score Improvement feature. Using the optional feats rule, you can forgo taking that feature to take a feat of your choice instead. You can take each feat only once unless the feat’s description says otherwise. 

You must meet any prerequisite specified in a feat to take that feat. If you ever lose a feat’s prerequisite, you can’t use that feat until you regain the prerequisite. 

 

Feat Categories 

Feats are divided into five categories based on for whom they would be most useful to General feats which are meant for everyone, spellcaster feats which are meant for spellcasters, defensive feats which are meant to make you tougher, martial feats which are meant for weapon users, and finally species feats which enhance the natural traits of your species.

Tuesday, May 18, 2021 

1:29 PM 

Animal Friend 

You have a way of pacifying even the most vicious of animals, granting you the following traits: 

•Increase you Wisdom score by 1, to a maximum of 20. 

•You are proficient in the Animal Handling skill. 

•As an action, you can attempt to convince a fey, beast, or monstrosity within 10 meters (30 feet) of you that you mean it no harm. The beast must make a Charisma saving throw equal to 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Wisdom modifier. It has advantage on its saving throw if you or one of your companions has caused harm to the beast or any of its allies. On a failed save, the creature is charmed by you until your next night's rest, or until you or one of your companions harm it. On a successful save, the beast is immune to further attempts to charm it for 24 hours. Once you have successfully charmed a creature using this ability, you can't use this ability again until you complete a night's rest.

Athlete 

Your body is a fine tuned machine, granting you the following benefits: 

• Increase your Strength or Dexterity score by 1, to a maximum of 20. 

• When you are prone, standing up uses only 1 meter (5 feet) of your movement. 

• You can make a running long jump or a running high jump after moving only 1 meter (5 feet) on foot, rather than 3 meters (10 feet).

Monday, May 17, 2021 

4:47 PM 

Attractive 

Even if you are not beautiful, you have something about you that makes you irresistible to the opposite sex (or anyone else interested in your gender and species), granting you the following benefits: 

•Increase your Charisma score by 1, to a maximum of 20. 

•You have advantage on Charisma (Deception) and Charisma (Persuasion) checks made against anyone who is attracted to members of your sex, and add +5 to the result of a Charisma check to seduce someone attracted to your sex.

Monday, May 17, 2021 

4:21 PM 

Downtown Delver 

You have learned much about the entities that inhabit the ruins of the Old World as well as how to resist them and the fog that spawns them, granting you the following benefits:  

•You have advantage on Wisdom (Perception) and Intelligence (Investigation) checks made to detect the presence of fiends and undead. 

•You have advantage on saving throws against the abilities and grapples of fiends and undead. 

•You have resistance to the necrotic damage dealt by the Black Fog.

Tuesday, May 18, 2021 

10:38 PM 

Magic Initiate 

Prerequisite: Level 1 

 

Choose either Divine Magic, an Elemental Lore (Lore of Force, Lightning, Frost, Light, Fire or Life), or Wild Magic. 

You learn the Channel I spell of that type of magic. In addition, choose two Novice spells from that same lore to learn. You may cast these spells by expending vitality equal to their Vitality Cost will but cannot upcast them. Your spellcasting ability for these spells is determined by the type of magic you use. 

This feat can be taken multiple times. If you take this feat again and choose the same lore of magic, you instead increase the level of spells you can cast by one, learn the corresponding channel spell and may learn two new spells of a level you can cast.

Sunday, May 30, 2021 

7:52 AM 

Freeruner 

You are incredibly agile and have learned how to quickly scale everything from buildings to mountains, granting you the following benefits: 

•Increase your Dexterity score by 1, to a maximum of 20. 

•You gain a climbing speed equal to your walking speed. 

•While not incapacitated, you are resistant to fall damage.

Wednesday, May 19, 2021 

8:33 AM 

Maneuver Initiate 

 

Your martial training allows you to use maneuvers in combat. 

You learn two maneuvers of your choice. If a maneuver you use requires your target to make a saving throw to resist the maneuver’s effects, the saving throw DC equals 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Strength or Dexterity modifier (your choice).

9:03 PM 

Merchant 

You have made trade your trade and can haggle with the best of them, granting you the following benefits: 

•Increase your Charisma score by 1, to a maximum of 20. 

•When haggling for services or items you can attempt to halve the price by making a Charisma (Persuasion) check contested by the other creature's Intelligence saving throw. This ability does not work on automated vending machines.

Wednesday, May 19, 2021 

2:26 PM 

Mounted Combatant 

You have mastered the use of your mount for combat, granting you the following benefits: 

• You have advantage on melee attack rolls against any unmounted creature that is smaller than your mount. 

• You can force an attack targeted at your mount to target you instead. 

• If your mount is subjected to an effect that allows it to make a Dexterity saving throw to take only half damage, it instead takes no damage if it succeeds on the saving throw, and only half damage if it fails.

2:22 PM 

Mobile 

You are exceptionally speedy and agile, granting you the following benefits: 

• Your speed increases by 3 meters (10 feet). 

• When you use the Dash action, difficult terrain doesn’t cost you extra movement on that turn. 

• When you make a melee attack against a creature, you don’t provoke opportunity attacks from that creature for the rest of the turn, whether you hit or not.

Monday, May 17, 2021 

5:02 PM 

Quick Pockets 

You are adept at secreting items about your person and recovering them quickly, granting you the following benefits: 

•Increase your Dexterity score by 1, to a maximum of 20. 

•You can retrieve items from your inventory (backpack) using your bonus action. 

•As long as a creature isn't paying attention to your hands, you have advantage on Dexterity (Sleight of Hand) checks you make to either conceal an item no larger than a tiny object somewhere on your person or remove such an item unnoticed from its hiding place on your person

Monday, May 17, 2021

Observant 

Quick to notice details of your environment, you gain the following benefits: 

• Increase your Intelligence or Wisdom score by 1, to a maximum of 20. 

• If you can see a creature’s mouth while it is speaking a language you understand, you can interpret what it’s saying by reading its lips. 

• You have a +5 bonus to your passive Wisdom (Perception) and passive Intelligence (Investigation) scores.

Wednesday, May 19, 2021 

2:36 PM 

Skulker 

You are expert at slinking through shadows, granting you the following benefits: 

•You have advantage on any checks to hide when you are at least heavily obscured, and can attempt to hide if you are only lightly obscured. 

•When you are hidden from a creature and miss it with a ranged weapon attack, making the attack doesn't reveal your position. 

•You can spend 1 minute creating camouflage for yourself. You must have access to fresh mud, dirt, plants, soot, and other naturally occurring materials with which to create your camouflage. Once you are done, your clothes are almost indistinguishable from the environment and you know how to abuse this camouflage perfectly. After that minute, you are camouflaged (see the condition) until you leave the area you camouflaged yourself for.  

•Attacking a surprised creature while camouflaged does not reveal your location and you have +10 on all stealth checks. If a creature loses track of you, (You succeed on a stealth check) you may meld back into the environment, becoming camouflaged once more.

Skulker 

You are expert at slinking through shadows, granting you the following benefits: 

You have advantage on any checks to hide when you are at least heavily obscured, and can attempt to hide if you are only lightly obscured. 

When you are hidden from a creature and miss it with a ranged weapon attack, making the attack doesn't reveal your position. 

You can spend 1 minute creating camouflage for yourself. You must have access to fresh mud, dirt, plants, soot, and other naturally occurring materials with which to create your camouflage. Once you are done, your clothes are almost indistinguishable from the environment and you know how to abuse this camouflage perfectly. After that minute, you are camouflaged (see the condition) until you leave the area you camouflaged yourself for.  

Attacking a surprised creature while camouflaged does not reveal your location and you have +10 on all stealth checks. If a creature loses track of you, (You succeed on a stealth check) you may meld back into the environment, becoming camouflaged once more.

Tuesday, May 18, 2021 

10:38 PM 

Witchcraft Initiate 

Prerequisite: Level 1 

 

Choose either Alchemy or Enchantments. You learn the Infuse I formula or enchantment of the chosen witchcraft. In addition, choose two Novice formulas or enchantments from that craft. You can infuse, but neither distill, nor refine these crafts. Your infusion ability for these crafts is Intelligence. 

During a long rest (1 week of downtime), you gain a number of Infusions = 1 + your Intelligence modifier.  

The infusions you gain from this feature do not stack with those gained from other sources (such as a witch's Infusions feature). You also gain the ability to sacrifice creatures for additional infusions as with the Witch's Blood Magic feature. 

This feat can be taken multiple times. If you take this feat again and choose the same witchcraft, you instead increase the level of formulas or enchantments you can infuse by one, granting you the corresponding infuse formula or enchantment. You may also learn two new formulas or enchantments of a level you can infuse.

Thursday, June 17, 2021 

1:13 PM 

Prepared Caster 

Prerequisite: The ability to cast at least one spell. 

 

You have honed your ability to cast spells at a moment's notice, granting you the following benefits: 

•If you choose to take the Cast a Spell action when you take the Ready action, you do not need to expend Vitality until the reaction is triggered. 

•When you roll for initiative and are not surprised, you can use your reaction to immediately cast a spell. To cast a spell in this way, it must have a casting time of 1 Action or less and must not allow you to make an attack roll or force any creature to make a saving throw.

Prerequisite: The ability to cast at least one spell. 

 

You have honed your ability to cast spells at a moment's notice, granting you the following benefits: 

If you choose to take the Cast a Spell action when you take the Ready action, you do not need to expend Vitality until the reaction is triggered. 

When you roll for initiative and are not surprised, you can use your reaction to immediately cast a spell. To cast a spell in this way, it must have a casting time of 1 Action or less and must not allow you to make an attack roll or force any creature to make a saving throw. 

Conflicting change.

Wednesday, May 19, 2021 

5:54 PM 

Spell Sniper 

Prerequisite: The ability to cast at least one spell 

 

You have learned techniques that enhance the stability of your spells, granting you the following benefits: 

•An ability score of your choice increases by 1, to a maximum of 20. 

•When you cast a spell that targets only a single creature or requires you to make an attack roll, the spell’s range is doubled. 

•Your spells ignore half- and three-quarters cover.

5:54 PM

Wednesday, May 19, 2021 

2:43 PM 

War Caster 

Prerequisite: The ability to cast at least one spell 

 

You have practiced casting spells in the midst of combat, granting you the following benefits: 

•You have advantage on Constitution saving throws that you make to maintain your concentration on a spell when you take damage. 

•You can perform the somatic components of spells even when you have weapons or a shield in one or both hands. 

•When a hostile creature’s movement provokes an opportunity attack from you, you can use your reaction to cast a spell at the creature, rather than making an opportunity attack. The spell must have a casting time of 1 action or less and can only target that creature. 

 

Wednesday, May 19, 2021 

2:43 PM 

War Caster 

Prerequisite: The ability to cast at least one spell 

 

You have practiced casting spells in the midst of combat, granting you the following benefits: 

You have advantage on Constitution saving throws that you make to maintain your concentration on a spell when you take damage. 

You can perform the somatic components of spells even when you have weapons or a shield in one or both hands. 

When a hostile creature’s movement provokes an opportunity attack from you, you can use your reaction to cast a spell at the creature, rather than making an opportunity attack. The spell must have a casting time of 1 action or less and can only target that creature. 

Conflicting change.

Resilient 

Choose one ability score. You gain the following benefits: 

•Increase the chosen ability score by 1, to a maximum of 20. 

•You gain proficiency in saving throws using the chosen ability. 

This feat can be taken multiple times in different ability scores.

Tough 

Your hit point maximum increases by an amount equal to 2 + your level when you gain this feat. Whenever you gain a level thereafter, your hit point maximum increases by an additional 1 hit point.

Monday, May 17, 2021 

4:45 PM 

Harmless 

You appear nonthreatening, causing others to underestimate you, and granting you the following benefits: 

•Increase your Charisma score by 1, to a maximum of 20. 

•You have advantage on Charisma (Deception) checks to appear weaker or more useless than you are. 

•When you roll initiative and are not surprised, you can make a Charisma (Deception) check against the passive Wisdom (Insight) of all hostile creatures that can see you. If you beat a creature's Wisdom (Insight), it can't attack you or choose to target you with a harmful effect until it sees you take a harmful action against one of its allies or itself. The creature may still include you within an area effect for which it doesn't declare targets.

Moderately Armored 

Prerequisite: Light armor proficiency 

 

You have trained in the use of medium armor and shields and can now use both effectively.

Wednesday, May 19, 2021 

2:47 PM 

Heavily Armored 

Prerequisite: Medium armor proficiency 

 

You have trained in the use of heavy armor and shields and can now use both effectively. 

• Increase your Strength score by 1, to a maximum of 20. 

• You gain proficiency with heavy armor and shields.

Wednesday, May 19, 2021 

2:25 PM 

Medium Armor Master 

Prerequisite: Proficiency with medium armor 

 

You have mastered the use of medium armor. 

• Wearing medium armor doesn’t impose disadvantage on your Dexterity (Stealth) checks. 

• When you wear medium armor, you can add 3, rather than 2, to your AC if you have a Dexterity score of 16 or higher.

Monday, May 17, 2021 

9:35 PM 

Critical Striker 

 

Your blows strike true and crush your foes, granting you the following benefits: 

•Increase your Strength score by 1, to a maximum of 20. 

•When you make a melee weapon attack, you score a critical hit on a roll of 19 or 20. If you already have an increased critical threat range, the range increases by 1 instead. 

•When you score a critical hit with a melee weapon, you can roll one of the weapon’s damage dice one additional time and add it to the extra damage of the critical hit.

Monday, May 17, 2021 

3:07 PM 

Dual Wielder 

 

You have mastered fighting with two weapons, granting you the following benefits: 

• You gain a +1 bonus to AC while you are wielding a separate melee weapon in each hand. 

• You can use two-weapon fighting even when the one-handed melee weapons you are wielding aren’t light.

Wednesday, May 19, 2021 

2:37 PM 

Firearms Training 

 

You have practiced extensively to effectively wield firearms properly. 

•Increase your Dexterity score by 1, to a maximum of 20. 

•You learn one Fighting Style from the Fighter class. 

•You gain proficiency with simple and martial firearms.

Monday, May 17, 2021 

4:35 PM 

Grappler 

Prerequisite: Strength 13 or higher 

 
You've developed the skills necessary to hold your own in close-quarters grappling.  

•When you are grappling a creature you can attempt to shove it prone as a bonus action. 

•You have Advantage on attack rolls against creatures you are grappling. 

•When a creature you are grappling attempts to break your grapple and fails, you can use your reaction to make a melee attack against them. 

•You can grapple creatures one size larger than normal. 

 

Monday, May 17, 2021 

4:35 PM 

Grappler 

Prerequisite: Strength 13 or higher 

 
You've developed the skills necessary to hold your own in close-quarters grappling.  

When you are grappling a creature you can attempt to shove it prone as a bonus action. 

You have Advantage on attack rolls against creatures you are grappling. 

When a creature you are grappling attempts to break your grapple and fails, you can use your reaction to make a melee attack against them. 

You can grapple creatures one size larger than normal.

Tuesday, May 18, 2021 

10:35 PM 

Mage Slayer 

 

You have practiced techniques useful in melee combat against spellcasters, gaining the following benefits: 

•When a creature within 1 meter (5 feet) of you casts a spell, you can use your reaction to make an attack of opportunity against that creature. 

•When you damage a creature that is concentrating on a spell, that creature loses concentration. 

•You have advantage on saving throws against spells cast by creatures within 1 meter (5 feet) of you.  

 

Tuesday, May 18, 2021 

10:35 PM 

Mage Slayer 

 

You have practiced techniques useful in melee combat against spellcasters, gaining the following benefits: 

When a creature within 1 meter (5 feet) of you casts a spell, you can use your reaction to make an attack of opportunity against that creature. 

When you damage a creature that is concentrating on a spell, that creature loses concentration. 

You have advantage on saving throws against spells cast by creatures within 1 meter (5 feet) of you.

Monday, May 17, 2021 

5:16 PM 

Rapid Shooter 

 

You have mastered the handling of your ranged weapons, granting you the following benefits: 

•You ignore the loading property. 

•You can reload weapons with the reload property as a bonus action. 

•Being within 1 meter (5 feet) of a hostile creature doesn't impose disadvantage on your ranged attack rolls.

Wednesday, May 19, 2021 

8:48 PM 

Skirmisher 

 

You are trained in the art of weakening opponents from range, then closing in for the kill.  

•When you attack a creature with a ranged attack, you have advantage on your next melee weapon attack against that creature until the end of your next turn. 

•When you take the Disengage action your speed increases by 5 meters (15 feet) for the rest of your turn. 

 

Wednesday, May 19, 2021 

8:48 PM 

Skirmisher 

 

You are trained in the art of weakening opponents from range, then closing in for the kill.  

When you attack a creature with a ranged attack, you have advantage on your next melee weapon attack against that creature until the end of your next turn. 

When you take the Disengage action your speed increases by 5 meters (15 feet) for the rest of your turn.

5:54 PM 

Spell Sniper 

Prerequisite: The ability to cast at least one spell 

 

You have learned techniques that enhance the stability of your spells, granting you the following benefits: 

•An ability score of your choice increases by 1, to a maximum of 20. 

•When you cast a spell that targets only a single creature or requires you to make an attack roll, the spell’s range is doubled. 

•Your spells ignore half- and three-quarters cover.

Species Feats offer specific benefits that are based on the traits of your species. If you are a hybrid between two species, you may choose a trait from either. If you have an acquired species, you may also choose a feat from your base species.

Species Feats offer specific benefits that are based on the traits of your species. If you are a hybrid between two species, you may choose a trait from either. If you have an acquired species, you may also choose a feat from your base species.

Dwarven Tradition 

Prerequisite: Dwarf 

 

You train to turn your already tough as nails dwarven body into the apex of stout hardines, granting you the following benefits: 

•Your hit point maximum increases by an amount equal to your level when you gain this feat. Whenever you gain a level thereafter, your hit point maximum increases by an additional 1 hit point. 

•Increase the number of magic items you may attune to by 1.

Tuesday, April 26, 2022 

10:17 PM 

Elvan Accuracy 

Prerequisite: Elf 

 

The grace and precision of elven warriors is legendary and your training in using your reflexes enhances your natural abilities to grant you the following benefits: 

•Whenever you hit with an attack that isn't a critical hit, your critical hit range increases by 1. This benefits stacks until you land a critical hit, miss an attack, or enter or exit combat, after which it resets to your default. 

•Whenever you have advantage on an attack roll, you roll three dice instead of two.
