Fortifying 

Expert aria 

 

Vitality Cost: 5 per Turn 

Casting Time: 1 Action 

Range: 30 Meters 

Duration: Concentration 

 

You begin a slow crescendo that builds in speed and volume over time. It strengthens the bodies of your allies, eventually fortifying them against all harm. 

 

For the duration, you may affect any creature of your choice in range until it starts its turn out of range. 

1st Verse: Affected creatures have advantage on death saving throws and whenever they regain hit points, they regain the maximum possible amount. 

2nd Verse: An affected creature is resistant to all damage. 

3rd Verse: At the end of each of your turns, affected creatures gain 1d6 + your Spellcasting Ability modifier temporary hit points.

Master 

Access to master arias grants you the ability described under Channel Performance V and allows you to learn the following spells. 

 

Channel Performance V 

Finally, as a master of performance your performances echo through creation, penetrating even the most powerful silence and ward. 

 

Penetrating Performance. Arias you cast can also affect creatures behind total cover. Abilities that provide advantage on saving throws against spells confer no advantage on saving throws against your arias. Traits or abilities that make a creature immune to magic do not protect against your arias.

Thursday, January 13, 2022 

8:44 AM 

Controlling 

Expert aria 

 

Vitality Cost: 4 per Turn 

Casting Time: 1 Action 

Range: 30 Meters 

Duration: Concentration 

 

With a powerful, melodic burst you enter into a fast tune. The aria penetrates the mind of your target. Its thoughts become muddled as its mind is opened to your will. After short lapses in attention, it finally becomes fully lost in your tune and slave to your commands.  

 

When you start this aria, target a creature in range for the duration. The aria ends if the target starts its turn out of range or behind total cover. 

1st Verse: At the end of each of your turns the target must succeed a Wisdom saving throw or be charmed by you for the duration. The affected creature may repeat the saving throw whenever it takes damage. On a successful save the target is no longer charmed until it fails another saving throw.  

2nd Verse: The charmed target must use its action before moving on each of its turns to make an attack against a creature other than itself that you verbally command it to attack (no action required). The target can act normally on its turn if you choose no creature or if none are within its reach or range.   

3rd Verse: The charmed creature no longer attacks at the start of its turn. It instead follows whatever you command it to do at the start of each of its turns (no action required).

Thursday, January 13, 2022 

8:44 AM 

Nullifying 

Master aria 

 

Vitality Cost: 10 per Turn 

Casting Time: 1 Action 

Range: Self (10-meter sphere) 

Duration: Concentration 

 

A slow and steady tune begins to warp the veil around you. At first it weakens energy fluctuations. As the melody slows, it calms the winds of magic blowing across the veil and finally stills them completely. 

 

1st Verse: Every creature in in the area of effect has advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects, as well as on saving throws against effects created by electric or mechanical devices. 

2nd Verse: Spells and other magical effects are suppressed in the sphere and can't protrude into it. Vitality expended to cast a suppressed spell is consumed. While an effect is suppressed, it doesn't function, but the time it spends suppressed counts against its duration. Electronic devices shut down and violent chemical reactions fail to trigger. 

Targeted Effects. Electronics, spells and other magical effects that target a creature or an object in the sphere have no effect on that target. 

Areas of Magic. The area of another spell or magical effect can't extend into the sphere. If the sphere overlaps an area of magic, the part of the area that is covered by the sphere is suppressed. For example, the explosion created by a Fireball is suppressed within the sphere, nullifying the explosion if the overlap is large enough. 

Spells. Any active spell or other magical effect on a creature or an object in the sphere is suppressed while the creature or object is in it. 

Items. The properties and powers of electronic or magic items are suppressed in the sphere. A magic weapon's properties and powers are suppressed if it is used against a target in the sphere or wielded by an attacker in the sphere. If an electronic or magic weapon or a piece of electronics or magic based ammunition fully leaves the sphere (for example, if you fire a magic arrow or throw a grenade at a target outside the sphere), the properties of the item ceases to be suppressed as soon as it exits. 

Magical Travel. Teleportation fails to work in the sphere, whether the sphere is the destination or the departure point for such magical travel. 

Dispel Magic. Spells and magical effects such as Dispel Magic from the Lore of Frost have no effect on the sphere. Likewise, the spheres created by different Nullifying arias don't nullify each other. 

Signals and EMPs. Signals cannot pass through the sphere and electronics inside it cannot be influenced or damaged by them.

Hideous Laughter 

Novice divine 

 

Vitality Cost: 3 

Casting Time: 1 Action 

Range: 20 Meters 

Duration: 1 Minute 

 

You smile and chuckle. Your opponent joins in on the hilarity and in fits of laughter, they fall to the ground. 

 

Target a creature in range that you can see. It must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw, or fall prone and be incapacitated for the duration. It may repeat the saving throw at the end of its turn and whenever it takes damage. If the saving throw is triggered by damage, the creature has advantage on it. 

A creature that is immune to being charmed is immune to this effect.

Vicious Mockery 

Novice divine 

 

Vitality Cost: 1 

Casting Time: 1 Action 

Range: 20 Meters 

Duration: 1 Minute 

 

You yell an insult. Using your opponent’s broken pride, you weaken their mental defenses and leave them open to further psychic attacks. 

 

Target a living creature in range that can hear you and force it to make a Charisma saving throw. On a failed save, the insult shakes the creature to its core. It has disadvantage on all Charisma and Wisdom checks and saving throws for the duration and becomes hostile towards you. A creature prone to violence may attack you and may focus their attacks on you in particular if it is reasonable to do so. 

A creature that is immune to being charmed is immune to this effect. 

 

Upcast: Spend an additional 1 Vitality to increase the duration by 1 minute.

Chapter 12: Divine Magic (39) 

Tuesday, July 13, 2021 

8:49 AM 

Chapter 12: Divine Magic 

Divine magic manipulates the bond between soul and body, allowing spellcasters to exert their influence over the thoughts and emotions of living beings. Mortals lack the ability to affect others in this way, but gods possess a secret pathway into the minds of both the living and the undead. By disguising their influence as emotions, the bridge between the machine-like processes of the body and the chaos of the soul, they can bypass the psychic shields that ward living beings from mental manipulation. However, the gods themselves lack the agency and sentience to wield their abilities to further any particular goal. Except for the rare cases where they do, because just like the souls formed from them they are chaotic and their actions even more random than those of mortals. However, in most cases their mindless pursuit to make mortals indulge in their domains requires a more specific, refined application. This is where mortal spellcasters come into play. Through rigorous mental training mortals can tap into the divine and manipulate both the minds and desires of their fellow mortals or immortals. 

The process of casting a divine spell starts with the caster using words and gestures to evoke a desired emotion or response in their target, laying the groundwork for their manipulation. They then channel divine energy and direct it towards their victim. Drawing upon the divine, the caster seeks to bypass the target's mental defenses through the backdoor provided by the god. This typically results in subtle influence, but skilled divine casters can even invoke their god to temporarily overwhelm and control another creature's will. 

Divine Source 

Broadly speaking, there are three ways to gain access to divine power. The first is by forming a bond with a deity by devoting oneself to its domain. Prophets do this, but this grants the deity that is being invoked the ability to manipulate its devote just as much as the prophet bends the god’s power to its will. How a god can be channeled depends on its unique nature. Each god has its own requirements and limitations for drawing upon its power, determining what methods and techniques can be employed by the caster to access its divine abilities. It's a delicate dance between mortal and god, with the caster tapping into the god's power while navigating its nature and intentions. 

The second way is through pure resolve and force of will. This approach allows paladins to wield divine power without risking any particular god influencing their perspective, however the complexity and intensity of influence this can produce is severely limited.  

The third way is by acquiring the blessing of all six gods and becoming a chosen. This approach has all the advantages of the first two approaches with none of the drawbacks, but there is no reliable way of becoming a chosen. It always involves finding one sanctuary of each god and a harrowing, personal journey. 

  

Your class determines what ability score you cast divine spells with. 

  

Power over the Living and Dead 

Divine magic focuses on mentally influencing foes. It provides large amounts of crowd control and utility, but little in the way of damage. However, few creatures have the willpower to effectively resist divine powers, but those that do are almost entirely invulnerable to your abilities. The undead of the fog are particularly vulnerable to these psionic abilities, making divine magic invaluable when venturing into the darkness.

Novice (12) 

Thursday, January 13, 2022 

5:05 PM 

Novice 

Access to novice divine spells grants you the abilities described under Channel Divinity I and allows you to learn the following spells. 

 

Channel Divinity I 

The most fundamental application of divine power enables the manipulation of emotions. At its simplest, this can enhance persuasive efforts or unleash potentially traumatizing surges of mental static. 

Divine Wrath. As a subtle action, expend a negligible amount of vitality and target a creature you can see no more than 20 meters away. The target must succeed a Charisma saving throw or take 1d6 psychic damage. 

This spell's damage increases by 1d6 when you reach 5th level (2d6) and 10th level (3d6). 

Friends. As a silent and subtle action, expend a negligible amount of vitality and target a creature you can see. As long as you maintain concentration, whenever a Charisma check is made to influence the target you may roll 1d6 and add or subtract the number rolled from the Charisma check. Halve the number rolled if the target has advantage on saving throws against being charmed and double it if it has disadvantage. A creature that is immune to being charmed is immune to this ability. 

 

 

Divine Wrath Description 

Manuela is sitting in the garage trying to project her will just like she did in her dream. Something falls in place as she reaches out with unwavering conviction. Her mind pulls on something greater than them just out of sight. For the first time she feels it. Compels it. She directs her focus towards Kilian. It triggers a mental reflex. She thinks short, random thoughts and the power responds. It lashes out like a whip. Violet-pink spots pop near the corners of her vision. As the mental whip cracks, Kilian flinches as though he’s been hit in the face by a literal whip. 

Friends Description 

Manuela can feel the tension rising. The others can’t help her here. Disgusted with herself, she reaches out with her mind. The presence or power that makes her magic work caresses the man’s mind. 

(…) 

As she speaks, Manuela’s influence strokes the emotions of the man like comb. It untangles the anger, mixing it with calm and brightening his terrible day just the tiniest amount. It doesn’t force him to believe her, but blends in just the smallest amount of good will. 

The man’s expression eases a little. Still hostile, but slightly more reserved he asks, “Okay, then how did you even come across this whole thing?” 

Friends used on Elf 

Her will reaches out and attempts to lighten the other woman’s mood just enough to make her ever so slightly more receptive of Manuela’s words.[LK1]  Yet the other elf’s mind feels much smoother than that of the giant. Like soap, it is almost impossible to get a grip on.  

 
 

 

 

Add toll the dead instead of wrath  

A hush falls—then a single, keening note, like the low toll of a funeral bell no one else can hear, threads from your lips to the target’s heart. 

Novice (12) 

Thursday, January 13, 2022 

5:05 PM 

Novice 

Access to novice divine spells grants you the abilities described under Channel Divinity I and allows you to learn the following spells. 

 

Channel Divinity I 

The most fundamental application of divine power enables the manipulation of emotions. At its simplest, this can enhance persuasive efforts or unleash potentially traumatizing surges of mental static. 

Divine Wrath. As a subtle action, expend a negligible amount of vitality and target a creature you can see no more than 20 meters away. The target must succeed a Charisma saving throw or take 1d6 psychic damage. 

This spell's damage increases by 1d6 when you reach 5th level (2d6) and 10th level (3d6). 

Friends. As a silent and subtle action, expend a negligible amount of vitality and target a creature you can see. As long as you maintain concentration, whenever a Charisma check is made to influence the target you may roll 1d6 and add or subtract the number rolled from the Charisma check. Halve the number rolled if the target has advantage on saving throws against being charmed and double it if it has disadvantage. A creature that is immune to being charmed is immune to this ability. 

 

 

Divine Wrath Description 

Manuela is sitting in the garage trying to project her will just like she did in her dream. Something falls in place as she reaches out with unwavering conviction. Her mind pulls on something greater than them just out of sight. For the first time she feels it. Compels it. She directs her focus towards Kilian. It triggers a mental reflex. She thinks short, random thoughts and the power responds. It lashes out like a whip. Violet-pink spots pop near the corners of her vision. As the mental whip cracks, Kilian flinches as though he’s been hit in the face by a literal whip. 

Friends Description 

Manuela can feel the tension rising. The others can’t help her here. Disgusted with herself, she reaches out with her mind. The presence or power that makes her magic work caresses the man’s mind. 

(…) 

As she speaks, Manuela’s influence strokes the emotions of the man like comb. It untangles the anger, mixing it with calm and brightening his terrible day just the tiniest amount. It doesn’t force him to believe her, but blends in just the smallest amount of good will. 

The man’s expression eases a little. Still hostile, but slightly more reserved he asks, “Okay, then how did you even come across this whole thing?” 

Friends used on Elf 

Her will reaches out and attempts to lighten the other woman’s mood just enough to make her ever so slightly more receptive of Manuela’s words.[LK1]  Yet the other elf’s mind feels much smoother than that of the giant. Like soap, it is almost impossible to get a grip on.  

 
 

 

 

Add toll the dead instead of wrath  

A hush falls—then a single, keening note, like the low toll of a funeral bell no one else can hear, threads from your lips to the target’s heart.

Monday, January 17, 2022 

2:20 PM 

Benediction- 

Novice divine 

 

Vitality Cost: 3 

Casting Time: 1 Action 

Range: 10 Meters 

Duration: Concentration, up to 1 Minute 

 

A violet glimmer flashes across your eyes as you unleash potent words upon your enemies or allies. The divine power woven into your speech fortifies your comrades with unshakable determination, while sowing seeds of uncertainty and hesitation among your foes. 

 

When you cast this spell, target up to three creatures you can see in range and choose either Bane or Bless. You may choose a different effect for every target. 

Bane. The target must succeed a Charisma saving throw, or whenever it makes an attack roll or a saving throw before the spell ends, it must roll a d4 and subtract the number rolled from the attack roll or saving throw. 

Bless. Whenever the target makes an attack roll or a saving throw before the spell ends, it may roll a d4 and add the number rolled to the attack roll or saving throw. 

 

Upcast: Spend an additional 2 vitality to target an additional creature.

Monday, January 17, 2022 

2:20 PM 

Command- 

Novice divine 

 

Vitality Cost: 3 

Casting Time: 1 Action 

Range: 20 Meters 

Duration: 1 Round 

 

A single word directs the mind of another. For a moment they believe it is their own will and obey. 

 

You speak a one-word command to a creature you can see within range. The target must succeed a Wisdom saving throw or be charmed until the end of its next turn. If it is charmed during its next turn, it will use its action to follow the command and then end its turn. If your command is directly harmful to the target, it passes its turn instead. 

Some typical commands and their effects follow. You might issue a command other than ones described here. If you do, the GM determines how the target behaves. If the target can't follow your command, it passes its turn. 

 

Approach. The target moves toward you by the shortest and most direct route, ending its turn if it moves within 1 meter of you. 

 

Drop. The target drops whatever it is holding and then ends its turn. 

 

Flee. The target spends its turn moving away from you by the fastest available means. 

 

Grovel. The target falls prone and then ends its turn. 

 

Halt. The target doesn't move and takes no actions. A flying creature stays aloft, provided that it is able to do so. If it must move to stay aloft, it flies the minimum distance needed to remain in the air. 

 

Upcast: Spend an additional 5 vitality to target an additional creature. You can only issue the same command to all targets.

Divine Strikes 

Novice divine 

 

Vitality Cost: 1 per Turn 

Casting Time: 1 Bonus Action 

Range: Self 

Duration: Concentration 

 

Hatred fills your heart, coiling like a whip drawn tight. Your eyes glow violet and subtle veins of purple cover your hands. Animosity lashes through your weapon, cracking across the souls of your foes with every strike. 

 

For the duration, your attacks deal an additional 1d6 psychic damage on a hit. 

 

Upcast: Spend an additional 2 vitality per turn to deal an additional 1d6 psychic damage. 

 

‐----------------- 

 

Manuela’s spell cuts its mind as pink stars flashes around the edges of her vision. 

 

 

Shards blow away, half suspended. From the impact, a violet-pink glow blooms inside the white figure behind the metal.  Manuela’s contempt and power paints itself as a transparent, barely visible nebula of purple stars and veins and streaks into the ethereal flesh of the dead the bullet just struck. Like paint shot into a gel. 

-Manuela using Divine Strikes on a Wight in Book 2, Descent into Darkness 

 

 

 

he moves her two fingers across her chest as she forces hatred of the creature into her heart.

Frighten 

Novice divine 

 

Vitality Cost: 1 

Casting Time: 1 Action 

Range: 20 Meters 

Duration: 1 Minute 

 

Your stoic gaze wanders across your foe and the fear in their heart consumes them. They cower and shake before your holy presence. 

 

Target creature you can see in range must succeed a Charisma saving throw or be frightened of you for the duration. The frightened target can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect for itself on a success. 

 

Upcast: Spend an additional 2 vitality to target an additional creature.

Thursday, January 13, 2022 

8:44 AM 

Horrible Sorrow- 

Novice divine 

 

Vitality Cost: 3 

Casting Time: 1 Action 

Range: 20 Meters 

Duration: Concentration, up to 1 Minute 

 

You let out a soft, sad sigh while a dull, violet glow flickers across your fingertips and gutters out. Your victim's chest tightens, their eyes gloss with sudden tears. Their legs buckle and they crumple to the ground, wracked by a depression so deep it feels as though the world will never be right again. 

 

Target creature you can see in range must succeed a Charisma saving throw or be charmed for the duration. While charmed this way a creature falls prone, is incapacitated and unable to stand. 

At the end of each of its turns, and each time it takes damage, the target can make another Charisma saving throw. The target has advantage on the saving throw if it’s triggered by damage. On a success, the spell ends. 

 

Upcast: Spend an additional 5 vitality to target an additional creature.

Monday, January 17, 2022 

2:20 PM 

Honeyed Words 

Novice divine 

 

Vitality Cost: 1 per Target 

Casting Time: 1 Action 

Range: Self 

Duration: Instant 

 

Using divine power you influence another to believe your reasonable words. 

 

After speaking a phrase or making an argument to another creature you may cast this spell. You may target anyone who paid attention to your words that you can see, paying 1 vitality for every creature you target. A target creature must succeed an Intelligence saving throw or believe your words. The words must sound reasonable and the spell has no effect on a creature if that creature is already convinced beyond any reasonable doubt that what you say is a lie. For example, if a creature finds you standing over a corpse with a bloody knife in hand, you may convince it that you just found the body and picked up the knife. You won’t be able to convince it if it saw you stab the other person to death. A creature convinced by this spell believes your words, but may still be convinced of something else by concrete proof.

For example, if a creature finds you standing over a corpse with a bloody knife in hand, you may convince it that you just found the body and picked up the knife. You won’t be able to convince it if it saw you stab the other person to death. A creature convinced by this spell believes your words, but may still be convinced of something else by concrete proof. 

Conflicting change.

Wednesday, June 29, 2022 

12:03 AM 

Protection of Faith 

Novice divine 

 

Vitality Cost: 4 per Turn 

Casting Time: 1 Bonus Action 

Range: Self 

Duration: Concentration 

 

Pink light flashes over your eyes and as a foe aims to strike you, the blessing of the divine causes fear and doubt to fester in their heart and lead their blows astray. 

 

For the duration, whenever a creature you can see targets you with an attack it suffers a -5 penalty to its attack roll. 

A creature that is immune to being frightened is immune to this effect.

Monday, January 17, 2022 

2:20 PM 

Whisper of Pain 

Novice divine 

 

Vitality Cost: 2 

Casting Time: 1 Action 

Range: 20 Meters 

Duration: 1 Turn 

 

You utter a malicious whisper and your victim is wracked by a wave of mental pain. 

 

Target a creature you can see in range. It takes 2d6 psychic damage and either has disadvantage on its next attack roll or suffers a -5 penalty to the save DC on the next spell it casts before the end of its next turn (whichever comes first). 

 

Upcast: Spend an additional 2 Vitality to deal an extra 1d6 psychic damage.

Wednesday, June 29, 2022 

12:03 AM 

Whisper of Silence 

Novice divine 

 

Vitality Cost: 3 

Casting Time: 1 Action 

Range: 1 Meter 

Duration: 1 hour 

 

From behind you let out a breath. Your influence amplifies the surprise within your foe. The shock overwhelms them, and they crumple to the ground, unconscious. 

 

Target a creature you are hidden from in range. The target takes 20 psychic damage. If this reduces a creature to 0 hit points, it falls unconscious for the duration and is stable.  

This spell is silent. 

 

Upcast: Spend an additional 4 vitality to deal an extra 10 psychic damage. 

 

 

 

 

Target a surprised creature in range. If the target has 20 hit points or fewer, it falls unconscious for the duration. 

A creature that is immune to psychic damage is immune to this effect. This spell is silent.

Thursday, January 13, 2022 

8:44 AM 

Calm Emotions- 

Apprentice divine 

 

Vitality Cost: 5 

Casting Time: 1 Action 

Range: 20 meters (10-meter sphere) 

Duration: Instant 

 

With a serene countenance, you make a deliberate downward gesture, exuding a subtle purple pulse that radiates outward and converges upon a focal point. The gentle magic envelops all who are touched by its glow, soothing their emotions and calming their inner turmoil. 

 

Each living creature of your choice in a 10-meter radius sphere centered on a point in range has its emotions calmed, ending any Charmed, Frightened, and Rage (such as the barbarian's rage ability) condition affecting it.  

 

Upcast: Spend an additional 2 Vitality to increase the radius of the sphere by 1 meter.

Clear Mind- 

Apprentice divine 

 

Vitality Cost: 3 

Casting Time: 1 Action 

Range: 10 meters 

Duration: 1 Minute 

 

With a soothing word and a glance, the webs of mental intrusion unravel and purity is restored to the mind. 

 

Target creature that can hear you in range gains +10 to Intelligence, Wisdom, and Charisma saving throws for the duration. If the target is affected by an effect that offers such a saving throw to end it, it may immediately make a saving throw against that effect. 

 

Upcast: Spend an additional 4 vitality to increase the duration of the effect by 1 minute.

Thursday, January 13, 2022 

8:44 AM 

Distract 

Apprentice divine 

 

Vitality Cost: 5 

Casting Time: 1 Reaction 

Range: 30 Meters 

Duration: Instant 

 

As your foe attempts to strike, you swiftly place your fingers in front of your nose, and your eyes emanate a brilliant purple glow. With divine precision, you shift their attention away from you, redirecting their focus elsewhere. In that crucial moment, their attack may be redirected at one of their own.  

 

As a reaction to being targeted by an attack from a creature in range that you can see, you can force the attacker to make a Wisdom saving throw. On a failure, you may choose a third creature that is no more than 5 meters away from you and in range of the attack. The attack targets that creature instead. 

 

Thursday, January 13, 2022 

8:44 AM 

Distract 

Apprentice divine 

 

Vitality Cost: 5 

Casting Time: 1 Reaction 

Range: 30 Meters 

Duration: Instant 

 

As your foe attempts to strike, you swiftly place your fingers in front of your nose, and your eyes emanate a brilliant purple glow. With divine precision, you shift their attention away from you, redirecting their focus elsewhere. In that crucial moment, their attack may be redirected at one of their own.  

 

As a reaction to being targeted by an attack from a creature in range that you can see, you can force the attacker to make a Wisdom saving throw. On a failure, you may choose a third creature that is no more than 5 meters away from you and in range of the attack. The attack targets that creature instead. 

Conflicting change.

Thursday, January 13, 2022 

8:44 AM 

Ignorance 

Apprentice divine 

 

Vitality Cost: 2 per Target 

Casting Time: 1 Reaction 

Range: 20 Meters 

Duration: 1 Minute 

 

Upon being detected, you tell your foes that everything is fine. Their eyes flash with purple light and their alarm is dispelled. 

 

You may cast this spell as a reaction to being detected by one or more creatures in range. A target must succeed an Intelligence saving throw or ignore you and your allies. The target detects your presence but does not realize that you are something out of the ordinary and continues on their way. When the spell ends, the creature can become alerted once more but does not realize anything out of the ordinary happened. A creature that is already alert has advantage on the saving throw. A creature in combat automatically succeeds its saving throw. 

A creature that is immune to being charmed is immune to this effect.

Thursday, January 13, 2022 

8:44 AM 

Mind Spike- 

Apprentice divine 

 

Vitality Cost: 4 

Casting Time: 1 Action 

Range: 30 Meters 

Duration: Concentration, up to 1 minute 

 

You point at your foe, and violet-pink light blazes in your eyes as you release a sharp exhale. A powerful psychic scream erupts within your victim's skull, shrouding their head in a haze of pink energy. The scream reverberates through their mind, echoing back to you, and providing you with a clear and unhindered means to track their movements. 

 

Target a creature you can see in range and force it to make a Charisma saving throw. On a failed save it takes 4d6 psychic damage and is revealed to you for the duration. On a successful save it takes half as much damage and is not revealed. This spell reveals a creature even through walls. 

 

Upcast: Spend an additional 2 Vitality to deal an extra 1d6 psychic damage.

Paralyzing Dread 

Apprentice divine 

 

Vitality Cost: 4 

Casting Time: 1 Action 

Range: 20 Meters 

Duration: 1 Minute 

 

With a glare you burn a feeling of overwhelming terror into your victim's mind. They fall to their knees, paralyzed by their depression. 

 

Target a creature you can see within range. The target must succeed a Charisma saving throw or be frightened for the duration. While frightened this way, a creature is paralyzed. At the end of each of its turns, the target can make another Charisma saving throw, ending the spell on a success. 

 

Upcast: Spend an additional 6 Vitality to target an additional creature in range.

8:44 AM 

Suggestion 

Apprentice divine 

 

Vitality Cost: 8 

Casting Time: 1 Action 

Range: 10 Meters 

Duration: 8 Hours 

 

With a calm and measured tone, you make a reasonable request, then weave a subtle influence into your victim's mind as they consider it. 

 

You may speak up to two sentences to a creature that can hear and understand you. The target must succeed an Intelligence saving throw or be charmed for the duration. While charmed this way, it pursues the course of action you described to the best of its ability. If the suggested activity can be completed in a shorter time, the spell ends when the creature finishes what it was asked to do. 

You can also specify conditions that will trigger an activity during the duration. If the condition isn’t met before the spell expires, the activity isn’t performed. The suggestion must be worded in such a manner as to make the course of action sound reasonable. Asking the creature to do something it would not even consider doing causes the spell to fail. Once the spell ends, the creature knows you convinced it to perform that action, but believes it was convinced of this course of action (unless provided evidence of the contrary). 

This spell is subtle.  

 

Upcast: Spend an additional 10 vitality to increase the duration to a day, or an additional 30 vitality to increase the duration to a year.

Adept (8)- 

Thursday, January 13, 2022 

5:05 PM 

Adept 

Access to adept divine spells grants you the abilities described under Channel Divinity III and allows you to learn the following spells. 

 

Channel Divinity III 

As you become adept at channeling your divine power, you gain the ability to project complicated ideas and images into the minds of others and even influence their subconscious.  

Show Vision. Using your action and a negligible amount of vitality, you touch a willing creature and begin concentrating. You either project a vision into its head or allow it to project one into yours. A vision can be a memory or a fabricated scenario. These visions are like a dream, only that the creator of the vision controls every aspect of it. The vision is not immersive and the viewer is fully aware that it is not real. Both you and the creature may end the vision at any time (no action required). 

 

Move modifmemory here 

 

Change of Mind. Touch a willing or unconscious creature, spend 1 hour and 15 vitality to change its alignment to one of your choice. This spell does not force a creature to abandon its allies or become friendly to you. Effects that cure madness remove this change. A creature that is immune to being charmed is immune to this spell.

Thursday, January 13, 2022 

8:44 AM 

Detect Thoughts- 

Adept divine 

 

Vitality Cost: 4 per Turn 

Casting Time: 1 Action 

Range: 10 Meters 

Duration: Concentration 

 

Purple veins snake out of your eyes, then disappear. The surface thoughts of those around you are revealed. 

 

When you cast this spell, all creatures in a 10-meter radius sphere centered on you that are not immune to psychic damage are revealed. Physical obstructions, such as walls, block this spell.  

When you cast the spell and as your action on each turn until the spell ends, you can focus your mind on a creature revealed this way. If the creature you choose has an Intelligence of 2 or lower, the creature is unaffected. You initially learn the surface thoughts of the creature – what is most on its mind at that moment. Questions verbally directed at the target creature naturally shape the course of its thoughts, so this spell is particularly effective as part of an interrogation unless a creature is actively suppresses its thoughts. 

As an action, you can either shift your attention to another creature’s thoughts or attempt to probe deeper into the same creature’s mind. If you probe deeper, the target must make a Wisdom saving throw. If it fails, you gain insight into its reasoning (if any), its emotional state, and something that looms large in its mind (such as something it worries over, loves, or hates). If it succeeds, it can choose to block your attempt, ending the spell. The creature may also pretend not to realize you are probing it and attempt to show you fabricated information. It makes a Charisma (Deception) check against your passive perception to fool you into believing the fabricated information is real. 

Either way, the target realizes that you are probing into its mind at the end of your turn, and can block further intrusion or manipulate what you are seeing. 

This spell is subtle and silent. 

 

MAKE APPRENTICE 

 

Descriptions for Thought Types 

 

-Emotion thinking: Instead of thoughts you feel... an emotion of anger and hatred directed to an area that seems... void of anything... 

 

-Feeling Thinking: Instead of thoughts you get an short odd feeling, you have an natural idea of which direction to go (roll Insight) 

 

-Image thinking: you see a transparent tower and the king tied up inside it, based on the surroundings you have an idea where it could be 

 

-Photographic thinking: you see the king tied up inside a room as if you are looking at a picture, a shadow of a person looming over him, the image is so clear you know that this guy has seen this exact scene personally 

 

-3d thinking: wis save (against psychic damage unless the character also has this type of thinking), on success: you find yourself inside a dungeon room, the king is tied up to a wall, you realize that you are inside some kind of memory room and that you can interact with this room, through you cannot free the king as this is a memory, you are able to go explore and investigate.

8:44 AM 

Frenzy 

Adept divine 

 

Vitality Cost: 5 

Casting Time: 1 Action 

Range: 20 Meters 

Duration: 1 Minute 

 

A faint violet glow emanates from your outstretched finger, seeping into the mind of your victim. Your virulent influence pierces their thoughts, blurring their perception of friend and foe. Paranoia fills their mind, and with a little luck, their allies may find themselves on the receiving end of their fear-induced rampage. 

 

Target creature you can see in range must succeed an Intelligence saving throw or become frightened for the duration. A surprised creature has disadvantage on its saving throw. While frightened this way, the creature loses the ability to distinguish between friend and foe and will attack the closest creature it can see, or the last creature that dealt damage to it. Whenever the frightened creature takes damage, it has disadvantage on the next saving throw it makes against this spell. At the end of each of its turns, the creature repeats the saving throw, ending the effect on a success. 

 

Upcast: Spend an additional 5 vitality to increase the duration by 1 minute. 

 

 

 

Whenever the affected creature chooses another creature as a target, it must choose the target at random from among the creatures it can see within range of the attack, spell, or other ability it’s using. It will choose the closest creature by default, or the last one that dealt damage to it. If a creature provokes an opportunity attack from the affected creature, the affected creature must make that attack if it is able to. 

A creature that is immune to being frightened is immune to this effect.

Monday, May 9, 2022 

9:28 AM 

Modify Memory 

Adept divine 

 

Vitality Cost: 15 

Casting Time: 1 Action 

Range: 10 Meters 

Duration: Concentration, up to 10 minutes 

 

There is a violet glint in your eyes as your victim's thoughts become blurred and its memory as clay, ready to be remolded. 

 

One creature that you can see must make an Intelligence saving throw. If you are fighting the creature, it has advantage on the saving throw. On a failed save, the target becomes charmed by you for the duration. The charmed target is incapacitated and unaware of its surroundings, though it can still hear you. If it takes any damage or is targeted by another spell, this spell ends, and none of the target's memories are modified. 

While this charm lasts, you can affect the target's memory of an event that it experienced within the last 24 hours and that lasted no more than 1 hour. You can permanently eliminate all memory of the event, allow the target to recall the event with perfect clarity and exacting detail, change its memory of the details of the event, or create a memory of some other event. 

You must speak to the target to describe how its memories are affected, and it must be able to understand your language for the modified memories to take root. Its mind fills in any gaps in the details of your description. If the spell ends before you have finished describing the modified memories, the creature's memory isn't altered. Otherwise, the modified memories take hold when the spell ends. 

A modified memory doesn't necessarily affect how a creature behaves, particularly if the memory contradicts the creature's natural inclinations, alignment, or beliefs. An illogical modified memory may be dismissed. 

This spell is subtle.

Thursday, January 13, 2022 

8:44 AM 

Phantasy 

Adept divine 

 

Vitality Cost: 6 per Hour 

Casting Time: 1 Action 

Range: 20 Meters 

Duration: Concentration 

 

Your eyes become a purple void filled with stars. Your open hand extends towards your victim and sends it into a dream of your creation. Should you wish them harm, you may add a phantasmal killer to the dream, slowly killing them in real life as the vision tortures their mind. 

 

Target a creature in range. It must succeed an Intelligence saving throw or fall unconscious. While unconscious, it experiences a vivid vision of your choice. The creature may repeat the saving throw whenever it takes damage or experiences extreme distress in the vision, ending the effect on a success. A target that knows or realizes that the vision is not real can use its action to end the spell. 

You are incapacitated while concentrating on this spell. This spell is silent. 

You may add a phantasmal killer to the vision. If you do, the target takes 4d6 psychic damage at the end of each of its turns for the duration. 

 

Upcast: Spend an extra 3 Vitality per turn to increase the vision’s damage by 1d6 psychic damage.

Shackles of Pain 

Adept divine 

 

Vitality Cost: 5 per Turn 

Casting Time: 1 Action 

Range: 20 Meters 

Duration: Concentration 

 

With a pointed gesture at your foe, you command them to feel your pain. Your eyes and theirs glisten with a radiant purple light as any trauma you endure is echoed onto them by a pulse of pink light.  

 

Target creature you can see in range must succeed a Charisma saving throw, or being cursed for the duration. While it is cursed this way, any damage you receive is dealt to the cursed creature as psychic damage. 

 

Upcast: Spend an additional 6 Vitality per Turn to target an additional creature.

Thursday, January 13, 2022 

8:44 AM 

Speak 

Adept divine 

 

Vitality Cost: 1 per Turn 

Casting Time: 1 Action 

Range: 10 Meters  

Duration: Concentration 

 

Purple veins snake through and around your eyes. The thoughts of whoever’s at the center of your gaze are driven towards truth or deceit. 

 

Target a living creature in range. It may make a Charisma saving throw. If it fails, choose one of the following effects: 

Speak Truth. The target becomes incapable of lying. If it tries, it instead blurts out the truth. 

Speak Lies. The target becomes incapable of speaking the truth. If it tries, it instead blurts out the exact opposite of the truth it’s trying to communicate. 

The spell fails if the target succeeds its saving throw, alerting the caster to that fact. The target knows that it is being influenced. 

 

Upcast: Spend an additional 2 Vitality per turn to target an additional creature.

Expert (5)- 

Thursday, January 13, 2022 

5:05 PM 

Expert 

Access to expert divine spells grants you the abilities described under Channel Divinity IV and allows you to learn the following spells. 

 

Channel Divinity IV 

As you become an expert at channeling divine power, you gain greater control over the connection with the divine and can channel psychic energy with greater precision. 

 

Enslave. As an action, target a creature you can see no more than 10 meters away and expend an amount of vitality equal to the target's current hit points. Expend an amount of vitality equal to twice the target's current hit points, if the target has advantage on saving throws against being charmed, and an amount of vitality equal to half its current hit points if it has disadvantage on saving throws against being charmed. Once you attempt to cast this spell, the GM tells you the spell's vitality cost. You may fizzle the spell before committing vitality, however, if you do the action used to cast the spell is wasted. 

The target is magically charmed by you until you die or choose to end the spell (no action required). While charmed this way it obeys your verbal or telepathic commands to the best of its ability. Whenever a creature charmed this way is dealt damage by you or your allies, or commanded to do something it would never consider doing on its own, it may make a Charisma saving throw, ending the spell on a success.  

No more than once every 24 hours, the target can also attempt the saving throw when it is at least 20 kilometers (12-miles) away from you.

Wednesday, January 19, 2022 

8:59 AM 

Dominate 

Expert divine 

 

Vitality Cost: 5 per Turn 

Casting Time: 1 Action 

Range: 30 Meters 

Duration: Concentration 

 

Your eyes burn purple, as veins of the same color snake out of them. The eyes of your victim are filled with holy purple light. Their will is burned away and they obey. 

 

Target a creature in range. It must succeed a Charisma saving throw or be charmed by you for the duration. While the target is charmed this way, you have a telepathic link with it that you can use to issue commands to the creature (no action required). It obeys these commands without question to the best of its ability. You can provide a specific, or a general course of action, such as attack that creature, run over there, or jump off that cliff. If the creature receives no direction from you, it defends and preserves itself to the best of its ability. 

You can use your action to take total and precise control of the target. The target loses concentration and until the end of your next turn, you use the creature’s senses and it only takes the actions you choose. While assuming direct control, your own body is incapacitated, blind and deafened.  

Whenever the dominated creature takes damage or is forced to do something it would never consider doing (such as attack a loved one or commit suicide), it may attempt a new Charisma saving throw against the spell. If the saving throw succeeds, the spell ends. 

 

 

 

 

 

 

You can also cast spells you know through the creature this way, using your spellcasting modifiers but its vitality instead of yours. If you cast a spell that requires concentration through a creature, the dominated creature maintains concentration on the spell for you until the domination ends or you order it to drop concentration.

Thursday, January 13, 2022 

8:44 AM 

Feedback Trap 

Expert divine 

 

Vitality Cost: 10 

Casting Time: 1 Action 

Range: Touch 

Duration: 24 Hours 

 

Touch someone’s forehead and tell them that they are protected. Your words place a trap in their mind. Psychic energy slumbers, ready to lash out at those who attempt to cause it psychic harm or spy upon its thoughts. 

 

One willing creature you touch has a trap placed in its mind for the duration. Whenever the target makes an Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma saving throw against a magical effect that attempts to influence its mind, takes psychic damage, or is subjected to an effect that would sense or influence its emotions or its thoughts, the creature that caused the attack takes 10d6 psychic damage and loses concentration. A creature that reaches out with its mind to target the creature with the trap inside its mind may make a Wisdom saving throw against your spell save DC as it targets its spell. On a success, it realizes that something is off with the other creature’s mind and may purposefully fizzle its own spell in response. It loses the action and vitality used to cast the spell but avoids the damage. 

Once triggered, the trap remains in place. The target of this spell can use its reaction to prevent the trap from activating on an effect it wishes to be affected by. A spell that triggers the trap still takes effect. 

 

Upcast: Spend an additional 2 Vitality to increase the damage of the trap by 1d6.

Thursday, January 13, 2022 

8:44 AM 

Incite Suicide- 

Expert divine 

 

Vitality Cost: 6 

Casting Time: 1 Action 

Range: 20 Meters 

Duration: 1 Turn 

 

Raise your hand above your head and open it. A burst of translucent purple rays explodes out of it. Purple light shoots through your victim's eyes. They are struck with existential dread so horrific, that they wish for their own death. They turn their own weapon upon themselves in a fit of lethal compulsion. 

 

Target creature in range must succeed a Charisma saving throw or be charmed until the end of its next turn. When a creature charmed this way starts its turn, it uses its entire turn to make one weapon attack with advantage against itself. On a hit, the attack is a critical hit. The spell then ends. 

 

Upcast: Spend an additional 8 vitality to target an additional creature.

Thursday, January 13, 2022 

8:44 AM 

Mass Phantasy 

Expert divine 

 

Vitality Cost: 10 per Hour 

Casting Time: 1 Action 

Range: 20 Meters (5-meter sphere) 

Duration: Concentration 

 

Your eyes become a purple void filled with stars. Your open hand extends and purple veins snake through the earth. Those standing on this hallowed ground are pulled into a dream of your creation. 

 

Target a point in range. Any creatures in a 5-meter radius sphere centered on that point must succeed an Intelligence saving throw or fall unconscious. While unconscious, all targets experience a vivid vision of your choice. An affected creature may repeat the saving throw whenever it takes damage or suffers extreme distress in the vision, ending the spell for itself on a success. A target that knows or realizes that the vision is not real can use its action to end the effect for itself. 

You are incapacitated while concentrating on this spell. 

You may add a phantasmal killer to the vision. If you do, all targets of your choice take 4d6 psychic damage at the end of each of their turns for the duration. 

 

Upcast: Spend an extra 5 Vitality per turn to increase the vision’s damage by 1d6 psychic damage.

Thursday, January 13, 2022 

12:43 PM 

Soul Unmoored 

Expert divine 

 

Vitality Cost: 4 per Turn 

Casting Time: 1 Bonus Action 

Range: Self 

Duration: Concentration 

 

A pink wave flashes over your body and with a single step your soul moves out of its mortal shell. Now visible as a figure made from black and purple mist that mirrors your form and connected to your body by a tether of liquid-like violet energy, you may move through walls and strike against the souls of others. 

 

Create an intangible proxy in your space and assume control of it. You may manipulate the appendages of the proxy into any shape, as long as it remains vaguely humanoid.  

Your proxy has a flying speed of 10 meters, can hover and may move through any physical object as if it were difficult terrain. The proxy has 10 meters of truesight and can see through walls. It is blind to anything outside this range. The proxy can be targeted by spells and abilities as if it were an undead creature. It is immune to all damage, except psychic damage, to which it is vulnerable. Any damage or effect that would affect it, affects you instead. You may use the proxy to cast spells as though it were you. Alternatively, you may use the proxy to make a melee spell attack as you slash its appendages at a creature, dealing 3d6 psychic damage on a hit. 

The proxy is connected to you by a tether that creates a direct connection, even through walls, and is visible to all. If the proxy is ever more than 30 meters away from you, the spell ends.  While you concentrate on this spell, your real body is incapacitated, has its speed reduced to zero, and is deaf and blind. You still notice if your real body is damaged.

Friday, January 28, 2022 

8:57 AM 

(Archived) Power Word Antipathy/Sympathy- 

Novice divine 

 

Vitality Cost: 15 

Casting Time: 1 Action 

Range: 10 meters 

Duration: 10 Days 

 

You speak either the word: fear, or: attract and then who is to be terrified or attracted. Waves of psychic energy move away from the one you target, making sure the world obeys your will. 

 

This spell attracts or repels creatures of your choice. You target something within range, either a Huge or smaller object, a creature, or an area that is no larger than a 100-meter radius. Then specify a kind of creature, such as dragons, elves, vampires, female humanoids, or a specific person. Choose antipathy or sympathy. If this spell is cast on the same target 3 times over the course of 30 days, its effects become until dispelled. 

Antipathy. Creatures of the kind you designated feel an intense urge to leave the area and avoid the target. When such a creature can see the target or comes within 20 meters of it, the creature must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or become frightened. The creature remains frightened while it can see the target or is within 20 meters of it. While frightened by the target, the creature must use its movement to move to the nearest safe spot from which it can't see the target. If the creature moves more than 20 meters from the target and can't see it, the creature is no longer frightened, but the creature becomes frightened again if it regains sight of the target or moves within 20 meters of it. 

Sympathy. Creatures of the kind you designated feel an intense urge to approach the target while within 20 meters of it or able to see it. When such a creature can see the target or comes within 20 meters of it, the creature must succeed a Wisdom saving throw or become charmed by the target. While charmed in this way a creature will approach the target and considers it something wonderful that is to be heeded and protected. 

If the target damages or otherwise harms an affected creature, the affected creature can make a Wisdom saving throw to end the effect, as described below. 

Ending the Effect. If an affected creature ends its turn further than 20 meters away from the target or unable to see it, the creature makes a Wisdom saving throw. On a successful save, the creature is no longer affected by the target and recognizes the feeling of repugnance or attraction as magical. 

A creature that successfully saves against this effect is immune to it for 1 minute, after which it can be affected again.

Thursday, January 13, 2022 

8:44 AM 

Power Word Madness (Encompassed by Enfeeble) 

Master divine 

 

Vitality Cost: 15 

Casting Time: 1 Action 

Range: 30 Meters 

Duration: Instant 

 

Your eyes burn purple, as veins of the same color snake out of them. You utter a single word: Madness. The eyes of your victim are filled with purple light. Divine power warps the way their soul perceives reality and shatters their sanity. 

 

Target a creature in range and permanently affect it with a madness of your choice. Your GM will provide you with a list of available madnesses, but may allow you to create your own.

Thursday, January 13, 2022 

8:44 AM 

Power Word Enfeeble- 

Master divine 

 

Vitality Cost: 15 

Casting Time: 1 Action 

Range: 30 Meters 

Duration: Until Dispelled 

 

With a sneer you say: Pathetic, and that is what your foe becomes. 

 

Target a creature you can see in range. The vitality cost of all abilities, including spells, maneuvers and magic items, the creature uses is doubled, the save DCs of the creature's abilities are all reduced by 5, it has disadvantage on attack rolls and can only communicate through incoherent rambling. 

Alternatively you may choose to afflict the creature with a madness of your choice. The GM has a list of available madnesses. 

A creature that is immune to psychic damage is immune to this effect. Any effect that cures a creature of madness may cure it of this effect.

Thursday, January 13, 2022 

8:44 AM 

Power Word Kill 

Master divine 

 

Vitality Cost: 15 

Casting Time: 1 Action 

Range: 30 Meters 

Duration: Instant 

 

You utter a single word: Die. It sears away the connection between body and soul and kills instantly. 

 

Target a creature in range. If it has 50 hit points or fewer, it dies instantly. Otherwise, the spell has no effect. If the target is resistant to psychic damage, it is only affected if it has 25 hit points or fewer. If the target is vulnerable to psychic damage, it is affected if it has 100 hit points or fewer. A creature that is immune to psychic damage is unaffected by this spell. 

Any spell or ability that would allow a creature killed this way to return to life is has no effect.

Monday, January 10, 2022 

1:59 PM 

Power Word Listen 

Master divine 

 

Vitality Cost: 10 per Turn 

Casting Time: 1 Action 

Range: 100 Kilometers  

Duration: Concentration 

 

You speak the word: Listen, and the world hears your words. 

 

As an action, you may project a psychic message into the minds of an unlimited number of creatures that you are familiar with within range. Instead of targeting individual creatures, you may instead project the message into the mind of every creature in an up to 10-kilometer radius sphere centered on a point within range. 

For every turn you concentrate on this spell, you may project 6 seconds worth of speech to the targets of this spell. A target understands your message, even if you don't share a language.

Thursday, January 13, 2022 

8:44 AM 

Power Word Obey 

Master divine 

 

Vitality Cost: 15 

Casting Time: 1 Action 

Range: 30 Meters 

Duration: See Description 

 

You utter the word: obey, and speak. Almost irresistible compulsion binds your new servant to your words. 

 

Target a creature in range and give it a command. The creature feels an almost irresistible compulsion to complete the task set for it. If it decides it will not obey the task you have set for it, it takes 1d6 psychic damage and must succeed on a Charisma saving throw or obey you anyway. On a successful save, the creature must repeat the saving throw again at the start of its next turn. If the creature succeeds three saving throws in a row, the spell ends. 

The spell ends once the creature has completed the command given to it or if you decide to end it. 

A creature that is immune to being charmed is immune to this effect. 

 

 

 

--------------------------------------------- 

A creature is bound to its own honest interpretation of the command, not by its precise wording. As long as the creature believes it is obeying, even as it unwittingly does something against it, the spell does not trigger. The spell does trigger if the creature decides it will undermine the purpose of your command. This includes exploiting a loophole or poor wording.

Wednesday, June 15, 2022 

9:02 PM 

Chapter 13: Elemental Magic 

The following six lores of magic are known as elemental magic, as they tap into the pure essence of energy from the boundless Void and shape it into potent spells. However, contrary to the traditional notion of the four elements (fire, water, earth, and air), the term "elemental" here refers to the fundamental energies that underpin the very fabric of the universe. From the mysterious forces of gravity and dark energy, to the illuminating power of light, the chilling grip of null point energy, the electrifying surge of electron concentrations, the blazing heat of fire, and the life-giving vitality of chemical energy - these energies are intrinsic to the existence of the cosmos itself, hence earning the moniker of elemental energy. 

  

The Six Lores 

Spells that rely on the same element are part of the same lore. For example, the lore of fire includes all spells that work by generating pure heat. 

Most mages focus their efforts on learning a single lore. Channeling energy is difficult to learn, but not very powerful on its own. Building on this knowledge by learning ways to form and channel more powerful or different types of the same energy provides a larger increase of power than learning how to channel it. Of course, knowing spells of different lores makes a caster more versatile, so some still choose to expand beyond one lore. Especially if they've already mastered their first one. 

Learning a lore is a commitment, as it not only demands learning mental exercises. While the soul draws the energy, it is the body that channels it. Every technique is enhanced by a certain ability of the caster.  

For example, the lore of force is based on forceful movements. Being strong and athletic (having a high Strength score) makes casting these spells easier.  

The lore of light requires very precise and fast movements. Quick reactions (high Dexterity) makes casting these spells easier and more efficient.  

The lore of frost is taxing on the body. Drawing the cold out of the Void is made easier by having a stalwart physique (high Constitution).  

The lore of lightning follows specific paths when summoned and wielded. These patterns are complicated but predictable. They can be understood with logic and the greater a caster's understanding of them is (high Intelligence), the more potent and efficient their spells become.  

Fire on the other hand is unpredictable. When drawn and wielded it its fickle nature needs to be paid constant heed. Those who have the ability to go with the flames and adjust their approach as needed (have high Wisdom) will have an easier time casting fire spells.  

The lore of life is not created easily and has to be forced into the world. A lot of willpower (high Charisma) makes this process easier.

Novice (8) 

Thursday, January 13, 2022 

5:05 PM 

Novice 

Access to novice force spells grants you the abilities described under Channel Force I and allows you to learn the following spells. 

 

Channel Force I 

The first step to learning the lore of force is learning to convert vitality into pure kinetic energy. At its simplest, this ability allows you to increase your own strength and expel force from your body to slow a descent. More advanced techniques let you expel it from your body in shockwaves. 

 

Force. At a negligible cost to your vitality you may use your action to magically increase your physical power until you lose concentration. If you are a medium or smaller creature, you make yourself count as one size bigger than you are in regards to wielding weapons, grappling, or carrying objects and have +5 on Strength checks. 

You can also use this increased strength to attack, although doing so ends the effect. When you cast this spell, or as an action on a later turn, you strike a creature with magical might. Make an attack with a melee or thrown weapon or a melee spell attack. On a hit, the attack deals an additional 1d6 damage or 1d12 force damage if it's a spell attack.  

This spell’s damage increases by 1d6 for a weapon attack and 1d12 for a spell attack when you reach 5th level (2d6 and 2d12) and 10th level (3d6 and 3d12). 

Crush. As an action you may spend up to an amount of vitality equal to your Proficiency Bonus to crush (or tear apart) a creature you are grappling, dealing 10 force damage for every vitality spent. 

Feather Fall. As a reaction to falling, you may expend 2 vitality. The rate of your descent slows to 20 meters per round for the next minute. If the you land before the spell ends, you take no fall damage and can land on your feet, and the effect ends. 

Leap. As a bonus action you may spend up to an amount of vitality equal to your Proficiency Bonus to leap up to 5 meters in any direction for each vitality expended. 

 

 

 

Modulate Mass. As a reaction, or as a bonus action on your turn, you may spend 2 vitality per minute and maintain concentration to double or halve your weight. Increase the cost of this ability by 6 vitality every time you double or halve it again as part of the same reaction or bonus action.  

The fall damage you take is multiplied by the same factor as your weight. The height and distance you can jump is instead divided by the amount your weight is multiplied by (the height and distance you can jump is doubled if your weight is halved). 

If your weight is doubled, you gain advantage on saving throws against being knocked prone or moved against your will. 

 

Spider Climb. As a bonus action you may start concentrating and expend 1 vitality per turn. For the duration, you gain the ability to move up, down, and across vertical surfaces and upside down along ceilings, while leaving your hands free. You also gain a climbing speed equal to your walking speed. This ability is subtle and silent. 

 

 

 

Conflicting change.

 Apprentice (6) 

Thursday, January 13, 2022 

9:30 PM 

Apprentice 

Access to apprentice force spells grants you the abilities described under Channel Force II and allows you to learn the following spells. 

 

Channel Force II 

As you become an apprentice, you gain the ability to produce sustained streams of force with greatly increased efficiency and more importantly modulate these streams precisely. At its simplest this allows you to lift your body from the ground in flight and more specialized techniques can make use of this ability to create sound-based effects. 

 

Fly. As a bonus action you may begin concentrating on this spell for 1 vitality per minute. As long as you maintain concentration, you have a flying speed of 20 meters and can hover. If you already have a flying speed, increase it by 10 meters instead. This spell is silent. For an additional 3 vitality per minute you may carry an extra medium creature or equivalent weight. 

 

Conflicting change.

Adept (6) 

Thursday, January 13, 2022 

10:18 PM 

Adept 

Access to adept force spells grants you the ability described under Channel Force III and allows you to learn the following spells. 

 

Channel Force III 

As an adept, you learn how to unleash very quick, stable bursts of force. At its simplest this allows you to move with extreme speed and with some skill you may form cutting edges of pure force. 

 

Blink. As a bonus action or a reaction, you expend 8 vitality to move almost instantly in a straight line to an unoccupied space no more than 10 meters away, provoking no attacks of opportunity and ignoring difficult terrain. If there is an obstacle between you and your destination, you slam into it, dealing 1d6 force damage both to it and yourself. 

If you blink as a reaction to a creature you can see targeting you with an attack, the triggering attack is made with disadvantage. 

If you blink as a reaction to a creature in range other than you being attacked, you may blink between the attacker and the target, forcing the attack to target you instead.  

In both cases you must take the reaction before the creature makes its attack roll. 

If you blink as a reaction to a creature you can see targeting you with an area of effect ability, you avoid being targeted if the blink moves you out of the area of effect. If the effect offers a saving throw, you must take the reaction before you roll. 

Conflicting change.

Expert (6) 

Thursday, January 13, 2022 

10:18 PM 

Expert 

Access to expert force spells grants you the ability described under Channel Force IV and allows you to learn the following spells. 

 

Channel Force IV 

As you become an expert at creating and manipulating force, you gain the ability to project it as precise telekinesis and use that telekinesis to create force fields. 

 

Telekinesis. As an action, target a creature or object you can see no more than 40 meters away and concentrate on this spell. 

Creature: If you target a medium creature, spend 4 vitality per minute. This cost is doubled for large creatures and halved for every size it is smaller than medium (4 for small, 2 for tiny). The target must succeed a Strength saving throw or be put under your telekinetic control and restrained for the duration. It may repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on a success. 

Object: If you target an object, expend 1 vitality per minute for every 20 kilograms the item weighs. If the object isn't being worn or carried, you gain telekinetic control over it. If the object is being worn or carried by a creature, that creature must succeed a Strength saving throw or have the object pulled out of its grip and put under your control. 

Controlling a Target: As a bonus action you may move a creature or object under your telekinetic control up to 10 meters in any direction. You can exert fine control on objects with your telekinetic grip, such as manipulating a simple tool, opening a door or a container, stowing or retrieving an item from an open container, or pouring the contents from a vial. 

Conflicting change.

Master (6) 

Friday, January 14, 2022 

12:13 AM 

Master 

Access to master force spells grants you the abilities described under Channel Force V and allows you to learn the following spells. 

 

Channel Force V 

As a master of force magic, you finally gain control over the source of force. By directly manipulating gravitons in an area and control gravity itself. Furthermore, you gain the ability to telekinetically manipulate more than one object with extreme precision. 

 

As an action you animate objects in range that you can see and that are not being worn or carried. Pay 1 vitality per turn for every object you animate. Medium targets count as two objects, large targets count as four objects, huge targets count as eight objects. You can't animate any object larger than huge. Each target animates and becomes a creature under your control until the spell ends, it leaves the range of the spell, or until reduced to 0 hit points. 

Animated objects take their turn with you. As a bonus action, you can mentally command any creature you made with this spell (if you control multiple creatures, you can command any or all of them at the same time). You decide what action the creature will take and where it will move. If you issue no commands, the creature simply hovers in mid-air. 

 

Animated Object Statistics

An animated object is a construct with AC, hit points, attacks, Strength, and Dexterity determined by its size. See the Animated Object Statistics table for details. Its Constitution is 10 and its Intelligence, Wisdom, and its Charisma are 0. The objects have a flying speed of 10 meters and can hover. If the object is securely attached to a surface or larger object, such as a chain bolted to a wall, its speed is 0. The objects are blind and rely on your senses to be guided. When the animated object drops to 0 hit points, it breaks and the magic keeping it animate fades. 

If you command an object to attack, it can make a single melee attack against a creature within 1 meter of it. It makes a slam attack with an attack bonus and bludgeoning damage determine by its size. The GM might rule that a specific object inflicts slashing or piercing damage based on its form. 

 

 

 

Conflicting change.

Monday, January 17, 2022 

2:20 PM 

Freeze Armament 

Apprentice frost 

 

Vitality Cost: 2 

Casting Time: Reaction 

Range: Self 

Duration: 1 Hour 

 

Upon being struck, you release a burst of frost that renders the attacker’s weapon useless. 

 

As a reaction to being hit with a melee attack you may coat the weapon used in ice, making it unwieldy. The attacking creature has disadvantage on all future attacks and deals half damage with the frozen weapon (or body part, in the case of unarmed or natural weapon attacks) for the duration.  

A creature may use its action to break the ice off the weapon, ending the spell. 

 

 

Old Name: Abate 

Conflicting change.

Novice (8) 

Thursday, January 13, 2022 

5:05 PM 

Novice 

Access to novice force spells grants you the abilities described under Channel Force I and allows you to learn the following spells. 

 

Channel Force I 

The first step to learning the lore of force is learning to convert vitality into pure kinetic energy. At its simplest, this ability allows you to increase your own strength and expel force from your body to slow a descent. More advanced techniques let you expel it from your body in shockwaves. 

 

Force. At a negligible cost to your vitality you may use your action to magically increase your physical power until you lose concentration. If you are a medium or smaller creature, you make yourself count as one size bigger than you are in regards to wielding weapons, grappling, or carrying objects and have +5 on Strength checks. 

You can also use this increased strength to attack, although doing so ends the effect. When you cast this spell, or as an action on a later turn, you strike a creature with magical might. Make an attack with a melee or thrown weapon or a melee spell attack. On a hit, the attack deals an additional 1d6 damage or 1d12 force damage if it's a spell attack.  

This spell’s damage increases by 1d6 for a weapon attack and 1d12 for a spell attack when you reach 5th level (2d6 and 2d12) and 10th level (3d6 and 3d12). 

Crush. As an action you may spend up to an amount of vitality equal to your Proficiency Bonus to crush (or tear apart) a creature you are grappling, dealing 10 force damage for every vitality spent. 

Feather Fall. As a reaction to falling, you may expend 2 vitality. The rate of your descent slows to 20 meters per round for the next minute. If the you land before the spell ends, you take no fall damage and can land on your feet, and the effect ends. 

Leap. As a bonus action you may spend up to an amount of vitality equal to your Proficiency Bonus to leap up to 5 meters in any direction for each vitality expended. 

 

 

 

Modulate Mass. As a reaction, or as a bonus action on your turn, you may spend 2 vitality per minute and maintain concentration to double or halve your weight. Increase the cost of this ability by 6 vitality every time you double or halve it again as part of the same reaction or bonus action.  

The fall damage you take is multiplied by the same factor as your weight. The height and distance you can jump is instead divided by the amount your weight is multiplied by (the height and distance you can jump is doubled if your weight is halved). 

If your weight is doubled, you gain advantage on saving throws against being knocked prone or moved against your will. 

 

Spider Climb. As a bonus action you may start concentrating and expend 1 vitality per turn. For the duration, you gain the ability to move up, down, and across vertical surfaces and upside down along ceilings, while leaving your hands free. You also gain a climbing speed equal to your walking speed. This ability is subtle and silent. 

 

 

 

Conflicting change.

Thursday, January 13, 2022 

8:44 AM 

Counter Strike 

Novice lightning 

 

Vitality Cost: 6 

Casting Time: Reaction 

Range: 10 Meters 

Duration: Instant 

 

With a flick of your arm you snip across your ring, middle and index finger to point your thumb at a foe. A short flash of blue leaps to your foe, disrupting their actions.  

 

You may cast this spell as a reaction to a creature you can see within range taking a non-subtle action (such as casting a spell, activating a maneuver, using a bonus action, or a reaction). Make a melee or ranged spell attack. On a hit, the target loses the triggering action. If the action was a spell, maneuver, or other vitality-consuming ability, it fizzles on a hit and the vitality is wasted. If the triggering action was an attack action, the spell counters only the triggering attack.  

This spell has no effect on a creature immune to the shocked or stunned conditions.

SoaNW Player's Handbook

Thursday, January 13, 2022 

8:44 AM 

Gathering Storm 

Novice lightning 

 

Vitality Cost: 2 per Turn 

Casting Time: 1 Bonus Action 

Range: Self 

Duration: Concentration 

 

You clap your palms together. As you pull them apart, lightning begins to charge in your hands. More and more electricity crackles over your body, enhancing your spells until every cast is a storm. 

 

For the duration, whenever you cast a spell that deals lightning damage, the first time in a turn that spell deals damage add an additional 1d10 lightning damage. At the start of each of your turns, increase this bonus damage by 1d10 up to a maximum of 3d10. 

If a spell deals damage that is first rolled and then divided among targets, as with the Static spell, this effect increases the damage when it is initially rolled, before being divided. If the spell reduces the targets current hit points, as with the Disintegration Beam spell, the amount its hit points are reduced is increased by the lightning damage this effect would deal. 

You are revealed while concentrating on this spell.

 Apprentice (6) 

Thursday, January 13, 2022 

9:30 PM 

Apprentice 

Access to apprentice force spells grants you the abilities described under Channel Force II and allows you to learn the following spells. 

 

Channel Force II 

As you become an apprentice, you gain the ability to produce sustained streams of force with greatly increased efficiency and more importantly modulate these streams precisely. At its simplest this allows you to lift your body from the ground in flight and more specialized techniques can make use of this ability to create sound-based effects. 

 

Fly. As a bonus action you may begin concentrating on this spell for 1 vitality per minute. As long as you maintain concentration, you have a flying speed of 20 meters and can hover. If you already have a flying speed, increase it by 10 meters instead. This spell is silent. For an additional 3 vitality per minute you may carry an extra medium creature or equivalent weight. 

 

Conflicting change.

SoaNW Player's Handbook

G
File
Home

Viewing

Styles
Tags

 

Thursday, January 27, 2022 

12:39 PM 

Boil Blood 

Apprentice lightning 

 

Vitality Cost: 2 per Turn 

Casting Time: 1 Action 

Range: 20 Meters 

Duration: Concentration 

 

You open your hand towards a foe. Only tiny lightning bolts are visible on your hand as you project invisible microwaves at them. Their body begins to heat up, subtly at first but soon their very blood begins to boil and their flesh starts to sizzle. 

 

Target creature in range takes 1d8 fire damage at the end of each of your turns. The damage of this spell then increases by 1d8 fire damage for the duration up to a maximum of 5d8 fire damage. The spell ends if the target is ever outside the spell’s range or if it has total cover from you. 

 

Upcast: Spend an additional 3 vitality per turn to increase the damage the spell deals by 1d8. This does not increase the amount by which the damage increases.

Adept (6) 

Thursday, January 13, 2022 

10:18 PM 

Adept 

Access to adept force spells grants you the ability described under Channel Force III and allows you to learn the following spells. 

 

Channel Force III 

As an adept, you learn how to unleash very quick, stable bursts of force. At its simplest this allows you to move with extreme speed and with some skill you may form cutting edges of pure force. 

 

Blink. As a bonus action or a reaction, you expend 8 vitality to move almost instantly in a straight line to an unoccupied space no more than 10 meters away, provoking no attacks of opportunity and ignoring difficult terrain. If there is an obstacle between you and your destination, you slam into it, dealing 1d6 force damage both to it and yourself. 

If you blink as a reaction to a creature you can see targeting you with an attack, the triggering attack is made with disadvantage. 

If you blink as a reaction to a creature in range other than you being attacked, you may blink between the attacker and the target, forcing the attack to target you instead.  

In both cases you must take the reaction before the creature makes its attack roll. 

If you blink as a reaction to a creature you can see targeting you with an area of effect ability, you avoid being targeted if the blink moves you out of the area of effect. If the effect offers a saving throw, you must take the reaction before you roll. 

Conflicting change.
