Thursday, January 13, 2022 

8:44 AM 

Lightning Lance 

Adept lightning 

 

Vitality Cost: 7 per Turn 

Casting Time: 1 Action 

Range: 40 Meters 

Duration: Concentration 

 

You slowly pull your hands apart. As electric energy hums and sparks over them, you slam them together. A beam of bright blue is unleashed that fries your foes. 

 

When you cast this spell, make up to two ranged spell attacks against targets in range. Attacks with this spell have advantage if you targeted the target with this spell since the start of your last turn. For the duration, you can use your action to make up to four such attacks. On a hit, a target takes 2d10 lightning damage and is shocked. 

 

Upcast: Spend an additional 3 vitality to increase the damage dealt by 1d10. 

 

 

 

 

Thursday, January 13, 2022 

8:44 AM 

Lightning Lance 

Adept lightning 

 

Vitality Cost: 7 per Turn 

Casting Time: 1 Action 

Range: 40 Meters 

Duration: Concentration 

 

You slowly pull your hands apart. As electric energy hums and sparks over them, you slam them together. A beam of bright blue is unleashed that fries your foes. 

 

When you cast this spell, make up to two ranged spell attacks against targets in range. Attacks with this spell have advantage if you targeted the target with this spell since the start of your last turn. For the duration, you can use your action to make up to four such attacks. On a hit, a target takes 2d10 lightning damage and is shocked. 

 

Upcast: Spend an additional 3 vitality to increase the damage dealt by 1d10. 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Target each creature in a 100-meter long and 1-meter wide line starting from you must make a Constitution saving throw. On a fail, they take 3d10 lightning damage and are shocked. On a success, they take half as much damage and are not shocked. 

If a creature in the bolt’s path is wearing metal armor and fails the saving throw, the line ends at the creature and its armor explodes in a shower of sparks, dealing an additional 2d10 fire damage to them. 

Conflicting change.

Expert (6) 

Thursday, January 13, 2022 

10:18 PM 

Expert 

Access to expert force spells grants you the ability described under Channel Force IV and allows you to learn the following spells. 

 

Channel Force IV 

As you become an expert at creating and manipulating force, you gain the ability to project it as precise telekinesis and use that telekinesis to create force fields. 

 

Telekinesis. As an action, target a creature or object you can see no more than 40 meters away and concentrate on this spell. 

Creature: If you target a medium creature, spend 4 vitality per minute. This cost is doubled for large creatures and halved for every size it is smaller than medium (4 for small, 2 for tiny). The target must succeed a Strength saving throw or be put under your telekinetic control and restrained for the duration. It may repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on a success. 

Object: If you target an object, expend 1 vitality per minute for every 20 kilograms the item weighs. If the object isn't being worn or carried, you gain telekinetic control over it. If the object is being worn or carried by a creature, that creature must succeed a Strength saving throw or have the object pulled out of its grip and put under your control. 

Controlling a Target: As a bonus action you may move a creature or object under your telekinetic control up to 10 meters in any direction. You can exert fine control on objects with your telekinetic grip, such as manipulating a simple tool, opening a door or a container, stowing or retrieving an item from an open container, or pouring the contents from a vial. 

Conflicting change.

SoaNW Player's Handbook


G

Thursday, January 13, 2022 

8:44 AM 

Shocking Stiletto 

Expert lightning 

 

Vitality Cost: 10 

Casting Time: 1 Action 

Range: 40 Meters 

Duration: 1 Minute 

 

Electric energy crackles over your fist and forms into a bolt of crackling blue energy. It hums and screeches as it is hurled at an opponent, embedding itself in their body and electrocuting them continuously. 

 

Make a ranged spell attack against a target in range. On a hit, the lightning embeds itself into the target's body. At the start of each of its turns for the duration, the target must succeed a Constitution saving throw at the start of each of its turns, or take 1d10 lightning damage and be shocked and if it was shocked, it is also stunned until the end of its next turn. 

 

 

 

 

-------------------- 

Upcast: Spend an additional 10 vitality to increase the damage dealt by an additional 1d10 lightning damage.

Thursday, January 13, 2022 

8:44 AM 

Storm Sphere 

Expert lightning 

 

Vitality Cost: 6 per Turn 

Casting Time: 1 Action 

Range: 20 meters (1-meter sphere) 

Duration: Concentration 

 

Slam your hands together. As you pull them apart a ball of lightning forms between them. Cast this sphere of death forth and watch it shock your enemies. 

 

A 1-meter diameter sphere of pulsating lightning appears in an unoccupied space in range and lasts for the duration. Any creature that ends its turn within 5 meters of the sphere takes 5d10 lightning damage. 

As a bonus action, you can move the sphere up to 20 meters. If you ram the sphere into a creature, that creature must succeed a Dexterity saving throw or take damage as though it had ended its turn within the sphere's area of effect, and the sphere stops moving this turn. The sphere hovers, and you may move it in any direction. 

 

Upcast: Spend an additional 4 vitality to increase the damage dealt by an additional 1d10 lightning damage.

Master (6) 

Friday, January 14, 2022 

12:13 AM 

Master 

Access to master force spells grants you the abilities described under Channel Force V and allows you to learn the following spells. 

 

Channel Force V 

As a master of force magic, you finally gain control over the source of force. By directly manipulating gravitons in an area and control gravity itself. Furthermore, you gain the ability to telekinetically manipulate more than one object with extreme precision. 

 

As an action you animate objects in range that you can see and that are not being worn or carried. Pay 1 vitality per turn for every object you animate. Medium targets count as two objects, large targets count as four objects, huge targets count as eight objects. You can't animate any object larger than huge. Each target animates and becomes a creature under your control until the spell ends, it leaves the range of the spell, or until reduced to 0 hit points. 

Animated objects take their turn with you. As a bonus action, you can mentally command any creature you made with this spell (if you control multiple creatures, you can command any or all of them at the same time). You decide what action the creature will take and where it will move. If you issue no commands, the creature simply hovers in mid-air. 

 

Animated Object Statistics

An animated object is a construct with AC, hit points, attacks, Strength, and Dexterity determined by its size. See the Animated Object Statistics table for details. Its Constitution is 10 and its Intelligence, Wisdom, and its Charisma are 0. The objects have a flying speed of 10 meters and can hover. If the object is securely attached to a surface or larger object, such as a chain bolted to a wall, its speed is 0. The objects are blind and rely on your senses to be guided. When the animated object drops to 0 hit points, it breaks and the magic keeping it animate fades. 

If you command an object to attack, it can make a single melee attack against a creature within 1 meter of it. It makes a slam attack with an attack bonus and bludgeoning damage determine by its size. The GM might rule that a specific object inflicts slashing or piercing damage based on its form. 

 

 

 

Conflicting change.

Thursday, January 13, 2022 

8:44 AM 

Final Disintegration 

Master lightning 

 

Vitality Cost: Suicide 

Casting Time: 2 Actions 

Range: Self (100-meter radius) 

Duration: Instant 

 

You lift your hands towards the sky, and lightning crackles and dances across your body, disintegrating your flesh. In a final, resounding thunderclap, your body erupts into a blinding blue nova that sweeps across the land. The world screeches as steel, bone, and stone dissolve into the blue. When the light fades, all that remains is a gentle fall of fine dust. 

 

When you use your second action to complete the spell, your body disintegrates in an explosion of blue-white. Every creature in a 500-meter radius is instantly disintegrated. 

In a 1-kilometer radius all buildings are collapsed and all creatures in the area have their current hit points reduced by 3d10 + 50. A creature is disintegrated if this leaves it with 0 hit points. 

In a 100-kilometer radius all electronic devices are destroyed.

pages.text:
SoaNW Player's Handbook


G

Wednesday, January 19, 2022 

8:59 AM 

Eye of the Storm 

Master lightning 

 

Vitality Cost: 10 per Turn 

Casting Time: 1 Action 

Range: Self (see description) 

Duration: Concentration 

 

Your entire body tenses. Your hair rises, standing on end and crackling with static. A blue aura of lightning surrounds your body as the sheer might of the electric energy you summon lifts you off the ground. Waves of blue energy and bolts of lightning explode away from you, shrieking and thundering in a raging storm. 

 

For the duration of the spell, you gain the following benefits: 

Charged Body. You gain immunity to lightning damage and have resistance to thunder damage. You float 3- meters above the ground, ignore difficult terrain, and have a speed of 20 meters. You shed bright light in a 3-meter radius and dim light for an additional 3 meters. Any creature of your choice that moves within 5 meters of you for the first time on its turn or ends ist turn there must succeed a Dexterity saving throw or take 2d10 lightning damage and be shocked. 

Magnetic Field. Electronic devices in a 100-meter radius are destroyed. Metal objects in a 20 meter radius sphere centered on you begin to heat up. Any creature in physical contact with a metal object takes 1d8 fire damage at the start of its turn for every metal object it is in contact with. If a creature is holding or wearing the object and takes damage from it, the creature must succeed on a Constitution saving throw or drop the object if it can. If it doesn’t drop the object, it has disadvantage on attack rolls and ability checks until the start of its turn and takes an additional 1d8 fire damage at the end of its turn. Loose grenades or pieces of ammunition instantly explode. 

Eye of the Storm. You can use your action to send three arcs of lightning at up to three targets. Make ranged spell attacks against up to three targets within 20 meters of you. On a hit, they deal 2d10 lightning damage and form a tether of crackling electric energy to you. On each of your turns for the rest of the duration, you can use your action to deal 2d10 lightning damage to every tethered target. If a tether breaks, you may make a new attack against another target within range as part of your action if you still have other active tethers. The tethers disconnect if you use your action to do anything else. A tether also breaks if a target gets behind full cover, moves more than 20 meters away, or falls to 0 hit points.

Thursday, January 13, 2022 

8:44 AM 

Wall of Disintegration 

Expert lightning 

 

Vitality Cost: 20 

Casting Time: 1 Action 

Range: 20 meters (see description) 

Duration: 1 minute 

 

You move a finger down in front of your face. Your other hand snaps to the side and you fire a sphere of electric energy from your fingertips. Upon impact, it expands, into a wall of electric energy. Whatever touches the wall simply evaporates with a screeching noise, leaving nothing but a bit of grey dust and smoke. 

 

A wall of translucent blue energy springs into existence at a point you choose within range. The wall appears in any orientation you choose, as a horizontal or vertical barrier or at an angle. It can be free-floating or resting on a solid surface. You can form it into a hemispherical dome or a sphere with a radius of up to 3 meters, or you can shape a flat 5-meter-by-5-meter surface. Regardless of its form, the wall is 1 millimeter thick. It lasts for the duration. If the wall cuts through a creature’s space when it appears, the creature must succeed a Dexterity saving throw, or be affected by the wall as though it ended its turn inside it. 

A creature or object that passes through the wall or ends its turn in its space has its current hit points reduced by 2d10 + 40. The target is disintegrated if this leaves it with 0 hit points. A disintegrated creature and everything it is wearing and carrying is turned into fine grey dust. 

If a projectile would have to pass through the wall, it is disintegrated. Magical projectiles are dispelled, but area-of-effects such as explosions pass through the barrier unimpeded. 

 

Upcast: Spend an additional 4 vitality to decrease a target's hit points by another 1d10.

Novice (8) 

Thursday, January 13, 2022 

5:05 PM 

Novice 

Access to novice force spells grants you the abilities described under Channel Force I and allows you to learn the following spells. 

 

Channel Force I 

The first step to learning the lore of force is learning to convert vitality into pure kinetic energy. At its simplest, this ability allows you to increase your own strength and expel force from your body to slow a descent. More advanced techniques let you expel it from your body in shockwaves. 

 

Force. At a negligible cost to your vitality you may use your action to magically increase your physical power until you lose concentration. If you are a medium or smaller creature, you make yourself count as one size bigger than you are in regards to wielding weapons, grappling, or carrying objects and have +5 on Strength checks. 

You can also use this increased strength to attack, although doing so ends the effect. When you cast this spell, or as an action on a later turn, you strike a creature with magical might. Make an attack with a melee or thrown weapon or a melee spell attack. On a hit, the attack deals an additional 1d6 damage or 1d12 force damage if it's a spell attack.  

This spell’s damage increases by 1d6 for a weapon attack and 1d12 for a spell attack when you reach 5th level (2d6 and 2d12) and 10th level (3d6 and 3d12). 

Crush. As an action you may spend up to an amount of vitality equal to your Proficiency Bonus to crush (or tear apart) a creature you are grappling, dealing 10 force damage for every vitality spent. 

Feather Fall. As a reaction to falling, you may expend 2 vitality. The rate of your descent slows to 20 meters per round for the next minute. If the you land before the spell ends, you take no fall damage and can land on your feet, and the effect ends. 

Leap. As a bonus action you may spend up to an amount of vitality equal to your Proficiency Bonus to leap up to 5 meters in any direction for each vitality expended. 

 

 

 

Modulate Mass. As a reaction, or as a bonus action on your turn, you may spend 2 vitality per minute and maintain concentration to double or halve your weight. Increase the cost of this ability by 6 vitality every time you double or halve it again as part of the same reaction or bonus action.  

The fall damage you take is multiplied by the same factor as your weight. The height and distance you can jump is instead divided by the amount your weight is multiplied by (the height and distance you can jump is doubled if your weight is halved). 

If your weight is doubled, you gain advantage on saving throws against being knocked prone or moved against your will. 

 

Spider Climb. As a bonus action you may start concentrating and expend 1 vitality per turn. For the duration, you gain the ability to move up, down, and across vertical surfaces and upside down along ceilings, while leaving your hands free. You also gain a climbing speed equal to your walking speed. This ability is subtle and silent. 

 

 

 

Conflicting change.

 Apprentice (6) 

Thursday, January 13, 2022 

9:30 PM 

Apprentice 

Access to apprentice force spells grants you the abilities described under Channel Force II and allows you to learn the following spells. 

 

Channel Force II 

As you become an apprentice, you gain the ability to produce sustained streams of force with greatly increased efficiency and more importantly modulate these streams precisely. At its simplest this allows you to lift your body from the ground in flight and more specialized techniques can make use of this ability to create sound-based effects. 

 

Fly. As a bonus action you may begin concentrating on this spell for 1 vitality per minute. As long as you maintain concentration, you have a flying speed of 20 meters and can hover. If you already have a flying speed, increase it by 10 meters instead. This spell is silent. For an additional 3 vitality per minute you may carry an extra medium creature or equivalent weight. 

 

Conflicting change.

SoaNW Player's Handbook

Adept (6) 

Thursday, January 13, 2022 

10:18 PM 

Adept 

Access to adept force spells grants you the ability described under Channel Force III and allows you to learn the following spells. 

 

Channel Force III 

As an adept, you learn how to unleash very quick, stable bursts of force. At its simplest this allows you to move with extreme speed and with some skill you may form cutting edges of pure force. 

 

Blink. As a bonus action or a reaction, you expend 8 vitality to move almost instantly in a straight line to an unoccupied space no more than 10 meters away, provoking no attacks of opportunity and ignoring difficult terrain. If there is an obstacle between you and your destination, you slam into it, dealing 1d6 force damage both to it and yourself. 

If you blink as a reaction to a creature you can see targeting you with an attack, the triggering attack is made with disadvantage. 

If you blink as a reaction to a creature in range other than you being attacked, you may blink between the attacker and the target, forcing the attack to target you instead.  

In both cases you must take the reaction before the creature makes its attack roll. 

If you blink as a reaction to a creature you can see targeting you with an area of effect ability, you avoid being targeted if the blink moves you out of the area of effect. If the effect offers a saving throw, you must take the reaction before you roll. 

Conflicting change.

Expert (6) 

Thursday, January 13, 2022 

10:18 PM 

Expert 

Access to expert force spells grants you the ability described under Channel Force IV and allows you to learn the following spells. 

 

Channel Force IV 

As you become an expert at creating and manipulating force, you gain the ability to project it as precise telekinesis and use that telekinesis to create force fields. 

 

Telekinesis. As an action, target a creature or object you can see no more than 40 meters away and concentrate on this spell. 

Creature: If you target a medium creature, spend 4 vitality per minute. This cost is doubled for large creatures and halved for every size it is smaller than medium (4 for small, 2 for tiny). The target must succeed a Strength saving throw or be put under your telekinetic control and restrained for the duration. It may repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on a success. 

Object: If you target an object, expend 1 vitality per minute for every 20 kilograms the item weighs. If the object isn't being worn or carried, you gain telekinetic control over it. If the object is being worn or carried by a creature, that creature must succeed a Strength saving throw or have the object pulled out of its grip and put under your control. 

Controlling a Target: As a bonus action you may move a creature or object under your telekinetic control up to 10 meters in any direction. You can exert fine control on objects with your telekinetic grip, such as manipulating a simple tool, opening a door or a container, stowing or retrieving an item from an open container, or pouring the contents from a vial. 

Conflicting change.

Monday, January 17, 2022 

2:20 PM 

Frostburst 

Expert frost 

 

Vitality Cost: 15 

Casting Time: 1 Action 

Range: 100 meters (20-meter radius) 

Duration: Instant 

 

You pull your hand back over your casually extended arm. Air swirls together to form a shard of true ice and glittering white mist. Upon opening your frost layered hand, it flies off, leaving a white trail. Upon impact it explodes with a snap, causing spikes of dry ice to grow in every direction. 

 

Target a point in range. Every creature in a 20-meter sphere centered on that point must make a Dexterity saving throw. A creature takes 8d6 cold damage, is chilled and frozen on a failure. It is dealt half as much damage and only chilled on a success. 

The area of effect is frozen and covered in sharp icicles, making it difficult terrain for 1 hour. Any creature that moves through it must succeed a Dexterity saving throw, or fall prone. Any creature that falls prone on affected ground or is forcefully moved into or over an affected surface takes 1d6 piercing damage. 

 

Upcast: Spend an additional 10 vitality to increase the damage by 1d6 and the radius by 5 meters.

Master (6) 

Friday, January 14, 2022 

12:13 AM 

Master 

Access to master force spells grants you the abilities described under Channel Force V and allows you to learn the following spells. 

 

Channel Force V 

As a master of force magic, you finally gain control over the source of force. By directly manipulating gravitons in an area and control gravity itself. Furthermore, you gain the ability to telekinetically manipulate more than one object with extreme precision. 

 

As an action you animate objects in range that you can see and that are not being worn or carried. Pay 1 vitality per turn for every object you animate. Medium targets count as two objects, large targets count as four objects, huge targets count as eight objects. You can't animate any object larger than huge. Each target animates and becomes a creature under your control until the spell ends, it leaves the range of the spell, or until reduced to 0 hit points. 

Animated objects take their turn with you. As a bonus action, you can mentally command any creature you made with this spell (if you control multiple creatures, you can command any or all of them at the same time). You decide what action the creature will take and where it will move. If you issue no commands, the creature simply hovers in mid-air. 

 

Animated Object Statistics

An animated object is a construct with AC, hit points, attacks, Strength, and Dexterity determined by its size. See the Animated Object Statistics table for details. Its Constitution is 10 and its Intelligence, Wisdom, and its Charisma are 0. The objects have a flying speed of 10 meters and can hover. If the object is securely attached to a surface or larger object, such as a chain bolted to a wall, its speed is 0. The objects are blind and rely on your senses to be guided. When the animated object drops to 0 hit points, it breaks and the magic keeping it animate fades. 

If you command an object to attack, it can make a single melee attack against a creature within 1 meter of it. It makes a slam attack with an attack bonus and bludgeoning damage determine by its size. The GM might rule that a specific object inflicts slashing or piercing damage based on its form.

Novice (10) 

Thursday, January 13, 2022 

5:05 PM 

Novice- 

Access to novice light spells grants you the abilities described under Channel Light I and allows you to learn the following spells. 

 

Channel Light I 

The first step in learning the lore of light is learning to convert your vitality into alterations to matter. Unlike other elements, light is produced indirectly by altering quantum properties of materials to emit or absorb photons. This makes light uniquely easy to summon, manipulate, or erase. In bright conditions, it’s easier to generate; in darkness, illusions are more stable. At its simplest, this allows you to unleash intense, multi-spectrum beams, alter appearances, and disrupt illusions with specially modulated light. 

 

Light. At a negligible cost to your vitality you may use your action to sheath your hand in light. You shed bright light in a 10-meter radius and dim light for an additional 10 meters. You may dismiss the light at any time (no action required). 

You can also attack with the light, although doing so ends the effect. When you cast this spell, or as an action on a later turn, you target a creature you can see and fire a beam of light into it. The target takes 1d4+1 radiant damage  and has its maximum hit points reduced by an amount equal to the radiant damage taken until it finishes a long rest. This ability is considered a spell attack. 

This spell's damage increases by 1d4 when you reach 5th level (2d4) and 10th level (3d4). 

Scope. As a bonus action, you may begin expending 1 vitality per minute to create a flat, transparent illusion no more than 1 meter in diameter that magnifies your vision up to 100 times, either as a tele- or microscope. You may place this illusion directly on your pupil. You may also cast this spell in a way that the effect acts as a scope for a weapon or spell. Whenever you make a ranged attack for the duration, double its normal and maximum range and increase your attack modifier by 2. This ability is silent and can be dismissed at any time (no action required). 

Spotlight. As an action you may start expending 1 vitality per minute to shed bright light in a 10-meter radius and dim light for an additional 10 meters. Invisible creatures and objects are visible and illusions are identified as such while in this light. 

Still Illusion. As an action, you expend vitality and touch a surface or space to imprint an image into it. The illusion lasts for 1 day for every 1 vitality you expend when creating it. If you create an image on a surface, it is 2-dimensional. Creating it in a space allows you to make it 3-dimensional. In either case, the illusion must fit into a 1-meter by 1-meter square. You can use several actions to create a larger illusion by weaving the images of multiple individual squares (each with their own vitality cost and duration) together. The image can’t create sound, light, smell, or any other sensory effect. Physical interaction with the image reveals it to be an illusion. If an object at least 10 centimeters (5 inches) in diameter moves through the illusion or it takes damage, the illusion dissolves. 

If a creature uses its action to examine the image from a distance, the creature can determine that it is an illusion with a successful Wisdom (Investigation) check against your spell save DC. 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Archive 

The first step to learning the lore of light is gaining the ability to turn your inner power, your vitality, into visual effects. Light is unique. It is created indirectly, by altering the sub-atomic quantum properties of a material, including air, causing photons to be emitted, reflected, or absorbed by something referred to as an illusion. This means that spells that want to generate light are much more efficient when used in bright environments and spells that wish to generate images or remove light are more efficient in dark environments. 

 

Simple Disguise. For a negligible cost to your vitality, you may use an action to make your skin or other body parts appear any color of your choice, including pitch black. You may change your hair, skin, or eye color and add freckles, tattoos, or other markings.  

 

 

 

The first step to learning the lore of light is gaining the ability to turn your inner power, your vitality, into light. However light is unique. It is created indirectly, by altering the sub-atomic quantum properties of a material, including air, causing photons to be emitted -or absorbed. Because of this, light is the only element that can be manipulated and annihilated just as easily as it can be summoned. The more light there is, the easier it is to excite particles into releasing it. The less light there is, the less has to be reflected and the less stress is put on the unstable quantum arrays that give illusions their look. At its simplest, this allows you to blast opponents with intense UV light, create simple alterations to a material's look, light that can be modulated to interfere with illusions and holographic lenses. 

Conflicting change.

Apprentice (7) 

Thursday, January 13, 2022 

5:05 PM 

Apprentice 

Access to apprentice light spells grants you the ability described under Channel Light II and allows you to learn the following spells. 

 

Channel Light II 

As you gain more control over the sub-atomic processes that create light and become an apprentice caster, you gain the ability to create moving illusions. By rapidly forcing the array that forms it to change, the illusion can change or react to certain conditions. You can also not just force objects to absorb, reflect or generate light, but also use energy to prevent a material from interacting with light at all, making it transparent. 

 

Moving Illusion. Using your action and a negligible amount of vitality, you may project a 2-dimensional moving or still image on a surface for as long as you maintain concentration. You may also use Minor Illusion (Channel Light I) to create moving illusions. 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Window. As an action you expend 4 vitality and touch any surface such as a door, a wall, or even a shield. An area up to 1 meter in diameter centered on the point of contact becomes transparent. You may choose whether the material becomes transparent only from your side, or from the other as well. Damaging spells cannot penetrate the transparent side, but illusions may be cast through it. The spell can see through up to 1 meter of material, but it is blocked by 30 centimeters of stone, 1 centimeter of common metal, or a thin sheet of lead.  

 

Conflicting change.

Thursday, January 13, 2022 

8:44 AM 

First Aid 

Novice life 

 

Vitality Cost: 5 

Casting Time: 1 Bonus Action 

Range: 20 meters 

Duration: Instant 

 

With a flick of the wrist, you send a tether of healing energy to an ally. It wraps around their wounds like a hundred golden threads and provides a short burst of healing. 

 

A willing creature that you can see in range expends a hit die and recovers a number of hit points equal to the amount rolled. You may expend your own hit dice to heal a creature this way. The healed creature is then cleansed of the chilled and scorched conditions.  

This spell has no effect on constructs or undead. 

 

Upcast: Spend an additional 7 vitality to expend an extra hit die. 

 

 

 

This spell can’t regenerate missing limbs or organs that wouldn't heal naturally. 

Conflicting change.

Adept (6) 

Thursday, January 13, 2022 

10:18 PM 

Adept 

Access to adept force spells grants you the ability described under Channel Force III and allows you to learn the following spells. 

 

Channel Force III 

As an adept, you learn how to unleash very quick, stable bursts of force. At its simplest this allows you to move with extreme speed and with some skill you may form cutting edges of pure force. 

 

Blink. As a bonus action or a reaction, you expend 8 vitality to move almost instantly in a straight line to an unoccupied space no more than 10 meters away, provoking no attacks of opportunity and ignoring difficult terrain. If there is an obstacle between you and your destination, you slam into it, dealing 1d6 force damage both to it and yourself. 

If you blink as a reaction to a creature you can see targeting you with an attack, the triggering attack is made with disadvantage. 

If you blink as a reaction to a creature in range other than you being attacked, you may blink between the attacker and the target, forcing the attack to target you instead.  

In both cases you must take the reaction before the creature makes its attack roll. 

If you blink as a reaction to a creature you can see targeting you with an area of effect ability, you avoid being targeted if the blink moves you out of the area of effect. If the effect offers a saving throw, you must take the reaction before you roll. 

Conflicting change.

Expert (6) 

Thursday, January 13, 2022 

10:18 PM 

Expert 

Access to expert force spells grants you the ability described under Channel Force IV and allows you to learn the following spells. 

 

Channel Force IV 

As you become an expert at creating and manipulating force, you gain the ability to project it as precise telekinesis and use that telekinesis to create force fields. 

 

Telekinesis. As an action, target a creature or object you can see no more than 40 meters away and concentrate on this spell. 

Creature: If you target a medium creature, spend 4 vitality per minute. This cost is doubled for large creatures and halved for every size it is smaller than medium (4 for small, 2 for tiny). The target must succeed a Strength saving throw or be put under your telekinetic control and restrained for the duration. It may repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on a success. 

Object: If you target an object, expend 1 vitality per minute for every 20 kilograms the item weighs. If the object isn't being worn or carried, you gain telekinetic control over it. If the object is being worn or carried by a creature, that creature must succeed a Strength saving throw or have the object pulled out of its grip and put under your control. 

Controlling a Target: As a bonus action you may move a creature or object under your telekinetic control up to 10 meters in any direction. You can exert fine control on objects with your telekinetic grip, such as manipulating a simple tool, opening a door or a container, stowing or retrieving an item from an open container, or pouring the contents from a vial. 

Conflicting change.

Monday, January 17, 2022 

2:20 PM 

Counterspell 

Adept frost 

 

Vitality Cost: 10 

Casting Time: Reaction 

Range: 20 meters 

Duration: Instant 

 

You flick a cloud of true ice at magic ready to form. The frigid spell seeks out and neutralizes the target partially. The flow of magic or power fails to stabilize and whatever remains sputters into nothingness. 

 

You may cast this spell as a reaction to a spell being cast, a magical ability being used, a magical effect being triggered, an electronic device being activated, or a firearm being fired, as long as you can see the source and the spell, ability, or effect is not subtle. The spell, ability, or effect fizzles and the vitality used to cast or activate it is wasted. An electronic device fails to activate and a firearm fails to fire, wasting the ammunition used. 

Conflicting change.

Chapter 14: Wild Magic (43) 

Tuesday, May 3, 2022 

5:59 PM 

Chapter 14: Wild Magic 

Wild magic can change the world. A caster that uses it disseminates energy into the world and manipulates it in subtle ways. The most important thing in this regard is, that wild magic cannot create. It can only change what is already there. The way it works is poorly understood, its true source a mystery. Many consider it a sort of sister school to the lore of life, as it interacts with living beings, and the chemical energy summoned by the lore of life can counter or dispel many wild magic effects. Others believe it’s a weakened mortal version of divine magic, as it has the ability to interact with the minds of living beings. However, unlike the magic of the gods, it creates no compulsions. The only conclusion is that wild magic is a different thing altogether that eludes mortal understanding of magic. 

To use wild magic, a caster must attune their body to the world around them. Some compare this process to tuning one's body like a lens to focus energy onto what one wants to achieve. This is why wild magic synergizes so well with shapeshifting. The mental practice used to attune one's body to wild magic changes the mortal form and is the same thing one must use to shapeshift or influence forceful transformations. 

  

Wild magic relies on your Wisdom score 

•The Difficulty Class of saving throws against wild spells you cast is determined as follows: 8 + your Proficiency Bonus + your Wisdom modifier.  

•If a wild spell requires an attack roll, then the modifier for that roll equals: your Proficiency Bonus + Your Wisdom modifier.  

  

Wild magic is extremely versatile, though it lacks direct firepower. Instead, it relies on summoning beasts or enhancing other attacks. Its greatest strength is its ability to divinate information from the environment. It excels at gathering intelligence, allowing its users to prepare and circumvent their spell’s lack of blunt destructive power with creative tactics and superior maneuvering. 

  

Active and Passive 

All wild spells have an active and a passive component. A wild spell's passive is an ability that the caster gains access to for as long as they have that spell prepared. It requires only minimal effort to maintain but still taxes the body slightly, represented by a reduction of the caster's maximum vitality equal to the level of the spell. 

The active component always requires conscious and intense effort from the caster to activate. The spell's vitality cost, casting time, range, and duration only apply to the spell's active ability. 

  

True Names 

A creature's true name holds significance for it. Those who know it find it far easier to connect to that creature and many wild magic spells are empowered if you know the target's true name. At its most basic a true name is the one given to a creature at its birth, or whichever name the creature uses to refer to its real self. It is a string of expressions that will identify that creature without fail. It is not enough for a caster to read the name off a passport, or know the words. Extracting the true meaning behind the noises that make up a true name requires magic. You must either hear the creature speak its own full name, hear it spoken by another creature that knows that creature intimately, or be told the name by another creature that has already obtained the creature's true name. For most modern people their true name is their full name, including any middle names and their surname. Their first name is usually not enough. 

  

Environment Based Damage 

Some wild spells draw power from the environment they are cast in to produce elemental energy. 

Whenever a spell calls for environmental damage, refer to the Environmental Damage Type table to determine the type of damage dealt. 

The type of environment is determined by the landscape surrounding the caster. The terrain underwater is just as varied as above the waves and different areas underwater provide different damage types, just like on land. The GM has final say on the damage type your spell deals. 

  

Environment Damage Type 

Environment 

	
 

Damage 




Coast 

	

Lightning 




Forest and Swamp 

	

Acid 




Plains and Hills 

	

Force 




Mountains 

	

Cold 




Underground 

	

Fire 




Urban 

	

Necrotic 




Wasteland 

	

Radiant 




  

Summoning Animals 

Many spells allow you to call on the wild for aid. These spells will cause animals from the surrounding wilderness to converge on your location and assist you. These spells work by convincing the world itself that those who you unleash your wrath upon are a danger to it. The summoned animals are compelled by your benign intentions to take your side. However, this requires that your intentions towards the summoned creatures are truly benign. The following intentions or actions will cause spells summoning beasts, fey, and monstrosities to fail, or end early: 

•Purposefully damaging or killing summoned creatures 

•Consistently disregarding the safety of summoned creatures 

•Ordering summoned creatures to commit suicide 

Your GM may determine that other actions disrupt the natural world's trust in you. Particularly egregious violations may even prevent you from ever successfully casting a spell that summons beasts, fey, or monstrosities again. 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Conflicting change.

Novice (12) 

Wednesday, February 23, 2022 

10:46 AM 

Novice 

Access to novice wild spells grants you the abilities described under Channel Wild I and allows you to learn the following spells. 

 

Channel Wild I 

The first step in wielding the power of the wild, is to attune oneself to the natural world. Nature’s hidden rhythms merge with your mind and heart, granting subtle insight and uncanny communion with nature and its children. 

 

Guidance. As a silent and subtle action you may expend a negligible amount of vitality and touch a willing creature. Once in the next minute, the target can roll a d4 and add the number rolled to one ability check of its choice. It can roll the die before or after making the ability check. The spell then ends. 

Sylvan. You can speak sylvan, the language of the wild. You understand all sentient creatures and can communicate meaning with them, even if you don't share a language or the creature cannot speak. How complicated these ideas can be depends on the intelligence of those who participate in the conversation. This form of communication works through a combination of speech, gestures, and magic. To communicate in this way, both participants must perceive each other with their own senses (sylvan cannot be recorded or transmitted electronically). As long as the participants can see each other, sylvan allows for silent communication. 

True Name. When a creature tells you its real full name, you may extract its true name. It is not enough that the creature speaks what it considers a pseudonym, or even just its first name. You know whether a you were able to extract its true name or not. You also learn the true name of any location you visit and may share it with others. A location's true name is akin to a set of coordinates that identify the location without fail. A location's true name can only refer to a fixed position. Knowing a true name has no effect on its own, but increases the potency of many wild spells. 

 

Description Guidance 

Fynn opens his mind and to his delight feint echoes of what occurred here are revealed to him. The land whispers of sure steps and heavy boots. 

 

‐----------‐ 

Sylvan Description: 

A long grumble escapes his lips. Instinctively, magically, you all know what it means as though the growl were a sentence whispered in your ear. "Are you predator, or prey?"  

 

 

Your body attunes to the wild magic that suffuses all things and opens up to the thoughts and emotions of all living things. None know how or why, but when a living thing attempts to communicate with you, there is no language or gesture you do not interpret perfectly.

How complicated these ideas can be depends on the intelligence of those who participate in the conversation. This form of communication works through a combination of speech, gestures, and magic and is called Sylvan. You must hear and see a creature with your own senses to communicate in Sylvan. 

 

 

Novice (12) 

Wednesday, February 23, 2022 

10:46 AM 

Novice 

Access to novice wild spells grants you the abilities described under Channel Wild I and allows you to learn the following spells. 

 

Channel Wild I 

The first step in wielding the power of the wild, is to attune oneself to the natural world. Nature’s hidden rhythms merge with your mind and heart, granting subtle insight and uncanny communion with nature and its children.

Primeval Awareness. Your natural connection to the world makes your senses particularly acute while in the wilderness. You gain the following passive benefits:  

Difficult terrain doesn't slow your group's travel. 

Even when you are engaged in another activity while traveling (such as foraging, navigating, or tracking), you remain alert to danger. 

If you are traveling alone or only with creatures that know this spell, you can move stealthily at a normal pace. 

When you make a Wisdom (Survival) check to track creatures, you can treat a d20 roll of 9 or lower as a 10.

Tuesday, May 3, 2022 

9:24 AM 

Absorb Elements- 

Novice wild 

 

Vitality Cost: 3 

Casting Time: 1 Reaction 

Range: Self 

Duration: 1 Round 

 

Energy dissipates more easily in your body, making you more resistant to hot weather. With a burst of effort you becomes conductive to elemental energy, lessening its lethal effect. 

 

Active: You may cast this spell as a reaction to taking acid, cold, fire, force, lightning, radiant or thunder damage.  

You have resistance to the triggering damage type until the start of your next turn. Also, the first time you hit with a melee attack on your next turn, the target takes an extra 1d6 damage of the triggering type, and the spell ends. 

Passive: While you have this spell prepared, you treat scorching heat as though it were pleasant.

Tuesday, May 3, 2022 

9:24 AM 

Animal Friendship 

Novice wild 

 

Vitality Cost: 1 per Turn 

Casting Time: 1 Action 

Range: 20 Meters 

Duration: Concentration 

 

You have a natural heir about you that speaks to the beasts of the land. With effort you can focus this power on a creature and by instinct it is compelled to assist you. 

 

Active: As an action, choose an animal (beast, fey, or monstrosity) that you can see within range. It must see and/or hear you. If the target's Intelligence is 6 or higher, the spell fails. Otherwise, the animal must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or be charmed by you for the spell's duration. If you speak the animal's true name as part of casting the spell, it automatically fails its saving throw. 

An animal charmed this way regards you and your allies as trusted friends to be heeded and protected. It will take your requests in the most favorable way it can. If you or one of your companions harm the charmed creature, the spell ends.  

This spell is subtle and silent. 

Passive: While you have this spell prepared, you have advantage on Wisdom (Animal Handling) checks.

Tuesday, May 3, 2022 

9:24 AM 

Find Familiar 

Novice wild 

 

Vitality Cost: 5 

Casting Time: 1 Hour 

Range: Self 

Duration: Instant 

 

You call for companionship and the wild responds. An ally comes to your side, ready to assist you in upholding the balance and with them you enter into an arcane bond that makes your spirits as one. 

 

Active: You magically call forth a CR 0 beast, fey, or monstrosity native to the local area. You may call out for a specific kind of creature, such as a fox or hawk, or a random one. Your familiar arrives from the nearby wilderness. 

Your familiar is an independent creature that shares your disposition and obeys your commands to the best of its abilities. It acts as an animal companion (see Chapter 7). In combat it shares your initiative and acts on your turn. 

The GM may decide your call yields no reply if there are no animals nearby, or you previously abused your ability to summon. A familiar may decide to abandon you if it's grossly neglected or betrayed. 

Instead of calling a creature, you may instead form a bond with an animal companion that is present during the casting of this spell if you know the target's true name. There is no limit to such a creature's CR. 

You can't have more than one familiar at a time. If you cast this spell while you already have a familiar, you instead call a new familiar to your side and loose the connection to your old one. Most creatures will consider this a gross insult and betrayal. 

Passive: While you have this spell prepared, and your familiar is no more than 5 kilometers (3 miles) away, you can communicate with it telepathically. Additionally, as a bonus action, you can see through your familiar's eyes and hear what it hears until the start of your next turn, gaining the benefits of any special senses that the familiar has. During this time, you are deaf and blind with regard to your own senses.

Novice wild

Tuesday, May 3, 2022 

9:24 AM 

Stride 

Novice wild 

 

Vitality Cost: 1 per Turn 

Casting Time: 1 Bonus Action 

Range: Touch 

Duration: Concentration 

 

You empower a creatures movements and inure them against harassment. The wind strengthens their stride and their feet seem to always land on solid ground, even in the most difficult terrain. 

 

Active: As a bonus action, you touch a creature. The target’s speed increases by 10 meters, its jump distance and height are tripled and it ignores difficult terrain for the duration. This spell is subtle and silent. 

Passive: While you have this spell prepared, your speed increases by 2 meters.

Saturday, May 7, 2022 

1:06 PM 

Summon Insects 

Novice wild 

 

Vitality Cost: 5 

Casting Time: 1 Action 

Range: 20 Meters 

Duration: 1 Hour 

 

The magic suffusing your body pacifies insects and other such animals. With effort, you may summon them from the surrounding wilderness to your cause and compel them to instinctively attack your foes. 

 

Active: You summon a Swarm of Insects (see the stat block below) in a space that you can see within range. The swarm consists of a variety of dangerous tiny invertebrates native to the area. The swarm disappears when it drops to 0 hit points, when the spell ends, or after you dismiss it as a bonus action. 

The swarm is an ally to you and your companions. In combat, it shares your initiative and takes its turn with you. It obeys your verbal commands (no action required). 

The GM may decide your call yields no reply if there are no insects nearby, or you previously abused your ability to summon. 

Passive: While you have this spell prepared, tiny beasts, fey, and monstrosities with an intelligence score of 2 or less will not attack you unless provoked. 

 

Swarm of Insects 

Medium swarm of tiny creatures, unaligned  

 

Armor Class 11 

Hit Points 18 (4d8) 

Vitality - 

Speed 8m., fly 8m. 

--- 

Str: 3 (-4) 

Dex: 13 (+1) 

Con: 10 (+0) 

Int: 1 (-5) 

Wis: 7 (-2) 

Cha: 1 (-5) 

--- 

Damage Vulnerabilities any damage from an area-of-effect 

Damage Resistances bludgeoning, piercing, slashing 

Condition Immunities charmed, frightened, grappled, paralyzed, petrified, prone, restrained, stunned 

Senses Blindsight 3 m., passive Perception 10 

Challenge 1/4 (50XP) Proficiency Bonus +2 

--- 

Swarm. The swarm can occupy another creature's space and vice versa, and the swarm can move through any opening large enough for a tiny insect. The swarm can't regain hit points or gain temporary hit points. 

 

Actions 

Bites. Melee Weapon Attack: +3 to hit, reach 0m., one target in the swarm's space. Hit: 5 (2d4) piercing damage, or 2 (1d4) piercing damage if the swarm has half of its hit points (9) or fewer.

Saturday, May 7, 2022 

1:06 PM 

Wild Smite 

Novice wild 

 

Vitality Cost: 2 

Casting Time: 1 Bonus Action 

Range: Self 

Duration: Concentration 

 

Your body pulls elemental energy from the surrounding world, the place you are in determining the element that is drawn. This power is siphoned into your fingers and released as you strike. With some effort you can cause the energy to flare over a weapon. 

 

Active: When you hit a creature with a melee attack you may use your bonus action and expend 2 vitality to deal extra 2d6 damage. The type of this damage depends on the environment you are in (see the Environment Damage Type table). 

Passive: While you have this spell prepared, when you make an unarmed attack, you may expend 1 vitality to deal an extra 1d6 damage. The type of this damage depends on the environment you are in (see the Environment Damage Type table).

Tuesday, May 3, 2022 

9:24 AM 

Zephyr Strike 

Novice wild 

 

Vitality Cost: 3 

Casting Time: 1 Bonus Action 

Range: Self 

Duration: 1 Turn 

 

Your movements have unearthly grace and when you channel this ability, the very wind seems to guide your blade, bow, or body. 

 

Active: Until the end of your turn, your speed increases by 10 meters and your movement doesn't provoke opportunity attacks. Once before the end of your current turn, you can give yourself advantage on one attack roll. If that attack deals damage, it deals an additional 1d8 damage on a hit. 

Passive: While you have this spell prepared, opportunity attacks made against you have disadvantage. 

 

Proto Zephyr Strike from Fynn, B2 A New Quest 

She moves to his side, but Fynn turns into her grapple. There is no opportunity for her to grab him as he takes two steps back. Fynn feels a surge of weightlessness, almost as though his feet are gliding over the ground with both perfect resistance and none at all. He hears a gust of wind. The surge of speed is followed by a surge of strain. Almost as though the fast move was far more exhausting than it should have been. Fynn pauses. 

 

 

 

 

Tuesday, May 3, 2022 

9:24 AM 

Zephyr Strike 

Novice wild 

 

Vitality Cost: 3 

Casting Time: 1 Bonus Action 

Range: Self

Passive: While you have this spell prepared, opportunity attacks made against you have disadvantage. 

 

Proto Zephyr Strike from Fynn, B2 A New Quest 

She moves to his side, but Fynn turns into her grapple. There is no opportunity for her to grab him as he takes two steps back. Fynn feels a surge of weightlessness, almost as though his feet are gliding over the ground with both perfect resistance and none at all. He hears a gust of wind. Book 

 

His feet are unnaturally light upon the ground and his clothes flutter in an unfelt wind. He holds his breath, aims, opens his mind, and releases. The arrow is accompanied by an otherworldly Whoosh, as though the wind itself propels it forward. Its flight curves, causing the arrow that almost missed the tree, to slam into it. 

 

Fynn is nearby, and unleashes a an arrow into a nearby tree, his feet unnaturally light upon the ground. That is his new power. Aside from the fact that his skills with the bow and sword have improved drastically, he also figured out how to somehow attune his body to a new kind of wild magic power. When he described it to Astrid, he told her that it felt like the air resisted his movements less and with effort it was almost as though the wind itself was guiding his attacks and propelling him through the air. Astrid pauses for a second as he jumps and cleaves the air with his machete. 

Conflicting change.

Apprentice (10) 

Wednesday, February 23, 2022 

10:47 AM 

Apprentice  

Access to apprentice wild spells grants you the abilities described under Channel Wild II and allows you to learn the following spells. 

 

Channel Wild II 

As your connection to the world expands, you gain a deeper understanding of the earth you walk upon. Through the strands of invisible magic that span the world you may navigate without fail. With additional training you may have a more active impact on the world and your own body. 

 

Find the Path. For a negligible amount of vitality you to may find the shortest, most direct physical route to a specific fixed location who's true name (see True Name of Channel Wild I) you know on the same world. 

As long as you maintain concentration and remain on the same world as the destination, you know how far it is and in what direction it lies. While you are traveling there, whenever you are presented with a choice of paths along the way, you automatically determine which path is the shortest and most direct (but not necessarily the safest) route to the destination.

Tuesday, May 3, 2022 

9:24 AM 

Barkskin 

Apprentice wild 

 

Vitality Cost: 3 

Casting Time: 1 Action 

Range: Touch 

Duration: 1 Hour 

 

Your skin becomes non-newtonian, causing it to harden when struck. With some effort you may amplify this effect to turn your skin as iron when struck by fast blows, but limiting your ability to move quickly. 

 

Active: You touch a willing creature. Until the spell ends, the target's Armor Class equals its Base Armor Class + 6, unless its Armor Class is already higher. 

Passive: While you have this spell prepared, your AC increases by 1.

Tuesday, May 3, 2022 

9:24 AM 

Cure Wounds 

Apprentice wild 

 

Vitality Cost: 2 

Casting Time: 1 Action 

Range: Touch 

Duration: Instant 

 

With little effort you can have your own flesh mend at an incredible rate at the cost of some efficiency. With some effort you may also bestow this gift on another. 

 

Active: Touch a willing or incapacitated living creature that has less than its maximum hit points. The target instantly expends 1 hit die and regains a number of hit points equal to the rolled amount. If you know the target's true name, you may add your Spellcasting ability modifier to the roll. 

This spell has no effect on constructs or undead. It can’t regenerate missing limbs or organs that wouldn't heal naturally. 

Passive: While you have this spell prepared, you may use an action to expend a hit die and regain a number of hit points equal to the rolled amount.

Tuesday, May 3, 2022 

9:24 AM 

Earthbind 

Apprentice wild 

 

Vitality Cost: 2 per Turn 

Casting Time: 1 Action 

Range: 100 Meters 

Duration: Concentration 

 

You exude an aura that makes the air seem thick to those who spark your ire. With a good deal of effort you may cause the air around a wing to become heavy, almost viscous to move. Those not strong enough to fly through the resistance are forced to glide to the ground.  

 

Active: Choose a creature you can see within range. The target must succeed on a Strength saving throw or have its speed halved and its flying speed (if any) is reduced to 0 meters for the duration. An airborne creature affected by this spell safely descends at 20 meters per round until it reaches the ground or the spell ends. 

Passive: While you have this spell prepared, the space in a 3-meter radius around you is difficult terrain for any creature of your choice that you can see. 

 

Tuesday, May 3, 2022 

9:24 AM 

Earthbind 

Apprentice wild 

 

Vitality Cost: 2 per Turn 

Casting Time: 1 Action 

Range: 100 Meters 

Duration: Concentration 

 

You exude an aura that makes the air seem thick to those who spark your ire. With a good deal of effort you may cause the air around a wing to become heavy, almost viscous to move. Those not strong enough to fly through the resistance are forced to glide to the ground.  

 

Active: Choose a creature you can see within range. The target must succeed on a Strength saving throw or have its speed halved and its flying speed (if any) is reduced to 0 meters for the duration. An airborne creature affected by this spell safely descends at 20 meters per round until it reaches the ground or the spell ends. 

Passive: While you have this spell prepared, the space in a 3-meter radius around you is difficult terrain for any creature of your choice that you can see. 

Conflicting change.

Tuesday, May 3, 2022 

9:24 AM 

Pass Without Trace 

Apprentice wild 

 

Vitality Cost: 3 per Hour 

Casting Time: 1 Action 

Range: Self (10-meter radius) 

Duration: Concentration 

 

As you move across the landscape your feet leave no distinguishable tracks and your scent simply dissolves before it can settle. Through a small effort, you can mask yourself and your allies with a cloak of shadows and silence, empowering and extending the effect to them. 

 

Active: You mask yourself and your companions from detection. For the duration, each creature you choose within 10 meters of you (including you) has a +5 bonus to Dexterity (Stealth) checks and can't be tracked, even by magical means. A creature that receives this bonus leaves behind no tracks or other traces of its passage. 

Passive: While you have this spell prepared, you leave behind no tracks or other traces of your passage.

Tuesday, May 3, 2022 

9:24 AM 

Protection from Poison 

Apprentice wild 

 

Vitality Cost: 3 

Casting Time: 1 Action 

Range: Touch 

Duration: 1 Minute 

 

Your body is nearly impervious to poison and with some effort you can neutralize toxic substances or venomous magic. 

 

Active: You touch a creature. If it is poisoned, you neutralize the poison. If more than one poison afflicts the target, you neutralize one poison that you know is present, or you neutralize one at random. For the duration, the target has advantage on saving throws against being poisoned, and is resistant to poison damage. 

Passive: While you have this spell prepared, you have advantage on saving throws against being poisoned, and are resistant to poison damage.

Tuesday, May 3, 2022 

9:24 AM 

Protection from Unnatural 

Apprentice wild 

 

Vitality Cost: 5 

Casting Time: 1 Action 

Range: Touch 

Duration: Instant 

 

Your body is shielded against unnatural creatures. Your skin seems to repel their touch and with some effort you may purge others of their presence. 

 

Active: You touch a creature that is charmed, frightened, or possessed by a celestial, an elemental, a fey, a fiend, or an undead. The target is no longer charmed, frightened, or possessed by such creatures. 

Passive: While you have this spell prepared, celestials, elementals, fey, fiends, and undead have disadvantage on attack rolls against you and you are immune to being charmed, frightened, or possessed by such creatures.

Saturday, May 7, 2022 

1:06 PM 

Silence 

Apprentice wild 

 

Vitality Cost: 2 per Turn 

Casting Time: 1 Action 

Range: 20 Meters 

Duration: Concentration 

 

Your step is silenced and with some effort, you may expand this space of absolute quiet to a nearby place. 

 

Active: For the duration, no sound can be created within or pass through a 10-meter radius sphere centered on you. Any creature or object entirely inside the sphere is deafened, immune to thunder damage, and cannot be affected by an aria spell. Casting a spell that requires a verbal component, such as an aria, is impossible there. 

Passive: While you have this spell prepared, your movements make no noise. You are also invisible to a creature that is blind while it is deaf.

Tuesday, May 3, 2022 

9:24 AM 

Smoke 

Apprentice wild 

 

Vitality Cost: 2 

Casting Time: 1 Action 

Range: 10 Meters 

Duration: Instant 

 

Your body exudes smoke-like wafts of grey magic. When attacked this mist can be waved at a foe, potentially throwing off their aim. With some effort you may also unleash a blast of it and hide within the grey mass. 

 

Active: Mist spreads from a point you choose within range to fill a 5-meter radius sphere. The sphere spreads around corners, and its area is heavily obscured.  

The mist lingers for 1 minute. It is dispersed by a breeze in 5 turns, by a gale at the start of your next turn, and has no effect during a storm. 

Passive: While you have this spell prepared, whenever you are targeted with an attack, you may use your reaction to add your Spellcasting Ability modifier to your AC against that attack. You must do this before you know whether the attack hits or misses.

Saturday, May 7, 2022 

1:06 PM 

Summon Critters 

Apprentice wild 

 

Vitality Cost: 8 

Casting Time: 1 Action 

Range: 20 Meters 

Duration: 1 Hour 

 

The magic suffusing your body attracts birds or other small animals to your location and causes them to prefer the areas you are in. With effort, you may summon them from the surrounding wilderness to your cause and compel them to instinctively attack your foes. 

 

Active: You summon a swarm of tiny animals, which are beasts, fey, or monstrosities with an Intelligence of 5 or less, in a space that you can see within range. Choose either Swarm of Birds or Swarm of Vermin (see the stat blocks below.) The swarm disappears when it drops to 0 hit points, when the spell ends, or after you dismiss it as a bonus action. 

The swarm is an ally to you and your companions. In combat, it shares your initiative and takes its turn with you. It obeys your verbal commands (no action required). 

The GM may allow you to summon a different type of swarm depending on the environment you are in. For example a Swarm of Bats may answer your call in a cave deep underground. The GM may also decide your call yields no reply if there are no animals nearby, or you previously abused your ability to summon. 

Passive: While you have this spell prepared, you may use an animal to deliver a message. Choose a tiny beast, fey, or monstrosity with 5 or less Intelligence you can see in range, such as a raven, a bat, or a squirrel. You specify a location, which you must have visited, and a recipient who matches a general description, such as "a man or woman with a gun dressed in a green patterned uniform" or "a black haired orc with a golden ear ring." The animal will allow you to attach a tiny item to its legs. The target creature then travels toward the specified location, covering about 80 kilometers (50 miles) per 24 hours for a flying messenger, or 40 kilometers (25 miles) for other animals. 

When the messenger arrives, it will approach the creature that you described and offer the message attached to its leg. If the beast cannot find a creature matching your description, or the location you described proves impossible to reach, the beast makes its way back to where you used this ability. 

If the target of this ability is a creature that can mimic humanoid language, you may have it deliver a reasonably long verbal message instead. 

 

Swarm of Birds 

Large swarm of tiny creatures, unaligned  

 

Armor Class 12 

Hit Points 40 (9d10-9) 

Vitality - 

Speed 8m., fly 20m. 

--- 

Str: 6 (-2) 

Dex: 14 (+2) 

Con: 8 (-1) 

Int: 4 (-3) 

Wis: 13 (+1) 

Cha: 7 (-2) 

--- 

Damage Vulnerabilities any damage from an area-of-effect

True Sight- 

Apprentice wild 

 

Vitality Cost: 5 

Casting Time: 1 Bonus Action 

Range: 20 Meters 

Duration: Concentration 

 

Your vision pierces through absolute darkness and with some effort you can even gaze through solid walls. 

 

Active: You gain truesight out to a range of 20 meters. 

Passive: While you have this spell prepared, you can see normally in absolute darkness, both magical and nonmagical, to a distance of 20 meters. 

 

 

 

 

Active: You gain the ability to see through solid walls out to a distance of 20 meters for the duration. You cannot see color through walls, only shades of gray. This vision can penetrate most barriers, but is blocked by 1 meter of wood or dirt, 30 centimeters of stone, 1 centimeter of common metal, or a thin sheet of lead.

Active: You gain the ability to see through solid walls out to a distance of 20 meters for the duration. You cannot see color through walls, only shades of gray. This vision can penetrate most barriers, but is blocked by 1 meter of wood or dirt, 30 centimeters of stone, 1 centimeter of common metal, or a thin sheet of lead. 

Conflicting change.

Adept (7) 

Wednesday, February 23, 2022 

10:47 AM 

Adept 

Access to adept wild spells grants you the abilities described under Channel Wild III and allows you to learn the following spells. 

 

Channel Wild III 

Your ability to read the world around you expands and allows you to divine information from a far greater area. In addition to being able to cast more complex and potent spells, you can open yourself to the world around you. The wind, soil and life itself whisper their secrets to you and with additional training you can make small  but widespread changes to the environment and yourself. 

 

Commune with Nature. As an action you may expend 4 vitality and briefly become one with nature to gain knowledge of the surrounding territory. Outdoors, the spell gives you knowledge of the land within 5 kilometers of you (on a 5-kilometer hex-map, this ability reveals your current hex and all adjacent hexes regardless of you location in the hex). In caves and other natural underground settings, the radius is limited to 100 meters. The spell doesn't function where nature has been replaced by construction, as is the case with cities and settlements. 

You instantly gain knowledge of one fact of your choice about any of the following subjects as they relate to the area: 

•terrain and bodies of water 

•prevalent plants, minerals, animals, or peoples 

•mythic creatures 

•influence from other planes of existence (particularly the Black Fog) 

•Changes to the natural landscape such as buildings 

For example, you could determine the location of a mythic elemental in the area, the location of major sources of safe drinking water, or the location of any nearby divine sanctuaries. 

 

 

Experimental 

 

You retain your alignment and personality. 

You assume the hit points of your new form. When it reverts to its normal form, the creature returns to the number of hit points it had before it transformed. If it reverts as a result of dropping to 0 hit points, any excess damage carries over to its normal form. As long as the excess damage doesn't reduce the creature's normal form to 0 hit points, it isn't knocked unconscious. 

 

The creature is limited in the actions it can perform by the nature of its new form, and it can't speak, cast spells, or take any other action that requires hands or speech. 

 

The target's gear melds into the new form. The creature can't activate, use, wield, or otherwise benefit from any of its equipment.  

 

 

You can stay in a beast shape for a number of hours equal to half your druid level (rounded down). You then revert to your normal form unless you expend another use of this feature. You can revert to your normal form earlier by using a bonus action on your turn. You automatically revert if you fall unconscious, drop to 0 hit points, or die. 

 

Adept (7) 

Wednesday, February 23, 2022 

10:47 AM 

Adept 

Access to adept wild spells grants you the abilities described under Channel Wild III and allows you to learn the following spells. 

 

Channel Wild III 

Your ability to read the world around you expands and allows you to divine information from a far greater area. In addition to being able to cast more complex and potent spells, you can open yourself to the world around you. The wind, soil and life itself whisper their secrets to you and with additional training you can make small  but widespread changes to the environment and yourself. 

 

Commune with Nature. As an action you may expend 4 vitality and briefly become one with nature to gain knowledge of the surrounding territory. Outdoors, the spell gives you knowledge of the land within 5 kilometers of you (on a 5-kilometer hex-map, this ability reveals your current hex and all adjacent hexes regardless of you location in the hex). In caves and other natural underground settings, the radius is limited to 100 meters. The spell doesn't function where nature has been replaced by construction, as is the case with cities and settlements. 

You instantly gain knowledge of one fact of your choice about any of the following subjects as they relate to the area: 

terrain and bodies of water 

prevalent plants, minerals, animals, or peoples 

mythic creatures 

influence from other planes of existence (particularly the Black Fog) 

Changes to the natural landscape such as buildings 

For example, you could determine the location of a mythic elemental in the area, the location of major sources of safe drinking water, or the location of any nearby divine sanctuaries. 

 

 

Experimental 

 

You retain your alignment and personality. 

You assume the hit points of your new form. When it reverts to its normal form, the creature returns to the number of hit points it had before it transformed. If it reverts as a result of dropping to 0 hit points, any excess damage carries over to its normal form. As long as the excess damage doesn't reduce the creature's normal form to 0 hit points, it isn't knocked unconscious. 

 

The creature is limited in the actions it can perform by the nature of its new form, and it can't speak, cast spells, or take any other action that requires hands or speech. 

 

The target's gear melds into the new form. The creature can't activate, use, wield, or otherwise benefit from any of its equipment.  

 

 

You can stay in a beast shape for a number of hours equal to half your druid level (rounded down). You then revert to your normal form unless you expend another use of this feature. You can revert to your normal form earlier by using a bonus action on your turn. You automatically revert if you fall unconscious, drop to 0 hit points, or die. 

 

As an action you may expend vitality and transform yourself into another creature. The vitality cost of the transformation equals half the maximum hit points  

The transformation lasts until you use an action to revert to your natural form, you drop to 0 hit points, or die. The new form can be any beast, fey, or monstrosity. Your game statistics, including mental ability scores, are replaced by the statistics of the chosen creature, but you retain your alignment, personality, and Intelligence, Wisdom, and Charisma scores. You also retain all of your skill and saving throw proficiencies, in addition to gaining those of the creature. If the creature has the same proficiency as you and the bonus in its stat block is higher than yours, use the creature's bonus instead of yours. If the creature has any legendary or lair actions, you can't use them. 

When you transform, you assume the creature's hit points and hit dice. When you revert to your normal form, you return to the number of hit points you had before you transformed. However, if you revert as a result of dropping to 0 hit points, any excess damage carries over to your normal form. For example, if you take 10 damage in animal form and have only 1 hit point left, you revert and take 9 damage. As long as the excess damage doesn't reduce your normal form to 0 hit points, you aren't knocked unconscious. 

    You can't cast spells, and your ability to speak or take any action that requires hands is limited to the capabilities of your beast form. Transforming doesn't break your concentration on a spell you've already cast, however, or prevent you from taking actions that are part of a spell, such as call lightning, that you've already cast. 

    You retain the benefit of any features from your class, race, or other source and can use them if the new form is physically capable of doing so. However, you can't use any of your special senses, such as darkvision, unless your new form also has that sense. 

    You choose whether your equipment falls to the ground in your space, merges into your new form, or is worn by it. Worn equipment functions as normal, but the DM decides whether it is practical for the new form to wear a piece of equipment, based on the creature's shape and size. Your equipment doesn't change size or shape to match the new form, and any equipment that the new form can't wear must either fall to the ground or merge with it. Equipment that merges with the form has no effect until you leave the form. 

 

 

Conflicting change.

Tuesday, May 3, 2022 

9:24 AM 

Decaying Smite 

Adept wild 

 

Vitality Cost: 4 

Casting Time: 1 Bonus Action 

Range: Self 

Duration: Concentration 

 

Your nails drip with black necromantic energy that corrupts the bodies of those you wound. With some effort you can empower this effect and extend it to your weapon. 

 

Active: The next time you hit a creature with a non-firearm weapon or unarmed attack during this spell's duration, the attack deals an extra 3d8 necrotic damage to the target. A creature that takes this damage can't regain hit points for 1 minute and regeneration effects are suppressed. At the end of each of its turns, an affected creature makes a Constitution saving throw, ending the effect on a success. 

This has no effect on constructs or undead. 

Passive: While you have this spell prepared, when you hit a living creature with an unarmed attack, the target can't regain hit points and regeneration effects are suppressed until the start of your next turn.

Tuesday, May 3, 2022 

9:24 AM 

Freedom of Movement 

Adept wild 

 

Vitality Cost: 2 per Turn 

Casting Time: 1 Action 

Range: Touch 

Duration: Concentration 

 

Magic frees your movement. When you focus on it, wild magic swirls around you or an ally, freeing them from all bonds. 

 

Active: As an action you touch a willing creature. For the duration, the target’s movement is unaffected by difficult terrain, and spells and other magical effects can't reduce the target’s speed. If you know the target's true name, spells and magical effects also can't cause the target to be paralyzed or restrained. 

The target can also spend 1 meter of movement to automatically escape from non-magical restraints, such as handcuffs or a creature that has it grappled. Finally, being underwater imposes no penalties on the target’s movement or attacks. 

Passive: While you have this spell prepared, you ignore difficult terrain.

Resistance 

Adept wild 

 

Vitality Cost: 5 per Turn 

Casting Time: 1 Action 

Range: 10 Meters 

Duration: Concentration 

 

Your own body seems to be harder to hurt. Through great effort you focus and harden the body of a creature against harm. As they are damaged, every cut is less severe and every burn smaller than it should be. 

 

Active: As an action you grant a creature you can see resistance to all damage for the duration. You must know the target's true name to target it with this ability. 

Passive: While you have this spell prepared, your maximum hit points increase by an amount equal to your level.

Tuesday, May 3, 2022 

9:24 AM 

Wild Vulnerability 

Adept wild 

 

Vitality Cost: 4 per Turn 

Casting Time: 1 Action 

Range: 20 Meters 

Duration: Concentration 

 

Your energy of your spells becomes laced with an influence that weakens the bonds of matter hardened against them. With effort you may focus this destabilizing energy onto foe, making them weak to wild energy attacks. 

 

Active: Choose one creature you can see within range. It must succeed a Constitution saving throw or become vulnerable to the damage type associated with the environment you are currently in (see the Environment Damage Type table) for the duration. If you know the target's true name, it automatically fails its saving throw against this spell. 

Passive: Your spells ignore resistance of creatures to the damage type associated with the environment you are in (see the Environment Damage Type table).

Expert (6) 

Wednesday, February 23, 2022 

10:47 AM 

Expert 

Access to expert wild spells grants you the abilities described under Channel Wild IV and allows you to learn the following spells. 

 

Channel Wild IV 

As an expert you learn to alter the very being of a creature and imbue it with magical abilities or alter its form. 

Awaken. Over the course of a 1 minute ritual you expend 20 vitality to awaken a huge or smaller beast you touch. The target must have either no Intelligence score or an Intelligence of 6 or less. The target gains an Intelligence of 10. The target also gains the ability to speak one language you know.
