Tuesday, May 3, 2022 

9:24 AM 

  

Expert wild 

 

Vitality Cost: 5 

Casting Time: 1 Bonus Action 

Range: 20 Meters 

Duration: Instant 

 

Your mind does not need to rest and with effort you may expand this clarity over your entire body, or that of another. 

 

Active: Target a creature in range and end any temporary blinded, deafened, frightened, paralyzed, poisoned, or stunned condition affecting it that can be ended by succeeding a saving throw. If you know the target's true name, you may also end a temporary aforementioned condition if it cannot be ended by succeeding a saving throw. 

You may also cast this spell at the start of your turn (no action required) to end any of the aforementioned conditions affecting you. 

Passive: You don't need to sleep. Instead, you can spend 4 hours a day doing menial tasks to gain the benefits of a night's rest.

Tuesday, May 3, 2022 

9:24 AM 

Intellect Fortress 

Expert wild 

 

Vitality Cost: 4 per Hour 

Casting Time: 1 Action 

Range: 10 Meters 

Duration: Concentration 

 

Your consciousness becomes more aware of other-worldly influence. With some effort you can harden your mind, or that of another, against any such influence. 

 

Active: For the duration, you or one willing creature you can see within range has resistance to psychic damage, as well as advantage on Intelligence, Wisdom, and Charisma saving throws. If you know the target's true name it is also immune to being charmed and frightened for the duration. 

Passive: While you have this spell prepared, when you are targeted by a divine spell or another mind altering effect, you know an attempt to influence you is being made and may use your reaction to gain advantage on any associated saving throw.

Tuesday, May 3, 2022 

9:24 AM 

Locate 

Expert wild 

 

Vitality Cost: 7 per Hour 

Casting Time: 1 Action 

Range: Self 

Duration: Concentration 

 

You mind is open and as though guided by an invisible hand you know how to find things you are connected to. With effort this sense may expand and guide you to what, or who, you seek. 

 

Active: Target a creature or object. You sense the direction to the target's location, as long as it is within 10 kilometers of you (on a 5-kilometer hex map, if it is within two hexes of you). If the target is moving, you know the direction of its movement. 

The spell can locate a specific creature or object known to you, or the nearest creature of a specific kind (such as a human or a unicorn), so long as you have seen such a creature or object up close —within 10 meters — at least once.  

To target a specific creature, you must know its true name. If the creature you described or named is in a different form, such as a druid in their hybrid form, this spell doesn't locate the creature. To target an object, you must have touched it at least once. 

This spell can't locate a target if running water at least 3 meters wide blocks a direct path between you and the target. 

Passive: While you have this spell prepared, you may use your action to instantly locate an object that you have touched at least once and that is no more than 100 meters away. This ability can't locate a target if running water at least 3 meters wide blocks a direct path between you and the target object.

Friday, May 27, 2022 

10:01 AM 

Redirect Energy 

Expert wild 

 

Vitality Cost: 6 

Casting Time: 1 Reaction 

Range: Self (10 meters) 

Duration: Instant 

 

Your body becomes hardened against elemental magic. With a good deal of effort you may even redirect a foes assault. 

 

Active: You may cast this spell as a reaction to taking acid, cold, fire, force, or lightning damage. The damage you take from the triggering damage type is halved. You may then target a creature no more than 10 meters away. It must succeed a Dexterity saving throw or take damage of the triggering type equal to the damage you prevented yourself from taking. 

Passive: While you have this spell prepared, when you take acid, cold, fire, force, or lightning damage, you may use your reaction to reduce the damage taken by 1d4.

Master (7) 

Wednesday, February 23, 2022 

10:47 AM 

Master 

Access to master wild spells grants you the abilities described under Channel Wild V and allows you to learn the following spells. 

 

Channel Wild V 

Finally, as a master of wild magic you gain the ability to make the natural world move in accordance with your wishes and rouse it against your foes. At its barest it causes plants and other mindless lifeforms to animate and assault your foes. With some more training, it allows you to control the natural elements of air, earth and water. 

Wrath of Nature. As an action, choose a point you can see that is no more than 20 meters away and cause trees and grasses in a 20-meter cube centered on that point to become animated until the spell ends. Maintaining this effect requires concentration and 5 vitality per turn. 

Grasses and Undergrowth. Any area of ground in the cube that is covered by grass or undergrowth is difficult terrain for each creature of your choice. 

Trees. At the start of each of your turns, each creature of your choice within 3 meters of any tree in the cube must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw or take 4d6 bludgeoning damage from whipping branches, roots and vines. 

At the end of each of your turns, one creature of your choice that is on the ground in the cube must succeed on a Strength saving throw or become restrained until the spell ends. A restrained creature can use an action to make a Strength (Athletics) check against your spell save DC, ending the effect on itself on a success.

Saturday, May 7, 2022 

1:06 PM 

Bend Air 

Master wild 

 

Vitality Cost: 2 per Turn 

Casting Time: 1 Action 

Range: 50 Meters 

Duration: Concentration 

 

The air obeys your will. With little effort you may command it to lift you and with a little more you cause powerful winds to batter your foes. 

 

Active: You take control of the air in a 50-meter cube centered on a point you can see in range. Choose one of the following effects when you cast the spell. The effect lasts for the spell's duration, unless you use your action on a later turn to switch to a different effect. You can also use your action to temporarily halt the effect or to restart one you've halted. 

Gusts. A wind picks up within the cube, continually blowing in a horizontal direction you designate. You choose the intensity of the wind: calm, breeze, gale, or storm. If the wind is a gale or storm, ranged weapon attacks that pass through it or that are made against targets within the cube have disadvantage on their attack rolls. If the wind is a storm, any creature moving against the wind must spend 1 extra meter of movement for each meter moved. 

Downdraft. You cause a sustained blast of strong wind to blow downward from the top of the cube. Ranged weapon attacks that pass through the cube or that are made against targets within it have disadvantage on their attack rolls. A creature must make a Strength saving throw if it flies into the cube for the first time on a turn or starts its turn there flying. On a failed save, the creature is knocked prone. 

Updraft. You cause a sustained updraft within the cube, rising upward from the cube's bottom side. Creatures that end a fall within the cube take only half damage from the fall. When a creature in the cube makes a vertical jump, the creature can jump up to 3 meters higher than normal. 

Passive: While you have this spell prepared, you may expend 1 vitality (no action required) at the start of your turn to gain a flying speed equal to your walking speed until the start of your next turn.

Tuesday, May 3, 2022 

9:24 AM 

Bend Earth 

Master wild 

 

Vitality Cost: 5 

Casting Time: 1 Action 

Range: 50 Meters 

Duration: Concentration, up to 1 minute 

 

The soil obeys you, shaping itself according to your will. It takes only a bit of work to move natural soil, however with effort you can create formations of jagged stone. 

 

Active: A non-magical wall of solid stone springs into existence at a point you choose within range. The wall is 20 centimeters thick and is composed of ten 3-meter-by-3-meter panels. Each panel must be contiguous with at least one other panel. Alternatively, you can create 3-meter-by-6-meter panels that are only 10 centimeters thick. 

If the wall cuts through a creature's space when it appears, the creature is pushed to one side of the wall (your choice). 

The wall can have any shape you desire, though it can't occupy the same space as a creature or object. The wall doesn't need to be vertical or rest on any firm foundation. It must, however, merge with and be solidly supported by existing stone. Thus, you can use this spell to bridge a chasm or create a ramp. 

If you create a span greater than 6 meters in length, you must halve the size of each panel to create supports. You can crudely shape the wall to create crenellations, battlements, and so on. 

The wall is an object made of stone that can be damaged and thus breached. Each panel has AC 15 and 30 hit points per inch of thickness. Reducing a panel to 0 hit points destroys it and might cause connected panels to collapse at the GM's discretion. 

If you maintain your concentration on this spell for its whole duration, the wall becomes a permanent formation and can't be dispelled. Otherwise, the wall collapses when the spell ends. 

Passive: While you have this spell prepared, you may use an action and a negligible amount of vitality to choose a portion of dirt or stone that you can see within range and that fits within a 1-meter cube. You manipulate it in one of the following ways: 

•If you target an area of loose earth, you can instantaneously excavate it, move it along the ground, and deposit it up to 1 meter away. This movement doesn't involve enough force to cause damage. 

•You cause shapes to appear on the dirt or stone, spelling out words, creating images, or shaping patterns. 

•If the dirt or stone you target is on the ground, you cause it to become difficult terrain. Alternatively, you can cause the ground to become normal terrain if it is already difficult terrain. 

•You use the dirt or stone to raise a 2 meter tall and 1 meter wide barricade that can act as cover.

Tuesday, May 3, 2022 

9:24 AM 

Hunger 

Master wild 

 

Vitality Cost: 7 

Casting Time: 1 Action 

Range: Self (3-Meter Radius) 

Duration: Instant 

 

Your body is a siphon of energy, draining the surrounding land and everything in it of energy. Should you empower this, you devour the strength of those around you and drain it into you. 

 

Active: Every living creature in a 3-meter radius sphere centered on you makes a Constitution saving throw. A creature takes 4d6 necrotic damage on a failed save and half as much on a successful one. You then regain 2d6 hit points. 

Passive:  While you have this spell prepared, as long as you perform a night's rest in a natural environment not devoid of life you do not need to expend rations or increase your hunger to gain the benefits of that rest.

Saturday, May 7, 2022 

1:06 PM 

Petrify 

Master wild 

 

Vitality Cost: 8 

Casting Time: 1 Action 

Range: 20 Meters 

Duration: Concentration, up to 1 minute 

 

Any creature that locks eyes with you will feel its body petrify. With great effort you can focus your ire onto a single creature, causing its flesh to first harden and, should they lack the constitution to resist, rapidly transform into a light, porous white stone. 

 

Active: You attempt to turn one creature  that you can see within range into stone. The creature must make a Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, the creature begins to turn to stone and is restrained. It must repeat the saving throw at the end of its next turn. On a success, the effect ends. On a failure, the creature is petrified until freed. Effects that dispel or detect magic have no effect once the petrification is complete. 

Instead of targeting a creature, you can also use this spell to petrify a 1-meter cube of organic material (including plastic). 

Passive: While you have this spell prepared, if a creature starts its turn within range and the two of you can see each other, you may expend 5 vitality (no action required) to affect it with this spell's active.  

A creature that isn't surprised can avert its eyes to avoid being targeted in this way at the start of its turn (however you may still use this spell's active to affect it). If it does so, it can't see you until the start of its next turn, when it can avert its eyes again. If it looks at you in the meantime, you may have it immediately make the save.

Tuesday, May 10, 2022 

7:18 PM 

Reincarnate 

Master wild 

 

Vitality Cost: 40 

Casting Time: 1 Minute 

Range: Touch 

Duration: Instant 

 

You secure yourself from death through a magical bond that will form a fresh body for you should your life be cut short. With great effort you may also use this power to form a new body from the surrounding wilderness for a deceased and attempt to return their soul to the body. 

 

Active: You touch a recently deceased creature or a piece of such a creature and make a resurrection check. Up to 3 other creatures may contribute to the casting of this spell via a contribution skill check. The GM asks them each to make a skill check based on their form of contribution, with the DC of the check adjusting to how helpful/impactful the contribution would be at reminding the creature who it is and why it should return. Contributors with a strong bond with the deceased have advantage on their contribution roll. Loving siblings, supportive parents and intimate lovers are considered to have a strong bond to the deceased. Being friends or acquaintances is only considered a strong bond if the relationship involves emotional intimacy. 

Once all contributions are made, you complete the spell and make a resurrection check by rolling a d20 and adding your Spellcasting modifier to the roll. The DC of this check equals 10. It increases by 2 for every time the target has already been successfully returned from the dead and by 1 for every day that has passed since its death. For each successful contribution skill check, the DC decreases by 3, whereas each failed contribution skill check increases the DC by 1. You have advantage on the resurrection check if you know the target's true name. 

On a failure, the spell fails and any future attempts to return the creature to life with this spell automatically fail. On a success, the creature returns to life with 1 hit point, 4 levels of exhaustion and a new body.  

The reincarnated creature recalls its former life and experiences. It retains the capabilities it had in its original form, except it returns to life as a wildling and changes its species' traits accordingly. The blessings that are applied to it are determined randomly as described in the Natural Blessings trait. 

The new body is not affected by diseases, permanent curses, or similar effects that affected the creature before its death.  

This spell has no effect on constructs or undead. 

Passive: You may spend a long rest implanting a piece of yourself into a living plant that is at least large in size to create a reincarnation totem. The totem activates 30 days after it is completed and is obviously magical to any onlooker. As long as the totem lives, if you are killed no more than 100 kilometers away from the totem, you are reincarnated as described in the active of this spell after 1d6 rounds, with the exception that your species does not change. Your  Contributions to your reincarnation check through this ability are not possible. The totem then deactivates. It reactivates after 30 days. 

You can only have one such totem active at a time. If you create a new totem, the old one becomes inert as soon as the new one is complete.

Tuesday, May 3, 2022 

9:24 AM 

Scrying 

Master wild 

 

Vitality Cost: 6 per Minute 

Casting Time: 1 Minute 

Range: Self 

Duration: Concentration 

 

Your ability to divinate has reached its apex, granting you the ability to watch anyone. 

 

Active. You attempt to spy on a creature on the same planet as you. The target must make a Wisdom saving throw, which is modified by how well you know the target and the sort of physical connection you have to it. If a target knows you're casting this spell, it can fail the saving throw voluntarily if it wants to be observed. If you know the target's true name, it automatically fails its saving throw. 

 

Knowledge of the Target 

Knowledge 

	
 

Save Modifier 




Secondhand (you have heard of the target) 

	

5 




Firsthand (you have met the target) 

	

0 




Familiar (you know the target well) 

	

-5 




Connection to Target 

Connection 

	
 

Save Modifier 




Likeness or picture 

	

-2 




Possession or garment 

	

-4 




Body part, blood, a lock of hair, or the like 

	

-10 




 

On a successful save, the target isn't affected, and you can't use this spell against it again for 24 hours. 

On a failed save, the spell creates an invisible sensor within 3 meters. You can see and hear through the sensor as if you were there. The sensor moves with the target, remaining within 3 meters of it for the duration. A creature that can see invisible objects (as with truesight) sees the sensor as a luminous orb about the size of your fist. 

Instead of targeting a creature, you may choose a location the true name of which you know or that you have seen before in person as the target of this spell. When you do, the sensor appears at that location and doesn't move. 

 

Passive. Any attempt to scry or determine your location using magic or technology that does not rely on visually seeing you as through a camera alerts you to the attempt. You may then have the attempt fail.

Saturday, May 7, 2022 

1:06 PM 

Enhance Ability- 

Novice wild 

 

Vitality Cost: 1 per Turn 

Casting Time: 1 Bonus Action 

Range: 20 Meters 

Duration: Concentration 

 

Flavor 

 

Active: Description 

 

Passive: Description 

 

 

 

 

Enhance Ability 

PHB'14 ʟ p237  




Level 2 Transmutation 




Casting Time: Action 




Range: Touch 




Components: V, S, M (fur or a feather from a beast) 




Duration: Concentration, up to 1 hour 




You touch a creature and bestow upon it a magical enhancement. Choose one of the following effects; the target gains that effect until the spell ends. 

 Bear's Endurance. The target has advantage on Constitution checks. It also gains 2d6 temporary hit points, which are lost when the spell ends. 

 Bull's Strength. The target has advantage on Strength checks, and his or her carrying capacity doubles. 

 Cat's Grace. The target has advantage on Dexterity checks. It also doesn't take damage from falling 20 feet or less if it isn't incapacitated. 

 Eagle's Splendor. The target has advantage on Charisma checks. 

 Fox's Cunning. The target has advantage on Intelligence checks. 

 Owl's Wisdom. The target has advantage on Wisdom checks.

Chapter 15: Witchcraft- 

Sunday, July 10, 2022 

11:00 PM 

 

Experimental 

 

Magic may be introduced into mundane materials in a practice know as witchcraft. The witch sacrifices their own strength and bleeds it into the items they craft. Literally. Blood can carry the life force of its owner and every item a witch crafts contains a small piece of its crafter, as the final infusion involves the witch concentrating their inner power and freeing it. In case of a healing potion a prick of the needle may be enough, while a master work vorpal blade may require a gush of blood to flow over the blistering hot metal.  

 

There are two major ways this can manifest. The first is alchemy, where the natural properties of substances are empowered and recombined by the witch.  

 

 

Note: The Value of Magic Items 

By default a magic item's value equals 10 x (the infusions spent to create it + the enchantment's level). Most vendors will sell them for that price and buy them for half as much. However, you may achieve a much higher price for your items, especially if they require special (high-level) skills to create and the buyer is someone who intends to use the item. 

 

Conflicting change.

Potion Formulas (34) 

Sunday, January 30, 2022 

11:33 AM 

Potion Formulas 

When a potion is brewed, magic is employed to enhance the natural characteristics of the ingredients used and to transfer specific properties of one substance to another. 

At the start of every potion lies a formula. This formula details the ingredients used, how they must be prepared, and how the final infusion must be performed. The infusion in this case is a magical process that energizes and enhances the natural properties of plants and compounds. More importantly, it speeds up the rate at which these compounds are absorbed by, and interact with, the body. This infusion is usually the most difficult part of creating a potion and is a supreme effort that will leave a brewer drained, unless they use blood magic to draw the needed power from another creature. This is the reason why it usually takes a brewer days to complete a potion and brewing massive batches is impossible. A brewer may brew a huge pot of ingredients for a healing potion, but will only have the strength needed to finish a few swigs. This can be alleviated by a coven since several brewers working together can mass-produce simple potions. Or the witch can make use of blood magic, sacrificing creatures to their brews to finish the infusion. 

Brewing is an art that is as flexible as it is time-consuming and complex. A formula can and must be adapted depending on the ingredients used. While most potions have ingredients that work best, or are simply so common that using them is easiest, in most cases any single ingredient can be replaced by a similar one, or in the New World an artificial chemical compound might take the place of a plant. This is the reason why an alchemist will be able to brew a healing potion in the mountains almost as easily as in the jungle, as long as they know the formula.  

  

Brewing potions relies on your Intelligence score 

•The Difficulty Class of saving throws against potions you have brewed is determined as follows: 8 + your Proficiency Bonus + your Intelligence modifier.  

  

By default brewing a potion creates the liquid, balm, or oil described in the description. However, in most cases, potions that are made to be drunk can also be incorporated into other foods and drinks, like pastries. If a potion does not specifically state that it can be mixed into food, it means that it has a unique taste that is impossible to mask or miss and a creature will not ingest it accidentally, even if it is incorporated into something like a praline.  

Consuming a Potion 

Drinking a potion requires an action. Unless another delivery method is specified in the description, a creature must drink the potion or have it injected directly into its bloodstream using a syringe or dart, for the potion to take effect. Potion descriptions are worded assuming that the potion was ingested orally or injected, but many potions offer alternative delivery methods in their description after their effects have been described. 

Distilling a Potion 

In some cases, you may want to create a potion with a more powerful immediate effect or one that lasts longer. In these cases, you may expend additional time and energy (represented by a higher brew point cost) to increase the potency of the potion. This process is called distilling but involves more than boiling the potion to reduce it to its essence. 

Coating a Weapon 

Many potions are poisons that can be used to coat a weapon or piece of ammunition. If this is the case, the potion's description will say so. Due to the heat generated when they are fired, firearm munitions can normally not be coated with potions. 

When a weapon is coated, the potion is considered part of the attack. Any poison damage it applies on a hit is thus doubled on a critical hit. This is not the case if the potion only applies a condition that deals damage over time, but deals no damage in the initial hit.

Monday, January 31, 2022 

3:39 PM 

Balm of Ash 

Novice formula 

 

Infusion Cost: 1 (100ml bottle) 

Duration: 1 Day 

 

Soak fine ash oak chips in alcohol for at least a day, then boil the mixture until there is only a thick, black, tar-like liquid left. Mix hades hair tee with crushed aspila and then add this to the black paste. After making the infusion the result is a pitch-black, thick liquid that can be smeared onto the skin. Once absorbed it inures the tissue against fire and acid. 

 

After spending 1 minute applying this balm to your skin, you gain resistance against fire and acid damage for the duration. 

 

Distill: Spend an additional 1 infusion to increase the duration by a day.

Monday, January 31, 2022 

3:39 PM 

Balm of the Living 

Novice formula 

 

Infusion Cost: 1 (100ml bottle) 

Duration: 1 Day 

 

Scrape the fluid out of an aloe vera and mix it into a tee made from lilac, peppermint, and lavender. Filter it through crushed sapphire salie, add akfruit juice and make the infusion. The resulting pink balm shimmers purple. When smeared on the skin it binds your life-force to your body and inures against the ravages of the black fog and necrotic energy. 

 

After spending 1 minute applying this balm to your skin, you gain resistance against necrotic damage for the duration. 

 

Distill: Spend an additional 2 infusions to increase the duration by a day.

Sunday, January 30, 2022 

11:38 AM 

Hallowing Oil 

Novice formula 

 

Infusion Cost: 3 (100ml bottle) 

Duration: 1 Minute 

 

Mix salt into oil extracted from the leaves of pink periwinkle, then make the infusion. The formula is simple, but exceptionally energy intensive to infuse. It results is a thick, pink liquid with a white sheen. It grants weapons it is applied to the ability to cut through magical defences and the intangible. 

 

You can use this oil to coat one melee weapon or up to three pieces of non-firearm ammunition. Applying the oil takes an action. A coated weapon or piece of ammunition deals magical damage. 

The oil can be applied in advance, however, once the weapon is unsheathed the oil dries out after one minute.

Sunday, January 30, 2022 

11:38 AM 

Healing Potion 

Novice formula 

 

Infusion Cost: 2 (100ml bottle) 

Duration: Instant 

 

Boil the redcap in water and add insect meal to the red liquid. Mix in aspila tea and apple juice. Once distilled and infused, you end up with a dark red fluid with an almost black color through which golden filaments and particles float. The liquid has a smooth, sweet, slightly bitter taste. Upon swallowing, a warm, almost uncomfortable burning sensation expands from the pit of your stomach. Bleeding stops and your wounds close with supernatural speed.  

 

Regain 1d4 + the brewer's infusion ability modifier hit points (minimum 1). 

 

Distill: Spend an additional 2 infusions to increase the healing by 1d4. 

 

Healing Effect, Book II, Order's End 

The moment the healing potion falls down her throat, a burning sensation spreads from her stomach. Her skin prickles and so does her chest. It lasts for just a moment. Then the magic fades.

Monday, July 4, 2022 

10:24 AM 

Nescience Poison 

Novice formula 

 

Infusion Cost: 1 (100ml bottle) 

Duration: Instant 

 

Mix blimberry juice into cannabis, chamomile and lavender tea. Add alcohol, honey, and the liquid crushed from a yellow slipper mushroom, before making the infusion. The result is a dark-golden liquid with a hint of purple, a poison known for taking effect almost instantly. It slows the victim's heart rate and widens blood vessels. The result is a rapid drop in blood pressure that, if the amount of poison is sufficiently large, simply causes the victim to fall unconscious until the effects wear off. Can also be used as a narcotic. 

 

You take 2d6 poison damage. If this damage reduces you to 0 hit points, you are stable but poisoned for 4 hours, even after regaining hit points, and are unconscious while poisoned in this way. 

You can use this poison to coat one slashing or piercing weapon or a piece of non-firearm ammunition. Applying the poison takes an action. Once applied, the poison affects the next creature hit by the coated weapon. 

 

Distill: Spend an additional 2 infusions to deal an extra 1d6 poison damage.

Sunday, January 30, 2022 

11:38 AM 

Night Draught Poison 

Novice formula 

 

Infusion Cost: 1 (100ml bottle) 

Duration: See Description 

 

Mix the chamomile with lamb's crest and add into boiling water. After the water is almost completely boiled away add a bit of alcohol and honey before making the infusion. The result is a thick yellow liquid that causes a deep, dreamless sleep. Useful to prevent nightmares, or to tranquilize animals or opponents. 

 

At the end of your next turn, roll 5d6. If the result exceeds your current hit points or you are willing, you fall unconscious and enter a dreamless sleep that lasts for as long as your species naturally sleeps (constituting a night's rest). You are also immune to psychic damage and cannot be targeted or detected by abilities that would affect or read your mind. The effect ends if you take damage, or someone uses an action to shake or slap you awake. If you are immune to the poisoned condition, you are immune to this effect. 

You can use this poison to coat one slashing or piercing weapon or a piece of non-firearm ammunition. Applying the poison takes an action. Once applied, the poison affects the next creature hit by the coated weapon. 

 

Distill: Spend an additional 1 infusion to roll an extra 2d6.

Sunday, January 30, 2022 

11:38 AM 

Silver Kiss Poison 

Novice formula 

 

Infusion Cost: 2 (100ml bottle) 

Duration: 1 Minute 

 

Crush mistletoe berries and oleander leaves into a paste. Add toadstool juice and mix in a few drops of hades hair tee before crushing the liquid out of the mixture and making the infusion. The result is a milky liquid with a silver sheen that causes its victim's heart rate and body temperature to spike. Their eyes become bloodshot and their skin red. It is a simple and deadly poison that is perfect for hunters and assassins alike. 

 

At the start of each of your turns for the duration, you must succeed a Constitution saving throw or take 1d6 poison damage.  

You can use this poison to coat one slashing or piercing weapon or a piece of non-firearm ammunition. Applying the poison takes an action. Once applied, the poison affects the next creature hit by the coated weapon. 

 

Distill: Spend an additional 4 infusions to deal an extra 1d6 poison damage. 

 

 

 

 

 

Sunday, January 30, 2022

Silver Kiss Poison 

Novice formula 

 

Infusion Cost: 2 (100ml bottle) 

Duration: 1 Minute 

 

Crush mistletoe berries and oleander leaves into a paste. Add toadstool juice and mix in a few drops of hades hair tee before crushing the liquid out of the mixture and making the infusion. The result is a milky liquid with a silver sheen that causes its victim's heart rate and body temperature to spike. Their eyes become bloodshot and their skin red. It is a simple and deadly poison that is perfect for hunters and assassins alike. 

 

At the start of each of your turns for the duration, you must succeed a Constitution saving throw or take 1d6 poison damage.

Archive 

Oil of Silver's Kiss+ 

Novice formula 

 

Brewing Cost: 1 (100ml bottle) 

Duration: 1 minute. 

 

Crush mistletoe berries into a paste. Add toadstool juice and mix in a few drops of hades hair tee before making the infusion. The result is a milky liquid with a silver sheen that causes its victim's heart rate and body temperature to spike. Their eyes become bloodshot and their skin red. It is a simple and deadly poison that is perfect for hunters and assassins alike. 

 

At the start of each of your turns for the duration, you must succeed a Constitution saving throw or take 1d6 poison damage.  

You can use this oil to coat one slashing or piercing weapon or up to three pieces of non-firearm ammunition. Applying the oil takes an action. A creature hit by the poisoned weapon or ammunition must succeed a Constitution saving throw or take 1d6 poison damage in addition to the weapon's normal effects. 

The oil can be applied in advance, however, once the weapon is unsheathed the oil dries out after one minute. 

 

Distill: Spend an additional 2 brew points to deal an extra 1d6 poison damage. 

 

Conflicting change.

Sunday, January 30, 2022 

11:38 AM 

Swiftshiver Potion 

Novice formula 

 

Infusion Cost: 3 (100ml bottle) 

Duration: 2 Turns 

 

Mix saphire sali with peppermint leaves and waterwisp to make a tea. Mix diced garlic with whole frost lichen and hades hair, then crush the liquid into the previously created tea, before making the infusion. The result is a bright green-blue liquid with black slivers moving through it. It has a refreshing, cold taste and is known for the violent shiver it induces when ingested. The potion increases one's speed and focus substantially, but when it wears off an incapacitating wave of lethargy washes over the user. 

 

You gain an additional action on each of your turns for the duration. 

During the round after this this potion wears off, your movement speed is zero and you are incapacitated until the end of your turn. 

 

Distill: Spend an additional 5 infusions to extend the duration by one turn.

Monday, January 31, 2022 

9:51 PM 

Vitality Potion 

Novice formula 

 

Infusion Cost: 3 (100ml bottle) 

Duration: Instant 

 

Boil sapphire sali and peppermint in water until most of the water is gone and a light blue liquid remains. Filter out the plant matter, add honey and liquid crushed from tobacco, and finally a tiny drop of mistletoe juice. Make the infusion to create a blue potion with yellow tendrils moving through it. It has a very bitter-sweet and cool taste as it goes down. Immediately the drinker will feel refreshed and refocused. 

 

Regain 1d4 + the brewer's infusion ability modifier vitality (minimum 1). 

 

Distill: Spend an additional 3 infusions to increase the recovered vitality by 1d4.

Apprentice (6) 

Sunday, January 30, 2022 

11:37 AM 

Apprentice 

Access to apprentice potion formulas grants you the abilities described under Infuse Potions II and allows you to learn the following formulas. 

 

Infuse Potion II 

As an apprentice you become better at extracting active ingredients and stabilizing your potions so that exposure to air causes them less harm. This allows you to make oils and concentrate potions to the point where they fit into darts, but also gives you another ability that will delight your consumers. With a small additional effort you can incorporate your potion into another treat, like a cookie, praline, a piece of cake or a similar treat without rising its functionality. Not only is such a potion usually tastier, the lack of a bottle also makes it much faster to consume. 

 

Concentrate. You spend an additional 1 infusion when you brew a drinkable potion (a potion that usually can't be used to coat a weapon) to concentrate the active ingredients. This concentrate can be used to fill a throwable dart (you may have the dart deal only 1 piercing damage on a hit), or needle ammunition for use in a blow- or dart gun (see equipment). On a hit, the potion is injected and takes effect on the target as though it has been consumed. Ranged attacks on willing targets have advantage. 

Tasty Treat. You spend an additional 1 infusion when you brew a drinkable potion to incorporate it into a small treat, like a small cookie, bun, praline, muffin, or similar small food type (you must have the ingredients for that food available).  

A potion that is incorporated into a bite sized snack only requires a bonus action to consume, instead of an action.

Sunday, January 30, 2022 

11:38 AM 

Antitoxin Potion 

Apprentice formula 

 

Infusion Cost: 1 (100ml bottle) 

Duration: 1 Hour 

 

Make tee from jewelweed. Crush stinkweed together with blimberry and add the juice before making the infusion. The result is a dark-blue liquid with a green tint. The potion smells of rot and has a sour taste. When consumed it suppresses the effects of poisons in the body and makes them easier to resist. 

 

You gain advantage on saving throws against poison and are resistant to poison damage for the duration. 

 

Distill: Spend an additional 1 infusion to increase the duration by 1 hour.

Sunday, January 30, 2022 

11:38 AM 

Blackhand Poison- 

Apprentice formula 

 

Infusion Cost: 3 (100ml bottle) 

Duration: 1 Minute 

 

Mix stinkpipe with toadstool and black poppy. Crush the mixture into a fine pulp. Squeeze the liquid from oleander and mix it into the pulp, then filter the juice. Repeat until you have enough for a small flask and make the infusion over a low flame. The result is a grey liquid with a pink sheen. When it enters the veins, it damages the body and temporarily inhibits the healing process. 

 

You must succeed a Constitution saving throw or become decayed for the duration. A decayed creature is unable to increase its current hit points and while it is downed, effects that increase its current hit points stabilize it instead. If you are immune to the poisoned condition, you are immune to being decayed by this poison. 

You can use this poison to coat one slashing or piercing weapon or a piece of non-firearm ammunition. Applying the poison takes an action. Once applied, the poison affects the next creature hit by the coated weapon. 

 

Distill: Spend an additional 4 infusions to increase the duration by 1 minute.

Sunday, January 30, 2022 

11:38 AM 

Bliss Poison 

Apprentice formula 

 

Infusion Cost: 2 (100ml bottle) 

Duration: 1 Minute 

 

Crush the juice from a mixture of angel's trumpet and cannabis. Mix the resulting liquid with a few drops of hades hair tee and make the infusion. The result is a pasty white liquid with traces of green. Once introduced into the body, it widens the mind. Those who experience it describe the high as pleasant, but it opens the spirit to psychic attack. 

 

You are poisoned for the duration. While poisoned this way, you automatically fail Intelligence, Wisdom and Charisma saving throws and are revealed by the Contact (Channel Divinity V) ability. At the end of each of your turns you may attempt a Constitution saving throw, ending the effect on a success. 

You can use this poison to coat one slashing or piercing weapon or a piece of non-firearm ammunition. Applying the poison takes an action. Once applied, the poison affects the next creature hit by the coated weapon.

Adept (6) 

Sunday, January 30, 2022 

11:37 AM 

Adept 

Access to adept potion formulas grants you the ability described under Infuse Potions III and allows you to learn the following formulas. 

 

Infuse Potion III 

As an adept you learn how to gas out the active components of mixtures. In the peaceful setting where you prepare your herbal remedies you may use censers and vaporizers for this effect. The smoke and vapors strengthen your patients and allow you to spend much less time on each individual. In potions, the active ingredient that assists in turning it into a so-called Air is often stinkhorn, or a similar herb.   

 

Aroma Remedy. When you use your Herbal Remedy (see Infuse Formula I), you may affect every creature in the immediate vicinity that can breathe in the fumes of your herbal remedy, instead of having to spend an hour on each.

Sunday, January 30, 2022 

11:38 AM 

Air of Sickness 

Adept formula 

 

Infusion Cost: 3 (1l bottle) 

Duration: 1 Minute 

 

Soak crushed pink ivy in a small quantity of alcohol over a low flame. Then add stinkweed juice, filter out the plant matter, add a few drops of a hades hair tee and black poppy, before making the infusion. The result is a volatile bright-green-pink mixture that evaporates the moment it leaves the bottle. The cloud it creates seeps through the skin and induces sickness in all who touch it. The affected vomit and stumble around in the fume. 

 

Open the bottle and throw it up to 20 meters. A green cloud streams out and fills a 5-meter radius sphere centered on the bottle. Its area is lightly obscured. 

Any creature that starts its turn in an affected area, or enters it during its turn, must succeed a Constitution saving throw or take 1d6 poison damage and be poisoned. While poisoned this way, a creature is incapacitated as it spends its turn vomiting. An affected creature that starts its turn outside the cloud may repeat the saving throw, ending the effect on a success. Creatures are affected even if they hold their breath or don't need to breathe. 

The gas lingers for 1 minute. It is dispersed by a breeze in 5 turns, by a gale at the start of your next turn, and has no effect during a storm. 

You may attach the bottle to an arrow, a crossbow bolt, or similar ammunition through crafting (no check required) or fire it from a sling. If you do, you may fire the bottle at any point within the weapon's long range. 

 

Distill: Spend an additional 1 infusion to increase the radius by 1 meter.

Sunday, January 30, 2022 

11:38 AM 

Focus Potion 

Adept formula 

 

Infusion Cost: 3 (100ml bottle) 

Duration: 1 Hour 

 

Make a tea from chamomile, sapphire sali, and tobacco. Distill, then add a single drop of black poppy juice and a few drops of hades hair tee. Once the  infusion is made, the result is an okay tasting brown-blue liquid with slivers of black moving through it. Upon being consumed, it clears the mind and induces a state of unparalleled focus. 

 

You have advantage on Intelligence, Wisdom and Charisma checks and saving throws and your concentration can't be broken for the duration. 

 

Distill: Spend an additional 4 infusions to increase the duration by 1 hour.

Sunday, January 30, 2022 

11:38 AM 

Petrification Poison- 

Adept formula 

 

Infusion Cost: 4 (100ml bottle) 

Duration: 1 Minute 

 

You crush bug carapace into a fine powder and mix it with buckthorn oil. Add sapphire Sali tee and a few drops of hades hair extract before making the infusion. The result is a thick yellow-grey, oily poison that turns the skin of those it touches into a grey, porous rock. 

 

You are poisoned for the duration. While poisoned this way you are restrained. At the end of each of your turns, reduce your current hit points by 2d6 and make a Constitution saving throw, ending the effect on a success. If this effect reduces you to 0 hit points, you are petrified.  

You can use this poison to coat one slashing or piercing weapon or a piece of non-firearm ammunition. Applying the poison takes an action. Once applied, the poison affects the next creature hit by the coated weapon. 

 

Distill: Spend an additional 3 infusions to increase the hit point reduction by 1d6.

Monday, January 31, 2022 

10:41 PM 

Liquid Lust Poison 

Expert formula 

 

Infusion Cost: 5 (100ml bottle) 

Duration: Instant 

 

Crush the fluid out of cannabis, peppermint, and waterwisp pulp. The resulting liquid is mixed with a spirit, before you add a few drops of angel's trumpet essence and the liquid crushed from an inkcap. The final ingredient before the infusion is boiled down redcone and lamb's crest tee that must first be allowed to cool. The resulting pink-red poison induces euphoric lust within its victim. 

 

The next time you see a creature within 10 minutes, you become charmed by that creature for 1 hour or until that creature takes a hostile action. If the creature is of a species and gender you are normally attracted to, you are overwhelmed by lust while you are charmed. If the blood of a creature is added before the potion is infused, the potion only takes effect when you see that creature. 

You can use this poison to coat one slashing or piercing weapon or a piece of non-firearm ammunition. Applying the poison takes an action. Once applied, the poison affects the next creature hit by the coated weapon. 

 

Distill: Spend an additional 1 infusion to increase the duration by 1 hour.

Sunday, January 30, 2022 

11:38 AM 

Panacea Potion- 

Expert formula 

 

Infusion Cost: 4 (100ml bottle) 

Duration: 1 Minute 

 

Crush pink ivy flowers and redcone into a paste. Add a few drops of hades hair and waterwisp tee. After making the infusion you end up with a glittering pink liquid with fog-like white wisps moving through it. Drinking it causes a warm sensation as the potion makes it easier for material needed for healing to move through the body. 

 

If you would roll a die to regain hit points, regain the maximum possible amount instead for the duration. 

 

Distill: Spend an additional 5 infusions to increase the duration by 1 minute.

Sunday, January 30, 2022 

11:38 AM 

Silvershot Potion 

Expert formula 

 

Infusion Cost: 6 

Duration: Instant 

 

Make sapphire sali, peppermint and pink ivy tea and boil it down. Cut garlic, crush the liquid from it and add it to the mix before making the infusion. The result is a silvery liquid with a subtle pink-blue sheen. When ingested it sends the body into overdrive.   

 

You instantly receive the benefits of a night's rest. Until you take a night's rest, you gain a level of exhaustion every hour.

Sunday, January 30, 2022 

11:38 AM 

Air of Mercy 

Master formula 

 

Infusion Cost: 8 (1l bottle) 

Duration: 1 minute 

 

Create a large batch of liquid crushed from stinkhorn and pink periwinkle. Add a few drops of hades hair tee then mix the thick mixture into brine and sunbush oil. The liquid requires massive amounts of energy during infusion to prime, at which point it becomes a swirling yellow-green liquid. It bubbles and boils, only to immediately liquify in the confines of the tightly sealed container. Once released, it creates a thick cloud that smells faintly of garlic. Those who hold their breath might think they are safe, however the poison does not care whether it touches lung or skin. There is no tissue it does not kill, no living thing it will not fell. Silent and painless. A horror and mercy. 

 

Open the bottle and throw it up to 20 meters. A green cloud streams out and fills a 5-meter radius sphere centered on the bottle. The fog spreads around corners and its area is lightly obscured. 

When a creature enters the gas for the first time on a turn or starts its turn there, that creature must make a Constitution saving throw. The creature takes 8d6 poison damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. Creatures are affected even if they hold their breath or don't need to breathe.  

This damage ignores resistance and immunity to poison damage of living creatures. It has no effect on undead or constructs. 

The gas lingers for 1 minute. It is dispersed by a breeze in 5 turns, by a gale at the start of your next turn, and has no effect during a storm. The vapors, being heavier than air, sink to the lowest level of the land, even pouring down openings. 

You may attach the bottle to an arrow, a crossbow bolt, or similar ammunition through crafting (no check required) or fire it from a sling. If you do, you may fire the bottle at any point within the weapon's long range. 

 

Distill: Spend an additional 3 infusions to deal an extra 1d6 poison damage and increase the radius by 1 meter.

Antidote Potion 

Master formula

Any effect you would be immune to if you were immune the poisoned condition ends. You are immune to poison damage and the poisoned condition for the duration.

Enchantements (34) 

Sunday, January 30, 2022 

9:30 PM 

Enchantments 

At the start of every magic item is an enchantment. An enchantment is what the techniques, both arcane and mundane, used to modify a material into being able to store and focus magical energy are referred to. How exactly a witch places an enchantment within a material depends on their specialization, but no matter the details of the process, a material must be malleable to enchant it. This is usually achieved by heating, choosing soft materials that can be backed later, like clay, or by using living materials like amber wood. Either way, the witch begins the infusion by weaving the magical pathways that form the enchantment into the material. This process can take hours, perhaps even days. It is made more difficult by the fact that once the witch starts, a sort of timer begins. The material must not only be kept in a malleable state (a problem for metals in particular), but the enchantment matrix must remain unstable until it is complete. If the enchantment is not finalized after around seven days, the initial stages of the matrix begin decaying and the work spent is wasted. Witches can mitigate this by preparing all mundane components in advance, but not eliminate it entirely. 

Working with other witches, proper tools, and/or blood magic can make enchanting easier. When a witch sacrifices a creature for an enchantment, their energy is used in the witch's stead during the infusion, reducing the strain on the witch, but also giving the material a permanent red tint when the infusion is complete. 

The most common types of enchantments are runes and glyphs.  

Runes are placed in an item and usually draw vitality from their wielder to power themselves. They almost always require attunement, a process during which the user connects with the item and opens themself so that the item may draw power from them.  

Glyphs are enchantments that can be deployed inside a surface at a moment’s notice. It is prepared by creating a glyph chisel, usually from clay. To use the chisel it is placed inside a wooden handle and geld against the surface that the enchantment is to be placed within. A single hammer strike breaks the chisel and a pulse of crack-like light moves through the material and enchants it almost instantly.  

Once an enchantment is complete, it takes the form of crack-like markings where the material is modified to contain magic. These 'cracks' discolor, but do not weaken the stone, metal, wood, or other matter they are placed within. They only modify its molecular structure to contain the arcane structures that contain the enchantment itself. 

While the enchantment is inactive these markings are only visible up close as slight discolorations. When activated though, the energy contained within them flows out and makes the markings shine with light, the color of which depends on the elemental energy used (green for force, prismatic for light, white for cold, blue-white for lightning, orange-red for fire and yellow-gold for acid) and is also occasionally random or a mix of all of the above. 

Since most enchantments have a small margin for error, skilled witches use this to add decorations or text to the enchanted weapon that glows when the enchantment is activated. 

  

Enchanting items relies on your Intelligence score 

•The Difficulty Class of saving throws against enchantments you infuse is determined as follows: 8 + your Proficiency Bonus + your Intelligence modifier.  

  

Every enchantment either tells you what item it creates, or, in the case of runes, what trait it provides an item with. 

You may place more than one enchantment in any given object, however, an item can only be enchanted once. Any enchantments you wish to place within it must be infused together. Because of this, you can only spend a single long rest working on any given item, as the number of infusions you can expend also represents how much exhaustion your body can take when infusing enchanting. 

Unless explicitly stated otherwise, enchanted ammunition or throwable items are always destroyed when used. 

Enchantements (34) 

Sunday, January 30, 2022 

9:30 PM 

Enchantments 

At the start of every magic item is an enchantment. An enchantment is what the techniques, both arcane and mundane, used to modify a material into being able to store and focus magical energy are referred to. How exactly a witch places an enchantment within a material depends on their specialization, but no matter the details of the process, a material must be malleable to enchant it. This is usually achieved by heating, choosing soft materials that can be backed later, like clay, or by using living materials like amber wood. Either way, the witch begins the infusion by weaving the magical pathways that form the enchantment into the material. This process can take hours, perhaps even days. It is made more difficult by the fact that once the witch starts, a sort of timer begins. The material must not only be kept in a malleable state (a problem for metals in particular), but the enchantment matrix must remain unstable until it is complete. If the enchantment is not finalized after around seven days, the initial stages of the matrix begin decaying and the work spent is wasted. Witches can mitigate this by preparing all mundane components in advance, but not eliminate it entirely. 

Working with other witches, proper tools, and/or blood magic can make enchanting easier. When a witch sacrifices a creature for an enchantment, their energy is used in the witch's stead during the infusion, reducing the strain on the witch, but also giving the material a permanent red tint when the infusion is complete. 

The most common types of enchantments are runes and glyphs.  

Runes are placed in an item and usually draw vitality from their wielder to power themselves. They almost always require attunement, a process during which the user connects with the item and opens themself so that the item may draw power from them.  

Glyphs are enchantments that can be deployed inside a surface at a moment’s notice. It is prepared by creating a glyph chisel, usually from clay. To use the chisel it is placed inside a wooden handle and geld against the surface that the enchantment is to be placed within. A single hammer strike breaks the chisel and a pulse of crack-like light moves through the material and enchants it almost instantly.  

Once an enchantment is complete, it takes the form of crack-like markings where the material is modified to contain magic. These 'cracks' discolor, but do not weaken the stone, metal, wood, or other matter they are placed within. They only modify its molecular structure to contain the arcane structures that contain the enchantment itself. 

While the enchantment is inactive these markings are only visible up close as slight discolorations. When activated though, the energy contained within them flows out and makes the markings shine with light, the color of which depends on the elemental energy used (green for force, prismatic for light, white for cold, blue-white for lightning, orange-red for fire and yellow-gold for acid) and is also occasionally random or a mix of all of the above. 

Since most enchantments have a small margin for error, skilled witches use this to add decorations or text to the enchanted weapon that glows when the enchantment is activated. 

  

Enchanting items relies on your Intelligence score 

The Difficulty Class of saving throws against enchantments you infuse is determined as follows: 8 + your Proficiency Bonus + your Intelligence modifier.  

  

Every enchantment either tells you what item it creates, or, in the case of runes, what trait it provides an item with. 

You may place more than one enchantment in any given object, however, an item can only be enchanted once. Any enchantments you wish to place within it must be infused together. Because of this, you can only spend a single long rest working on any given item, as the number of infusions you can expend also represents how much exhaustion your body can take when infusing enchanting. 

Unless explicitly stated otherwise, enchanted ammunition or throwable items are always destroyed when used. 

If an enchantment can be placed on a melee weapon, unless stated otherwise, it can also be infused into small beads, or plates that are woven into gloves or hand wraps and used to enhance the wearer's unarmed strikes. 

  

Item Names 

Many enchantments can be applied to a variety of different items, usually weapons. The name of that item then becomes the name of the enchantment, with the generic descriptor replaced with the original name of the item. For example, if the Hallow Rune enchantment is applied to a longsword, that longsword becomes a Hallow Longsword. 

  

Attunement 

Some enchantments require a creature to form a bond with the magic item before its magical properties can be used. 

Without becoming attuned to an item that requires attunement, a creature gains only its nonmagical benefits, unless its description states otherwise. For example, a magic shield that requires attunement provides the benefits of a normal shield to a creature not attuned to it, but none of its magical properties.  

Attuning to an item requires a creature to spend a long rest with the item in its position, as it slowly gets used to using its arcane properties. This focus can take the form of weapon practice (for a weapon), casting practice (for a wand), or some other appropriate activity. If the long rest is interrupted, the attunement attempt fails. Otherwise, at the end of the long rest, the creature learns how to use the item and forms the arcane bond required for activating its magical properties. When you use a long rest to produce an item that requires attunement, you may attune a creature to it as part of the construction process, as long as that creature is present during the creation of the item. 

An item can be attuned to only one creature at a time, and a creature can only be attuned to a number of magic items equal to its Proficiency Bonus.  

A creature’s attunement to an item ends if the item is destroyed, the creature no longer satisfies the item's prerequisites for attunement, if the creature dies, or if another creature attunes to the item. A creature can also voluntarily end attunement to an item at any time (no action required) unless the item is cursed (see Infuse Enchantment IV). 

  

Wielding Enchanted Items  

Using a magic item’s properties might mean wearing or wielding it. A magic item meant to be worn must be donned in the intended fashion: boots go on the feet, gloves on the hands, hats and helmets on the head, and rings on the fingers. Magic armor must be donned, a shield strapped to the arm, a cloak fastened about the shoulders. A weapon or wand must be held. 

 

Attunement description 

As his hand touches the item he feels a warm impedance, a dullness to its warmth. A presence, like a static charge that permeates the material of the handle. Yet there is a distinct sense of friction that is preventing him from pushing his energy into the object. He can feel patterns inside the magical item and as he slowly pushes his will and energy against these patterns, the energy diffuses in. Like water being slowly pushed into a pipe. Once the connection is complete the flow starts. This initial connection takes a minute and while it is enough to slowly cast the spell out of combat, casting quickly and under pressure requires full attunement.  

 

 

 

acid, cold, fire, force, or lightning damage (your choice during creation) 

 

Attunement, Book II, A New Home 

Kilian reaches past the kobold and closes his hand around the hilt. He feels a presence, like a static charge that permeates the metal in the handle. His strength seeps into the weapon, making it almost an extension of his body.

Wednesday, February 9, 2022 

12:34 PM 

Blinding Rune 

Novice enchantment 

 

Construction Cost: 2 

Requires: - 

 

This enchanted object glistens with white and prismatic markings. Upon impact, the material deforms and explodes in several multi-colored rays that are instantly followed by a blinding flash of white. 

 

You infuse this enchantment into a piece of ammunition or a throwable object. The item gains the following property: 

Flash. As an action, you may throw this bomb at a location no more than 20 meters away. Each creature in a 5-meter radius sphere centered on that point that can see the target location must succeed a Dexterity saving throw or be blinded until the end of its next turn. 

Instead of being thrown, this bomb may be attached to a piece of non-firearm ammunition, extending its range to the range of the weapon it is fired from.

Monday, May 30, 2022 

5:02 PM 

Elemental Rune 

Novice enchantment 

 

Construction Cost: 5 

Requires: Attunement 

 

You enchant a weapon so that when the wielder wills it, it becomes sheathed in elemental energy. 

 

As you create a melee weapon, you may infuse this enchantment into it. The item gains the following property: 

Elemental. While attuned to this item you may spend a bonus action and 2 vitality per turn to deal an additional 1d6 acid, cold, fire, force, or lightning damage (your choice during creation). This effect lasts until you end it (no action required), or the weapon leaves your grip. 

 

Refine: Spend an additional 6 infusions and increase the vitality consumed per turn by 3 to deal an extra 1d6 damage of the chosen type on a hit. 

 

Fire Rune Activation, Book II, Confrontation 

As the orc’s grip tightens around her weapon, lines of orange light race down the geometric patterns that adorn the blade. It hisses, as a burst of heat makes the air around it shimmer.

Friday, May 27, 2022 

9:39 AM 

Glyph of Disenchantment 

Novice enchantment 

 

Construction Cost: 2 

Requires: - 

 

As your glyph chisel strikes a surface, multicolored lines move through it. Glittering dust sparks off the material as the enchantment contained within is purged. It leaves nothing but a grey shadow where the magic once resided. 

 

Expend 2 infusions to create the following item: 

Glyph Chisel of Disenchantment. As an action, strike this glyph chisel against an item that isn't being worn or carried or a surface containing an enchantment and destroy the chisel. The enchantment or electronics inside the material are destroyed without damaging the material that contains it. 

You may also use this chisel to make an improvised melee weapon attack against a celestial, construct, elemental, fiend or undead. On a hit, the creature has its current and maximum hit points reduced by 4d10 and the chisel is destroyed. On a miss, the chisel is not destroyed. 

 

Refine: Spend an additional 1 infusion to reduce a magical construct's current and maximum hit points by an extra 1d10.

Friday, May 27, 2022 

9:39 AM 

Glyph of Harming 

Novice enchantment 

 

Construction Cost: 2 

Requires: - 

 

As the glyph chisel strikes, energy is infused into the ground. Upon activation, elemental energy seeps out of the ground. Those who attempt to run over the glyph will find an area of burning, sparking, freezing, or flesh-melting ground is not easy to cross. 

 

Expend 2 infusions to create the following item: 

Glyph Chisel of Harming. As an action, strike this glyph chisel against a surface and destroy the chisel. You create a glyph that covers no more than a 3-meter radius circle centered on the struck point. After a delay of your choice (you may have the glyph activate instantly), or when you use an action to touch and activate the glyph, it becomes difficult terrain. Any creature that steps on the glyph during its turn, or starts its turn there takes 3d8 acid, cold, fire, force, or lightning damage (your choice during creation). The effect lasts for 1 minute, or until the glyph is deactivated. 

You may connect this glyph to a surveillance sigil (see Infuse Enchantment III) and activate or deactivate it through that sigil. Recharging the glyph after it has run out requires 4 vitality. 

 

Refine: Spend an additional 3 infusions to deal an extra 1d8 and its maximum radius by 1 meter.

Wednesday, February 2, 2022 

11:26 PM 

Smoke Rune 

Novice enchantment 

 

Construction Cost: 1 

Requires: - 

 

You infuse clay with energy as you mold it into shape. Thin runic lines suffuse the material and should it be damaged, the enchantment activates. The rune emits a high-pitched hissing noise and after a short delay explodes into a billowing cloud of smoke that has the same color as the vessel from which it was created.  

 

You infuse this enchantment into a piece of ammunition or a throwable object. The item gains the following property: 

Smoke Bomb. As an action, you may throw this bomb at a location no more than 20 meters away. You create a 5-meter-radius sphere of smoke centered on that point. The sphere spreads around corners, and its area is heavily obscured. The smoke may have any color (your choice during creation), but is dark gray by default. The smoke lingers for 1 minute. It is dispersed by a breeze in 5 turns, by a gale at the start of your next turn, and has no effect during a storm. 

Instead of being thrown, this bomb may be attached to non-firearm ammunition, extending its range to the range of the weapon it is fired from. 

 

Refine: Spend 2 additional infusions to deal an extra 1d6 damage and increase the radius by 1 meter.

Apprentice (6) 

Sunday, January 30, 2022 

11:37 AM 

Apprentice 

Access to apprentice potion formulas grants you the abilities described under Infuse Potions II and allows you to learn the following formulas. 

 

Infuse Potion II 

As an apprentice you become better at extracting active ingredients and stabilizing your potions so that exposure to air causes them less harm. This allows you to make oils and concentrate potions to the point where they fit into darts, but also gives you another ability that will delight your consumers. With a small additional effort you can incorporate your potion into another treat, like a cookie, praline, a piece of cake or a similar treat without rising its functionality. Not only is such a potion usually tastier, the lack of a bottle also makes it much faster to consume. 

 

Concentrate. You spend an additional 1 infusion when you brew a drinkable potion (a potion that usually can't be used to coat a weapon) to concentrate the active ingredients. This concentrate can be used to fill a throwable dart (you may have the dart deal only 1 piercing damage on a hit), or needle ammunition for use in a blow- or dart gun (see equipment). On a hit, the potion is injected and takes effect on the target as though it has been consumed. Ranged attacks on willing targets have advantage. 

Tasty Treat. You spend an additional 1 infusion when you brew a drinkable potion to incorporate it into a small treat, like a small cookie, bun, praline, muffin, or similar small food type (you must have the ingredients for that food available).  

A potion that is incorporated into a bite sized snack only requires a bonus action to consume, instead of an action. 

Conflicting change.

Friday, May 27, 2022 

9:39 AM 

Binding Bands 

Apprentice enchantment 

 

Construction Cost: 2 

Requires: - 

 

You weave magic into threads or bands of metal or fabric. When triggered, the bands wrap around their victim, binding soul and body with their magic. Not even a ghost or fiend can escape this arcane bind. 

 

Expend 2 infusions to create the following item: 

Binding Bands. You may use an action to throw this magical trap at a tiny, small, or medium creature no more than 20 meters away. The target is affected. 

Alternatively, you may spend 1 minute placing the trap. The first time a tiny, small, or medium creature comes within 1 meter of the trap, it triggers and affects that creature. You may also add a sigil (see Infuse Enchantment II) that will allow a creature to bypass the trap unaffected. 

Choose either Damage or Disable during creation. 

Damage. The bands flare with magical energy. An affected creature must succeed a Strength saving throw, or take 2d8 acid, cold, fire, force, or lightning damage (your choice during creation) and be restrained. It may then use its action during its next turn to repeat the save, ending the effect on a success. Otherwise, it takes 3d8 damage of the chosen type at the end of that turn and the effect ends. 

Disable. An affected creature must succeed a Strength saving throw, or be restrained. It may then use its action during its next turn to repeat the save, ending the effect on a success. At the end of that turn, the bands completely envelop it, paralyzing the creature until the bands are destroyed (your choice whether the bands restrict its ability to speak). 

Regardless of the effect, the bands ignore the paralyzed and restrained condition immunities of celestials, elementals, fiends, and undead. An affected creature cannot teleport or leave the current plane and a creature possessing it cannot end the possession. The bands are an object (see the rules on objects) with 11 AC and 11 HP. Once this item has been used, the trap becomes inert when the creature it binds is freed (it can only be used once). 

 

Refine: Spend an additional 4 infusions to affect large creatures, or an additional 8 infusions to affect huge creatures.

Adept (6) 

Sunday, January 30, 2022 

11:37 AM 

Adept 

Access to adept potion formulas grants you the ability described under Infuse Potions III and allows you to learn the following formulas. 

 

Infuse Potion III 

As an adept you learn how to gas out the active components of mixtures. In the peaceful setting where you prepare your herbal remedies you may use censers and vaporizers for this effect. The smoke and vapors strengthen your patients and allow you to spend much less time on each individual. In potions, the active ingredient that assists in turning it into a so-called Air is often stinkhorn, or a similar herb.   

 

Aroma Remedy. When you use your Herbal Remedy (see Infuse Formula I), you may affect every creature in the immediate vicinity that can breathe in the fumes of your herbal remedy, instead of having to spend an hour on each. 

Conflicting change.

Friday, May 27, 2022 

9:39 AM 

Glyph of Protection 

Adept enchantment 

 

Construction Cost: 6 

Requires: - 

 

As the glyph chisel strikes, energy is infused into the ground. Magical energy manifests a storm of magic around the outer perimeter of the glyph. This can act as a barrier keeping undesirables in or out and act as cover. 

 

Expend 4 infusions to create the following item: 

Glyph Chisel of Protection. As an action, strike this glyph chisel against a surface and destroy the chisel. You create a glyph that covers no more than a 3-meter radius circle, or a 9-meter long line. After a delay of your choice (you may have the glyph activate instantly), or when you use an action to touch and activate the glyph, a roaring wall of elemental energy up to four meters high and 1 centimeter thick is emitted by the glyph. If the glyph is a circle, the barrier runs along its circumference. If it's a line, the barrier follows the line. If the barrier cuts through a creature's space when it appears, the creature must succeed a Dexterity saving throw or be affected as though it attempted to pass through the wall. The creature may then move to either side of the wall on its next turn. Any creature that moves through the barrier takes 4d8 acid, cold, fire, force, or lightning damage (your choice during creation). The effect lasts for 1 minute, or until you use an action to deactivate the glyph. The barrier is partially opaque and lightly obscures the area behind it. Any physical projectile, such as an arrow or a bullet, that attempts to pass through it is destroyed. 

You may connect this glyph to a surveillance sigil (see Infuse Enchantment III) and activate or deactivate it through that sigil. Recharging the glyph after it has run out requires 6 vitality. 

 

Refine: Spend an additional 3 infusions to deal an extra 1d8 damage and increase the glyph's maximum radius or length by 1 meter.

Friday, May 27, 2022 

9:39 AM 

Glyph of Warding 

Adept enchantment 

 

Construction Cost: 3 

Requires: See Description 

 

As the glyph chisel strikes a surface, a deadly arcane ward is placed. It waits, almost invisible for a victim to enter its sensor. Should they not wear a sigil key or speak the password, the trap triggers. 

 

Expend 3 infusions to create the following item: 

Glyph Chisel of Warding. As an action, strike this glyph chisel against a surface and destroy the chisel to create a magical trap. The glyph can cover an area no larger than a 3-meter radius but requires a solid, rigid surface no smaller than a 1-meter diameter circle. If it is placed into an object, it expands into the surrounding area. If the surface or object is moved, the glyph either triggers or is dispelled. 

The glyph is nearly invisible and requires a successful Intelligence (Investigation) check against your enchantment save DC to be found. A creature may spend 1 minute scraping off or otherwise damaging the glyph to dispel the enchantment. 

You decide what triggers the glyph when you place it. For glyphs inscribed on a surface, the most typical triggers include touching or standing on the glyph, removing another object covering the glyph, approaching within a certain distance of the glyph, or manipulating the object on which the glyph is inscribed. For glyphs inscribed within an object, the most common triggers include opening that object or approaching within a certain distance of the object.  

You can further refine the trigger so the spell activates only under certain circumstances or according to physical characteristics such as weight or size. You may also add a sigil (see Infuse Enchantment II) that allows the enchantment to be suppressed. This may take the form of a password that, when spoken near the object, suppresses this enchantment for 1 minute, or a sigil key that allows creatures wearing it to pass unharassed. Alternatively, you may connect the glyph to a surveillance sigil. The glyph shares with the sigil whether it is active or not and may either be suppressed or triggered using the connected surveillance sigil. 

When you create the glyph, choose explosive runes or a spell glyph. 

Explosive Runes. When triggered, the glyph explodes in a 5-meter radius sphere centered on the glyph. Each creature in the area must make a Dexterity saving throw. A creature takes 4d8 acid, cold, fire, force, or lightning damage (your choice during creation) on a failed saving throw, or half as much damage on a successful one. 

Spell Glyph. A staff may be infused into the glyph chisel. The staff must be created with the glyph and cannot be created in advance. This effect costs an amount of vitality to place equal to the cost casting the spell placed within the wand would cost the placing creature. When triggered, the glyph casts the spell placed within it, targeting the triggering creature. If the spell allows you to choose a point instead of a creature, you may have it cast at a preprogrammed point, or centered on the triggering creature. If the spell requires concentration, the glyph maintains concentration for as long as the staff was provided energy for. If the spell has a range of self, you may have it be cast within the triggering creature's space, or from a predetermined point on the glyph.  

Once triggered the glyph becomes inert.

Friday, May 27, 2022 

9:39 AM 

Surveillance Glass 

Adept enchantment 

 

Construction Cost: 2 

Requires: Attunement 

 

You form glass, or similar, clear material and infuse into it a surveillance sigil. The material receives an ice-like texture and instead of requiring a cumbersome pool of water, connected enchantments may be controlled and information viewed on this (smaller) object. 

 

When you create a surveillance sigil, you may instead spend 2 infusions to create a surveillance glass. The surveillance glass takes the form of a glass disk or ball, monocle, telescope, or any similar device that contains a transparent or reflective surface that can contain the enchantment. The surveillance glass may have a surface no smaller than 5 centimeters in diameter and no larger than 1 meter in diameter. 

Whenever an enchantment has an interaction with a surveillance sigil, it may instead be connected with a surveillance glass.  

As long as you have the surveillance glass in your possession, you may use an action to expend 1 vitality per hour and use the surveillance glass as though it were a surveillance sigil (see Infuse Enchantment III). 

 

Refine: Spend an additional 2 infusions to increase the maximum diameter of the glass by 1 meter.

Expert (5) 

Sunday, January 30, 2022 

11:37 AM 

Expert 

Access to expert potion formulas grants you the ability described under Infuse Potions IV and allows you to learn the following formulas. 

 

Infuse Potion IV 

You further refine your crafting ability, gaining enough experience to adapt your formulas to different ingredients and harmonize compounds that could normally not be combined into stable, effective potions. In addition to granting you access to more complex formulas, it allows you to combine formulas to create unique combinations. 

 

Fuse Formulas. You may fuse the effects of a potion into another. If over the course of a single long rest you've brewed two potions, you may combine the effects into a single potion. 

Potions that have to be applied to the skin cannot be combined with potions that have to be consumed or ones that create a cloud. Potions that create a cloud also cannot be combined with potions that need to be consumed or injected through application to a blade. 

 

 

Archive 

Harmonious Formulations 

Also at 3rd level, you may combine the effects of a number of potions equal to your Proficiency Bonus into a single potion. This potion's infusion cost equals the cost of the combined potions added together. If you wish to combine an air spell, you may only combine it with another air potion. The same rule applies to oils and balms. Combining two of the same potion has no effects. 

Conflicting change.

Wednesday, February 2, 2022 

9:54 AM 

 

Absorbing Rune 

Expert enchantment 

 

Construction Cost: 6 

Requires: Attunement 

 

Often used with weapons, or placed in small plates that are woven into a glove, this enchantment enhances your attacks with every strike you deliver. As you hit, you drain a small amount of energy from your target. They won’t notice it, but with every attack, the enchantment begins to hum with energy. 

 

As you create a melee weapon, you may infuse this enchantment into it. The item gains the following property: 

Absorb. After hitting a creature with an attack, increase the damage of this weapon by 1 acid, cold, fire, force, lightning, or radiant damage (your choice during creation). This amount resets if you do not damage a creature with an attack using the enchanted weapon before 1 minute has passed.
