Mittwoch, 16. November 2022 

23:43 

Corrupting Rune 

Expert enchantment 

 

Construction Cost: 25 

Requires: Attunement 

Vitality Reduction: 8 

 

As this armament touches flesh it releases corrupting energy into the tissue it passes through. Its touch is lethal. Its wounds do not heal. 

 

As you create a melee weapon, you may infuse this enchantment into it. The item then gains the following property: 

Corrupting. Decrease your maximum vitality by 8. Whenever this weapon deals damage to a creature, reduce that creature's maximum hit points by an amount equal to the damage dealt until the creature finishes a long rest. This trait has no effect on constructs or undead.

Chapter 16: Maneuvers (23) 

Tuesday, July 13, 2021 

9:32 AM 

Maneuvers are exhausting, almost magical martial actions that can be taken in combat. They consist of a specific technique that has a specific effect when performed on an opponent and, once learned, can provide a variety of new options to combatants. From damage to crowd control, there is a maneuver for almost anything. The only downside is that performing a maneuver requires focus and fast and precise movements. Their vitality cost represents the physical exertion a warrior experiences when they push themselves to the limit of their abilities in combat. 

 

Maneuvers rely on your Strength or Dexterity score (your choice) 

•The Difficulty Class of saving throws against maneuvers you use is determined as follows: 8 + your Proficiency Bonus + your Strength or Dexterity modifier, whichever is higher.   

•If a maneuver requires an attack roll, then the modifier for that roll is the same it always is for that type of attack. 

 

 

Maneuvers in Heavy Armor 

Armor makes the fast movements of maneuvers much more tiring. 

While you are wearing heavy armor, double the vitality cost of your maneuvers (if another feature would double the cost of a maneuver, this penalty triples it instead). 

 

 

 

 

Maneuvers provide a variety of different options to martial combatants. They can be used to increase their damage, mobility or to apply crowd control to enemies. There is no disadvantage to using maneuvers, except that they are exhausting and require time and effort to learn. 

Conflicting change.

Adrenaline- 

Beginner maneuver 

 

Vitality Cost: 2 

Use Time: 1 Bonus Action 

Requires: -

Duration: Instant 

 

You push yourself beyond your limit, before you take a moment to gather yourself and regain your footing. 

 

You gain an additional action this turn. A the end of your turn, you are incapacitated until the end of your next turn.

Thursday, January 13, 2022 

8:44 AM 

Cripple 

Beginner maneuver 

 

Vitality Cost: 3 

Use Time: Free 

Requires: Hit 

Range: Attack Range 

Duration: 1 Turn 

 

Aim at their legs just right. With any luck you’ll cripple them. 

 

When you hit a creature with an attack, you may spend 3 vitality and reduce the damage of the attack by half. If you do, the target has its speed reduced to 0 and can’t benefit from an increase to its speed until the end of your next turn.

Precision Strike 

Beginner maneuver 

 

Vitality Cost: 1 

Use Time: Free 

Requires: Weapon Attack 

Range: Attack Range 

Duration: Instant 

 

Aim for a weak spot. Might be harder to hit, but you’ll kill'em if you do. 

 

When you make a weapon attack, you may expend 1 vitality to take a -5 penalty to the attack roll. On a hit, the target takes an additional 10 damage of the attack’s type.

Thursday, January 13, 2022 

8:44 AM 

Taunt 

Beginner maneuver 

 

Vitality Cost: 2 

Use Time: 1 Bonus Action 

Requires: Attack 

Range: Attack Range 

Duration: 1 Minute 

 

Strike someone and taunt them. Do it right and your foe won’t be able to focus on attacking anyone else. 

 

When you hit a creature with a weapon attack, you can use your bonus action and expend 2 vitality to force the creature to make a Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, the target is taunted for the duration, with you being the subject of the taunt. It may repeat the saving throw at the end of its turn. 

A taunted creature has disadvantage on attack rolls not directed at the subject of its taunt. 

 

(Also change giant feature if modified) 

A taunted creature focuses its entire attention on assaulting a single opponent, the subject of its taunt. 

On its turn, a taunted creature must use its action to attack the subject of its taunt with an attack or a similar harmful ability. Attacks it makes against the subject of its taunt have advantage, but all attacks made against the taunted creature have advantage as well, as it fights with reckless abandon.

Thursday, January 13, 2022

Disarm 

Veteran maneuver 

 

Vitality Cost: 3 

Use Time: 1 Bonus Action 

Requires: Melee Hit and a Free Hand 

Range: Attack Range 

Duration: Instant 

 

Strike and as your foe recovers from the trauma, you tear their weapon from their grasp. 

 

After hitting a creature with a melee attack, if you have a free hand, you may use a bonus action and spend 3 vitality to attempt to disarm it. The creature must succeed a Strength or Dexterity saving throw or drop a weapon of your choice that it is wielding. The weapon falls to the ground next to the creature unless you use your free hand to grab it (no action required).

Fleche 

Veteran maneuver 

 

Vitality Cost: 4 

Use Time: 1 Bonus Action 

Requires: - 

Range: Self 

Duration: Concentration, up to 1 Minute 

 

With an intense burst of focus you maneuver across the battlefield, weaving past foes before they have a chance to harass you. Once in the perfect position, you deliver a precise attack. 

 

As a bonus action, you expend 4 vitality to rapidly maneuver. For the duration, you no longer trigger attacks of opportunity, your movement speed increases by 2 meters, and the next attack you make has advantage.

Overwatch 

Veteran maneuver 

 

Vitality Cost: 3 

Use Time: 1 Bonus Action 

Requires: Ranged Attack 

Range: Self (attack range cone) 

Duration: 1 Turn, Concentration 

 

Designate an area and shoot all who dare show themselves.

Thursday, January 13, 2022 

8:44 AM 

Compounding Barrage 

Elite maneuver 

 

Vitality Cost: 5 

Use Time: 1 Action 

Requires: Attack 

Range: Attack Range 

Duration: Instant 

 

One to the body, one to the heart, one to the head. They will be dead. 

 

As an action, expend 5 vitality to make a single attack. On a hit, you may attack the same target again with advantage. If you hit with the second attack, you may attack the target a third time, again with advantage. If the third attack hits, it deals maximum damage. 

 

Make three attacks against a single target. If an attack hits, you gain advantage on the next. If two attacks hit, the third is a critical hit.

Thursday, January 13, 2022 

8:44 AM 

Sweep 

Elite maneuver 

 

Vitality Cost: 6 

Use Time: 1 Action 

Requires: Melee Attack 

Range: Melee Range 

Duration: Instant 

 

You make a sweeping attack, spinning around to hit several foes at once. You either aim for their body for maxim damage, or their legs to knock them down. 

 

As an action, expend 6 vitality to make an unarmed or melee weapon attack that targets every creature in melee range (you roll to hit only once and hit every creature whose armor class you beat). This attack cannot critically strike.  

You may choose to target the creatures' legs instead of their bodies. If you do, the attack deals half damage and each target you hit that is equal to your size or smaller must succeed a Strength saving throw or be knocked prone.

Dash Strike- 

Beginner maneuver 

 

Vitality Cost: 4 

Use Time: 1 Bonus Action 

Requires: Melee Hit 

Range: Attack Range 

Duration: Instant 

 

Flavor 

 

Descr 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Dash Strike 

With a deft flourish, you propel yourself up to 10 feet and then can immediately make an unarmed strike or a melee attack with a weapon you are holding against a creature within your reach. Immediately after you hit or miss with this attack, you can move up to 10 feet without provoking opportunity attacks.   The distance up to which you can travel with this spell increases by 5 feet when you reach 5th level (15 feet), 11th level (20 feet), and 17th level (25 feet). 

 

From <https://www.worldanvil.com/block/1471077>

Descr 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Dash Strike 

With a deft flourish, you propel yourself up to 10 feet and then can immediately make an unarmed strike or a melee attack with a weapon you are holding against a creature within your reach. Immediately after you hit or miss with this attack, you can move up to 10 feet without provoking opportunity attacks.   The distance up to which you can travel with this spell increases by 5 feet when you reach 5th level (15 feet), 11th level (20 feet), and 17th level (25 feet). 

 

From <https://www.worldanvil.com/block/1471077>

Appendix A: Conditions 

Wednesday, December 30, 2020 

2:05 PM 

Appendix A: Conditions 

Conditions alter a creature's capabilities in a variety of ways and can arise as a result of a spell, a class feature, a monster's attack, or other effect. Most conditions, such as blinded, are impairments, but a few, such as invisible, can be advantageous. 

A condition lasts either until it is countered (the prone condition is countered by standing up, for example), for a duration specified by the effect that imposed the condition, or by a duration specified in the condition itself. 

If multiple effects impose the same condition on a creature, each instance of the condition has its own duration, but the condition's effects don't stack. However, some conditions cause effects that would apply them a second time to apply a different condition instead. This does not end the original condition. 

 

Blinded 

A blinded creature can’t see and automatically fails any ability check that requires sight. 

Unless it has tremorsense or blindsight, attack rolls against a blinded creature have advantage, and the creature’s attack rolls have disadvantage. 

 

Camouflaged  

A camouflaged creature blends in with its environment. It is invisible (see the condition) while hidden. It is not invisible to a creature that has successfully determined its approximate location. 

 

Charmed 

A charmed creature can’t attack the charmer or target the charmer with harmful abilities or spells. The charmer has advantage on any ability checks to interact socially with the creature. 

 

Chilled 

A chilled creature has its movement speed halved and can only use an action or a bonus action during its turn, not both.  

Taking a short rest or night's rest or being restored to one's hit point maximum removes the chilled condition. 

A creature that would be chilled while chilled is frozen instead. 

 

Frozen 

A frozen creature is incapacitated and vulnerable to bludgeoning, force and thunder damage. The condition ends at the end of a creature's turn or when it takes bludgeoning, fire, force or thunder damage. If a frozen creature would be scorched, the frozen condition ends and the scorched condition is not applied. 

 

Deafened 

A deafened creature can’t hear and automatically fails any ability check that requires hearing. 

 

Disabled 

A vehicle, construct, or similar mechanical contraption may be disabled. Living creatures cannot be affected by this condition. 

The affected is incapacitated  

The affected automatically fails Strength and Dexterity saving throws. 

Attack rolls against the affected have advantage. 

Any attack that hits the affected is a critical hit if the attacker is within 1 meter of the affected. 

 

Downed 

A creature that is reduced to 0 hit points but not killed instantly is downed. It receives one level of exhaustion (see the condition), falls prone (see the condition), and can't get up before the downed condition ends. While downed, a creature is incapacitated and makes a death saving throw at the end of each of its turns. A creature that is downed by a critical hit fails a death saving throw as part of being downed. When a creature fails a death saving throw it falls unconscious (see the condition) until the downed condition ends. 

While downed but not unconscious, a creature can use its entire turn to: 

Drink a potion.  

Give itself advantage on the death saving throw it makes at the end of its turn. 

Move up to half its speed without provoking opportunity attacks. 

Pretend to be dead by making a Charisma (Performance) check against the Passive Perception of those it is trying to fool. 

If a creature fails three death saving throws, it dies. If a creature succeeds three death saving throws, it becomes stable. A stable creature remains downed, but no longer makes death saving throws. Rolling a natural 20 on a death save causes the creature to regain 1 hit point. Rolling a natural 1 causes it to fail 2 death saving throws. 

The downed condition ends when a creature's hit points increase above 0. 

 

Exhaustion 

Some special abilities, starvation, healing, recovering vitality, and environmental hazards, such as the long-­term effects of freezing or scorching hot temperatures, can lead to exhaustion. 

While you have the Exhaustion condition, you experience the following effects: 

Exhaustion Levels. This condition is cumulative. Each time you receive it, you gain 1 level of Exhaustion. You die instantly if your Exhaustion level reaches 6. 

D20 Rolls. When you make a D20 roll, the result is reduced by 2 for each level of Exhaustion. 

Save DC. Subtract your exhaustion level from your maneuver or spell save DC. 

Speed Reduced. Your Speed is reduced by 1.5 meters (5 feet) for each level of Exhaustion (rounded down). 

Removing Exhaustion Levels. An effect that removes exhaustion reduces the condition's level as specified in the effect’s description, with all exhaustion effects ending if a creature’s exhaustion level is reduced below 1. Finishing a long rest removes all exhaustion, unless that exhaustion is caused by starvation or a similar effect. 

 

Frightened 

A frightened creature has disadvantage on ability checks and attack rolls while the source of its fear is within line of sight. 

The creature can’t willingly move closer to the source of its fear. 

 

Grappled 

A grappled creature’s speed becomes 0, and it can’t benefit from any bonus to its speed. The condition ends if the grappler is incapacitated (see the condition). The condition also ends if an effect removes the grappled creature from the reach of the grappler or grappling effect. 

 

Incapacitated 

An incapacitated creature can’t take actions or reactions. 

 

Invisible 

An invisible creature is impossible, or almost impossible, to see without magic or special senses. The creature’s location can be detected by the noise it makes, the tracks it leaves, or by imperfections in the invisibility. 

Attack rolls against an invisible creature have disadvantage, and the creature’s attack rolls have advantage. An attacking creature still needs to guess the invisible creature's approximate location to have any chance of hitting. 

 

Ionized 

An ionized creature is revealed, lightning spell attacks against it have advantage, it has disadvantage on saving throws against lightning spells, and it cannot activate electronic devices or magic items that aren't wands.  

When an ionized creature takes lightning damage, it takes an additional 1d10 lightning damage and the ionized condition ends. 

Constructs and electronic devices are disabled while ionized. 

	

 

 

Paralyzed 

A paralyzed creature is incapacitated (It can’t take actions or reactions) and can’t move or speak.  

The creature automatically fails Strength and Dexterity saving throws, attack rolls against the creature have advantage, and any attack that hits the creature is a critical hit if the attacker is within 1 meter of the creature. 

 

Petrified 

A petrified creature is transformed, along with any nonmagical object it is wearing or carrying, into a solid inanimate substance (usually stone or metal). Its weight increases by a factor of ten, and it ceases aging. 

The creature is incapacitated (It can’t take actions or reactions), can’t move or speak, and is unaware of its surroundings. 

Attack rolls against the creature have advantage. The creature automatically fails Strength and Dexterity Saving Throws. 

The creature has resistance to all damage. 

The creature is immune psychic damage, poison and disease, although a poison or disease already in its system is suspended, not neutralized. 

 

Poisoned 

A poisoned creature has disadvantage on attack rolls and ability checks and casts spells for twice their final cost. 

 

Prone 

A prone creature moves by crawling at half speed, has disadvantage on attack rolls not made with firearms or crossbows and melee attack rolls against the creature have advantage. Attack rolls against the creature from outside the attacker's melee range have disadvantage. 

A creature can end the condition by using half its movement to stand up. 

 

Restrained 

A restrained creature’s speed is 0, it can’t benefit from any bonus to its speed and has disadvantage on Dexterity saving throws. Attack rolls against the creature have advantage, and the creature’s attack rolls have disadvantage. The creature cannot use maneuvers and casts spells for twice their final cost. 

 

Revealed 

A revealed creature cannot benefit from concealment or invisibility and cannot be hidden. 

 

Scorched 

Any healing the creature receives is halved. If a scorched creature would be scorched again, it takes 1d8 fire damage instead.  

Taking a short or night's rest or being restored to one's hit point maximum removes the scorched condition. 

 

Shocked 

When a creature is shocked, it loses concentration and can't take reactions until the start of its next turn.  

 

Stunned 

A stunned creature is incapacitated (It can’t take actions or reactions), can’t move, and can speak only falteringly.  

The creature automatically fails Strength and Dexterity Saving Throws and attack rolls against the creature have advantage. 

 

Unconscious 

An unconscious creature is incapacitated (It can’t take actions or reactions), can’t move or speak, and is unaware of its surroundings. The creature drops whatever it's holding and falls prone (see the condition), automatically fails Strength and Dexterity saving throws, and is immune to psychic damage.  

Any attack that hits the creature is a critical hit if the attacker is within 1 meter of the creature. 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

-------------------------------------------- 

If a chilled creature would be chilled again, it is frozen instead (see the condition).  

Taking a short rest or night's rest or being restored to one's hit point maximum removes the chilled condition. 

 

Frozen 

A frozen creature is encased in a shell of ice. The creature is incapacitated (It can’t take actions or reactions) and can’t move or speak. 

Attack Rolls against the creature have advantage. 

The creature automatically fails Dexterity Saving Throws. 

The creature has Resistance to all damage. 

At the start of each its turn, a frozen creature may attempt a DC 10 Strength saving throw, ending the condition on a success. Taking fire damage ends the condition instantly. 

 

 

Conflicting change.

Sunday, January 16, 2022 

9:01 PM 

Academy 

The air smells dry, dusty, and of aging paper. Hundreds of books from the Old World and the New archive knowledge both arcane and mundane for future generations. Teachers and professors preserve the wisdom of a long-gone past for a brighter future. 

BP Cost: 10 (once) 

Requirements: School Project 

Score Increase: SOC +2d6, TEC +2d6 

Losing a settler does not remove the bonus it provided to a settlement score by adding one of its skill modifiers to the score. The bonus is lost if the project that allowed the settler to add its skill modifier to the score is abandoned.

Communications Array 

An antenna reaches towards the sky, sending invisible waves to faraway places. 

BP Cost: 3 

Requires: Electrical Grid Project or Adept Enchanting 

Score Increase: TEC +1d6, DEF +1d6 

Allows for long-range communication with any other radio that is no more than 30 kilometers away. This range extends by 10 kilometers every time this project is undertaken after the first. You may also use it to monitor unsecured radio transmissions in range.

Sunday, January 16, 2022 

9:01 PM 

Court of Law 

Dressed in a black jacket, a crude reference to the black robe he always wore in court, the former lawyer carefully listens to the two bickering settlers, while carefully flipping the cracking pages of his old civil code. The people of the New World respect the laws of the old one, for they provide a semblance of order. Those who still understand them provide an alternative to solving disputes through violence. Corporal punishments, hard labor, and exile are the most common penalties for crimes.  

BP Cost: 6 (once)  

Requirements: None 

Score Increase: SOC +4d6, TEC +2d6, DEF +2d6 

Competence: One settler adds its Insight skill modifier to your Society score. 

 

 

 

Sunday, January 16, 2022 

9:01 PM 

Court of Law 

Dressed in a black jacket, a crude reference to the black robe he always wore in court, the former lawyer carefully listens to the two bickering settlers, while carefully flipping the cracking pages of his old civil code. The people of the New World respect the laws of the old one, for they provide a semblance of order. Those who still understand them provide an alternative to solving disputes through violence. Corporal punishments, hard labor, and exile are the most common penalties for crimes.  

BP Cost: 6 (once)  

Requirements: None 

Score Increase: SOC +4d6, TEC +2d6, DEF +2d6 

Competence: One settler adds its Insight skill modifier to your Society score. 

 

 

Conflicting change.

Distillery 

Saturday, January 15, 2022 

1:17 PM 

Distillery 

Dust floats from large windows. In the dim light large tanks filled with yeast and grain produce alcohol that can be served directly or distilled into harder liquor and ethanol fuel. 

BP Cost: 2 

Requirements: Science +4 

Score Increase: SOC +2d6, TEC +3d6 

Score Decrease: FOD -3d6 

Competence: One settler adds its Science skill modifier to your Food score. 

You may refuel vehicles at this settlement for 2 resources per unit of liquid fuel.

Farm 

Sunday, January 9, 2022 

4:51 PM 

Farm 

Fields growing fruits, vegetables, or grain. A farm provides a steady source of high-quality sustenance, but only if it is protected from wild animals and saboteurs. 

BP Cost: 1 

Score Increase: FOD +2d6 

Score Decrease: DEF -1d6 

Competence: One settler adds its Nature skill modifier to your Food score.

Fight Club 

Sunday, January 9, 2022 

4:51 PM 

Fight Club 

A simple pit, cage, or a similar place where the toughest settlers fight each other for sport and to hone their skills. Those less physically inclined watch the matches and bet on the outcome. 

BP Cost: 2 

Score Increase: SOC +1d6, DEF +1d6 

Competence: One settler adds its Athletics skill modifier to your Defense score.

Monday, January 17, 2022 

10:10 AM 

Garage 

In the dim light shining through the yellowed plastic windows in the ceiling, a dwarf searches a box full of parts for just the one the old Mercedes he's repairing needs. The garage is where vehicles go to either be stripped for parts or repaired and set loose on the world. 

BP Cost: 4 

Requirements: Mechanics +4 

Score Increase: TEC +1d6 

A land vehicle that spends a long rest (7 days) in the garage regains all lost hit points. You may also craft land vehicles by expending an amount of scrap equal to half the vehicle's value and succeeding the crafting check specified in the vehicle's stat block next to its value.  

 

 

 

 

 

Many garages include an airstrip, a helicopter landing space, and/or a harbor to service air, land, and water vehicles.

Sunday, January 16, 2022 

9:00 PM 

Harbor 

A light, salty breeze blows waves across the sea. With quiet slaps, they rock the boat of an aging fisher pulling his nets back in. His gaze wanders across the yellowing plastic of his own vessel, onto the bright wood of a brand new boat touching water for the first time. 

BP Cost: 2 

Requirements: Mechanics +4, Access to pen water 

Score Increase: FOD +2d6 

Score Decrease: DEF -1d6 

A water vehicle that spends a long rest (7 days) in the harbor regains all lost hit points. You may also craft water vehicles by expending an amount of scrap equal to half the vehicle's value and succeeding the crafting check specified in the vehicle's stat block next to its value.

Sunday, January 9, 2022 

4:51 PM 

Hospital 

In the bright light of a ceiling lamp, a doctor uses a scalpel and magic to keep the cold hand of death at bay. 

BP Cost: 4 

Requirements: Medicine +4 or Life Magic 

Score Increase: SOC +2d6 

Competence: One settler adds its Medicine skill modifier to your Society score. 

Reduce the body count by 3. When a creature expends hit dice to heal in the hospital, it recovers the maximum amount possible instead of rolling. When a creature finishes a long rest in this settlement, remove one illness or lingering injury that can be cured by succeeding an ability check or a saving throw.

BP Cost: 2 

Score Increase: FOD +3d6 

Competence: One settler adds its Survival skill modifier to your Food score. 

Increase the body count by 1.

Sunday, January 16, 2022 

9:01 PM 

Patrols 

Two men step out of their settlement, weapons in hand. Their eyes are peeled as they walk towards the fields and begin their patrol. As the militia, these men are charged with keeping the peace and warding off would be intruders day and night. 

BP Cost: 6 

Score Increase: DEF +2d6 

Competence: One settler adds its Perception skill modifier to your Defense score. 

Unless a group moves stealthily and succeeds a Dexterity (Stealth) check against your settlement's passive Defense score, you may choose to have your militia confront the creatures.

Saturday, January 15, 2022 

12:12 PM 

Scavenging 

Equipped with tools and rudimentary weapons, settlers organize small expeditions to nearby ruins to gather gear and supplies. Such sorties are lucrative, but also extremely dangerous.  

BP Cost: 4 

Score Increase: FOD +2d6, SOC +2d6, TEC +1d6 

Competence: One settler adds its Investigation skill modifier to a settlement score of your choice. 

Increase the body count by 2.

Monday, January 17, 2022 

10:10 AM 

Scouts 

Armed and hidden by camouflage, the two men watch a settlement, taking careful notes of how many people they see and what they are doing. The purpose of scouts is to watch, maybe even infiltrate, other settlements to gather intelligence and sabotage their operations. 

BP Cost: 4 

Score Increase: DEF +1d6 

Competence: One settler adds its Stealth skill modifier to your Defense score. 

Increase the body count by 1. At the start of a phase, your settlement may use either the sabotage or scout action. 

Sabotage. You attempt to sabotage another settlement's efforts. Choose a development score and make a Defense check against the settlement's passive defense score. On a success, reduce the targeted development score by 1d6. 

Scout. Target another settlement. You learn the settlement's population, development scores, and which projects, if any, it has completed.

Sunday, January 9, 2022 

4:51 PM 

Slave Trade 

The windows are boarded and armed guards watch the entrance and exits. In cages or chained to the walls and floors, slaves wait to be sold or put to use. 

BP Cost: 4 

Requires: At least 4 slaves 

Competence: One settler adds its Intimidation skill modifier to all settlement scores. 

You may enslave creatures and employ them as labor in your settlement. A slave counts as one settler when determining your build points. You may also sell a captured intelligent humanoid for resources equal to half its recruitment value.

Sunday, January 9, 2022 

4:51 PM 

Stable 

The distinct smell of animals permeates the dusty air. Bright rays of light shine onto the axebeak devouring seeds from a troth, separated from the gripper sleeping just one stall away by thick planks of wood and bars of steel. The stable is where animals are kept safe and fed. 

BP Cost: 2 

Requirements: None 

Score Increase: TEC +1d6 DEF +1d6 

Score Decrease: FOD -3d6 

Tamed pets and mounts kept in the stable will not leave, even while they are not currently your companion, and require no upkeep as long as they return to the stable before the next long rest.

Sunday, January 9, 2022 

4:51 PM 

Surveillance Array 

Digital cameras or wards of senses surveil the surrounding area and warn of any incoming danger. 

BP Cost: 4 

Requirements: Electrical Grid Project or Glyph of Senses Enchantment 

Score Increase: DEF +5d6 

You automatically detect any creature moving through your zone of control, unless that creature is invisible or takes some other measure to avoid detection.

Sunday, January 9, 2022 

4:51 PM 

Temple 

A place of worship and contemplation. Some may pray to one of the old gods, some to the new, and others to the spirits or the Void. A priest provides spiritual guidance, emotional support, and training to the willing. 

BP Cost: 1 

Requirements: None 

Score Increase: SOC +1d6 

Competence: One settler adds its Religion skill modifier to your Society score.

Monday, January 17, 2022 

10:10 AM 

Training Field 

An angry drill sergeant shouts at recruits, young and old, as they practice their combat skills. On the training field maggots are made into a military. 

BP Cost: 1 

Requirements: None 

At the start of a phase, you may train one settler by paying the recruitment fee of the stat block they should receive. Refer to the section on training companions in Chapter 7 for details. Some stat blocks may only be available if you have certain equipment or meet other requirements, as per the GM's discretion.

Thursday, January 20, 2022 

2:27 PM 

Witch's Workshop 

The air is dry and smells of alcohol. Herbs hang from the ceiling, while beakers and potions line the walls. The laboratory is where the witch infuses the spark of magic into mundane materials. 

BP Cost: 2 

Requirements: Witch 

Score Increase: SOC +1d6, TEC +1d6, DEF +1d6 

Score Decrease: FOD -1d6 

Competence: One settler adds its Arcana skill modifier to your Technology score. 

While taking a long rest (or otherwise crafting) within the settlement, a witch doubles its proficiency bonus when determining the number of infusions it has available. 

Conflicting change.

Saturday, July 9, 2022 

9:02 AM 

Appendix C: Vehicles (add per round speed to actions) 

 

The following is a list of common vehicles that may be encountered or crafted in the New World with relative ease. They rust away as their internal electronics were damaged, if not completely fried by the radiation emitted during the initial stages of the onsetting fog. However, most parked and unoccupied vehicles remain in working condition. The problem is that all highways are completely clogged with abandoned cars and the condition of country roads worsens with every day they are left unmaintained. While road vehicles are easy to come by, most survivors prefer off-road vehicles.

Friday, March 11, 2022 

12:45 PM 

Bicycle 

A simple, leg-powered vehicle that requires no fuel and is overall a great way to get around the New World. 

 

 

Bicycle 

Medium land vehicle (2m. by 1m.), simple 

 

Armor Class 14 (2 armor) 

Hit Points 30 

Fuel Capacity - 

Fuel per Hour - 

Travel Pace 20 km/h 

--- 

Str: 10 (+0) 

Dex: 14 (+2) 

Con: 10 

Int: N/A 

Wis: N/A 

Cha: N/A 

--- 

Creature Capacity 1 crew, 1 passenger 

Cargo Capacity 20 

Fuel Type manual  

Value 30 

--- 

Man-Powered. The bicycle can only be piloted for as long as the pilot could travel on foot. 

Off-Road. The bicycle can drive over non-road terrain as though it were difficult terrain. 

 

Actions 

Handlebars Control Component, 1 crew 

Move. The bicycle moves up to its speed.

Thursday, June 30, 2022 

9:17 AM 

Hang Glider 

A hang glider is an unpowered flying apparatus for use by a single person, consisting of a frame with a fabric stretched over it. The pilot is suspended from a harness below and controls flight by body movement. 

 

Hang Glider 

Huge air vehicle (2m. by 10m.), simple 

 

Armor Class 6 

Hit Points 10 

Fuel Capacity - 

Fuel per Hour - 

Travel Pace 10 km/h, fly 80 km/h 

--- 

Str: 6 (-2) 

Dex: 6 (-2) 

Con: 10 (+0) 

Int: N/A 

Wis: N/A 

Cha: N/A 

--- 

Creature Capacity 1 crew 

Cargo Capacity 0 

Fuel Type manual  

Value 30 

--- 

Prone. A creature using the hang glider is considered prone. 

Glide. The hang glider must descend at least 1 meter for every 20 meters it moves. 

Stall. The hang glider must move at least 40 meters per turn, or begin to fall. 

 

Actions 

Harness Control Component, 1 crew 

Move. The hang glider moves up to its speed.

Wednesday, March 9, 2022 

1:02 PM 

Car 

A normal car is the most common vehicle type seen today, encompassing almost all modern street cars. These cars litter the roads of the New World.  

 

 

Car 

Large land vehicle (4m. by 2m.), car 

 

Armor Class 12 (5 armor) 

Hit Points 30 

Fuel Capacity 52 

Fuel per Hour 8 

Travel Pace 100 km/h 

--- 

Str: 19 (+4) 

Dex: 9 (-1) 

Con: 17 (+3) 

Int: N/A 

Wis: N/A 

Cha: N/A 

--- 

Creature Capacity 1 crew, 4 passengers 

Cargo Capacity 200 

Fuel Type liquid 

Value 150 

--- 

Cover and Prone. Creatures inside the car have three-quarters cover and are considered prone. Creatures leaning out of the windows only have half cover. 

Electronics. The car contains sensitive electronics and is permanently disabled if hit by an effect that destroys electronics. 

Road Vehicle. The car can only be driven on a paved road. Driving on other terrain causes the car to become disabled until it is towed back to a paved road. 

 

Actions 

Steering Wheel Control Component, 1 crew 

Move. The car moves up to its speed.

Wednesday, March 9, 2022 

1:02 PM 

Pickup Truck 

A two-seated car with a large open deck in the back that can transport a decent amount of goods. 

 

 

Pickup Truck 

Large land vehicle (5m. by 2m.), car 

 

Armor Class 11 (5 armor) 

Hit Points 40 

Fuel Capacity 80 

Fuel per Hour 10 

Travel Pace 100 km/h 

--- 

Str: 19 (+4) 

Dex: 6 (-2) 

Con: 17 (+3) 

Int: N/A 

Wis: N/A 

Cha: N/A 

--- 

Creature Capacity 1 crew, 1 passenger 

Cargo Capacity 700 

Fuel Type liquid 

Value 250 

--- 

Cover. Creatures inside the pickup truck have three-quarters cover and are considered prone. Creatures leaning out of the windows and in the back only have half cover. 

Electronics. The pickup truck contains sensitive electronics and is permanently disabled if hit by an effect that destroys electronics. 

Road Vehicle. The pickup truck can only be driven on a paved road. Driving on other terrain causes the pickup truck to become disabled until it is towed back to a paved road. 

 

Actions 

Steering Wheel Control Component, 1 crew 

Move. The pickup truck moves up to its speed.

Wednesday, March 9, 2022 

1:02 PM 

SUV 

A large, gas-guzzling vehicle that provides ample space and comfort to its passengers. In addition to that, most SUVs come with all-wheel drive, giving them (some) off-road capabilities. This makes SUVs great for the New World, if you can find one in working order and have the gas necessary to fuel it. 

 

 

SUV 

Large land vehicle (5m. by 2m.), car 

 

Armor Class 11 (5 armor) 

Hit Points 40 

Fuel Capacity 80 

Fuel per Hour 10 

Travel Pace 100 km/h 

--- 

Str: 20 (+5) 

Dex: 7 (-2) 

Con: 19 (+4) 

Int: N/A 

Wis: N/A 

Cha: N/A 

--- 

Creature Capacity 1 crew, 7 passengers 

Cargo Capacity 200 

Fuel Type liquid 

Value 200 

--- 

Cover. Creatures inside the SUV have three-quarters cover and are considered prone. Creatures leaning out of the windows only have half cover. 

Electronics. The SUV contains sensitive electronics and is permanently disabled if hit by an effect that destroys electronics. 

Off-Road. The SUV can drive over non-road terrain as though it were difficult terrain. 

 

Actions 

Steering Wheel Control Component, 1 crew 

Move. The SUV moves up to its speed.

Wednesday, March 9, 2022 

1:02 PM 

Airboat 

An airboat is a flat-bottomed amphibious craft propelled by an aircraft-type propeller and powered by an engine. It is specifically designed to navigate shallow and marshy areas by skimming over the water's surface, and has the ability to glide over flat and muddy terrain. 

 

Airboat 

Large land water vehicle (4m. by 2m.), simple 

 

Armor Class 10 

Hit Points 20 

Fuel Capacity 28 

Fuel per Hour 4 

Travel Pace 10 km/h, swim 20 km/h 

--- 

Str: 12 (+1) 

Dex: 14 (+2) 

Con: 10 (+0) 

Int: N/A 

Wis: N/A 

Cha: N/A 

--- 

Creature Capacity 1 crew, 4 passengers 

Cargo Capacity 50 

Fuel Type liquid 

Value 150 

--- 

Surface Bound. The airboat can travel only over the water's surface.  

 

Actions 

Steering Stick Control Component, 1 crew 

Move. The airboat moves up to its speed.

11:31 PM 

Motorcycle 

A motorcycle is a two-wheeled vehicle powered by a motor with no pedals. It can't carry much but can fit through tighter spaces than a car and requires much less fuel. 

 

 

Motorcycle 

Medium land vehicle (2m. by 1m.), motorcycle  

 

Armor Class 13 (2 armor) 

Hit Points 20 

Fuel Capacity 18 

Fuel per Hour 2 

Travel Pace 80 km/h 

--- 

Str: 15 (+2) 

Dex: 13 (+1) 

Con: 12 (+1) 

Int: N/A 

Wis: N/A 

Cha: N/A 

--- 

Creature Capacity 1 crew, 1 passenger 

Cargo Capacity 20 

Fuel Type liquid 

Value 100 

--- 

Road Vehicle. The motorcycle can only be driven on a paved road. Driving on other terrain causes the motorcycle to become disabled until it is towed back to a paved road. 

 

Actions 

Handlebars Control Component, 1 crew 

Move. The motorcycle moves up to its speed.

Wednesday, March 9, 2022 

1:02 PM 

Bus 

A massive vehicle that can transport a large number of passengers and some cargo over vast distances, but is limited by its need for a road and substantial fuel consumption. 

 

 

Bus 

Huge land vehicle (10m. by 3m.), heavy 

 

Armor Class 7 (4 armor) 

Hit Points 65 

Fuel Capacity 300 

Fuel per Hour 20 

Travel Pace 80 km/h 

--- 

Str: 24 (+7) 

Dex: 4 (-3) 

Con: 19 (+4) 

Int: N/A

Cover. Creatures inside the bus have three-quarters cover and are considered prone. Creatures leaning out of the windows only have half cover.

Actions 

Steering Wheel Control Component, 1 crew 

Move. The bus moves up to its speed.

Tuesday, March 8, 2022 

11:31 PM 

Tractor 

A massive, slow, and versatile industrial vehicle designed for working the fields of a farm. 

 

 

Tractor 

Huge land vehicle (5m. by 2m.), heavy 

 

Armor Class 8 (5 armor) 

Hit Points 60 

Fuel Capacity 160 

Fuel per Hour 20 

Travel Pace 30 km/h 

--- 

Str: 26 (+8) 

Dex: 5 (-3) 

Con: 18 (+4) 

Int: N/A 

Wis: N/A 

Cha: N/A 

--- 

Creature Capacity 1 crew 

Cargo Capacity 500 

Fuel Type liquid 

Value 500 

--- 

Cover. Creatures inside the tractor have half cover. 

Off-Road. The tractor can drive over non-road terrain as though it were difficult terrain. 

 

Actions 

Steering Wheel Control Component, 1 crew 

Move. The tractor moves up to its speed. 

 

Note: Trailer 

Value: 100 

You may attach this trailer to a vehicle, increasing its cargo capacity to 3500. A vehicle must have at least 25 Strength to draw this trailer.

Friday, March 11, 2022 

1:04 PM 

Propeller Plane 

A small, four seated air plane with a single propeller, giving it the ability to start and land on its own. Perhaps the safest and most comfortable way to travel, this airplane contains very few and only sturdy electronic components. Most flying creatures simply cannot match this plane's speed, but it is fragile. Spells and anti-air weapons will make quick work of it. 

 

 

Propeller Plane 

Huge air vehicle (8m. by 11m.), plane 

 

Armor Class 7 

Hit Points 20 

Fuel Capacity 200 

Fuel per Hour 40 

Travel Pace 100 km/h, fly 250 km/h 

--- 

Str: 14 (+2) 

Dex: 12 (+1) 

Con: 8 (-1) 

Int: N/A 

Wis: N/A 

Cha: N/A 

--- 

Creature Capacity 1 crew, 3 passengers 

Cargo Capacity 200 

Fuel Type liquid 

Value 600 

--- 

Cover and Prone. Creatures inside the propeller plane have three-quarters cover and are considered prone. Creatures leaning out of the windows only have half cover. 

Land/Take-Off. The propeller plane must move 100 meters in a straight line during a single turn over even ground to take off or land. 

Stall. The propeller plane must move at least 100 meters per turn, or begin to fall. 

 

Actions 

Yoke Control Component, 1 crew 

Move. The propeller plane moves up to its speed.

Tuesday, March 8, 2022 

11:31 PM 

Sailboat 

A boat with an internal engine and massive sails. As long as there is wind this vehicle requires no fuel and can drive massive distances without stopping. The best way to travel at sea. 

 

 

Sailboat 

Huge water vehicle (15m. by 5m.), boat 

 

Armor Class 5 

Hit Points 120 

Fuel Capacity 1500 

Fuel per Hour 15 

Travel Pace 0 km/h, swim 10 km/h 

--- 

Str: 13 (+1) 

Dex: 8 (-1) 

Con: 14 (+2) 

Int: N/A 

Wis: N/A 

Cha: N/A 

--- 

Creature Capacity 3 crew, 4 passengers 

Cargo Capacity 2000 

Fuel Type liquid 

Value 800 

--- 

Cover. The sailboat provides half cover to occupants in the cockpit or stern, three-quarters cover to occupants below deck, and no cover to those forward of the cockpit. 

Surface Bound. The sailboat can travel only over the water's surface.  

 

Actions 

Helm Control Component, 1 crew 

Move. The sailboat moves up to its speed. 

 

Sails Support Component, 2 crew 

As long as this component is fully crewed, and there is any type of wind except still, the sailboat consumes no fuel.

What Comes Next 

Montag, 26. Juni 2023 

13:04 

 

 

The Old World has ended, your patron determined and the New World dawned. You now know how to create a character and how to interact with the many systems needed to accurately simulate survival in a fantastic post-apocalyptic world. 

Even though experimenting with character builds is fun, you will now most likely want to play a Saga of a New World campaign. Content, such as a Game Master Guide, a Starter Adventure and much more is in the works, but for now SoaNW GMs will have to rely on playtest material, or come up with their own content. The Saga of a New World series of novels (which this rules set is being used to write) is an excellent source of inspiration and a glimpse of the world you may explore in your own campaign. It is also an excellent source of a few hours of good entertainment. 

If you are looking for a group to play with, want to stay up-to-date, or just want to provide feedback on what you thing this system could do better, please join our Discord Server using the address or QR code below. If you have any particular praise or criticism, feel free to contact us under contact@saga-of-a-new-world.com. 

If you want to know more about what we're up to, our website saga-of-a-new-world.com provides a handy list of all available content. 

Please enjoy this world and good luck on your journey. 

 

 

 

Archive 

HAVING DELVED INTO T H E DEPTHS OF 

character creation, it's time to consider 

your next steps. The fun of D&D is in 

playing the game, not just making up 

characters (though that's fun, too). 

The Starter Set is a great next step if you want to try 

your hand at being a Dungeon Master and introduce 

your friends to the game. I f you don't know any DMs, try 

taking on that role yourself. The Starter Set is the best 

place to begin your journey. 

The D & D basic rules include some of the information 

provided in this book, along with the material necessary 

to create your own campaign. After absorbing the 

Starter Set, check out the D&D basic rules, available as 

a free download at DungeonsandDragons.com. 

The Monster Manual describes the most important 

monsters in the DUNGEONS & DRAGONS universe. 

The manual is aimed at DMs, but it's also a useful 

reference for players. 

The Dungeon Master's Guide is the ultimate tome 

of DM lore. It includes magic items, optional rules, 

and guidelines for creating everything from a simple 

dungeon to an entire cosmos for your campaign. 

Each year brings the publication of new D&D 

adventures. These scenarios and campaigns are the 

perfect way to try your hand at running a D&D game 

with a minimu m of work.

on our website to see if your local game store is running 

events such as D & D Encounters or D&D Expeditions. 

WHAT C O M E E.IT 

• 

Bring your games to life with a suite of enhancements 

from a robust set of player and DM digital tools to 

accessories like miniatures, vinyl mats, campaign maps, 

clothing, and more. 

It isn't a lways possible to gather a regular gaming 

group. In such situations, D UNGEONS & DRAGONS 

board games present a n ideal casual play experience. 

You'll also find a variety of d i gital games that offer 

a D & D experience across M ac, P C , tablets, and 

mobile devices.
